name: AI Applications in Ireland's Revenue Agency
description: The innovation involves the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in various functions of Ireland's Revenue agency, including customer service, records management, summarizing call center conversations, drafting tax and duty manuals, and predictive analytics for fraud detection.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive application of AI across multiple functions within a government agency, improving efficiency and accuracy in handling data-rich tasks and enhancing customer service through AI-driven query categorization and response systems.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the staff of Ireland's Revenue agency, citizens and businesses interacting with the agency, and potentially other government finance departments observing the implementation for similar applications.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is an initiative by the UK government aimed at fostering AI innovation across various sectors, with a primary focus on driving AI adoption within the public sector. It is supported by a £14 billion investment from private lending technology firms and is expected to influence industries such as financial services and wealth management.
novelty: The plan represents a significant commitment to AI, marking a shift from AI being a niche technology to a strategic necessity, particularly in wealth management. It emphasizes the integration of AI-driven solutions to enhance efficiency, client experience, and regulatory compliance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, private lending technology firms, wealth managers, financial services firms, and tech-savvy clients who benefit from improved services. UK investors are also stakeholders, preferring AI to complement human advisors.

name: Continuous Video Surveillance System in Correctional Facilities
description: A comprehensive video surveillance system implemented across 78 correctional facilities in Kazakhstan, featuring over 39,500 high-definition cameras with AI-driven analytics for real-time facial recognition, event detection, and license plate identification. The system has improved safety, transparency, and accountability in prison management.
novelty: The integration of advanced AI-driven analytics with high-definition video surveillance in correctional facilities, enabling real-time monitoring and proactive incident management, represents a significant innovation in prison management.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the Ministry of Internal Affairs, prison staff, inmates, and the broader community benefiting from enhanced safety and accountability in correctional facilities.

name: AI in Federal Government Procurement
description: The General Services Administration (GSA) implemented artificial intelligence technology to streamline federal government procurement processes, which involve spending around $100 billion a year on goods and services. The initiative includes providing a guide to federal workers on best practices for incorporating AI into public services.
novelty: The innovation lies in using AI to enhance data insights and streamline procurement processes in the federal government, leveraging large language models and governmental data.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal government procurement officers, federal agencies, and ultimately taxpayers who benefit from more efficient government spending.

name: AI Roadmap for Health and Human Services
organisation: Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
description: The AI Roadmap is a comprehensive plan by HHS to integrate artificial intelligence into the health sector, focusing on improving quality, safety, efficiency, accessibility, equitability, and outcomes. It includes goals like catalyzing AI innovation, promoting ethical AI use, and developing AI-ready data repositories.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its 'whole-of-nation' approach, promoting collaboration between industry, academia, and government, and focusing on democratizing AI technology and creating secure environments for AI development.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include AI developers, National Institute of Health (NIH) funded investigators, healthcare organizations, patients, and the general public.

name: BridgeAI programme
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, Innovate UK
description: The BridgeAI programme is a government initiative investing £7 million in AI projects to help small businesses improve productivity and efficiency. It supports 120 projects across various sectors, including agriculture and construction, to adopt AI technology.
novelty: The programme introduces AI to traditional sectors like baking, farming, and road maintenance, demonstrating AI's potential to predict sales, prevent potholes, and detect mould growth, thus showcasing its wide-ranging benefits.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include small businesses such as bakeries, farmers, and road maintenance workers, as well as government bodies and AI experts involved in the implementation and scaling of AI solutions.

name: Safety by Design Toolkit
organisation: Australia’s eSafety Commissioner, Department of Homeland Security
description: The Safety by Design Toolkit is being developed to ensure child safety is prioritized in the development of online products and services. It aims to collate best practices and share innovative approaches for companies to tackle online child sexual exploitation and abuse, particularly in the context of new and emerging technologies like artificial intelligence.
novelty: The project is innovative as it integrates Safety by Design principles into the development of online technologies, ensuring child safety is considered from the outset. It represents a collaborative effort between governments, industry, and civil society to address online child sexual exploitation and abuse.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include technology companies, governments, regulators, law enforcement agencies, non-governmental organizations, and civil society. The beneficiaries are children globally, who will be better protected from online sexual exploitation and abuse.

name: Executive Order on Advancing AI Infrastructure
description: The executive order aims to advance the United States' leadership in AI infrastructure by integrating innovation with sustainability and security. It outlines principles to guide federal agencies in ensuring AI infrastructure supports national priorities while fostering innovation, sustainability, and inclusivity.
novelty: The initiative integrates AI development with clean energy solutions, emphasizing sustainability and security while fostering economic competitiveness. It involves collaboration between federal agencies and the private sector to build advanced computing clusters and secure supply chains.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, private sector companies, small developers, local communities, workers, and state public utility commissions are engaged in the development and implementation of AI infrastructure.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a roadmap commissioned by the UK Government to position Britain as a world leader in AI. It includes 50 recommendations focusing on building AI infrastructure, unlocking data assets, enabling safe AI development, and fostering public-private sector collaboration. The plan aims to improve efficiency and productivity in public services and drive AI adoption across the economy.
novelty: The plan marks a shift in the UK's approach to AI from focusing on safety and risk to opportunity and growth. It promotes a sector-specific, principles-based regulatory framework, contrasting with the EU's comprehensive AI Act, and includes innovative measures like creating a UK Sovereign AI unit.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK Government, private sector AI developers, public sector entities, regulators, and industries such as life sciences, financial services, and creative industries. Beneficiaries are expected to be both public and private sectors, alongside AI companies and innovators.

name: AI Strategic Plan in Healthcare
organisation: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
description: The AI Strategic Plan released by HHS provides recommendations for integrating AI in healthcare, focusing on research, drug development, medical product development, and ensuring safety and effectiveness. It aims to enhance healthcare delivery by leveraging AI technologies.
novelty: The plan emphasizes transparency in AI technologies used in certified EHRs and outlines a detailed value chain for AI enablement in healthcare, marking a structured approach to integrating AI in the sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include healthcare providers, EHR vendors, policymakers, and the federal government. Beneficiaries are patients and healthcare systems that will experience improved healthcare delivery through AI.

name: Standardized Processes Initiative
description: An initiative to consolidate back-office computer systems for over 118 government departments into five shared services centers, replacing around 286 legacy IT systems. The aim is to streamline government processes and improve efficiency, ultimately supporting around half a million civil servants and over a million military personnel, reservists, and veterans.
novelty: The project focuses on creating improved, standardized processes across government departments, utilizing Celonis to identify and optimize cross-departmental processes. It emphasizes the importance of process analysis and change management over simply applying AI as a solution.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include civil servants, military personnel, reservists, and veterans. Stakeholders engaged include the UK Cabinet Office and business process vendor Celonis.

name: Executive Order on National Cybersecurity
description: An Executive Order issued by President Joe Biden to enhance the cybersecurity of the United States by defending digital infrastructure, securing essential services, and developing capacities to counter significant cyber threats. It includes measures to increase accountability for software and cloud service providers, streamline sanctions against hacking groups, and promote secure software development and supply chain practices.
novelty: The Executive Order emphasizes the integration of cybersecurity into the acquisition lifecycle, the use of AI to enhance cyber defense, and the adoption of innovative identity technologies and phishing-resistant authentication options. It also addresses the cybersecurity of space systems and promotes the use of post-quantum cryptography.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, software and cloud service providers, private sector entities, and critical infrastructure operators. Beneficiaries are U.S. citizens and businesses, as well as U.S. allies and partners.

name: Humphrey
organisation: Department for Science, Technology and Innovation (DSIT)
description: Humphrey is a package of AI tools designed to modernize and improve the efficiency of public services in the UK. It aims to speed up the work of civil servants, reduce reliance on consultants, and enhance the policy-making process by quickly analyzing public consultation responses. The tools include Consult, Parlex, Minute, Redbox, and Lex, each serving specific functions to streamline tasks and improve productivity.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive AI toolset designed specifically for public sector productivity, reducing the need for external consultants and enabling faster, more efficient policy-making and service delivery.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are civil servants, policymakers, and public sector organizations. Indirectly, citizens and businesses benefit from improved public services and faster policy implementation.

name: Government Innovation in Pennsylvania
description: The innovation involves updating data systems, AI, and IT infrastructure to improve digital services and customer experience in Pennsylvania's public sector. It aims to enhance government efficiency and accessibility while setting ethical standards for AI and data management.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its integration of advanced technologies like big data into government systems and its focus on ethical standards for AI and data management.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Pennsylvania's government agencies, citizens who benefit from improved public services, and technology providers involved in the infrastructure upgrade.

name: Connect
organisation: Department for Energy, Security and Net Zero, Ofgem, National Energy System Operator
description: Connect is an AI tool developed to speed up the connection of clean energy projects to the national grid, reducing delays for large-scale energy generation projects like wind and solar farms.
novelty: The tool uses AI to better match energy generation projects to available grid capacity, addressing the bottleneck in the grid connection queue and facilitating quicker integration of renewable energy sources.
stakeholders: Energy producers, big tech companies, government departments, and ultimately the general public who benefit from reduced energy bills and increased renewable energy integration.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is an initiative by the UK government to embed artificial intelligence across the country, supported by £14 billion in investments from leading tech firms. The plan aims to boost economic growth, enhance public services, and position the UK as a global leader in AI innovation. It includes proposals for AI Growth Zones, creating 13,250 new jobs, and investing in infrastructure like supercomputers and data centres.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into public services and economic development, establishing AI Growth Zones, and focusing on infrastructure investments to support AI technologies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, leading tech firms, public sector workers, businesses, and the general public who will benefit from enhanced services and job opportunities.

name: AI Sandbox
description: The AI Sandbox is an initiative by the City of Hartford to establish a center for artificial intelligence development. It aims to build a workforce skilled in AI and accelerate innovations in healthcare, insurance, manufacturing, and municipal government. The project involves creating a space for collaboration between industry, academia, non-profits, and government to solve real-world problems using AI.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on creating a collaborative environment that bridges the gap between large corporations and startups, allowing for the development and application of AI solutions to practical problems. It also emphasizes training a future workforce tailored to industry needs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the City of Hartford, local universities and colleges, major corporations like Travelers, Cigna, CVS Health, Hartford Healthcare, and Stanley Black & Decker, as well as the Connecticut AI Alliance. Beneficiaries are Hartford residents and businesses that will gain from AI-driven solutions and job opportunities.

name: Humphrey AI tool
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: A package of AI tools designed to streamline the NHS and other public services, including tools for analyzing government consultations, transcribing meetings, summarizing policies, and researching laws.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across various public service functions, aiming to reduce bureaucracy and improve efficiency in the NHS and other government services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include NHS patients, civil servants, and public sector organizations. Stakeholders engaged include the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology and potentially smaller UK start-ups and scale-ups.

name: Executive Order 14144 on Cybersecurity
description: Executive Order 14144, issued by former President Joe Biden, focuses on strengthening cybersecurity measures across federal agencies and promoting innovation through emerging technologies. It includes actions to improve software supply chain security, enhance endpoint detection and response capabilities, secure space systems, advance cryptographic infrastructure, and encourage digital identities to combat cybercrime.
novelty: The order introduces several innovative measures such as machine-readable secure software development attestations, prioritization of endpoint detection and response, protection of space systems as critical infrastructure, and the use of AI in cybersecurity. It also emphasizes the adoption of digital identities and post-quantum cryptographic algorithms.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, software and cloud service providers, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), National Cyber Director, private sector partners, and beneficiaries include federal systems and the general public.

name: Massachusetts AI Hub
organisation: Executive Office of Technology Services and Security (EOTSS)
description: The Massachusetts AI Hub is an initiative aimed at positioning Massachusetts as a leader in artificial intelligence. It involves strategizing and implementing recommendations from the Governor’s Strategic Task Force on Artificial Intelligence to harness AI's potential for state challenges.
novelty: The project is innovative as it not only creates strategic recommendations for AI leadership but also actively implements them, establishing Massachusetts as a leader in AI through initiatives like the AI Hub and a center of excellence for AI.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Massachusetts state government, technology teams within EOTSS, AI thought leaders, and potentially the residents of Massachusetts who will benefit from improved state services.

name: U.S. Army IT Modernization and Development
description: The U.S. Army's fiscal 2025 IT budget focuses on improving security of electronic communications, data sharing and utilization capabilities through AI tools, and creating a virtual training environment. Key investments include modernization of geospatial intelligence capabilities, automation systems, cryptographic systems, and virtual training environments.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI tools for data sharing and utilization, the creation of a virtual training environment, and the development of a real-time Electro-Magnetic Spectrum situational awareness system.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the U.S. Army personnel, including those involved in intelligence, security, and training operations. Stakeholders include Army Intelligence and Security Enterprise, Army Cyber Command, and various IT and defense contractors.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a strategic roadmap by the UK government to position the nation as a global leader in AI. It includes 50 recommendations to accelerate AI adoption across sectors, foster economic growth, and enhance public services. Key objectives include infrastructure development, economic growth, and AI integration in public services.
novelty: The Action Plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to AI adoption, focusing on infrastructure, economic growth, and public service enhancement. It also addresses legal challenges such as IP rights, regulatory frameworks, and data protection, aiming to balance innovation with ethical considerations.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include various sectors adopting AI, small businesses, and public services. Stakeholders engaged include the UK government, legal professionals, and potentially international AI talent.

name: Executive Order on Strengthening and Promoting Innovation in the Nation’s Cybersecurity
description: A comprehensive initiative aimed at addressing cybersecurity challenges by implementing measures such as minimum cybersecurity standards, enhanced public-private collaboration, sanctions on foreign cyber actors, quantum-resistant cryptography, and AI in cybersecurity.
novelty: The executive order introduces quantum-resistant cryptography and AI-driven cybersecurity applications, emphasizing proactive measures against future quantum computing threats and leveraging AI for real-time threat detection.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, contractors, private sector partners, and businesses involved in cybersecurity.

name: UK AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The UK AI Opportunities Action Plan aims to make the UK a global leader in AI technology by leveraging AI for private businesses and public services. It includes creating data centers, accelerating planning permissions, establishing a National Data Library, and focusing on AI in public services.
novelty: The plan focuses on integrating AI across all public services and creating a National Data Library to provide datasets for AI development, aiming to make the UK a global AI hub.
stakeholders: Beneficiaries include UK residents, private businesses, and global AI developers. Stakeholders engaged include the UK government, AI developers, and international partners for AI safety.

name: AI in Energy project
organisation: Centre for the Fourth Industrial Revolution (C4IR) Azerbaijan
description: The AI in Energy project aims to integrate artificial intelligence into Azerbaijan's energy sector to improve efficiency, sustainability, and grid optimization. It includes pilot projects for optimizing energy consumption, enhancing predictive maintenance, and improving energy efficiency through AI-driven demand forecasting.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to localizing global AI solutions for Azerbaijan's unique energy challenges, focusing on AI-driven energy optimization and sustainability, and serving as scalable models for other countries.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the Azerbaijani energy sector, public and private sector entities, universities, research institutions, and potentially other countries looking to adopt similar models. Stakeholders engaged include the World Economic Forum, BrainBox, and Vestas.

name: Humphrey
description: The UK Government's 'Humphrey' suite is a collection of AI tools designed to modernize public services by streamlining civil service tasks and enhancing decision-making processes. It aims to reduce reliance on outdated systems and improve efficiency in handling tasks such as parliamentary analysis, meeting transcription, legal research, and public consultation processing.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its comprehensive integration of AI tools across various public service functions, offering predictive insights, automated processing, and enhanced information retrieval, which collectively aim to modernize and improve the efficiency of public services.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include civil servants, policymakers, and public sector organizations. Beneficiaries are the general public, including individuals with long-term health conditions or disabilities, who will experience improved public services and accessibility.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
organisation: UK Department for Science, Innovation & Technology (DSIT)
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a strategic initiative by the UK government to leverage public procurement to enhance public services, shape AI markets, and boost the domestic AI ecosystem. It includes a 'Scan > Pilot > Scale' approach to identify AI opportunities, streamline procurement, and scale successful pilots across the public sector.
novelty: The innovation lies in its structured approach to AI procurement, emphasizing rapid scaling of pilots, reducing bureaucratic barriers for startups, and fostering public-private collaboration through initiatives like the UK Sovereign AI unit.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government bodies, AI vendors, startups, NHS Trusts, and private sector partners involved in AI development and deployment.

name: AI Pilot Projects for Public Services
organisation: West Northamptonshire Council, North Northamptonshire Council
description: The councils are piloting AI projects to enhance public services, including homelessness prevention, special educational needs assessments, and adult social care note-taking. The aim is to improve efficiency and service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in the application of AI to identify at-risk individuals earlier, streamline educational assessments, and automate note-taking in social care, shifting staff focus from repetitive tasks to more rewarding work.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include residents requiring council services such as homelessness support, special educational needs assessments, and adult social care. Stakeholders include council staff and AI technology providers like Humley.

name: Executive Order on Strengthening and Promoting Innovation in the Nation’s Cybersecurity
organisation: United States Government, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
description: A comprehensive directive issued by President Joe Biden to address cyber threats by establishing a national strategy for improving cybersecurity across federal agencies, private businesses, and critical infrastructure sectors. It includes new cybersecurity standards for federal contractors, enhanced public-private information sharing, promotion of advanced technologies like quantum-resistant cryptography and AI, and sanctions on foreign cyber actors.
novelty: The Executive Order promotes the adoption of quantum-resistant cryptography and AI-driven cybersecurity tools, which are advanced technologies aimed at countering sophisticated cyber threats. It also emphasizes enhanced public-private information sharing and imposes sanctions on foreign cyber actors, demonstrating a comprehensive approach to national cybersecurity.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, private businesses, critical infrastructure sectors, federal contractors, technology innovators, industry leaders, and government stakeholders are involved. Beneficiaries include the manufacturing sector and other industries vulnerable to cyber threats.

name: Autonomous Public Bus Services Pilot
description: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) is launching a pilot program to test autonomous public bus services. The pilot will start with smaller buses with at least 16 seats on Services 400 and 191. The goal is to assess the technical feasibility and operational aspects of autonomous vehicle technology for public bus services. The pilot will run for an initial period of three years starting mid-2026.
novelty: This project is innovative as it involves the integration of autonomous vehicle technology into public transportation, specifically for bus services, which includes real-time monitoring and a phased approach to ensure safety and reliability.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Land Transport Authority (LTA), SBS Transit Ltd (SBST), the selected tenderer, bus captains as safety operators, customer service officers, and the commuting public in Singapore.

name: A-cubed and Aigent
description: A-cubed was designed to assist staff in guiding jobseekers into employment, while Aigent aimed to expedite personal independence payments for people with disabilities. These AI prototypes were part of efforts to enhance efficiency in the welfare system.
novelty: The innovation lies in the application of AI to streamline and improve the efficiency of welfare services, specifically targeting job placement and disability benefit processing.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are jobseekers and individuals with disabilities relying on personal independence payments. Stakeholders include the Department for Work and Pensions and technology companies involved in AI development.

name: National AI Strategy (NAIS) 2.0
organisation: Ministry of Digital Development and Information, Government Technology Agency (GovTech)
description: Singapore's National AI Strategy 2.0 aims to apply AI for public good by empowering people and addressing societal needs. It includes broad-based adoption of AI tools for public officers, domain transformation in specialized areas like education and maritime, and building ecosystem capabilities through platforms like LaunchPad and programs like LAUNCH!.
novelty: The strategy focuses on making every public officer a 'technologist' with central AI tools, transforming traditional workflows in specialized domains, and fostering a culture of AI innovation through collaborative platforms and events.
stakeholders: Public officers, government agencies, students, maritime sector professionals, local communities, and industries in Singapore.

name: BharatGen
description: BharatGen is the world's first government-funded multimodal large language model (LLM) initiative. It aims to develop a language model tailored to India's linguistic and cultural diversity, enhancing India's influence in AI research and application.
novelty: BharatGen is innovative as it is the first government-funded initiative to create a multimodal large language model specifically designed for a country's diverse linguistic and cultural landscape.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Ministry of Science and Technology, AI researchers, developers, and the diverse linguistic communities of India who will benefit from AI applications tailored to their cultural context.

name: Access Assure, Technology Enabled Care (TEC)
description: Access Assure is a technology-enabled care solution that helps residents live independently by monitoring their data and alerting carers and family/friends where necessary. It uses AI-enabled sensors to detect changes in daily routines of vulnerable people, such as missed meals or skipped cups of tea, to identify potential health declines and send alerts to family or carers.
novelty: The innovation uses AI and machine learning to shift from a reactive to a proactive care model, allowing for early intervention and enabling individuals to live independently for longer.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are vulnerable residents of Sutton who wish to live independently. Stakeholders include the London Borough of Sutton, The Access Group, Medequip Connect, family members, and carers of the residents.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a series of 50 recommendations aimed at maximizing AI opportunities to drive economic growth and improve public services in the UK. It includes initiatives like creating a National Data Library, enhancing AI regulation, investing in AI infrastructure, and promoting AI adoption across public and private sectors.
novelty: The Action Plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across various sectors, focusing on foundational investments, cross-economy adoption, and positioning the UK as a leader in AI development rather than a follower.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI researchers, innovators, public sector entities, private sector industries, regulators, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services and economic growth.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a set of recommendations aimed at establishing the UK as a global AI superpower by leveraging advancements in AI to drive economic growth, enhance public services, and foster innovation. It includes the development of a sovereign AI compute environment supported by a national cloud infrastructure to securely store, process, and analyze sensitive data within the UK.
novelty: The plan emphasizes the creation of a sovereign AI ecosystem with a national cloud infrastructure, aligning with national security, privacy, and data sovereignty requirements. It also involves innovative energy solutions like Small Modular Reactors to address the energy demands of AI data centers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, tech entrepreneurs, AI data center operators, energy providers, and the general public who will benefit from enhanced public services and economic growth.

name: Tech Incubator Enhancement Grants Program
organisation: Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity
description: The Tech Incubator Enhancement Grants Program provides $7 million in funding to support startups and small businesses in Illinois, focusing on key growth industries such as life sciences, quantum computing, AI, and more. It aims to modernize existing incubators and establish new ones in underserved areas.
novelty: The program is innovative in its focus on modernizing incubators and establishing new ones in underserved areas, thereby strengthening the state's innovation system and supporting emerging technology startups.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include technology startups, small businesses, and entrepreneurs, particularly in underserved areas. Stakeholders engaged include the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity and the state government.

name: Small Business Research Initiative (SBRI) Cancer Challenge
organisation: Welsh Government, NI Department for the Economy
description: The SBRI Cancer Challenge funds five innovative projects aimed at reducing waiting times and improving outcomes for cancer patients through technology. The projects focus on early and faster diagnosis, treatment efficiency, and palliative care support.
novelty: This initiative is the first of its kind, focusing on leveraging technology and innovation to address cancer diagnosis and treatment challenges, including AI-powered diagnostics and genomic data usage.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include cancer patients, healthcare providers, the NHS, and the companies involved in developing the technologies.

name: AI Strategic Plan
description: TxDOT's AI Strategic Plan includes 230 AI use cases to enhance operations, safety, and service delivery. It features pilot programs like AI-driven pricing prediction for construction projects, automatic incident detection on roadways, and automated invoice verification for engineering services.
novelty: The plan positions TxDOT at the forefront of AI adoption in transportation, with a comprehensive approach to integrating AI across various operational areas, setting a benchmark for other states.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Texas drivers and communities, while stakeholders engaged include TxDOT, industry partners, and the Texas Legislature.

name: Public Sector AI Framework
description: The Public Sector AI Framework is a non-binding guideline released by the New Zealand Government to support public sector agencies in the responsible use of artificial intelligence. It aims to modernize public services and deliver better outcomes for New Zealanders by promoting best practices in AI use.
novelty: The Framework is innovative as it aligns with the OECD's AI principles and incorporates a comprehensive approach with six pillars, including governance, guardrails, capability, innovation, social licence, and global voice, to ensure safe and trustworthy AI use in the public sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies in New Zealand, government officials, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services. The Government Chief Digital Officer is leading the implementation.

name: AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service
description: The AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service is Canada's first comprehensive strategy aimed at aligning and accelerating responsible AI adoption across the government. It focuses on enhancing service delivery, automating tasks, and bolstering research capacity while ensuring ethical and responsible use of AI.
novelty: This strategy is innovative as it integrates AI into the federal public service with a focus on ethical standards, collaboration, and inclusivity. It emphasizes human-centered design, transparency, and accountability, setting a precedent for AI use in government.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canadian citizens, public servants, research institutes, academia, industry, civil society, Indigenous organizations, and the Digital Governance Council.

name: Generative AI (Gen AI) Integration
description: The Trump administration's deregulation efforts aim to accelerate the development and integration of Generative AI (Gen AI) across various sectors, including public services and businesses. This initiative allows for the rapid deployment of AI-driven systems without extensive regulatory scrutiny, potentially increasing productivity and transforming service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in the removal of regulatory constraints, allowing for faster experimentation and integration of Gen AI, which can produce human-like text, images, and code at an unprecedented scale.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include businesses, public sector agencies, technology developers, and end-users such as citizens and consumers who benefit from enhanced services and productivity.

name: Public Service Artificial Intelligence (AI) Framework
description: The Public Service AI Framework is designed to support leaders, decision-makers, practitioners, and influencers in New Zealand's public service agencies to adopt AI safely and securely. It aims to improve customer experience, boost efficiency and productivity, and create value for New Zealanders while maintaining trust in public services.
novelty: The framework is innovative as it provides a structured approach to adopting AI in public services, focusing on safety, security, and trust, while also building AI knowledge and skills within the public sector.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public service leaders, decision-makers, practitioners, influencers, and the general public in New Zealand. Stakeholders engaged include the Government Chief Digital Officer and key public service agencies.

name: Centre of Excellence in AI for Education
description: A Centre of Excellence in Artificial Intelligence (AI) for education will be set up with a total outlay of Rs 500 crore to foster advanced research, AI-enabled learning tools, and industry-academia partnerships, equipping the next generation with skills needed for transformative change.
novelty: The project aims to close the AI talent gap in India, ensuring the country is not just a consumer of AI but a creator of cutting-edge solutions.
stakeholders: Students, educational institutions, industry partners, and the government are the primary stakeholders, benefiting from enhanced AI education and research capabilities.

name: AI-enabled sensors for home care
description: An initiative using AI sensors in the homes of vulnerable people to assist in their care, allowing them to remain in their own home for longer while providing peace of mind for families and carers. The sensors track movement, temperature, light, and appliance use to identify routines and alert carers when help is needed.
novelty: The project uses AI to learn and recognize individual routines over time, detecting deviations such as missed meals or falls, and alerting carers without using intrusive cameras or microphones.
stakeholders: The users are vulnerable individuals, such as the elderly or those with dementia, while the beneficiaries include their families and carers. The stakeholders engaged include Sutton Council and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

name: Responsible AI Guidance for the Public Service
description: Guidelines released to set clear expectations for how agencies should adopt AI to improve productivity and service delivery in the public sector, ensuring safe and responsible use.
novelty: The guidance provides a structured approach to safely deploy AI in the public service, emphasizing transparency, trust, and accountability.
stakeholders: Public sector agencies, Government Chief Digital Officer, Ministry of Business Innovation and Employment, New Zealand citizens as beneficiaries.

name: Pro-Innovation Culture in UK Public Services
description: The National Audit Office (NAO) is advocating for a pro-innovation culture in the UK public sector to boost productivity and resilience. This includes setting a clear risk appetite for innovation investments, fostering a culture of fast learning, ensuring effective accountability, and harnessing new technologies like AI.
novelty: The innovation focuses on creating a structured approach to innovation in public services, emphasizing a culture of fast learning, risk management, and the use of AI to enhance productivity and resilience.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, public sector employees, and citizens who benefit from improved public services.

name: Tawakkalna 2.0
organisation: Saudi Data and Artificial Intelligence Authority
description: Tawakkalna is a comprehensive digital hub providing access to over 600 government services, originally introduced to manage COVID-19 restrictions and now evolved to include a wide range of services such as personal data management, medical records, and more. It is available in seven languages and accessible in 77 countries.
novelty: Tawakkalna integrates AI to streamline government services, enhance user experience, and consolidate multiple services into a single platform, setting a precedent for digital governance.
stakeholders: Users include Saudi citizens, residents, and visitors. Beneficiaries are the general public and government/private sector partners who engage with users through the platform.

name: Louisiana Innovation (LA.IO)
description: Louisiana Innovation (LA.IO) is a new division of LED aimed at making Louisiana a leader in developing high-growth, technology-enabled startups. It includes initiatives like the Louisiana Growth Fund and the Louisiana Institute for Artificial Intelligence, focusing on AI research, commercialization, and workforce development.
novelty: The initiative unites various partners to create a comprehensive innovation ecosystem in Louisiana, focusing on AI and startup growth, with significant funding and collaboration across multiple sectors.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include startups, small businesses, and entrepreneurs in Louisiana. Stakeholders engaged include LED, equity and incubator partners, universities, and innovation funds.

name: Cloud First Policy
description: The Cloud First Policy is a government initiative aimed at driving digital transformation in the public sector by prioritizing cloud services. It seeks to improve the quality and efficiency of public services through updated cloud policies and a holistic approach to digital transformation.
novelty: The policy emphasizes a modern, data-driven approach to civil service operations, moving beyond traditional procurement to leverage cloud computing, data analytics, and AI for more agile and efficient public services.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include government departments and citizens who will experience improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include technology providers and public sector organizations involved in implementing cloud solutions.

name: Artificial Intelligence Action Plan
organisation: White House Office of Science & Technology Policy, National Science Foundation
description: The Artificial Intelligence Action Plan is an initiative to develop a comprehensive strategy to sustain and enhance America's global AI dominance, promoting human flourishing, economic competitiveness, and national security. It seeks public input on priority policy actions across various AI-related topics.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves a broad public consultation process to shape national AI policy, focusing on removing barriers to AI development and deployment while addressing a wide range of policy topics.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include AI developers, private sector companies, government agencies, and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include policymakers, industry experts, and the public providing input.

name: TAMM 3.0
organisation: Department of Government Enablement – Abu Dhabi (DGE)
description: TAMM 3.0 is an AI-powered government services platform that leverages Microsoft technologies to provide over 950 services with 24/7 personalised recommendations and a multilingual AI-powered assistant, transforming public service delivery in Abu Dhabi.
novelty: TAMM 3.0 integrates advanced AI technologies such as Dynamics 365, Power BI, and Azure to create a seamless, personalised, and efficient public service experience, setting a global benchmark for AI-driven governance.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries of TAMM 3.0 include citizens, residents, visitors, and businesses in Abu Dhabi. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the Department of Government Enablement and Microsoft.

name: AI Stack as Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)
description: The AI Stack as DPI is a comprehensive framework designed to integrate AI into public services, enhancing efficiency and citizen experiences. It includes infrastructure, data, model development, application, and governance layers, enabling ministries and departments to build AI applications for predictive and proactive services.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a unified AI stack as a digital public infrastructure that can be used across various government ministries and departments, preventing duplication and fostering a collaborative AI ecosystem.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Indian government ministries and departments, citizens, businesses, startups, and the private industry, all of whom can benefit from or contribute to the AI stack.

name: National Artificial Intelligence Policy, 2081
organisation: Ministry of Communications and Information Technology
description: The National Artificial Intelligence Policy, 2081 aims to systematically develop and encourage the use of artificial intelligence in Nepal. It seeks to transform socio-economic and administrative sectors, improve governance, create business opportunities, and promote innovation through AI. The policy outlines objectives for a secure AI ecosystem, skilled human resources, and enhanced public service delivery.
novelty: The policy introduces a comprehensive framework for AI development and regulation in Nepal, including the formation of an AI Regulatory Council, National AI Centre, and AI Excellence Centres to coordinate and guide AI use and growth.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include sectors such as education, health, transport, tourism, agriculture, finance, and entertainment. Stakeholders engaged include government bodies, AI developers, businesses, and the general public.

name: IKN Green Energy Grid
description: Development of a green energy grid to power the new capital city, IKN, using an 86-hectare solar farm integrated with Kalimantan’s conventional grid system. The project includes a substation with automated Gas Insulated Switchgears to supply 120 MVA, sufficient for IKN’s administrative area.
novelty: Integration of solar power with conventional grid systems using advanced technology like automated Gas Insulated Switchgears, enabling a hybrid energy supply for a new capital city.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the residents and administrative bodies of IKN. Stakeholders engaged include PLN, Hitachi Energy, and potentially local communities benefiting from sustainable energy sources.

name: Advancing Computing Analytics, Big Data, and Artificial Intelligence in the Philippines (ACABAI-PH)
description: ACABAI-PH aims to make AI more accessible to various stakeholders, allowing them to utilize AI solutions without requiring extensive technical expertise. It addresses issues like supply chain inefficiencies and improves disaster response through an AI virtual hub offering accessible AI tools.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its AI-as-a-service ecosystem, which democratizes AI tools for businesses, researchers, and communities, enabling them to implement AI solutions without needing extensive technical expertise.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include businesses, researchers, communities, and micro, small, and medium enterprises. Stakeholders engaged include the Department of Science and Technology and other AI R&D initiatives.

name: Thang Rath Superapp
description: Thang Rath is a superapp developed by the Thai government that integrates a wide range of government services into one platform. Initially launched with 30 services, it has expanded to 173 services, facilitating efficient citizen-service delivery and aiming to incorporate AI for smarter service search and personalized citizen services.
novelty: The innovation lies in its integration of multiple government services into a single platform, transforming service delivery and planning to incorporate AI for enhanced personalization and proactive governance.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are Thai citizens who access government services through the app. Stakeholders include government agencies involved in service delivery and digital governance.

name: AI Growth Zones
description: The AI Growth Zones initiative aims to establish dedicated areas across the UK for AI infrastructure development, attracting private investment and creating thousands of jobs. These zones will focus on revitalising local communities, particularly in deindustrialised areas, by speeding up planning permissions and building necessary infrastructure like data centres.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to transforming deindustrialised areas into AI development hubs, leveraging existing power connections and infrastructure to rapidly establish AI Growth Zones. It combines AI technology with sustainable energy solutions to drive economic growth and public service improvements.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include local and regional authorities, industry partners such as energy companies and data centre developers, and the UK government. Beneficiaries are local communities, particularly in deindustrialised areas, and the broader UK economy.

name: AI Growth Zones
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The UK government is developing AI growth zones to speed up planning permission for AI-led initiatives, focusing on deindustrialised areas. These zones aim to attract investment, create jobs, and enhance public services by building datacentre capacity and energy infrastructure.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to regional development by integrating AI infrastructure with local planning, aiming to transform deindustrialised areas into tech hubs.
stakeholders: Local and regional authorities, energy companies, datacentre developers, AI companies, and local communities in deindustrialised areas.

name: CivTech 10 AI Projects
description: CivTech 10 is a programme by the Scottish Government that funds AI projects to address challenges faced by charities and public sector organisations. It has awarded up to £9 million to 14 companies developing AI products, including software to identify toxic contaminants for firefighters, AI systems for teachers' administrative tasks, and drones for monitoring puffin populations.
novelty: CivTech 10 is the first round of the CivTech programme to focus specifically on AI, aiming to revolutionize public sector operations by collaborating with businesses to develop innovative AI products.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector organisations, charities, companies developing AI technologies, and beneficiaries such as firefighters, teachers, entrepreneurs, and wildlife conservationists.

name: Billington State and Local CyberSecurity Summit
description: The Billington State and Local CyberSecurity Summit is an event designed to address cybersecurity challenges faced by state, local, and federal governments. It brings together stakeholders from various sectors to discuss and operationalize cybersecurity harmonization across government and industry.
novelty: The summit is unique in its focus on building cross-public and private relationships at both state and local levels, as well as with the federal sector. It includes discussions on proactive actions against cyber adversaries, AI's role in cybersecurity, and regulatory harmonization through public-private partnerships.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include state and local government cybersecurity officials, federal cybersecurity leaders, private sector entities, AI researchers, and public-private partnership participants.

name: USPTO Artificial Intelligence (AI) Strategy
organisation: United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO)
description: The USPTO AI Strategy outlines responsible implementation of AI within the agency and across the intellectual property landscape. It identifies five focus areas: IP Policy Development, AI Capabilities, Responsible AI Use, Workforce Expertise, and Collaboration, each with specific actions to advance AI innovation and maintain US leadership in the field.
novelty: This is the first comprehensive effort by the USPTO to outline challenges and goals for utilizing AI, demonstrating a commitment to fostering AI development and leveraging its benefits across the IP landscape.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include inventors, attorneys, AI researchers, practitioners, USPTO staff, and the broader innovation ecosystem, including under-resourced communities and international partners.

name: Whole-of-Government agreement with AWS
organisation: Australian Government’s Digital Transformation Agency (DTA) and Amazon Web Services (AWS)
description: A new agreement between the Australian Government's Digital Transformation Agency and Amazon Web Services to enhance digital transformation across the Australian public sector, offering benefits like a standardised contracting framework, cloud upskilling, and access to AWS services.
novelty: The agreement expands on previous collaborations by providing a standardised contracting framework, ethical supplier conduct, and access to cutting-edge technologies like AI and quantum computing.
stakeholders: Australian federal, state, and territory agencies, local councils, public sector entities, AWS, and Australian citizens.

name: Centralised AI-powered Data Platform
description: A centralised, AI-powered data platform aimed at enabling real-time sharing of insights and collaboration between government agencies like health, education, and housing. It is intended to identify connections that may not be immediately obvious, improving public service outcomes.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to facilitate real-time data sharing and collaboration across multiple government sectors, which is expected to lead to better public service outcomes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies such as health, education, and housing, as well as the general public who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Idaho IT Consolidation Initiative
organisation: Idaho Office of Information Technology Services (ITS)
description: Idaho's IT consolidation initiative aims to centralize IT services across more than 50 state agencies, enhancing cybersecurity and AI capabilities. The initiative has led to improved citizen engagement through state-controlled digital services and websites.
novelty: The initiative is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to IT consolidation, real-environment cybersecurity testing, and the development of AI policies and frameworks.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state agencies, local governments, state employees, and citizens of Idaho. Beneficiaries are primarily state agencies and localities that receive enhanced IT and cybersecurity support.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a government initiative involving a multibillion-pound investment to enhance the UK's AI capacity. It aims to increase AI computing power under public control by 20 times by 2030 and integrate AI into the public sector to improve services and reduce costs through automation.
novelty: The plan emphasizes a decentralized innovation ecosystem to serve the public good, moving beyond unbalanced relationships with digital monopolies and aiming to treat AI as a general-purpose technology that can transform all sectors.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, tech firms, public sector entities, and the general public who will benefit from improved services and reduced costs.

name: New Zealand Public Service AI Framework
description: A framework introduced by the New Zealand Government to guide the responsible use of AI technologies across the public sector. It sets out best practice principles for AI adoption to modernise public services and deliver better outcomes for New Zealanders.
novelty: The framework is non-binding and principles-based, aligning with the government's light-touch, proportionate, and risk-based approach to AI regulation. It leverages existing laws and introduces new regulation only to unlock innovation or address acute risks.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include New Zealand citizens, taxpayers, and public service workers. Stakeholders engaged include public service agencies, the Government Chief Digital Officer, and the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE).

name: TraffEase
organisation: Loughborough University, Nottingham City Council, Transport for Greater Manchester
description: TraffEase is a research project exploring the application of generative AI innovations in transport, aiming to demonstrate scalable AI solutions for public services.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on generative AI applications in transport, showcasing scalable solutions and the art of the possible in AI-driven public service improvements.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include local authorities, policymakers, and the general public benefiting from improved transport services. Stakeholders engaged include Loughborough University researchers, Nottingham City Council, Transport for Greater Manchester, and national policymakers.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a UK government initiative aimed at significantly boosting the country's AI capacity. It involves a multibillion-pound investment to increase AI computing power under public control by 20 times by 2030 and to integrate AI into public services to enhance efficiency and reduce costs.
novelty: The plan represents a large-scale, government-led effort to transform AI into a general-purpose technology that can improve public services and economic productivity. It emphasizes public control and strategic investment in AI infrastructure.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK government bodies, tech firms, public service users, and potentially small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) that could benefit from government support to grow in the AI sector.

name: Claude
description: The UK government is exploring the use of Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude to improve interactions with public information and services, aiming to boost productivity in public services through AI.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves using an advanced AI chatbot to enhance accessibility and efficiency in public services, aligning with the UK's goal to become an AI superpower.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public and businesses in the UK, with stakeholders including the UK government and Anthropic.

name: Claude
description: The UK government is exploring the use of Anthropic's AI chatbot, Claude, to improve interactions with public information and services. This initiative aims to enhance productivity in public services through AI.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves using a cutting-edge AI chatbot to streamline access to public information, potentially transforming how citizens interact with government services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, public service users, and Anthropic as the technology provider.

name: InvestAI
description: InvestAI is an initiative by the European Union to invest €50 billion in the development and application of artificial intelligence. It aims to support industrial and mission-critical AI applications, including the establishment of AI gigafactories to train large AI models. The initiative is part of a larger effort to mobilize €200 billion in AI investment over five years, combining public and private funds.
novelty: InvestAI represents the largest public-private partnership in the world for developing trustworthy AI, focusing on industrial applications and leveraging Europe's unique industrial data and know-how. It includes the creation of AI gigafactories with significant computing power, aiming to foster innovation beyond just financial capabilities.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include researchers, companies, start-ups, SMEs, entrepreneurs, investors, and EU member states. Beneficiaries are AI developers and businesses applying AI, as well as industries collaborating on AI projects.

name: Food Tech Valley
description: Food Tech Valley is a technology hub in Dubai designed to foster clean, tech-based food and agricultural products. It aims to triple the UAE’s food production and increase agricultural resilience.
novelty: The project will feature a high-tech, waste-to-value gigafarm capable of growing over 3 million kilograms of produce annually with on-site food waste recycling, using smart AI-driven vertical farming technology.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include local farmers, agricultural entrepreneurs, and the UAE population. Stakeholders engaged include the government, private sector partners like ReFarm, and technology providers.

name: House Bill 1622
description: A proposed bill that would grant state government employees in Washington the bargaining power over the use of artificial intelligence tools within their agencies, impacting job descriptions and salaries.
novelty: The bill introduces the concept of bargaining over AI usage in public sector employment, recognizing the significant impact AI can have on job roles and compensation.
stakeholders: State government employees, labor unions, Washington State Legislature, Office of Financial Management, Washington State AI Task Force, Washington Technology Solutions.

name: Harnessing Subscribers and Data in the Public Sector
description: The innovation focuses on using subscribers and data to enhance citizen engagement, improve public services, and build stronger relationships between local government and constituents. It involves leveraging email, AI, and data-driven insights to personalise communication and services, thereby increasing efficiency and trust.
novelty: The project is innovative in its strategic use of AI and data to personalise citizen experiences, moving beyond traditional newsletters to create targeted, relevant communications that pre-empt public demand and reduce costs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local government authorities, public sector organisations, and citizens who are the users and beneficiaries of the enhanced communication and services.

name: Smart Government
organisation: Various government agencies and private sector partners
description: Smart Government uses advanced technologies like IoT, AI, big data, and cloud computing to enhance public services, improve governance efficiency, and promote citizen engagement. It aims to optimize decision-making, deliver real-time services, and ensure transparency and accountability in government operations.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating IoT, AI, and big data analytics to create sustainable, data-driven solutions for urban development, public safety, transportation, healthcare, and environmental management, reducing bureaucracy and enhancing citizens' quality of life.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens of urban areas, government agencies, and private sector partners involved in public services and infrastructure development.

name: Advanced AI Technologies for Public Services
organisation: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) and Anthropic
description: The UK government and Anthropic have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to explore the use of advanced AI technologies to enhance public services for UK citizens. The collaboration aims to improve how citizens access government information and services online, while ensuring privacy and building trust.
novelty: The project focuses on the responsible deployment of frontier AI capabilities in the public sector, leveraging Anthropic's AI model, Claude, to enhance citizen experiences and improve government service responsiveness.
stakeholders: UK citizens, UK government, Anthropic, AI Security Institute, and potentially other government agencies and organizations involved in AI innovation and deployment.

name: Simply Readable
description: Simply Readable is a Claude-based tool hosted on Amazon Bedrock that reformats documents to be more accessible for people with disabilities by increasing font size, spacing, and adding images. It significantly reduces the cost of converting documents, allowing funds to be redirected to other social services.
novelty: The tool offers a cost-effective solution for making documents accessible, achieving a 749,900 percent return on investment by reducing conversion costs from around £600 to just 7-10 pence per document.
stakeholders: The primary beneficiaries are people with disabilities who require accessible documents. Swindon Borough Council and the UK's Local Government Association are stakeholders involved in implementing and promoting the tool.

name: Maha Hackathon Challenge 1.0
organisation: Government of Maharashtra, Bhashini, Government of India
description: Maha Hackathon Challenge 1.0 is an initiative launched to harness AI and technology for solving real-world governance and disaster management challenges in Maharashtra. It aims to drive AI-led innovation and digital inclusivity across the state through AI-driven governance solutions, real-time speech translation, and drone-based disaster management.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on integrating AI-driven multilingual solutions into governance and disaster management, including real-time speech translation and drone-based technologies, to enhance digital inclusivity and administrative efficiency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Government of Maharashtra, Bhashini, participants of the hackathon, citizens of Maharashtra, and various government platforms like e-Gram Swaraj, Aadhaar, and CPGRAMS. Beneficiaries are primarily the citizens who will experience improved access to services.

name: Data-Driven Approach and AI in Public Sector
description: The innovation involves integrating trusted data and advanced technologies, particularly AI, to revolutionize public sector service delivery, enhance citizen experiences, and drive operational efficiency. It includes unifying data to reduce manual workloads, using AI for personalized services, and ensuring data security while fostering innovation.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on unifying data across government departments and leveraging AI to provide personalized, efficient, and secure public services. It also highlights the potential economic benefits of AI adoption in the public sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, public sector employees, and citizens who benefit from improved service delivery and personalized experiences. Salesforce is a key stakeholder providing the technology and insights.

name: AI Security Institute Revamp
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The former AI Safety Institute has been renamed to the AI Security Institute, focusing on serious AI risks with security implications, such as the use of AI in developing chemical and biological weapons, cyberattacks, and enabling crimes like fraud and child sexual abuse. The institute will work closely with the Ministry of Defence and the Home Office to address these issues.
novelty: The innovation lies in the institute's narrowed focus on security risks associated with AI, moving away from issues like bias and free speech, and the establishment of a 'criminal misuse team' to tackle AI-related crime and security threats.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, particularly the Ministry of Defence and the Home Office, as well as the citizens of the UK who are the beneficiaries of enhanced security measures.

name: Commercial Innovation Hub and AI Tool for Contracts
description: The innovation involves the creation of a Commercial Innovation Hub to improve procurement processes by sharing best practices and embedding lessons from past events. Additionally, an AI tool is being developed to assist commercial teams in redacting contracts and providing quality assurance for procurement documents.
novelty: The project introduces a centralized hub to act as a 'golden link' across government departments, fostering innovative commercial solutions and leveraging lessons from extraordinary events like the Covid-19 pandemic. The AI tool aims to streamline contract management processes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government departments, procurement professionals, councils, NHS bodies, small businesses, and firms that win government contracts.

name: Government Digitisation Initiative
description: An initiative to modernise the New Zealand government's use of data and AI, aiming to improve efficiency and service delivery.
novelty: The project focuses on integrating advanced technologies like AI and data analytics into public services, encouraging a shift from a culture of saying 'no' to embracing technological change.
stakeholders: New Zealand public servants, government agencies, and the general public as beneficiaries of improved services.

name: Digital Government Index (DGI)
description: The Digital Government Index (DGI) measures the extent to which government services are available, accessible, and responsive to the needs of all citizens. It focuses on improving citizen experience by making government services more seamless and cost-effective.
novelty: The project introduces a citizen-centric approach to digital government services, emphasizing user-friendly design, accessibility, and the integration of AI for personalized experiences.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Singaporean citizens as users and beneficiaries, government agencies implementing the services, and Adobe providing the technological support.

name: AI in Tamil Nadu's E-Governance
description: Tamil Nadu is leveraging AI to enhance e-governance by implementing AI-driven monitoring systems across various departments to improve citizen service delivery. This includes digitalizing land, revenue, and registration-related transactions and exploring the use of OpenAI's models to speed up e-sevai services.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI with existing e-governance systems to monitor application processes, introduce public reporting systems, and use intelligent systems for departmental performance assessment, potentially disrupting traditional bureaucratic processes.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include Tamil Nadu's government departments, citizens who use public services, and private sector entities involved in AI development. Beneficiaries are the general public who will experience improved service delivery.

name: DeepSeek AI Model Integration
organisation: Guangzhou Municipal Government, Hohhot Government, DeepSeek
description: The integration of DeepSeek's open-source AI model into local government services to improve access to policy information, boost document processing efficiency, and enhance hotline services for complaints and suggestions.
novelty: The use of an open-source AI model to lower the threshold for AI applications in government services, improving efficiency and accuracy in public service delivery.
stakeholders: Local residents, government officials, business professionals, and enterprises in cities like Guangzhou, Hohhot, and others in China.

name: IDA Ireland Strategy 2025-2029
description: The IDA Ireland Strategy 2025-2029 aims to strengthen Ireland's foreign direct investment (FDI) base by focusing on long-term investment, sustainable change, cutting-edge innovation, and maximizing regional opportunities. The strategy sets ambitious targets, including securing €7bn in new RD&I investment, delivering 550 regional investments, reducing client carbon emissions by 35%, creating 75,000 jobs, and upskilling 40,000 people.
novelty: The strategy is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating digitalisation, AI, sustainability, and regional development into Ireland's FDI framework. It emphasizes collaborative RD&I, leveraging EU instruments like IPCEI, and developing next-generation strategic sites to attract large-scale, sustainable investments.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include IDA Ireland client companies, the Irish government, regional authorities, Enterprise Ireland, Research Ireland, and other national and EU-level partners. Beneficiaries are the Irish economy, regional communities, and sectors like technology, life sciences, and green economy.

name: Strategic Innovation Center
organisation: New Jersey Economic Development Authority (NJEDA) and Nokia Bell Labs
description: The Strategic Innovation Center (SIC) consists of the NJ Nokia Bell Labs Innovation Center and Bell Labs Venture Studio, aimed at enabling startups to accelerate and commercialize intellectual property from Nokia Bell Labs and local universities, focusing on communication, AI, cloud computing, and optical and wireless networks.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on accelerating and commercializing intellectual property from a renowned research institution like Nokia Bell Labs, combined with local university resources, to foster cutting-edge technology development and bolster the state's innovation economy.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include entrepreneurs, startups, researchers, local universities, and the broader New Jersey innovation ecosystem. Beneficiaries are primarily emerging companies and startups in the fields of communication, AI, cloud computing, and networks.

name: CivTech 10 Projects for Scottish Fire and Rescue Service
organisation: Scottish Government, Scottish Fire and Rescue Service
description: The CivTech 10 programme includes projects focused on improving firefighter situational awareness and monitoring contaminants. The situational awareness project, developed by Rowden, aims to provide firefighters with real-time data to enhance decision-making during emergencies. The contaminants project, led by FireHazResearch, aims to detect and monitor firefighters' exposure to toxins.
novelty: The CivTech 10 programme is the first to focus on AI solutions for public sector challenges, specifically targeting firefighter safety and operational efficiency through real-time data and contaminant monitoring.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the Scottish Fire and Rescue Service, firefighters, and the public. The projects also involve tech firms like Rowden and FireHazResearch, as well as academic experts.

name: AI-Driven e-Government Transformation
description: Lạng Sơn is implementing artificial intelligence to digitize its e-government system, aiming for 80-95% of administrative processes to be conducted online. This includes a digital administrative database and a real-time management information center to improve decision-making and service delivery.
novelty: The project leverages AI to streamline workflows, enhance information retrieval, and predict trends and risks, significantly improving efficiency and service quality in public administration.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include government officials, residents, and businesses in Lạng Sơn who benefit from improved public services and streamlined administrative processes.

name: DeepSeek
organisation: Meizhou government, Urban Operation Management Center in Wuxi, Futian district in Shenzhen, Second Affiliated Hospital of Fujian Medical University
description: DeepSeek is an AI technology integrated into public service systems to streamline tasks such as intelligent text-based chatbot responses, AI-assisted form filling, intelligent work order classification, and automated case routing. It improves response speed and resolution time for public inquiries and assists in administrative governance and clinical assistance.
novelty: DeepSeek introduces AI capabilities into public services, reducing call waiting times and improving document processing accuracy. It enhances productivity by providing rapid responses and relevant data pools, and supports analysis in various sectors, including government and healthcare.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens using public service hotlines, government employees, community workers, and hospital staff. Stakeholders engaged include local government bodies, hospitals, and AI experts.

name: Diia Ecosystem
organisation: Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine
description: The Diia ecosystem is a comprehensive digital platform that simplifies government services, supports education and businesses, and enhances digital literacy. It includes the Diia app, Diia Web Portal, Diia.Business, Diia.Education, Diia.City, and Diia.Engine, each designed to empower citizens and build resilience.
novelty: The Diia ecosystem is innovative due to its mobile-first design, integration of AI for proactive services, open-source technology, and focus on inclusivity and interoperability. It offers fully digital services like passports and business registration, and has been recognized globally as a model for digital transformation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Ukrainian citizens, entrepreneurs, SMEs, government agencies, and international partners. Beneficiaries are citizens accessing digital services, businesses using Diia.Business, and individuals improving digital skills through Diia.Education.

name: National Data Library (NDL)
organisation: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, The Entrepreneurs Network
description: The National Data Library (NDL) aims to unlock the full potential of public-sector data by enabling secure, seamless, quick, and scalable access to linked data sets. It is designed to drive AI-enabled innovation, transform public services, and fuel the development of solutions to national challenges tailored to the UK context.
novelty: The NDL is innovative in its approach to reimagining data infrastructure for an AI-enabled future, focusing on creating a secure, federated system for accessing linked data sets without centralizing data. It aims to establish legal, technical, and governance structures to efficiently use high-value data while maintaining security and trust.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government departments, researchers, businesses, and citizens.
