Beneficiaries are expected to be policymakers, researchers, and businesses who will have improved access to data for innovation and decision-making, ultimately benefiting the public through enhanced public services.

name: AIOps
description: AIOps uses AI and machine learning to optimize IT operations by analyzing IT data, detecting anomalies, and enabling proactive issue resolution. It helps federal agencies improve productivity and citizen services by automating IT processes and reducing administrative burdens.
novelty: AIOps is innovative because it leverages AI and machine learning to automate IT operations, providing proactive solutions and reducing the need for manual data analysis. When combined with unified observability, it offers comprehensive insights and automates complex troubleshooting workflows.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal IT teams, agency leaders, and the American public who benefit from improved citizen services and productivity. Industry partners are also engaged to implement AIOps effectively.

name: AI-powered chatbot "Reggie"
description: An AI-powered chatbot named "Reggie" facilitates real-time, two-way communication for Kent County residents, providing instant answers to inquiries by pulling data from multiple systems such as real estate property records and public meeting schedules.
novelty: Reggie not only answers questions but learns from interactions, improving its responses over time. It enhances citizen services by offering instant information and reducing wait times for county office inquiries.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are Kent County residents who need information about county services. Stakeholders include the Kent County IT department and local government officials.

name: Modernization of IT Procurement Processes
organisation: Pennsylvania Department of General Services’ Bureau of Procurement, Commonwealth Office of Digital Experience (CODE PA)
description: The innovation involves streamlining and standardizing Pennsylvania's IT procurement practices by removing unnecessary bureaucracy, increasing competition for state contracts, and protecting taxpayer dollars from project delays and cost overruns. Key changes include reducing the number of questions agencies must answer before purchasing IT by 75%, consolidating IT policies, and piloting generative AI for policy drafting.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its approach of breaking down IT projects into smaller, manageable scopes, using generative AI for policy drafting, and creating a new digital service called Invitation to Qualify (ITQ) for vendor pre-qualification.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state agencies in Pennsylvania, IT vendors, and taxpayers who benefit from more efficient and cost-effective procurement processes.

name: AI-driven remote notification systems for fire stations
organisation: City of Tampa, Department of Technology and Innovation
description: The City of Tampa is modernizing its fire station operations by incorporating AI-driven remote notification systems in each firehouse. These systems include sensors for fuel and gas spills, ensure bay doors are closed at night, automate tasks, and notify only necessary personnel, enhancing safety and efficiency.
novelty: The innovation introduces AI-driven systems similar to Alexa for fire stations, providing automated notifications and task management to improve safety and operational efficiency.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the firefighters and fire station personnel in Tampa. The stakeholders include the City of Tampa's Department of Technology and Innovation and the broader community benefiting from improved fire response services.

name: TrackInspect
organisation: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), Google Public Sector
description: TrackInspect is a pilot program that retrofits subway trains with sensors and uses cloud and AI capabilities to detect and analyze potential track defects. It aims to enhance the track inspection process, making the subway system safer and more reliable by detecting potential track issues before they escalate into operational problems.
novelty: The innovation integrates sensor hardware with advanced cloud and AI capabilities, including Generative AI for natural language processing, to proactively detect track defects. It is the first of its kind to use AI technology for subway maintenance, combining real-time sensor data with predictive insights to improve railway reliability.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include subway riders and track inspectors. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation are the MTA, Google Public Sector, and New York City Transit (NYCT) track inspectors.

name: 21st Century Integrated Digital Experience Act (IDEA)
description: The 21st Century Integrated Digital Experience Act (IDEA) mandates federal agencies to update their websites and digital services to be more user-friendly, secure, and efficient. The act aims to improve service delivery for millions of Americans relying on federal digital platforms.
novelty: IDEA focuses on modernizing federal digital services by enhancing website usability, security, and efficiency, incorporating human-centered design, and leveraging data and AI for improved service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include American citizens and organizations relying on federal services. Stakeholders engaged include federal agencies, the Office of Management and Budget, and private industry experts.

name: EU AI Act
description: The EU AI Act is a comprehensive legal framework designed to regulate the development and use of AI systems within the EU, particularly focusing on high-risk AI systems to ensure they are safe, ethical, and transparent.
novelty: The EU AI Act is the world's most comprehensive legal guideline for AI regulation, introducing a risk classification system and imposing strict requirements on high-risk AI systems.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI developers, tech companies, European startups, and EU citizens who are the beneficiaries of safer AI practices.

name: Quantum Corridor
organisation: State of Illinois, University of Illinois, several universities, IBM
description: The Quantum Corridor is a quantum computing complex designed to synergize with Chicago's economy and expand North America's competitiveness. It links downtown business and international research networks with Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park facilities, and includes AI-related applications.
novelty: The project integrates quantum computing with AI applications to enhance regional economic competitiveness and includes a federal-funded quantum proving ground facility. It also features shared cryogenic cooling facilities and aims to transform neighborhoods through data-driven community partnerships.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include businesses, research networks, educational institutions, and local communities. Stakeholders engaged include the State of Illinois, universities, IBM, and various community organizations.

name: All-flash, power-efficient storage environment
description: The innovation involves migrating to an all-flash, power-efficient storage environment to support modern and user-focused capabilities in Singapore's public sector. This infrastructure is designed to handle AI workloads, reduce data footprint, and improve productivity, cost savings, and uptime for government agencies.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of all-flash storage solutions that provide low latency, high speed, and scalability, enabling efficient management of growing data volumes from AI adoption. It also significantly reduces energy consumption and space requirements compared to legacy HDD systems.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are Singapore's government agencies, IT teams, and developers who benefit from improved data infrastructure, as well as the general public who receive enhanced digital services.

name: AI Sandbox and XR Sandbox
organisation: Ministry of Communications and Information Technology (MCIT)
description: The AI Sandbox and XR Sandbox are experimental initiatives launched within the TASMU Innovation Lab to drive technological progress and innovation in Qatar’s digital entrepreneurship landscape. They enable startups, researchers, and developers to explore experimental solutions in AI and extended reality, expanding their applications to advance economic growth.
novelty: These initiatives provide a platform for experimentation with AI and extended reality technologies, allowing for the development of innovative solutions and applications that can drive economic growth and digital entrepreneurship.
stakeholders: Startups, researchers, developers, digital entrepreneurs, and the broader ICT sector in Qatar.

name: AI Framework for Public Service
description: The New Zealand government has announced an AI framework for the public service, providing guidance and a roadmap for safely deploying AI across government departments. This initiative aims to enhance digital interactions between citizens and government departments, improve efficiency, productivity, and modernise legacy systems.
novelty: The framework focuses on leveraging AI to modernise legacy systems and improve service offerings, while also promoting cross-border collaborations and creating communities of practice for knowledge sharing.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government departments, public service officials, citizens using digital services, and digital identity service providers. The private sector is also involved in integrating digital identity credentials.

name: GenAI-powered tools for public auditing
description: The UK’s Government Internal Audit Agency (GIAA) has developed GenAI-powered tools, including Risk Engine and Writing Engine, to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of audit management. These tools assist auditors in identifying risks related to business objectives and reduce manual effort in writing audit reports.
novelty: The innovation lies in the application of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) tools to improve audit management processes, enhancing risk identification and reducing manual reporting efforts.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include public auditors in the UK, the Government Internal Audit Agency (GIAA), and the broader public finance sector that benefits from improved audit processes.

name: Canadian AI Strategy
description: The Canadian AI Strategy outlines the responsible adoption of AI and its enablers, including infrastructure, data, cybersecurity, and talent. It aims to deliver modern services with a digital mindset, ensuring fairness, transparency, and privacy in AI applications.
novelty: The strategy integrates AI with existing digital ambitions and data strategies, emphasizing responsible AI use, transparency, and privacy to maintain public trust while leveraging AI for efficiency gains.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Canadian government, public service employees, Canadian citizens, and the broader Canadian AI ecosystem, including public and commercial sectors.

name: AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service
description: The AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service aims to responsibly and securely adopt AI across the Canadian public service to improve productivity, efficiency, and service delivery. It includes a governance framework to ensure transparency and trust in AI use.
novelty: This is the first-ever AI Strategy for the Canadian federal public service, focusing on responsible AI adoption with a robust governance framework and public engagement to reflect democratic values.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canadian citizens, public servants, federal organizations, Canadian innovators, businesses, and equity-deserving groups.

name: Generative AI Chatbot for Benefits Communication
description: Xerox developed a generative AI chatbot specifically trained on the new paid family leave program to handle employee inquiries efficiently. This chatbot provides accurate and contextually relevant responses, improving employee access to information and satisfaction.
novelty: The chatbot uses generative AI to engage in fluid, conversational interactions, understanding nuances and adapting to different phrasing, unlike traditional rule-based chatbots.
stakeholders: The primary users are the county's public employees, with the Human Resources department benefiting from reduced inquiry volumes, allowing them to focus on complex tasks.

name: Canada's AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service
description: Canada's first AI Strategy for the federal public service aims to improve government operations and services through the safe, ethical, and responsible use of AI. It focuses on establishing an AI Centre of Expertise, ensuring secure and responsible AI systems, providing training and talent development, and building trust through transparency.
novelty: This is the first AI Strategy specifically for the federal public service in Canada, emphasizing democratic values, transparency, and ethical use of AI to enhance digital service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Canadian public, government departments, AI experts, and researchers involved in the development and implementation of the strategy.

name: AI Integration in Opelika
description: The City of Opelika is exploring the integration of AI-driven solutions to improve municipal operations, enhance public services, and drive economic growth. This includes applications like streamlining trash pickup, real-time traffic signal adjustments, and road damage detection.
novelty: The project leverages cutting-edge AI research from Auburn University and involves collaboration with the SEC Artificial Intelligence & Data Science Consortium, positioning Opelika as a leader in AI adoption for local government.
stakeholders: Residents of Opelika, Auburn University researchers, SEC Artificial Intelligence & Data Science Consortium, local government officials.

name: Indiana Management Performance Hub (MPH)
description: The Indiana Management Performance Hub (MPH) is a state initiative focused on enhancing data-driven decision-making and collaboration among state agencies while ensuring responsible and ethical data use. It includes a strong privacy program and a statewide AI policy for government use.
novelty: The innovation lies in its pioneering efforts in shaping legislation for data privacy, developing robust privacy programs, and creating a risk management framework that enhances data ethics and innovation in the state.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state agencies, government officials, and the citizens of Indiana who benefit from improved data-driven decision-making and enhanced data privacy.

name: Modernization and Innovation in the California Department of Education
description: The California Department of Education is undergoing a transformation by integrating new software and technology to improve workflows, automate processes, and enhance data strategy. This includes moving from paper-based processes to digital solutions and developing a data strategy and AI guidance.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves the first state education agency to develop a comprehensive data strategy and issue AI guidance, along with the establishment of an AI task force.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include students, families, educators, industry partners, parents, and teachers who are involved in or benefit from the modernization efforts.

name: Digital-first strategies for government efficiency
description: The Trump administration, through the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), is implementing digital-first strategies to maintain or improve service delivery with a reduced federal workforce. This involves leveraging digital technology, such as AI-powered customer support, automated document processing, and expanding self-service portals, to enhance efficiency and service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive adoption of digital-first strategies across government agencies to maintain service levels despite significant workforce reductions. This includes the use of AI, automation, and improved user experience to streamline processes and reduce the need for human intervention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal employees, government agencies, and the general public who rely on government services. Beneficiaries are citizens who will experience improved service delivery through digital means.

name: AI powered Azure Region
description: The AI powered Azure Region is a cloud infrastructure initiative developed by Microsoft in partnership with the Government of Kuwait. It aims to accelerate digital transformation, enhance public services, drive economic growth, and create value for citizens and residents by utilizing AI technologies.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on integrating AI technologies to drive economic diversification, enhance public services, and prepare the workforce for the future. It also includes the establishment of a Microsoft Technology Innovation Hub and an AI Innovation Center.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Government of Kuwait, Microsoft, global businesses, researchers, and the citizens and residents of Kuwait who will benefit from enhanced public services and economic growth.

name: IndiaAI Mission
organisation: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
description: The IndiaAI Mission launched several initiatives to advance AI research, innovation, and skill development, including AIKosha, the AI Compute Portal, and various programs for upskilling and supporting startups.
novelty: The mission introduces a comprehensive approach to AI development with a secured dataset platform, high-performance computing resources, and a focus on ethical AI governance, aiming to democratize AI adoption in India.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, AI startups, researchers, enterprises, and students, with beneficiaries spanning across sectors such as healthcare, education, manufacturing, and agriculture.

name: Vehicle Theft Prevention Challenge
description: The Vehicle Theft Prevention Challenge is an initiative to fund innovative projects aimed at reducing auto theft. It provides over $1.1 million to eight projects proposing solutions like biometric security, AI monitoring, and gesture recognition sensors. Successful projects may receive additional funding to develop functional prototypes.
novelty: The challenge encourages the development of cutting-edge technologies such as AI-powered devices, biometric authentication, and miniaturized components to prevent vehicle theft, showcasing a novel approach to crime prevention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canadian SMEs developing the solutions, Transport Canada, the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, Public Safety Canada, and vehicle owners who will benefit from reduced auto theft.

name: Vehicle Theft Prevention challenge
organisation: Government of Canada, Transport Canada, Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP), Public Safety Canada
description: The Vehicle Theft Prevention challenge is an initiative to fund innovative projects aimed at reducing auto theft. It involves providing over $1.1 million to eight projects that propose solutions like smartphone-based security, AI monitoring, and fingerprint authentication. Successful projects may receive further funding to develop functional prototypes.
novelty: The challenge encourages Canadian SMEs to develop affordable, safe, and commercially viable solutions using advanced technologies like biometrics, AI, and gesture recognition to prevent vehicle theft.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canadian small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) as developers, the Government of Canada as the funder, and Canadian citizens as the beneficiaries of enhanced vehicle security.

name: Caddy
description: Caddy is an AI helper developed to reduce phone call waiting times for public services by assisting workers in answering queries faster and more confidently. It was trialed in Citizens Advice Bureau teams in Stockport, Oldham, Rochdale, and Trafford, successfully cutting average call lengths from eight minutes to four.
novelty: Caddy allows phone handlers to quickly receive answers to queries based on approved sources, reducing the need for manual consultation and documentation searches, thus halving call times without compromising the personal touch of service.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the public seeking assistance from Citizens Advice Bureau, while the stakeholders include CAB staff, volunteers, and the government interested in expanding the technology to other public services.

name: Kazakhstan's GovTech Digital Transformation
description: Kazakhstan is implementing digital technologies like AI, e-government services, and blockchain to improve public services, simplify government processes, and drive economic growth. Key achievements include expanding electronic government services, launching 5G networks, and digitizing medical records and education services.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to digital transformation, including the use of AI, blockchain, and IoT to enhance public service efficiency, accessibility, and transparency. It also introduces new digital platforms and international cooperation in IT and aerospace industries.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Kazakhstani citizens, government agencies, IT and tech companies, and international partners. Beneficiaries are the general public who receive improved public services, and digital professionals attracted by new visa categories.

name: Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO)
description: The Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO) aims to streamline regulations to accelerate the market introduction of high-growth innovations, such as drones for healthcare logistics and AI in healthcare, ensuring they are brought to market quickly and safely.
novelty: The RIO is innovative in its approach to cutting red tape and creating a pro-innovation environment that allows new technologies to be implemented safely and efficiently, thus supporting economic growth and improving public services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include NHS patients and healthcare providers, while stakeholders engaged include the UK government, Apian, Synnovis, and other technology innovators.

name: Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO)
organisation: Department of Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO) aims to cut red tape, remove barriers to innovation, and accelerate the time it takes to bring emerging technologies to market. It focuses on areas like engineering biology, space, AI in healthcare, and autonomous technology.
novelty: The RIO is innovative in its approach to shaping regulatory frameworks that empower new technologies, incorporating existing functions of the Regulatory Horizons Council and the Regulators’ Pioneer Fund to streamline innovation.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include technology innovators, businesses, and industries such as healthcare and transport. Stakeholders engaged include government departments like the Department for Transport, Department for Health and Social Care, and Department for Environment Food and Rural Affairs.

name: AI Opportunity Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunity Action Plan is an initiative by the UK government to incorporate AI into public services to improve user and staff experiences, increase efficiency, and transform public services. It aims to save staff time on administrative tasks, speed up planning consultations, and address specific public sector challenges.
novelty: The plan is innovative as it leverages AI to automate large parts of the public sector workforce, potentially reducing workforce time by 20% and saving up to £10 billion annually. It also includes unique applications like analyzing camera footage for pothole repairs and supporting healthcare professionals with medical diagnoses.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector employees, healthcare professionals, government officials, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services and efficiency.

name: AI Strategy for Federal Public Service
description: The Canadian government has launched its first strategy for integrating artificial intelligence into the federal public service, aiming to enhance service delivery and operational efficiency.
novelty: This is the first AI strategy specifically targeted at the federal public service in Canada, marking a significant step towards modernizing government operations through AI.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include Canadian federal public service employees, government agencies, and Canadian citizens who will benefit from improved services.

name: Military Officer Training Simulators and XR Laboratory
description: Hungary has modernized its military officer training by introducing cutting-edge simulators and an extended reality (XR) laboratory at the Szolnok campus of the National University of Public Service. These tools are designed to better prepare university instructors, officer candidates, and Hungarian Defence Force officers for evolving national security challenges.
novelty: The innovation integrates advancements in artificial intelligence, drone technology, and cybersecurity research, offering realistic, technologically advanced environments for decision-making and skill development. It includes a modern airport control tower simulator and an XR laboratory for exploring virtual, augmented, and mixed reality applications.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include university instructors, officer candidates, Hungarian Defence Force officers, and students in the state aviation training programme. Stakeholders also include the Ministry of Defence and international research collaborators.

name: Zero Touch Services
organisation: Digital and Population Data Services Agency
description: The Finnish government is implementing 'zero touch' services to automate the delivery of public services, reducing the need for citizens to interact with government processes. This initiative includes services like tax declaration, renewing passports, and applying for identity cards, leveraging cross-agency data sharing and strong authentication.
novelty: The innovation lies in its proactive service delivery model, where services are automated and require minimal user interaction, enabled by cross-agency data sharing and cloud adoption.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are Finnish citizens and businesses, while stakeholders include public agencies and the Digital and Population Data Services Agency.

name: AI Governance and Policy Executive
description: North Carolina has appointed its first-ever AI governance and policy executive to establish and oversee the state's AI governance and framework for NCDIT and state agencies. The role focuses on ensuring the ethical, transparent, and accountable integration of AI technologies into public services.
novelty: This is the first time North Carolina has appointed an AI governance and policy executive, marking a significant step towards structured AI governance in public services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state residents, businesses, visitors, and various state agencies that will benefit from the ethical and secure integration of AI technologies.

name: Gobernarte Contest: Pablo Valenti Award
description: The Gobernarte Contest recognizes the best subnational government initiatives in Latin America and the Caribbean, focusing on innovative uses of artificial intelligence and digital identification to enhance citizen services.
novelty: The contest highlights innovative uses of AI and digital identification in government services, encouraging the adoption of cutting-edge technologies to improve public service delivery.
stakeholders: Subnational governments in Latin America and the Caribbean, including second-level and third-level governments, civil society organizations, private sector entities, and academic institutions.

name: Automation in Government Operations
description: Automation is being used to streamline government operations by reducing manual workloads, minimizing human error, and accelerating workflows. AI-driven tools enhance efficiency by processing and analyzing data in real time, while robotic process automation handles high-volume, rules-based tasks. Cloud-based automation facilitates remote work and improves interagency collaboration.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI-driven tools and robotic process automation to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making in government operations, while also ensuring cybersecurity.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, public service employees, and citizens who benefit from improved efficiency and service delivery.

name: Generative AI in Procurement
organisation: Office of Information Technology Services, Idaho
description: The Office of Information Technology Services in Idaho is experimenting with generative AI to accelerate the procurement process for public transit systems. This approach aims to reduce the time it takes to complete procurement cycles, which can sometimes take years.
novelty: The use of generative AI in the procurement process is innovative as it seeks to significantly reduce the time required for procurement cycles, which is traditionally a lengthy and complex process.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Idaho Office of Information Technology Services, transit agencies, and potentially vendors involved in the procurement process. Beneficiaries would be the transit agencies and their passengers, who would experience improved services and technologies.

name: Innovation 2025
description: Innovation 2025 is a government reform plan aimed at making the UK public sector more innovative and efficient. It includes measures such as sending teams into government departments to drive change, focusing on technology like AI, and restructuring the civil service to be more agile and mission-focused.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to embedding innovation within government operations, particularly through the use of AI and digital credentials, and by restructuring the civil service to focus on core purposes and delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK civil servants, government departments, and ultimately the UK public who will benefit from more efficient public services.

name: AI Safety Institute
organisation: Department of Commerce’s National Institute for Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: The AI Safety Institute is a temporary agency established within NIST to evaluate the capabilities of frontier AI models and assess their risks related to national security and public safety. It aims to develop standards for safety, security, and testing of AI models, and provide testing environments for researchers.
novelty: The institute focuses on evaluating AI models for specific, realistic risks rather than speculative existential threats, and aims to create a voluntary testing institution for AI models to ensure they are secure and trustworthy.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include AI companies, researchers, government agencies, and the public. Stakeholders engaged include U.S. companies, universities, think tanks, and advocacy groups with expertise in AI.

name: AI Action Plan
organisation: White House Office of Science & Technology Policy (OSTP)
description: The AI Action Plan is a strategic initiative by the Trump Administration to sustain and enhance America's global AI dominance, promoting human flourishing, economic competitiveness, and national security. It seeks public input on priority policy actions to ensure AI innovation is not hampered by unnecessary regulations.
novelty: The AI Action Plan is innovative in its approach to actively seek public input on AI policy priorities, emphasizing a balance between fostering AI innovation and avoiding excessive regulation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, industry leaders, civil society, and the general public who are encouraged to provide input on AI policy priorities.

name: National Artificial Intelligence Platform
description: The first national artificial intelligence platform in Iran, designed as an open-source system optimized with local electronic circuits and modules, ensuring high speed, low cost, scalability, and security. It operates independently of external APIs and remains functional even if the internet is disconnected.
novelty: The platform is innovative due to its independence from external APIs, its ability to function without internet connectivity, and its optimization with local electronic circuits, enhancing security and scalability.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include 16 faculty members, over 70 researchers, and the Iranian government. Beneficiaries are likely to be Iranian citizens and government agencies utilizing AI for public services.

name: Llama Incubator Program
description: The Llama Incubator Program is an initiative to drive AI innovation and support local businesses, start-ups, and public sector agencies in Singapore by leveraging Meta’s open-source AI model, Llama, to develop innovative AI solutions.
novelty: This is the first initiative of its kind by Meta in the Asia-Pacific region, focusing on democratizing AI and aligning with Singapore’s Smart Nation 2.0 AI vision.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include local businesses, start-ups, public sector agencies, and government officers. Stakeholders engaged include Meta, Singapore Government, MDDI, IMDA, GovTech, DISG, EnterpriseSG, AI Singapore, SGInnovate, e27, and Deloitte.

name: Agentic AI for Scholar Management
description: IMDA implemented low-code technologies, intelligent workflows, and AI-powered chatbots to automate its scholar management process, making it more efficient and user-friendly. This included developing over 50 workflows and integrating chatbot interactions, saving more than 1,000 man-hours over 12 months.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI, which acts as a 'digital twin' or 'nervous system', capable of understanding the context of problems and executing next steps autonomously, transforming internal operations into a cohesive ecosystem.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include IMDA staff and scholars, while stakeholders engaged include public sector agencies and Workato as the technology partner.

name: Digital Transformation of Azerbaijan's Social Protection Fund
organisation: Azerbaijan's Ministry of Labour and Social Protection of the Population
description: The Social Protection Fund in Azerbaijan underwent a significant digital transformation to improve service delivery, particularly for vulnerable groups. This included implementing a unified system with national standards, leveraging AI, big data, and digital platforms to process applications digitally, increase case resolution, and reduce complaints.
novelty: The innovation lies in the centralised approach that eliminated regional inefficiencies, the use of AI and big data for proactive benefit assignment, and the creation of a citizen-centric, efficient, and transparent social protection system.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the citizens of Azerbaijan, especially vulnerable groups such as the elderly, disabled, and low-income families. The Ministry of Labour and Social Protection of the Population and the State Social Protection Fund are also key stakeholders.

name: OMB Memorandums M-25-21 and M-25-22
organisation: White House's Office of Management and Budget (OMB)
description: The OMB issued two memorandums establishing new federal policies for the use and acquisition of AI by federal agencies. M-25-21 focuses on fostering AI innovation, governance, and responsible use, while M-25-22 updates guidance for AI procurement. The memos aim to accelerate AI adoption while minimizing regulatory burdens.
novelty: The memorandums introduce a unified risk category for 'high-impact' AI systems, simplifying assessments and focusing on systems with significant consequences. They also provide more flexible risk-based classifications and detailed procurement standards.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, private contractors developing or deploying AI systems for federal customers, and the general public benefiting from responsible AI use.

name: AI & Data Implementation for Government Efficiency
description: The initiative aims to implement AI and data analytics to streamline government operations, reduce costs, and enhance public services, particularly in healthcare modernization and fraud detection.
novelty: The project focuses on using AI and centralized data systems to improve efficiency and detect fraud in government operations, drawing on successful models from the private sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, federal employees, healthcare providers, and taxpayers who would benefit from reduced fraud and improved service delivery.

name: Advisory Council on Artificial Intelligence for Public Good
description: The Advisory Council on Artificial Intelligence for Public Good is established to ensure AI is used to improve public services, enhance data-driven decision-making, and build public trust in Montgomery County. It aims to guide the responsible and ethical deployment of AI across various county operations.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a dedicated council to guide the ethical and responsible integration of AI in government operations, focusing on transparency, accountability, and public trust.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include experts from the private sector, academia, public service, and nonprofits, as well as county employees who will implement the council's guidance. The beneficiaries are the residents of Montgomery County.

name: National Data Library
description: The National Data Library is a UK government initiative aimed at enhancing the use of public data for research, policymaking, and innovation. It focuses on updating outdated infrastructure and systems to improve data sharing and use, while ensuring robust data privacy and equitable access.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to modernizing data infrastructure, ensuring privacy, and promoting inclusivity in data access, while leveraging AI technologies to enhance data utility.
stakeholders: Researchers, policymakers, educators, data professionals, and the general public who will benefit from improved data access and utility.

name: AI Integration in Public Sector
description: The initiative focuses on integrating AI into public sector services to enhance efficiency, reduce errors, and improve citizen experiences. It emphasizes a holistic approach aligning AI initiatives with national priorities, such as healthcare and economic resilience.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its emphasis on ethical AI frameworks, data security, and creating sovereign AI ecosystems through public-private collaboration. It also focuses on upskilling the public sector workforce to work alongside AI.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, public sector employees, AI specialists, local AI startups, research institutions, and citizens who benefit from improved public services.

name: Combat Autonomous Maritime Platform (CAMP)
description: The Combat Autonomous Maritime Platform (CAMP) is a project by the Defense Innovation Unit to develop commercially available uncrewed underwater vehicles with exceptional range and payload capacity. These vehicles are intended to enhance the U.S. Navy's capabilities by providing scalable and cost-effective solutions for long-range, high-capacity payload deployment in contested environments.
novelty: CAMP represents a new class of uncrewed underwater vehicles that are significantly larger, have much longer range, and can carry larger payloads compared to previous prototypes like the LDUUV. They are designed to be pier launched and can operate in GPS-denied environments, offering enhanced communication capabilities across the air/water interface.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the U.S. Navy and other organizations within the Department of Defense. Beneficiaries are the U.S. military personnel who will have reduced risk exposure, and the vendors who will participate in the development and prototyping phases.

name: AI Advisory Council for Public Good
description: The AI Advisory Council is a task force aimed at guiding Montgomery County in the ethical and responsible use of artificial intelligence to improve public services. It will focus on areas such as finance, health services, legal compliance, and smart governance.
novelty: The project is innovative in its proactive approach to integrating AI into public services with a strong emphasis on ethics, transparency, and public trust, involving a diverse group of experts from various sectors.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Montgomery County residents, county officials, and experts from academia, nonprofits, public service, and the private sector.

name: Digital Government Strategies
description: Digital government strategies leverage cloud, AI, data analytics, and participatory platforms to improve public services, making them more agile, cost-effective, resilient, and transparent. These strategies aim to meet rising citizen expectations by providing seamless, 24/7 access to services.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of advanced technologies like AI, blockchain, and IoT to create a citizen-centric approach to public service delivery, emphasizing co-creation, participatory policymaking, and real-time feedback loops.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include citizens, public administrators, policy advisors, tech partners, NGOs, developers, and privacy commissioners. Beneficiaries are primarily the citizens who receive improved public services.

name: Extract
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: Extract is an AI tool designed to digitise old planning documents, converting them into machine-readable, shareable data to improve efficiency and reduce errors in record-keeping. It aims to drastically cut the time required to process these documents, supporting the government's goal to build 1.5 million homes.
novelty: The tool can perform tasks in 40 seconds that typically take planners one to two hours, significantly speeding up the digitisation process of old paper records.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are local councils and planners who will use the tool to streamline planning processes. The broader public sector could also benefit from its application in delivering services and informing government policy.

name: Extract
description: Extract is an AI tool designed to convert poor scans of old, PDF, and paper planning documents into machine-readable, shareable data in seconds. It aims to reduce the time needed to digitize planning documents, thereby speeding up planning decisions and reducing errors.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to transform outdated paper documents and scanned PDFs into digital data in just 40 seconds, a task that typically takes 1-2 hours, thus drastically improving efficiency in the planning process.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are local councils and planning authorities who will use the tool to make faster, more informed planning decisions. Beneficiaries include the general public and the housing sector, as the tool supports the government's housing targets.

name: Agentic AI
organisation: JTC Corporation, Singapore's Ministry of Health (MOH), Digital and Intelligence Service (DIS), Workato
description: Agentic AI is being integrated into public sector systems to perform tasks autonomously, enhancing efficiency and quality of services. It is used in areas like building management and healthcare to streamline processes and improve decision-making.
novelty: Agentic AI can reason, plan, remember, and act without continuous human intervention, making it a transformative tool for enhancing public service efficiency.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public sector employees, facility managers, healthcare professionals, and ultimately the general public benefiting from improved services. Stakeholders engaged include government agencies and technology providers.

name: Biometric Boarding System at Tan Son Nhat International Airport
description: The biometric boarding system at Terminal 3 of Tan Son Nhat International Airport in Ho Chi Minh City is a pilot project aimed at modernising Vietnam's aviation infrastructure. It includes digital check-in kiosks, facial recognition cameras, and automated security lanes to enhance passenger experience and operational efficiency.
novelty: The project introduces a seamless and secure boarding process using biometric recognition, reducing wait times and increasing efficiency while enhancing safety by minimizing physical contact and identity fraud.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Vietnam Government, ministries of construction, public security, and national defence, Ho Chi Minh City Party organisation and administration, and passengers using the airport.

name: Artificial Intelligence Playbook for the UK Government
description: The UK Government has published the Artificial Intelligence Playbook to integrate AI into public services, aiming to position the UK as a global AI leader. The playbook outlines initiatives like deploying large language models to improve citizen interactions and exploring AI applications in policymaking, supply-chain management, and scientific research.
novelty: The innovation lies in the UK's attempt to define a 'third way' on AI policy, balancing between the EU's overregulation and the US's underregulation, and its focus on integrating AI into public services to modernize government operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK citizens, who are the primary beneficiaries of improved public services, and the UK Government, which is responsible for implementing the AI initiatives. Private companies like Anthropic are also involved as partners in deploying AI technologies.

name: Executive Order 14179 and AI Memoranda
description: The Trump Administration issued Executive Order 14179 and two memoranda (M-25-21 and M-25-22) to promote the use of AI in federal government operations. These policies aim to enhance America's global AI dominance, ensure safety and public trust, and streamline AI procurement processes.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive approach to integrating AI into federal operations, emphasizing American-made AI, nontraditional procurement methods, and robust risk management practices for high-impact AI use cases.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, government contractors involved in AI development and procurement, and the general public who benefit from improved government services.

name: Next New Jersey Program — AI and AI Innovation Challenge Administration Grant Program
description: The Next New Jersey Program — AI is a $500 million initiative providing tax credits to businesses investing in AI data centers and activities, requiring partnerships with local research entities. The AI Innovation Challenge Administration Grant Program offers $3.8 million to develop a statewide AI challenge to solve government problems.
novelty: The programs incentivize private-sector investment in AI, aiming to make New Jersey an East Coast AI hub by connecting businesses with the AI ecosystem and solving government issues through AI challenges.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include businesses investing in AI, government entities seeking AI solutions, and residents providing feedback. Stakeholders engaged include NJEDA, Princeton University, Microsoft, CoreWeave, and AI startups.

name: Digital Identity Ecosystem
organisation: Federal Government of the United States
description: The initiative aims to implement modernized digital credentials, including mobile driver's licenses, to enhance security, efficiency, and trust in government services. It leverages technologies like biometrics and AI to streamline processes such as accessing healthcare, renewing passports, and filing taxes, while reducing administrative costs and bolstering security against identity fraud.
novelty: The innovation lies in the adoption of advanced biometrics and AI technologies to create a secure, interoperable digital identity system that enhances user experience and reduces identity fraud. It represents a transformative approach to how citizens interact with government services.
stakeholders: Users include U.S. citizens accessing government services. Beneficiaries are federal agencies that will save on administrative costs. Stakeholders include private-sector innovators, policymakers, and federal, state, and local agencies involved in implementing the digital identity system.

name: Unlimited Smart Travel Service
organisation: General Directorate of Identity and Foreigners Affairs – Dubai
description: The Unlimited Smart Travel Service is a passport control initiative launched in the First and Business Class lounges at Dubai International Airport – Terminal 3. It aims to improve the quality of life for travelers by enhancing the efficiency and smoothness of travel procedures using AI technologies for facial recognition and identity verification.
novelty: The service represents a qualitative leap in smart mobility by integrating the latest AI technologies, allowing for increased throughput capacity from one traveler to ten individuals simultaneously, and reducing service steps to accelerate passage through travel gates.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include travelers at Dubai International Airport, particularly those in First and Business Class lounges. Stakeholders engaged include government entities, technology partners, and airport authorities.

name: AI Continent Action Plan
description: The AI Continent Action Plan is a comprehensive roadmap designed to establish Europe as a global leader in artificial intelligence. It includes initiatives like the InvestAI Initiative, AI Factories, Gigafactories, the Cloud and AI Development Act, and the Data Union Strategy to enhance AI capabilities, infrastructure, and talent across Europe.
novelty: The plan is innovative due to its scale and comprehensive approach, integrating investment, infrastructure development, data policy, and talent initiatives into a cohesive strategy to promote ethical, secure, and inclusive AI development.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include European citizens, businesses, the telecommunications industry, and sectors like healthcare and public services. Beneficiaries are companies like Telefónica, which will leverage enhanced AI infrastructure and data capabilities.

name: TrojAI
description: TrojAI is a program aimed at developing means to detect adversarial attacks on AI systems, particularly focusing on ensuring that large language models (LLMs) can be trained on classified data without leaking it. The program addresses challenges such as training skews, hallucinations, and prompt injections in AI models.
novelty: The program focuses on understanding and mitigating the risks associated with training large language models on classified data, ensuring they do not leak sensitive information even when manipulated through techniques like jailbreaking or prompt injections.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are intelligence agencies and the broader intelligence community, who are the users and beneficiaries of secure AI systems. Vendors like Microsoft and Palantir, who are working to integrate LLMs into classified networks, are also stakeholders.

name: India's AI-Driven Healthcare Transformation
organisation: Indian Government, Sun Pharma, Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories
description: India is leveraging AI and digital tools to transform its healthcare system, focusing on AI-powered drug discovery, patient engagement through digital tools, strategic investments, and health data interoperability. This approach aims to improve national health outcomes and offers scalable models for other low- and middle-income countries.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI for drug discovery, digital tools for patient engagement, and health data interoperability, positioning India as a leader in affordable healthcare innovation for low- and middle-income countries.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Indian pharmaceutical companies, healthcare providers, patients, government bodies, and global organizations like WHO and OECD.

name: Device Direct-Patching Initiative
description: A cybersecurity and modernization effort that involved implementing a device direct-patching initiative, saving the Air Force 220,000 man-days and $20 million in annual contract costs.
novelty: The initiative dramatically transformed how the Air Force updates computers with remote patching and automation, updating nearly half a million computers to run the latest version of Microsoft Office in less than 10 days.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the U.S. Air Force personnel who benefit from improved cybersecurity and modernization, as well as the broader federal IT community.

name: AI-enabled Data Management Tool
description: A cross-government, AI-enabled data management tool designed to expand citizens’ access to federal information and resources. It aims to make data from different government agencies searchable and usable, enhancing transparency and efficiency in public services.
novelty: The tool is innovative due to its cross-governmental approach, making data from various agencies accessible and searchable, thus improving data usability and transparency in public services.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are Estonian citizens who will have improved access to government data. Stakeholders include various government agencies involved in providing data.

name: AI Use and Procurement Memos (M-25-21 and M-25-22)
description: The AI Use and Procurement Memos aim to accelerate the federal use of AI through innovation, governance, and public trust, while driving efficient acquisition of AI in government. They focus on removing bureaucratic barriers, enhancing agency efficiency, and ensuring responsible AI governance and procurement.
novelty: The memos promote a pro-innovation mindset, redefine the role of Agency Chief AI Officers as change agents, and emphasize responsible AI governance and procurement, aligning with state AI legislation and focusing on high-impact AI use cases.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are U.S. federal agencies, their AI contractors and subcontractors, and indirectly, the American public who will benefit from improved government services.

name: PLLuM
description: PLLuM is Poland's first large language model tailored for public administration, designed to streamline processes and support government teams. It aims to make AI an integral tool for public service, providing a ready-made chatbot tool to ease administrative burdens, especially for smaller municipalities.
novelty: PLLuM is innovative as it is a country-specific large language model designed specifically for public administration, making it a practical tool for government services and supporting smaller municipalities with AI capabilities.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public servants, government teams, smaller municipalities, and potentially the private sector who can benefit from the model's applications.

name: AI and Machine Learning in Construction
organisation: Naval Facilities Engineering and Expeditionary Warfare Center (EXWC), Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command (NAVFAC) Washington
description: The initiative involves integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning applications into the construction industry to enhance speed of delivery and reduce workload. It also includes advancements in critical power supply systems and battery storage technologies to improve backup power system operability and effectiveness.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on integrating AI and machine learning into everyday construction applications, which is expected to streamline processes and improve efficiency in infrastructure projects.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the Navy and Marine Corps installations, Public Works Departments, and the broader Naval District Washington, along with federal activities in the National Capital Region.

name: Generative AI Technologies for City Services
description: The City of Anchorage, under the leadership of its first chief innovation officer, Brendan Babb, is experimenting with generative AI technologies to reduce friction in accessing city services. This initiative is part of a broader effort to cultivate a culture of innovation within the city's public services.
novelty: The project introduces generative AI technologies in the public sector to streamline access to city services, representing a novel approach in municipal governance.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the residents of Anchorage who will benefit from improved access to city services, as well as government employees who are being trained in new techniques.

name: GenAI Technologies in California State Government
description: The California State Government is implementing GenAI technologies across various departments to improve efficiency, enhance traffic management, increase road safety, and improve customer service. These technologies include AI solutions for traffic congestion, safety analysis, customer service enhancement, and productivity tools for state departments.
novelty: This initiative is pioneering in its comprehensive use of GenAI technologies across state government operations, including the first-in-the-nation digital democracy platform, and the first-of-its-kind AI collaboration with NVIDIA.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include California state departments such as Caltrans and CDTFA, technology partners like Microsoft, Accenture, Deloitte, Anthropic, and NVIDIA, as well as the general public, including vulnerable road users and taxpayers.

name: Public Sector AI Integration
organisation: Government of Kazakhstan, Government of South Korea
description: Kazakhstan and South Korea are collaborating to integrate artificial intelligence (AI) into public sector applications. This initiative aims to enhance government processes and improve local economies through AI-backed projects.
novelty: The innovation lies in the bilateral collaboration between Kazakhstan and South Korea to integrate AI into public sector applications, focusing on ethical standards and global AI governance frameworks.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the governments of Kazakhstan and South Korea, public sector organizations, and the residents of these countries who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Generative AI Initiative in California State Government
description: The initiative integrates generative AI technologies across California's state government to improve traffic flow, road safety, and customer service. It involves using AI to analyze highway data to reduce congestion, streamline customer service systems, and enhance public safety.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of generative AI to enhance government efficiency and service delivery, leveraging California's unique procurement process to test and scale tech projects quickly.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include California residents, particularly those affected by traffic congestion and in need of improved public services. Stakeholders include the California Department of Transportation, Department of Tax and Fee Administration, and the California Government Operations Agency.

name: AI Continent Action Plan
description: The AI Continent Action Plan is an initiative by the European Commission to invest €200 billion to enhance AI capabilities in the EU. It includes setting up AI Factories and Gigafactories, increasing data access, fostering AI adoption in strategic sectors, strengthening AI skills, and ensuring compliance with the EU AI Act.
novelty: The plan is innovative due to its comprehensive approach, including the establishment of AI Factories and Gigafactories, a Data Union Strategy, and an Apply AI Strategy to boost AI adoption and compliance with the EU AI Act.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include EU companies, AI developers, public and private sectors, educational institutions, and the EU workforce.

name: AI Employees
description: The innovation involves the creation of 'AI employees' to support human decision-making in government operations. This initiative aims to deploy thousands of AI employees across federal agencies to enhance government services and research.
novelty: The project introduces the concept of 'AI employees' as distinct from 'AI agents,' emphasizing the need for significant investment in AI infrastructure, training, and risk management to maintain and enhance performance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, government employees, and potentially the general public who will benefit from improved government services.

name: Louisiana Connected and Automated Vehicle Innovation Day
organisation: Louisiana Department of Transportation and Development (DOTD), Partners for Automated Vehicle Education (PAVE), Stantec
description: The Louisiana Connected and Automated Vehicle Innovation Day is an event hosted by the Louisiana Department of Transportation and Development to showcase the latest in connected and automated vehicle technologies. The event aims to highlight how these technologies can enhance transportation safety and efficiency in Louisiana.
novelty: The event is innovative as it focuses on the integration and demonstration of cutting-edge connected and automated vehicle technologies, providing a platform for discussing their potential impact on transportation safety and efficiency.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Louisiana residents and businesses who will benefit from improved transportation safety and efficiency. Stakeholders engaged include DOTD, PAVE, Stantec, and companies like Waymo testing autonomous vehicles in Louisiana.

name: Smart Rutherford
description: Smart Rutherford is an AI-powered community engagement tool designed to enhance communication between residents and local government by providing seamless access to property data and public services. It delivers vital assessment information based on a citizen’s address and is also used by the Tennessee Department of Transportation to communicate state highway projects.
novelty: The project integrates AI-driven virtual assistance to transform citizen access to property and public information, making assessments more accessible and user-friendly, and providing real-time updates and personalized interactions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include residents of Rutherford County, local government officials, and the Tennessee Department of Transportation.

name: Prompt Engineering Training Pilot
description: A pilot program in Pennsylvania where 175 employees from 14 agencies used ChatGPT Enterprise to enhance their skills in prompt engineering, which is crucial for effectively utilizing generative AI. The program aimed to train non-technical staff to craft effective prompts for AI models, resulting in over 85% of participants reporting a positive experience.
novelty: First-of-its-kind pilot focusing on training public sector employees in prompt engineering to improve their interaction with generative AI.
stakeholders: Public sector employees in Pennsylvania, particularly non-technical staff from 14 different agencies, and state tech leaders.

name: Township Innovation Management Application (TIMApp)
description: TIMApp is a digital platform designed to enhance local governance, streamline city planning, and bolster disaster preparedness in Iligan City through the integration of technology, data, and innovation. It allows residents to access key government services more conveniently and efficiently.
novelty: TIMApp integrates technology and data to create a smarter urban environment, focusing on innovation governance and people-centered programs. It includes a Smart City Command Center and a Resilience Innovation Hub supported by AI-powered CCTV cameras for real-time monitoring and decision-making.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include residents of Iligan City, local government units, and regional stakeholders. The project involves collaboration with national agencies, local stakeholders, and technology partners.

name: KolokoloChat
organisation: Hawaiʻi State Judiciary, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa
description: KolokoloChat is an AI-powered chatbot that provides 24/7 access to court information and resources, including FAQs, online forms, and self-help resources for court users. It uses natural language processing to engage users in real-time conversations, guiding them to relevant information to make court interactions more efficient and accessible.
novelty: The innovation lies in its use of artificial intelligence and natural language processing to make complex legal information more accessible and easier to navigate, enhancing public access to court resources.
stakeholders: Users include court users and self-represented litigants. Beneficiaries are the people of Hawaiʻi who need access to legal information. Stakeholders involved include the Hawaiʻi State Judiciary, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, IBM, Google, and Research CyberInfrastructure.

name: AI-Enabled Modernisation in Singapore's Public Sector
organisation: GovTech Singapore, Ministry of Manpower, Ministry of Education
description: The innovation involves leveraging AI to modernise public sector services in Singapore, focusing on aligning technology with business goals, simplifying workflows, and improving user outcomes. It includes transforming the Work Pass system and adopting a product-centric approach in education to enhance long-term outcomes.
novelty: The project is innovative in its strategic integration of AI not as a standalone solution but as an enabler of broader goals, focusing on simplifying systems, rethinking outdated policies, and embedding technology into a broader shift prioritising public value.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies in Singapore, such as GovTech, Ministry of Manpower, and Ministry of Education, as well as public sector employees, teachers, and citizens who benefit from improved services.

name: K-AI Based Government
description: The K-AI Based Government initiative aims to transition South Korea's government from a Digital Platform Government to an AI Government by developing a generative AI (GenAI) common foundation. This shared AI infrastructure will enable public agencies to improve public services through AI technology, focusing on operational efficiency and public service delivery.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its emphasis on creating a generative AI common foundation that leverages public-private partnerships and private sector cloud and AI solutions. It also focuses on using South Korean large language models and AI platforms, operating on a usage-based subscription model.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include South Korean government departments, public officers, public transport companies, young farmers in rural communities, and private cloud service providers. Beneficiaries are the general public who will experience improved public services.

name: Small Business Innovation Research programme (SBIR)
organisation: Department of Public Expenditure and Reform, Enterprise Ireland, Office of the Government Chief Information Officer (OGCIO)
description: The initiative aims to connect public sector bodies with innovative ideas and technology businesses to provide solutions for public sector challenges, specifically to enhance access to and use of Government online services through artificial intelligence and machine learning.
novelty: The project leverages artificial intelligence and machine learning to improve accessibility and usability of digital public services, focusing on inclusivity and catering to diverse needs.
stakeholders: Users of government online services, technology businesses, public sector bodies, and individuals needing assistance with digital services.

name: Healthcare R&D Partnerships
organisation: Sharjah Research, Technology and Innovation Park (SRTIP)
description: A series of strategic MoUs signed by SRTIP with Emirates Health Services, Sharjah Health Authority, and BEEAH Group to advance healthcare research and innovation in Sharjah. The agreements aim to foster a dynamic, research-driven healthcare ecosystem, accelerate healthcare innovation, and integrate sustainability into medical advancements.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its multi-pronged approach combining healthcare R&D with sustainability practices, regulatory experimentation, and integration of AI-driven solutions and telemedicine infrastructure.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include healthcare professionals, patients, and the wider community in Sharjah and the UAE. Stakeholders engaged are Emirates Health Services, Sharjah Health Authority, BEEAH Group, academic institutions, and industry leaders.

name: AI Genomics Schema Harmonizer
organisation: Digital Transformation Hub (DxHub) at California Polytechnic State University, Wisconsin State Laboratory of Hygiene (WSLH), Virginia Department of General Services Division of Consolidated Laboratory Services (DCLS)
description: AI Genomics Schema Harmonizer is a generative AI-powered application that automates the alignment of diverse laboratory terminologies with standardized formats for genomic data submission to public repositories, significantly reducing the time and errors associated with manual data preparation.
novelty: The innovation uses generative AI and natural language processing to automatically standardize genomic data, eliminating the need for manual data transformations and increasing accuracy by reducing typographical errors.
stakeholders: Public health laboratories, genomic researchers, public health partners, and the broader scientific community are the primary users and beneficiaries of this innovation.

name: AI Center of Excellence and Fellowship Program
description: Govern for America proposes the creation of an AI Center of Excellence and a fellowship program to connect young workers with available roles in government, addressing workforce gaps in AI and technology sectors.
novelty: The innovation lies in establishing a centralized AI leadership and structured fellowship program to effectively match young talent with government roles, leveraging peer learning and state collaboration.
stakeholders: Young workers interested in government roles, state governments, public sector leaders, educational institutions.

name: DSIT Fellowship
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The DSIT Fellowship is a program offering year-long, part-time placements for science and tech experts to advise on policy and build technology for public services. It aims to drive AI use, bolster tech and research policy, and foster innovation in the public sector, contributing to a productive and agile state.
novelty: The Fellowship creates a unique bridge between government and the tech ecosystem, embedding expert knowledge from academia and industry into policymaking and providing professionals with direct experience in government decision-making.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include leading scientists, technologists, innovators, government policymakers, academia, industry partners, and the broader UK public benefiting from enhanced public services.

name: Devolved AI Service (DAIS)
organisation: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change
description: The Devolved AI Service (DAIS) is proposed as a cooperative platform to support local governments in the UK with AI-enabled innovation. It aims to provide vision and governance for local innovation, build and incubate AI tools and services, transform infrastructure for scaling innovation, and improve local capabilities. DAIS would initially receive seed funding from the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology and the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government, transitioning to self-sufficiency through a subscription model and monetizing international access to tools and services.
novelty: DAIS is innovative because it proposes a shared platform for local governments to collaboratively develop and scale AI tools, breaking down siloes and reducing duplication of efforts. It aims to create a sustainable funding model by monetizing international access to its tools and services, thus reducing dependency on government funding.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local governments in the UK, the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government, and potentially international clients who would access the tools and services developed by DAIS. Beneficiaries include local councils, their staff, and citizens who would experience improved public services.

name: AI Tools 'Connect' and 'Scout'
description: The UK Government is introducing new AI tools, 'Connect' and 'Scout', to streamline processes and improve service delivery in the public sector. These tools are part of a broader initiative to transform public services through technology, with a focus on efficiency and effectiveness.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI tools specifically designed to address public sector challenges, such as outdated systems and disconnected data, by streamlining processes and improving service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK government agencies, public sector leaders, and citizens who will benefit from improved public services. Additionally, data management professionals and AI developers are engaged in implementing these tools.

name: Evolv AI-powered screening systems
description: Rahway Public Schools has implemented Evolv Technology’s AI-powered screening systems, Evolv Express and Evolv eXpedite, to enhance security at school entrances. These systems support real-time threat detection without requiring students to remove items from their bags, aiming to maintain a smooth and welcoming entry process while ensuring safety.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI-powered screening systems that automate threat detection at school entry points, minimizing disruption and maintaining a welcoming environment while enhancing security.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Rahway Public Schools' administrators, staff, students, and families, as well as Evolv Technology as the provider of the systems.

name: EVA
organisation: Union County Department of Human Services, TipCo Automated Systems
description: EVA is an AI assistant designed to support the Union County Department of Human Services Action Line by handling high volumes of client inquiries across various services, such as SNAP, TANF, Medicaid, and more. It aims to improve accessibility, reduce wait times, and free up staff for complex casework.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to enhance the efficiency and accessibility of human services, allowing for timely information access and improved resource allocation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Union County residents who are the users and beneficiaries of the services, the Union County Department of Human Services, and TipCo Automated Systems.

name: AI Assurance Framework
description: The AI Assurance Framework is a policy initiative by the Australian Government to ensure the responsible use of AI in government. It includes a National framework for AI assurance, a Voluntary AI Safety Standard, and proposed mandatory guardrails for AI in high-risk settings. The framework aims to provide guidelines for safe AI adoption across the government.
novelty: The framework provides a structured approach to AI governance in government, combining top-down policy with bottom-up multidisciplinary involvement to drive ideation, experimentation, and adoption.
stakeholders: Government agencies, public service employees, Australian citizens, and industries collaborating with the government.

name: Drone Medicine Delivery Pilot
description: A pilot project using drones to deliver medicines to rural and remote areas in Malaysia, aiming to improve access to healthcare services for communities facing geographical barriers.
novelty: The project leverages drone technology to overcome logistical challenges in healthcare delivery, particularly in hard-to-reach areas, and integrates advancements in 5G and AI.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include rural and remote communities in Malaysia. Stakeholders engaged in the project include the Ministry of Health, MRANTI, local drone technology providers, and the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia.

name: Procurement Frameworks for AI Innovation in NHS
description: The innovation involves using procurement frameworks to support the implementation of artificial intelligence (AI) in the National Health Service (NHS). The initiative aims to address challenges such as visibility of AI procurement processes, uncertainty in the value of AI products, process inefficiencies, sustainability, and framework design, while identifying opportunities for responsible AI innovation in healthcare.
novelty: The project is innovative as it explores the role of procurement frameworks specifically in the context of AI innovation within healthcare, highlighting the potential for these frameworks to enable responsible AI implementation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include clinical AI innovators, NHS procurement officials, healthcare providers, and other cross-sector participants involved in the workshop to explore AI procurement challenges and opportunities.

name: Cleveland's Government Services Enhancement
description: The initiative aims to enhance Cleveland's government services and community programs through the effective use of technology, information systems, digital and mobility devices, and modern industry solutions.
novelty: The project integrates technology planning, acquisition, application development, automation, system integration, and network security to modernize government services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Cleveland government departments, city employees, and the community benefiting from improved services.

name: OMB AI Use & Procurement Requirements
organisation: White House Office of Management & Budget (OMB)
description: The OMB issued two memoranda to guide federal agencies on the use and procurement of AI, focusing on innovation, governance, and public trust. The memos aim to enhance America's global AI dominance by establishing governance and risk management requirements for AI use and procurement preferences for AI developed in the U.S.
novelty: The memos shift U.S. AI policy to a forward-leaning, pro-innovation, and pro-competition mindset, making federal agencies more agile, cost-effective, and efficient.
