stakeholders: Federal agencies, AI developers, U.S. government, and potentially U.S. citizens benefiting from improved government services.

name: Digital Platform Government (DPG)
description: The Digital Platform Government (DPG) initiative in South Korea aims to provide integrated, personalised, and proactive services from the citizen's perspective by leveraging high-quality, interoperable open data and AI technologies. The initiative focuses on integrating data across various government agencies and strengthening public-private cooperation to deliver platform-based governance.
novelty: The innovation shifts from traditional e-government to a digital platform government model, emphasizing integration of data and public-private cooperation to provide personalized services. It leverages a common generative AI foundation and cloud-native technologies to enhance flexibility and service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include South Korean citizens who receive improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include central ministries, local governments, public institutions, and private sector companies providing AI and cloud technologies.

name: Consult
organisation: Department of Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: Consult is an AI-based tool used to analyze consultation responses, saving significant admin time and costs. It was used in a trial by the Scottish Parliament to analyze feedback on non-surgical cosmetic procedure regulations, identifying key themes from over 2,000 responses.
novelty: Consult uses AI to process and summarize consultation responses, providing results nearly identical to manual analysis, thus reducing human biases and making feedback analysis more consistent.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government officials, experts, and the general public providing feedback. Stakeholders engaged include the Scottish government and DSIT.

name: Targeted Call - Health System Solutions program
description: The Targeted Call - Health System Solutions program is a funding initiative by the Cook Government, investing $3 million in six innovative projects to improve Western Australia's healthcare system. The projects aim to enhance healthcare delivery, efficiency, and patient outcomes through smart solutions, including AI-powered tools and telehealth services.
novelty: The program fosters partnerships between healthcare providers, researchers, and industry leaders to swiftly develop and implement innovative healthcare solutions, addressing critical business challenges identified by WA health service providers.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include patients across Western Australia, healthcare providers, researchers, industry leaders, and community organisations involved in the development and implementation of the projects.

name: Smart Education of China
description: Smart Education of China is an online platform for digital education that offers a wide range of educational resources, including primary, secondary, vocational, higher education, and lifelong learning courses. It integrates 51 services across eight categories, focusing on areas like learning, teaching, school governance, and educational innovation.
novelty: The platform features deep integration of AI and education, making it one of the world's largest repositories of education and teaching resources, and it supports a modern digital education system that is fair, high-quality, intelligent, and accessible for lifelong learning.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include students, educators, and institutions from primary to higher education levels, as well as individuals seeking lifelong learning opportunities. Stakeholders also include the Chinese government and international visitors from over 220 countries and regions.

name: AI-Driven Governance
description: The Tripura government is integrating Artificial Intelligence into its governance system to improve service delivery and administrative efficiency. The initiative includes training officials on AI tools, digitizing key documents, and moving approval processes for infrastructure projects online.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its phased approach to AI adoption, focusing on building digital readiness and creating a strong data ecosystem to enhance government efficiency and transparency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, and the citizens of Tripura who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Minister of Artificial Intelligence and Digital Innovation
description: The creation of a new cabinet position, Minister of Artificial Intelligence and Digital Innovation, to oversee the integration of AI into the economy and public services, aiming to enhance productivity, job creation, and service delivery.
novelty: First-ever ministerial position dedicated to artificial intelligence in Canada, signaling a governmental commitment to integrating AI across various sectors and transforming public service delivery.
stakeholders: Canadian citizens, businesses, government agencies, AI researchers, and innovators.

name: UK-Ukraine Techbridge
description: The UK-Ukraine Techbridge is an initiative focused on bringing innovative technologies to address critical tasks such as clearing landmines and unexploded bombs. It also focuses on AI, health, cyber security, education, and agritech, building opportunities for trade, upskilling, and investment in both countries.
novelty: The project is innovative in its collaborative approach to addressing both immediate and long-term technological challenges, leveraging joint expertise to enhance security and economic growth in both the UK and Ukraine.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK and Ukrainian governments, academic institutions, researchers, businesses, and industries involved in technology, health, and energy sectors.

name: OneGov Slack Agreement
description: The U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) and Salesforce have partnered to offer Slack Enterprise Grid at a significant price reduction to federal agencies, enhancing productivity and collaboration with AI-powered tools.
novelty: The innovation lies in the strategic partnership that provides a unified pricing model for Slack across all federal agencies, offering substantial cost savings and streamlined IT acquisition.
stakeholders: Federal government agencies, GSA, Salesforce, and potentially other industry partners involved in the OneGov initiative.

name: Civil Service Relocation and Themed Campuses
description: The UK Government is relocating civil service jobs from London to other parts of the country, aiming to move 50% of UK-based senior civil servants outside of London by 2030. This includes the creation of themed campuses in cities like Darlington, Sheffield, Aberdeen, and Manchester to diversify thinking and attract new talent.
novelty: The innovation lies in the creation of themed campuses that focus on specific policy areas, such as energy in Aberdeen and digital and AI in Manchester, to decentralize decision-making and embed diverse voices into policymaking.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include civil servants, local communities, and government departments. Beneficiaries are the civil servants who gain career opportunities outside London and the local economies that benefit from increased employment and investment.

name: IndiaAI Mission
organisation: Intel Corporation, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
description: A strategic partnership to foster AI capabilities across various sectors in India by equipping students, startups, and government officials with essential AI skills. It includes introducing AI-powered personal computers in classrooms and supporting AI startups with mentorship and resources.
novelty: The initiative is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to building an inclusive AI ecosystem, focusing on skill development, AI literacy, and integration of AI in public services, while also promoting ethical AI use.
stakeholders: Students, startups, government officials, policymakers, public sector leaders, and rural communities in India.

name: TraceTogether app with AI sentiment analysis
description: During the COVID-19 pandemic, Zendesk powered Singapore's TraceTogether app by integrating AI for sentiment analysis to understand the emotional context behind citizen inquiries, enabling more compassionate and tailored responses.
novelty: The innovation lies in using AI for sentiment analysis to enhance empathy in digital services, allowing for more compassionate responses to citizen inquiries.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are the citizens of Singapore, while the stakeholders include public officers and the team at Zendesk responsible for implementing the AI technology.

name: Smart City Initiatives in Aurora
description: Aurora is implementing next-phase initiatives focusing on artificial intelligence, infrastructure expansion, and smart city development. The city aims to support a net-zero smart neighborhood, upgrade network infrastructure, expand fiber capacity, and enhance desktop services, with the long-term goal of becoming a regional technology hub.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating artificial intelligence and smart city technologies to transform Aurora into a regional technology hub.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the residents of Aurora, city management, IT department staff, and external partners like International Data Corporation.

name: Electronic Plan Review
description: Electronic plan review is a digital solution that allows local governments to streamline the permit approval process, making it more efficient and transparent. It has led to significant improvements in permit issuance, cost savings, and economic development outcomes in cities like Detroit and Pleasanton.
novelty: The innovation integrates Esri ArcGIS into the review workflow, allowing for seamless switching between 2D plan views and geospatial maps, and uses AI to improve submission quality. It also extends capabilities to field personnel through mobile access and transforms permit data into actionable insights.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include local government agencies, developers, architects, lenders, and citizens. Stakeholders engaged include city departments like Detroit's Building, Safety Engineering and Environmental Department and the Chicago Department of Transportation.

name: Minute
description: 'Minute' is an AI tool designed to automate note-taking and streamline administrative tasks in local councils, helping to improve efficiency in services such as planning and social care. It is part of the 'Humphrey' suite of AI tools aimed at enhancing public service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in its use of generative AI to automate meeting note-taking, reducing administrative burdens and allowing officials to focus on more critical tasks. It is the first time AI has been used in a meeting chaired by a UK Prime Minister.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are local council officials and staff, including those involved in planning and social care. The broader beneficiaries are local residents who receive improved services.

name: Cultivating Growth through Learning and Development Boot Camp
organisation: Lagos State Ministry of Establishments and Training
description: A three-day boot camp aimed at institutionalising structured learning and professional development for Learning and Development Schedule Officers in the Lagos State Public Service. The initiative focuses on continuous training, lifelong learning, and leveraging emerging technologies to enhance workforce performance.
novelty: The boot camp represents a strategic shift from ad-hoc training to a structured, long-term approach to workforce development, incorporating emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence.
stakeholders: Public service workers in Lagos State, particularly Learning and Development Schedule Officers, and the broader workforce of the state's ministries, departments, and agencies.

name: AI-powered weapons detection system
description: An AI-powered weapons detection system is being piloted at Colchester East Hants Health Centre to enhance safety by screening for potential weapons or dangerous items at the emergency department entrance. The system uses artificial intelligence for non-invasive threat detection, aiming to create a safer environment for patients, visitors, and healthcare providers.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of artificial intelligence for non-invasive threat detection in a healthcare setting, being the first of its kind in Atlantic Canada.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include patients, visitors, and healthcare providers at the Colchester East Hants Health Centre. Stakeholders engaged include Nova Scotia Health, Paladin Security staff, and the Nova Scotia Nurses’ Union.

name: Integration of Practice Management Software with LegalTech and RegTech
description: The integration of Practice Management Software (PMS) with Legal Technology (LegalTech) and Regulatory Technology (RegTech) is transforming the legal sector in Rwanda by enhancing efficiency, client engagement, and compliance. This digital transformation supports Rwanda's Vision 2050, which prioritizes digital governance and innovation.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of PMS with LegalTech and RegTech, which automates administrative tasks, enhances client communication, and ensures compliance with national legislation. This integration also introduces AI-enhanced research tools and e-filing capabilities, streamlining legal processes and supporting e-justice reforms.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include Rwandan law firms, particularly small to mid-sized practices, their clients who benefit from improved service delivery, and the government which supports digital governance initiatives.

name: UK's AI Factory Antenna
description: The UK's AI Factory Antenna is a proposed facility aimed at linking British research expertise with advanced supercomputers across Europe. It is part of the government's Plan for Change to drive growth and innovation in AI, particularly in healthcare and clean energy technologies.
novelty: The innovation lies in its collaborative approach, connecting UK researchers with Europe's top supercomputers to accelerate AI breakthroughs and address global challenges like climate change and disease.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK public research organisations, startups, scientists, and public institutions, as well as European AI Factories and the EuroHPC Joint Undertaking.

name: Integrated Marketplace
description: The Integrated Marketplace is a platform that helps local companies in British Columbia test technologies in real-world environments. It aims to drive innovation, AI adoption, and economic growth by supporting projects that integrate AI into various sectors, such as transportation and healthcare.
novelty: The project is innovative because it provides a real-world testing environment for new technologies, reducing the risk of adopting made-in-B.C. technologies while retaining intellectual property and economic benefits within Canada.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local tech companies, travelers with mobility challenges at Vancouver International Airport, healthcare professionals, and patients benefiting from improved disease detection processes.

name: Ai Everything Kenya x GITEX Kenya
organisation: KAOUN International, Office of the Special Envoy on Technology of the Republic of Kenya, dx⁵
description: Ai Everything Kenya x GITEX Kenya is an event set to debut in Nairobi from 19-21 May 2026, focusing on showcasing AI and tech innovation. It aims to highlight Kenya's leadership in AI and digital transformation, catalyzing partnerships and investments, and accelerating the deployment of emerging technologies.
novelty: The event is East Africa's largest tech and startup event, bringing together a diverse range of stakeholders to foster collaboration and investment in AI and digital technologies, positioning Kenya as a leader in digital transformation.
stakeholders: Policymakers, technologists, academia, private sector, civil society, creatives, development partners, global innovators, local talent, governments, enterprises, experts, investors, SMEs, startups, digital promotion agencies.

name: North Bethesda Metro Station Mixed-Use Development
description: A transformative mixed-use development at the North Bethesda Metro Station, anchored by the University of Maryland Institute for Health Computing. The project aims to create a hub for life sciences and AI innovation, integrating research, commercial space, and housing in a transit-oriented environment.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its integration of life sciences and AI research with transit-oriented development, creating a unique environment for collaboration and economic growth.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include scientists, researchers, businesses in life sciences and AI, local residents, and commuters. Stakeholders engaged include WMATA, Montgomery County, University of Maryland, and various government officials.

name: Reimagined State Accelerator for Climate and Agriculture
organisation: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change (TBI), Planet
description: A partnership to integrate satellite imagery and AI into government decision-making for climate adaptation, agriculture, and disaster risk management. It involves using geospatial data to monitor forests, manage fisheries, track coastal erosion, predict landslides and flooding, and improve crop monitoring.
novelty: Embedding geospatial data and AI into public-service delivery and government decision-making, providing real-time monitoring and predictive insights for proactive climate adaptation and agricultural transformation.
stakeholders: Government ministries, departments, and agencies in Zanzibar, Philippines, and Sierra Leone; local AI firms; beneficiaries include local populations affected by climate change and agricultural challenges.

name: AI Hackathon 2025
organisation: San José State University, City of San José, Microsoft, Slalom, Spektra Systems
description: A full-day AI Hackathon hosted by San José State University, in collaboration with the City of San José and tech industry leaders, where participants applied AI tools to real use cases like multilingual chatbots and data-driven solutions for civic engagement.
novelty: The hackathon fostered collaboration between students, city employees, and technologists to develop AI solutions for public service challenges, demonstrating the power of public-private-academic partnerships.
stakeholders: Participants included SJSU students, City of San José employees, and industry professionals. Beneficiaries are the residents of San José who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Generative AI for Legislative Cost Analysis
description: The California Department of Finance is using generative AI tools to analyze the cost and budget impact of legislative bills. This innovation aims to reduce the manual workload of drafting bill summaries and analyses, and improve the efficiency and accuracy of financial analysis for policymakers.
novelty: The project leverages generative AI to automate and enhance the analysis of legislative bills, integrating AI into government workflows to modernize public service delivery.
stakeholders: State employees, California policymakers, and the general public who benefit from more efficient and transparent government services.

name: Generative AI for Public Services in California
description: The California Department of Technology, under the leadership of State Chief Technology Innovation Officer Vera Zakem, is implementing generative AI tools to improve public services such as traffic management, wildfire response, and digital service delivery. The initiative aims to enhance government efficiency and service delivery to Californians.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating generative AI across various public sectors, focusing on transparency, accountability, and responsible use. It includes the first-in-the-nation deployment of GenAI technology for government efficiency and digital service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include California residents, government agencies, technology developers, and industry partners. Beneficiaries are the citizens of California who will experience improved public services.

name: Uncrewed Triple Challenge (UTC)
organisation: Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC), Michigan Department of Transportation (MDOT), Department of Military and Veterans Affairs (DMVA), Michigan National Guard (MING)
description: The Uncrewed Triple Challenge (UTC) is a state-sponsored competition in Michigan that tests autonomous drones across air, land, and water. It aims to push the boundaries of autonomous drone capabilities and establish Michigan as a leader in uncrewed systems and advanced mobility.
novelty: The UTC is the first-of-its-kind competition that challenges drones to complete a three-part course autonomously, covering maritime, airborne, and ground routes without human intervention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector entities, defense industry, tech entrepreneurs, and participants from seven U.S. states and two countries. Beneficiaries include the defense and manufacturing sectors, as well as potential future users of autonomous systems for tasks like delivering medicine and equipment.

name: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Strategy for the Federal Public Service
description: The AI Strategy aims to enhance government services, foster innovation, and ensure the well-being of Canadians by integrating AI into the federal public service. It seeks to transform service delivery and reinforce Canada's position as a leader in responsible AI adoption.
novelty: The strategy is innovative as it integrates AI with the government's digital ambition and data strategy, focusing on responsible and ethical AI adoption. It involves collaboration with diverse stakeholders to ensure a comprehensive approach.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Canadian citizens and government agencies. Stakeholders engaged include government agencies, industry experts, bargaining agents, academia, and civil society.

name: Generative AI-powered Virtual Assistant
organisation: North Carolina Department of Commerce’s Division of Employment Security (NCDES)
description: An intelligent virtual assistant powered by generative AI to help individuals navigate the unemployment insurance claim application process, providing consistent answers 24/7 and reducing call volumes.
novelty: One of the first generative AI-powered, public-facing solutions in North Carolina, integrating AI to enhance citizen services while improving operational efficiency.
stakeholders: Users are individuals filing unemployment claims; beneficiaries include North Carolinians seeking unemployment benefits; stakeholders include NCDES, AWS, and Ernst & Young.

name: ePerolehan
description: ePerolehan is a digital system launched in 2000 by CDC to digitalise Malaysia's public procurement process. It connects over 180,000 registered suppliers, including SMEs and micro-enterprises, to democratise access, enhance transparency, and reduce friction in public-sector spending.
novelty: ePerolehan is innovative for its focus on ethical values, integrity, and inclusion in digital transformation. It pioneers the digitalisation of public procurement with AI-driven analytics, real-time decision dashboards, and user-centric design updates.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government entities, registered suppliers, SMEs, micro-enterprises, and the general public benefiting from enhanced transparency and access in public procurement.

name: Core42 Sovereign Public Cloud
description: The Core42 Sovereign Public Cloud, powered by Microsoft Azure, is a secure, compliant, and scalable cloud solution tailored for regulated sectors such as government, healthcare, finance, and energy in the UAE. It ensures data sovereignty and regulatory compliance while enabling AI and cloud capabilities.
novelty: The innovation eliminates the traditional trade-off between innovation and compliance by integrating Microsoft's hyperscale infrastructure with Core42's sovereign controls, creating a secure environment for regulated sectors.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include UAE government entities, healthcare providers, financial institutions, and energy companies. Stakeholders engaged include technology leaders, CIOs, CTOs, and regulatory bodies.

name: AI Leadership Training Programme
description: A programme to upskill AI leaders across federal government entities in the UAE, focusing on equipping chief AI officers with knowledge on AI trends, governance models, and sector-specific applications to drive innovation and transformation within their entities.
novelty: The programme is innovative in its exclusive focus on chief AI officers, providing them with cutting-edge knowledge and tools to lead AI projects, and fostering dialogue between UAE AI executives and global thought leaders.
stakeholders: Chief AI officers in UAE federal government entities, UAE Government, University of Birmingham in Dubai, international AI experts and academics.

name: Automation of Routine Tasks in Civil Service
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The innovation involves automating routine tasks performed by civil servants, particularly at lower grades, to increase productivity and achieve significant cost savings. The analysis suggests that automating these tasks could save £36bn, contributing to a total anticipated £45bn in productivity gains from better digital services.
novelty: The project is innovative in its large-scale assessment of the potential for automation within the public sector, focusing on the routine nature of tasks and projecting significant long-term savings.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include civil servants, particularly those in lower grades who perform routine tasks, government departments, and the broader public sector workforce.

name: AI Agents for HR and Financial Operations
description: AI agents are used to streamline HR processes by automating job description creation, candidate shortlisting, and policy compliance. This allows HR managers to focus on strategic support rather than manual processes. The AI also aids in policy analysis and contract intelligence, improving efficiency and decision-making in government operations.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI agents to automate and enhance HR and financial operations, reducing manual effort and increasing efficiency in government agencies. It also includes AI-driven insights for skills-based talent strategies and policy compliance.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are HR managers, government agencies, and public sector employees. The stakeholders engaged include government policymakers and public sector organisations.

name: National Legal Portal
description: The National Legal Portal is a centralized platform providing citizens and businesses with access to comprehensive legal information and services. It supports legal information searches, responses to legal queries, and a digital system for managing feedback and petitions related to legal documents. The portal is integrated with the national digital identity system, VNeID, for secure access.
novelty: The portal uses advanced technologies like artificial intelligence and multilingual functionality to enhance user experience and engagement. It represents a shift from passive legal service provision to proactive government engagement, simplifying compliance and reducing administrative burdens.
stakeholders: Users include citizens, businesses, public officials, and civil servants. Beneficiaries are the general public and the business community, who gain improved access to legal information and services. Stakeholders engaged include the Ministry of Justice, relevant ministries, agencies, and local authorities.

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
description: A trial involving over 20,000 civil servants using Microsoft 365 Copilot, a generative AI tool, to assist with tasks such as drafting documents, summarising emails, and updating records. The trial demonstrated significant time savings, with users saving an average of 26 minutes per day, equating to nearly two weeks per year.
novelty: The innovation lies in the large-scale application of AI to routine government tasks, demonstrating substantial productivity gains and the potential to transform public service delivery by reducing administrative burdens.
stakeholders: The primary users are civil servants across various departments, including policy officials and Work Coaches. Beneficiaries include job seekers and the general public who receive more efficient public services.

name: Arizona's AI Steering Committee
description: Arizona's first Artificial Intelligence Steering Committee is established to guide AI-related policies ensuring ethical and transparent use across the state. The committee is tasked with creating a plan for the responsible use of AI within 12 months.
novelty: This is the first AI Steering Committee in Arizona, focusing on balancing AI's potential with ethical governance and transparency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Arizona State University faculty and alumni, state government officials, and the general public of Arizona who will benefit from responsible AI policies.

name: Technology Modernization Fund (TMF) New Funding Model
description: The General Services Administration (GSA) is proposing a new funding model for the Technology Modernization Fund (TMF) to make it a revolving or working capital fund. This model allows GSA to collect funding from other agencies using funds that are no longer available for obligation, providing the TMF with necessary funds to address critical technology challenges, modernize high-priority systems, improve AI adoption, and support cross-government collaboration.
novelty: The innovation lies in the 'pass the hat' funding model, which allows the TMF to collect expired funds from other agencies, increasing the fund's resources without relying solely on congressional appropriations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies that will benefit from IT modernization, the GSA, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), and ultimately the American public who will benefit from improved government services.

name: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in Australian Public Sector
description: The Australian public sector is exploring the use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) to improve productivity and efficiency in policy work. The focus is on using GenAI for administrative tasks that do not involve sensitive data, with the aim of augmenting rather than replacing human workers.
novelty: The innovation lies in the cautious and experimental approach to integrating GenAI in public sector policy work, with clear outcomes and evaluation processes to ensure value for money and risk mitigation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include senior public servants, government agencies, and the Australian public, who are the beneficiaries of improved public services.

name: Artificial Intelligence Steering Committee
description: The Artificial Intelligence Steering Committee is tasked with guiding Arizona's AI-related policies to ensure ethical and transparent use across the state. The committee will evaluate existing policies on AI use in state government, define oversight roles, and build procurement and audit toolkits to support transparent and accountable AI implementation.
novelty: This is Arizona's first AI Steering Committee, focusing on balancing the promotion and governance of AI use in the public sector to ensure responsible implementation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Arizona State University faculty and alumni, Arizona state government, and the citizens of Arizona who will benefit from responsible AI policies.

name: AI Continent Action Plan
description: The AI Continent Action Plan is a strategy aimed at improving infrastructure, streamlining regulations, and broadening access to capital to support the EU's AI ecosystem. It includes legal reforms, technical infrastructure upgrades, and workforce development to make Europe a global leader in AI.
novelty: The plan combines regulatory frameworks with efforts to build institutional, financial, and technical capacity, including the creation of high-performance computing centers and a new AI Office for compliance guidance.
stakeholders: AI researchers, startup entrepreneurs, investors, EU member states, and the general public benefiting from improved AI services and safety measures.

name: Private 5G Network for Public Sector
description: Verizon is implementing private 5G networks for public sector organizations, providing ultra-fast, low-latency wireless connectivity tailored to specific needs, such as ports, educational institutions, and rural communities. This infrastructure supports advanced technologies like AI and IoT, enhancing service delivery and operational efficiency.
novelty: The innovation lies in the deployment of dedicated private 5G networks that offer high-reliability, low-latency connectivity, enabling the use of AI-driven applications and IoT devices in challenging environments, thus supporting digital transformation in the public sector.
stakeholders: Public sector organizations including ports, educational institutions, rural communities, and their constituents who benefit from improved connectivity and service delivery.

name: Combined Joint All-Domain Command and Control (CJADC2)
description: CJADC2 is a military initiative aimed at creating a meta-network for digital command and data-sharing to improve command and control systems, decision-making, and execution across the joint force globally.
novelty: The innovation lies in its focus on integrating advanced technology, AI, and digital networks to bridge jurisdictional divides and enhance military operations on a global scale.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the United States military, interagency allies, private sector entrepreneurs, and technology innovators.

name: AI and Biometrics Strategy
description: The AI and biometrics strategy aims to ensure organisations develop and deploy AI and biometric technologies lawfully, supporting innovation and growth while protecting public privacy. It includes reviewing automated decision-making systems, auditing facial recognition technology use, setting expectations for AI model training, and developing a statutory code of practice.
novelty: The strategy focuses on scrutinising emerging AI risks and trends, such as agentic AI, and aims to balance innovation with privacy protection by setting clear expectations and developing a statutory code of practice.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public, organisations using AI and biometric technologies, and government bodies like the Department for Work and Pensions. Stakeholders engaged include Parliamentarians, industry representatives, and civil society.

name: National Digital Exchange (NDX)
description: The National Digital Exchange is a digital marketplace designed to modernise how the UK public sector purchases technology. It aims to save £1.2 billion annually by providing faster access to pre-approved tech deals at nationally negotiated prices, using an AI-powered engine to match public sector needs with suppliers.
novelty: The platform introduces a first-of-its-kind digital marketplace for the public sector, allowing for real-time rating and reviewing of tech products, similar to an app store, and uses AI to match buyers with suppliers efficiently.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public sector teams across hospitals, schools, and government departments, as well as UK tech firms, particularly small businesses aiming to increase their involvement in government contracts.

name: Federal Government AI Deployment
organisation: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
description: The initiative focuses on the federal government's adoption of artificial intelligence to enhance efficiency, improve public services, and reduce costs for taxpayers. It aims to eliminate obstacles and accelerate responsible AI innovation within federal agencies.
novelty: The project emphasizes the integration of AI into federal systems and processes to unleash significant productivity gains, potentially worth up to $532 billion annually by 2028.
stakeholders: Federal government agencies, taxpayers, House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, AI developers and partners.

name: U.K. Sovereign AI Industry Forum
description: The U.K. Sovereign AI Industry Forum is an initiative to build sovereign compute infrastructure, invest in AI research and skills, and foster AI leadership across sectors in the U.K. It aims to strengthen the nation's economic security by advancing sovereign AI infrastructure and accelerating the growth of the U.K. AI startup ecosystem.
novelty: The project is innovative as it unites leading U.K. businesses to develop and deploy AI infrastructure, positioning the U.K. as a global leader in AI. It also includes the deployment of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs and the establishment of an AI Technology Center for training developers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include leading U.K. businesses such as Babcock, BAE Systems, BT, National Grid, and Standard Chartered, as well as AI developers, startups, and public services like the NHS. Beneficiaries include the U.K. economy and AI sector.

name: Private 5G Network in Istres
description: Deployment of a private 5G network in Istres to support critical communications, enhance public safety, and enable AI-powered urban solutions. It improves emergency response capabilities and offers cost-effective surveillance solutions.
novelty: First mid-sized European city to implement a sovereign private 5G infrastructure, significantly reducing costs compared to traditional fiber installations and supporting scalable public sector applications.
stakeholders: Citizens of Istres, local government, emergency response teams, public safety officials.

name: Total Experience Management (TXM)
description: Total Experience Management (TXM) is a customer experience solution powered by Amazon Web Services (AWS) that enables government agencies to implement advanced CX capabilities. It includes features like intelligent document processing, AI-assisted customer interactions, topic mining, and sentiment analysis, aimed at improving service delivery, efficiency, and leadership value.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive integration of FedRAMP-authorized technologies and AWS services, enabling advanced capabilities such as AI-powered document processing and customer interaction enhancements, which streamline operations and improve mission outcomes for government agencies.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are federal agencies, including the Office of Personnel Management, Department of Veterans Affairs, and Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, which benefit from improved service delivery and operational efficiency. The end users are the citizens receiving government services.

name: Software Fast Track (SWFT)
description: The Software Fast Track (SWFT) program is designed to allow the Pentagon to integrate software capabilities much faster than currently possible by replacing outdated acquisition processes with a modernized approach that uses AI and continuous monitoring.
novelty: SWFT replaces the traditional Risk Management Framework (RMF) and authority to operate (ATO) processes with a faster, AI-driven approach that includes continuous monitoring and third-party assessments, allowing for rapid software adoption.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Department of Defense, service CIOs, chief information security officers, the acquisition and sustainment directorate, Pentagon directorates, and industry partners providing input and tools for the SWFT program.

name: Veterans Benefits Management System (VBMS) AI and Automation
description: The Veterans Benefits Management System (VBMS) implemented AI and automation to improve efficiency in processing disability claims, particularly following the PACT Act. This system automates the review of claims, which often contain extensive documentation, and provides AI-recommended answers to key questions, significantly reducing manual processing time.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI and automation to handle large volumes of documentation and streamline the claims process, which was previously a tedious manual task. This approach has automated over 700,000 claims and saved hundreds of thousands of hours annually.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include veterans who benefit from faster claims processing, the Department of Veterans Affairs which manages the system, and the government which aims to enhance efficiency and reduce costs.

name: One Big Thing 2025
description: The One Big Thing 2025 initiative aims to upskill half a million civil servants in artificial intelligence to improve public services. The training will provide civil servants with a working-level knowledge of AI, enabling them to drive innovation, streamline processes, and enhance technological expertise.
novelty: The initiative focuses on equipping all civil servants with AI literacy, allowing them to integrate AI into their work to improve efficiency and service delivery. It emphasizes practical application and encourages civil servants to take calculated risks and learn continuously.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are civil servants across various departments, from policymakers to frontline workers, who will receive AI training. The beneficiaries are the public, who will experience improved public services.

name: Self-driving vehicle pilots
description: The UK government is fast-tracking pilots for self-driving taxi- and bus-like services, allowing them to operate without a safety driver from 2026. These services will be available for public booking via an app, aiming to improve road safety, create jobs, and boost the economy.
novelty: The innovation involves the introduction of self-driving vehicles without safety drivers for public use, supported by robust legislation and safety measures, positioning the UK as a leader in autonomous vehicle technology.
stakeholders: Users include the general public who will book the services via an app. Beneficiaries include local communities, particularly in rural areas, and those unable to drive. Stakeholders engaged include the UK government, technology companies like Wayve and Oxa, and partners such as Uber and Nissan.

name: AI Governance and Integration in Public Services
description: Several states in the U.S. have established public-private AI task forces to assess risks and opportunities and make recommendations on leveraging AI for public service delivery. North Carolina, in particular, has partnered with OpenAI to use ChatGPT for analyzing publicly available data to improve government service efficiency.
novelty: The innovation lies in the collaboration between state governments and the private sector to establish AI governance frameworks and integrate AI technologies like ChatGPT into public services, ensuring ethical and efficient use.
stakeholders: State governments, private sector companies, public service users, citizens benefiting from improved government services.

name: AI Continent Action Plan
description: The AI Continent Action Plan is a multi-billion euro strategy to build Europe's AI future, focusing on infrastructure, data access, AI adoption in strategic sectors, skills development, and regulatory support. It aims to foster innovation, scientific collaboration, and increase AI adoption across various sectors.
novelty: The plan represents a shift from cautious oversight to proactive enablement, proposing large-scale AI infrastructure, unified regulatory approaches, and comprehensive support for AI development and adoption.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI developers, businesses operating in the EU, strategic sectors like manufacturing and pharma, educational institutions, and regulatory bodies.

name: Federal AI Sandbox for Weather Forecasting
organisation: MITRE, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service (NWS)
description: Development of new weather forecasting methods using the Federal AI Sandbox and NVIDIA Earth-2 to advance high-resolution spatial and temporal weather forecasts, improving public safety and national security.
novelty: Integration of NVIDIA technologies to create high-resolution forecasts and visualizations, achieving 1-kilometer level precision for oceanic regions, matching land-based prediction accuracy.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public safety officials, national security agencies, maritime industry stakeholders, and citizens relying on accurate weather forecasts.

name: Public Service Reform for Long-term Challenges
description: The innovation focuses on reforming public service systems to align with the government's agenda while strengthening capabilities to address long-term complex challenges. This includes improving productivity, enhancing social engagement, reforming systems for transitions like energy, and increasing regional engagement.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to reallocate resources and capabilities within the public service to focus on long-term challenges, breaking down silos, and using data, digital, and AI for improved delivery. It emphasizes a relational approach to policy and process, involving all stakeholders in collaboration and co-design.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public service leaders, government agencies, communities, and regional partners in the Asia-Pacific. Beneficiaries are Australian citizens who will experience improved public services and engagement.

name: Data Use and Access Bill
description: The Data Use and Access Bill provides a framework for enabling data-driven innovation across sectors in the UK, modernising how individuals, businesses, and public services can access, share, and use data. It aims to support the digital economy by avoiding premature legislative obligations on AI and copyright, instead focusing on evidence-led policymaking.
novelty: The Bill adopts a pragmatic approach by avoiding immediate legislative obligations on AI and copyright, opting for faster reporting timelines and considering global AI development, which helps prevent unintended consequences for the UK's AI ecosystem.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include individuals, businesses, public services in the UK, and policymakers involved in digital and AI sectors.

name: FedRAMP High and DOD Impact Levels 4 and 5 Authorization for Claude and Llama AI Models
description: Amazon Web Services has received federal authorizations allowing Anthropic’s Claude and Meta’s Llama AI models to be used within high-sensitivity government computing environments. This includes achieving FedRAMP 'High' authorization and the Defense Department’s Impact Levels 4 and 5, enabling the use of these AI models in AWS GovCloud for sensitive civilian and military information.
novelty: This is the first time a cloud provider has received such a high level of authorization for Claude and Llama AI models, allowing them to be used in high-security government environments.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, defense agencies, and public sector entities that require secure and compliant access to advanced AI tools for mission-critical applications.

name: Secure Cloud Landing Zone and DevSecOps Tools for DCSA
description: Omni Federal has been contracted to modernize the IT system used for background investigations by deploying a secure cloud landing zone based on AWS GovCloud and developing DevSecOps tools and pipelines for software integration, testing, and deployment.
novelty: The project introduces a secure cloud landing zone and automated DevSecOps pipelines to enhance the efficiency and security of background check processes for the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency, DOD employees, contractors, and federal employees in non-sensitive public trust positions.

name: QuProtect
description: QuProtect is an all-in-one network security solution designed to empower cybersecurity leaders with a flexible and resilient framework, enabling government agencies to remain compliant with quantum mandates and regulatory requirements while securing critical data against AI and quantum computing threats.
novelty: QuProtect offers a seamless transition through every stage from discovery to remediation, ensuring effortless crypto-agility and robust protection without disrupting communication or uptime. It provides administrators with full control over cryptography algorithms, aligning with industry standards like NIST and the Quantum Computing Cyber Security Preparedness Act.
stakeholders: Government agencies, cybersecurity leaders, Carahsoft's reseller partners, AWS Marketplace, NASA Solutions for Enterprise-Wide Procurement (SEWP) V, IT Enterprise Solutions – Software 2 (ITES-SW2), and OMNIA Partners.

name: Citizen Services AI Market
description: The Citizen Services AI Market involves the integration of artificial intelligence by public sector entities to improve service delivery, operational efficiency, and citizen engagement. It includes applications such as virtual assistance, predictive analytics, and intelligent case management to streamline processes and enhance citizen interactions.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to automate repetitive administrative tasks, provide continuous support via virtual assistants, and enhance planning with predictive analytics, leading to smarter, more efficient, and citizen-centric public services.
stakeholders: Governments, public sector organizations, citizens, AI technology providers, and data security experts are key stakeholders. Beneficiaries include citizens who receive improved services and public agencies that achieve cost reductions and efficiency gains.

name: HealthHub AI
description: HealthHub AI is an innovation that integrates AI agents into the national healthcare app HealthHub, allowing users to book appointments and access health information through a conversational assistant. It aims to make healthcare services more accessible and personalized by customizing health content and recommendations based on users' life stages and healthcare needs.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its use of AI agents to execute tasks within a healthcare app, enhancing user experience by simplifying appointment bookings and providing multilingual support. It also incorporates local-language large language models to expand language accessibility.
stakeholders: Users of the HealthHub app, public healthcare institutions, private sector partners, and developers using healthcare APIs are the primary stakeholders. Beneficiaries include patients and healthcare providers who will experience improved service accessibility and efficiency.

name: HKPilot
organisation: Digital Policy Office (DPO), Civil Service College (CSC), Hong Kong Generative AI Research and Development Center (HKGAI)
description: HKPilot is a generative AI-based document processing assistant designed to support civil servants with tasks such as document drafting, translation, and summarisation. It aims to reduce administrative workload, enhance efficiency, and free up time for strategic work.
novelty: HKPilot is innovative as it is a locally developed AI application tailored specifically for government use in Hong Kong, taking into account the city's linguistic, cultural, and policy contexts.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include civil servants in various government bureaux and departments, as well as the general public who will benefit from more efficient public services.

name: Office of AI and Digital
description: The Office of AI and Digital is a new tech unit created to help Northern Ireland become a leader in responsible AI and transform public sector delivery. It will work alongside the Office of Science and Technology and collaborate with various stakeholders to advise on tech services and policy, and support the creation of a national AI strategy and action plan.
novelty: The project is innovative as it aims to position Northern Ireland at the forefront of AI innovation, focusing on responsible AI and transforming public services through a dedicated governmental office.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the citizens of Northern Ireland, government departments, industry, and academia. Stakeholders engaged include the Northern Ireland Civil Service, Queen’s University, and Ulster University.

name: Unleashing American Drone Dominance and Restoring American Airspace Sovereignty Executive Orders
description: The Executive Orders aim to expand the production and use of uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) and electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing (eVTOL) aircraft, safeguard critical infrastructure, and enhance federal and local authorities' ability to manage UAS operations. They include initiatives like the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program and measures to facilitate U.S. drone production, export, and procurement.
novelty: The Executive Orders introduce new programs such as the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program and emphasize the use of AI tools for expediting UAS waiver applications. They also set aggressive deadlines for regulatory actions and expand federal authority in counter-UAS activities.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies like the FAA, Department of Defense, and Department of Transportation, state and local governments, private sector partners in UAS and eVTOL development, and the general public benefiting from enhanced airspace security and drone technology.

name: AI Government Transformation
description: Cohere is partnering with the Canadian and UK governments to transform government operations using AI. The initiative aims to enhance government services, improve national sovereignty, and collaborate on AI research, focusing on practical risks and security safeguards for AI models.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its large-scale deployment of AI to transform government operations, enhance service delivery, and improve national sovereignty, while also focusing on AI safety and security.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Canadian and UK governments, Cohere, the Canadian AI Safety Institute, and the broader Canadian research community. Beneficiaries are government service users in Canada and the UK.

name: AI.gov
description: AI.gov is a federal website designed to accelerate government innovation with AI. It includes tools for analyzing agency-wide AI implementation, an AI chatbot, and an API to connect federal systems with private AI models. The initiative aims to integrate AI into federal agencies and promote public-private AI partnerships.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into federal agencies, promoting public-private partnerships, and providing tools like a groundbreaking analysis console, AI chatbot, and API for system integration.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, private sector partners, and ultimately, the American public who will benefit from improved government services through AI integration.

name: GenAI Applications in Public Sector
description: Public sector organisations are increasingly adopting Generative AI (GenAI) applications to improve constituent and employee support, experience solutions, and content generation. The focus is on leveraging AI to enhance productivity, automation, and efficiency while addressing security and privacy concerns.
novelty: The innovation lies in the widespread adoption of GenAI applications across the public sector, transforming traditional processes and prioritizing data security and privacy in AI implementations.
stakeholders: Public sector leaders, employees, constituents, IT decision-makers, and organisations leveraging GenAI applications.

name: G7 GovAI Grand Challenge and G7 AI Network (GAIN)
description: A suite of new AI initiatives aimed at identifying, developing, and implementing government AI solutions for community benefit, enhancing public services, and increasing government efficiency while respecting human rights and privacy.
novelty: The initiative focuses on using AI for public good by keeping humans in the loop, promoting transparency, fairness, and accountability, and ensuring responsible design and deployment of AI systems.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens and businesses who will receive enhanced public services. Stakeholders engaged include government officials, small- and medium-size enterprises, and the broader public sector.

name: Humphrey AI
description: Humphrey AI is a suite of AI tools designed to streamline public sector services, cut delays, and improve productivity by addressing bureaucratic pain points such as duplicated administration, siloed data, and slow decision-making. It aims to reduce reliance on external consultants and enhance citizen experiences.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to digitizing and automating routine administrative tasks across the public sector, aiming to unlock significant productivity gains and improve service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK civil servants, public sector organizations, and citizens who will benefit from more efficient public services.

name: G7 GovAI Grand Challenge
description: The G7 GovAI Grand Challenge is an initiative to facilitate the adoption of artificial intelligence in the public sector. It aims to share information on effective AI tools to enhance public administration efficiency.
novelty: The project is innovative in its international collaboration to promote AI adoption in public services and its focus on creating a network to share successful AI tools across countries.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public sector organizations and small companies. Stakeholders engaged include G7 member countries and EU representatives.

name: AI-powered chatbot on Goa Online portal
description: An AI-powered chatbot is set to launch on the Goa Online portal to simplify citizen access to over 270 government services, improving public service efficiency and engagement.
novelty: The chatbot leverages AI to enhance citizen interaction with government services, making access more efficient and user-friendly.
stakeholders: Citizens of Goa, government service providers, IT and digital economy stakeholders.

name: AI Cabinet Retreat
description: A two-day Cabinet retreat led by Ghana's Minister for Communication, Digital Technology and Innovations to explore AI applications in governance and public service delivery, aiming to develop strategic plans and foster inter-ministerial collaboration.
novelty: The retreat focuses on integrating AI into governance to enhance public services and drive economic growth, with plans to potentially create an AI Commission for overseeing national strategies.
stakeholders: Government ministers, AI experts, and citizens who will benefit from improved public services.

name: AI Capabilities Rollout
description: The Public Service Commission is implementing AI capabilities using Microsoft's tools to improve productivity, decision-making, service delivery, and operational efficiencies. The rollout is structured in four phases: readiness, deployment, adoption, and monitoring and improvement.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its structured, phased approach to AI adoption, focusing on governance, secure deployment, and continuous improvement, leveraging Microsoft's advanced AI tools.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Public Service Commission staff, who will use and benefit from the AI tools, and the broader public who will experience improved public service delivery.

name: AI Training for Public Servants
description: The UAE government has made an AI campus course mandatory for its entire federal workforce of 54,000 public servants to improve AI fluency and skills.
novelty: Mandatory AI training for the entire federal workforce to enhance AI literacy and skills at scale.
stakeholders: Public servants in the UAE federal workforce, government departments, policymakers.

name: Open Government Data and AI Integration
organisation: Digital Transformation Office of the Presidency of Türkiye
description: The integration of open government data and artificial intelligence (AI) in Türkiye aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and innovation in public administration. By making non-confidential public data accessible and reusable, and analyzing it with AI, the initiative seeks to optimize decision-making processes and foster economic and social innovation.
novelty: The project is innovative in its combination of open government data with AI technologies to improve public sector efficiency, policy-making, and civic engagement. It leverages AI's analytical capabilities to extract meaningful insights from large datasets, driving smart city initiatives and economic innovation.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens, researchers, entrepreneurs, tech companies, and public sector agencies. Stakeholders engaged include the Ministry of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change, the Ministry of Health, metropolitan municipalities, and the Digital Transformation Office of the Presidency of Türkiye.

name: National AI Action Plan
description: The National AI Action Plan is a comprehensive roadmap launched by Thailand to become the ASEAN hub for AI by 2027. It focuses on infrastructure development, cutting-edge research, digital talent cultivation, and creating ethical and legal frameworks for responsible AI use. The plan includes initiatives to enhance public service delivery, train AI specialists, and establish regulatory safeguards.
novelty: The plan is innovative due to its comprehensive approach, including the establishment of a National AI Committee, significant investment in AI infrastructure and talent development, and partnerships with global tech players. It also emphasizes ethical AI development and inclusive access.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Thai citizens, government agencies, educational institutions, private sector partners, and SMEs. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the Thai government, global tech companies, and the Electronic Transactions Development Agency (ETDA).

name: Samarkand Ministerial Declaration on Advancing Digital Transformation, Innovation, and Robust Data Governance in the Public Sector
organisation: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UN DESA), Ministry of Digital Technologies of the Government of the Republic of Uzbekistan
description: The Samarkand Ministerial Declaration aims to advance digital transformation, innovation, and robust data governance in the public sector. It identifies seven key priorities related to digitalisation and promotes cooperation between the North and the South to enable digital transformation.
novelty: The Declaration promotes a two-way cooperation between the North and the South in enabling digital transformation, focusing on digital information, responsible AI adoption, data governance, cybersecurity, and global cooperation.
stakeholders: Public servants, government officials, and citizens who will benefit from improved digital public services and governance.

name: GovInsider's Festival of Innovation 2025
description: An annual event showcasing tech and strategy sessions from around the world to foster public sector innovation and sustain positive change in society.
novelty: The event brings together global leaders to discuss and share innovative approaches in public sector innovation, featuring exclusive interviews and discussions on future-ready public sector workforce, AI efficiency, government resilience, and citizen-centric services.
stakeholders: Public sector agencies, government leaders, digital governments, and public service professionals.

name: San Jose AI Incentive Program
description: The San Jose AI Incentive Program awards up to $50,000 in grants to three early-stage AI companies that establish operations in the city. The program aims to support the development of these companies by providing resources, training, and consulting expertise through local business partnerships.
novelty: The program is part of a broader initiative to integrate AI into public services, creating a 'civic sandbox' for experimentation and innovation in government operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include early-stage AI companies, local business partners, city officials, and the residents of San Jose who benefit from improved public services.

name: CORE: COrner Reflector Enabled Remote Sensing
description: A system that tracks tiny movements in buildings, bridges, and other structures to spot potential safety issues early using radar technology and AI.
novelty: Uses special radar technology and artificial intelligence to constantly monitor infrastructure, providing a safer, more efficient way to survey large sites without manual checks.
stakeholders: Public sector partners, engineers, infrastructure managers, local councils, government agencies.

name: One-stop digital channels for government services
description: The initiative aims to transform the delivery of government services into a single-access, seamless, and virtual constituent experience through one-stop digital channels. These channels enhance customer engagement, automate service delivery, provide proactive services, improve decision-making, and increase efficiency in fraud detection and prevention.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a unified digital platform for government services that leverages emerging technologies like AI and blockchain, requiring modernization of legacy systems and automation of processes.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are the citizens and residents of GCC countries, while stakeholders include government IT departments, technology providers, and cybersecurity experts.

name: Restoring Common Sense to Federal Procurement
description: The innovation involves an executive order aimed at modernizing federal procurement processes by reducing red tape and increasing efficiency. It includes changes to the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to streamline government operations and enhance procurement strategies.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on leveraging emerging technologies, such as AI, to analyze acquisition vehicles and streamline contracting processes. It emphasizes speed and commercial solutions, departing from traditional, process-heavy approaches.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, contractors, and government officials who are involved in or benefit from streamlined procurement processes. Beneficiaries also include the Department of Defense and other federal offices that rely on efficient acquisition systems.

name: Automated Birth Certificate Filing System
organisation: New Jersey Department of Health, New Jersey Department of Human Services, New Jersey Innovation Institute, RWJBarnabas Health
description: A pilot project that automates the filing and creation of birth certificates by electronically transmitting birth record data from hospital EHR systems directly to the State’s Vital Statistics Birth Registry, reducing administrative burdens and increasing data accuracy.
novelty: The project automates the data exchange process for birth certificates, reducing manual data entry, minimizing errors, and improving workflow efficiency by using the New Jersey Health Information Network (NJHIN).
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include hospital staff, birth registrars, new parents, and the New Jersey Department of Health. Stakeholders engaged include the New Jersey Department of Health, New Jersey Department of Human Services, New Jersey Innovation Institute, RWJBarnabas Health, and hospitals in New Jersey.

name: HealthSpark
description: HealthSpark is an initiative to fast-track AI innovation in Canadian health care and services by providing training, mentorship, and access to networks and AI engineering expertise to high-potential scaleups and startups.
novelty: HealthSpark is innovative because it accelerates the development of AI solutions in healthcare, addressing critical issues like staffing shortages, wait times, and patient outcomes through a secure health AI network.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include high-potential scaleups and startups in the AI sector, healthcare providers, patients, and the Canadian government.

name: San Jose AI Incentive Program
description: The San Jose AI Incentive Program awards grants to early-stage AI companies to establish operations in the city, aiming to support their development and integrate AI innovations into public services.
novelty: The program fosters a pro-business environment by providing financial support and resources to AI startups, encouraging them to contribute to public sector advancements.
stakeholders: Early-stage AI companies, local business partners, city officials, and residents of San Jose, especially those benefiting from AI-driven public services.

name: Citywide Digital Transformation Project
organisation: Sandy Springs community, in partnership with Georgia Tech
description: A citywide digital transformation initiative in Sandy Springs, Georgia, aiming to enhance city operations and services through advanced analytics and AI. The project focuses on automating the permitting process to improve efficiency and reduce human error.
novelty: The project leverages AI for optical character recognition and image recognition to automate the permitting process, reducing time-consuming tasks and human error. It also involves developing a centralized data warehouse for better data management and insights.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Sandy Springs residents, developers, city staff, and city leaders. Stakeholders engaged in the project include the city's steering committee, technical working group, and digital development team.

name: AI-Enabled Project Management with OnePlan
description: Sonoma County implemented OnePlan, a GenAI-powered software as a service solution, to enhance its project portfolio tracking and reporting capabilities. It allows staff to query data and gain insights without custom programming, streamlining access to information.
novelty: The use of GenAI to empower staff to directly query data without custom programming, enhancing efficiency and accessibility.
stakeholders: County staff who manage project portfolios and require insights for decision-making.

name: CMMI model for prior authorization
organisation: Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation
description: The Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation (CMMI) is introducing a model to add prior authorization requirements to certain Medicare services. This initiative aims to streamline and regulate the approval process for specific healthcare services under Medicare.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves the integration of prior authorization into Medicare services, potentially leveraging AI to enhance efficiency and decision-making processes.
stakeholders: Healthcare service companies, Medicare beneficiaries, healthcare providers, and regulatory bodies are the primary stakeholders involved in this innovation.

name: Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) manufacturing facility
organisation: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
description: A new Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) manufacturing facility in Bengaluru with a 5 GWh annual capacity and fully automated cell-to-pack assembly, aimed at strengthening energy infrastructure and supporting clean energy ambitions.
novelty: The facility is among the most advanced in the country, contributing to grid stability and reducing dependence on imported technologies, while being part of a tech-forward manufacturing ecosystem.
stakeholders: Beneficiaries include the energy sector, grid operators, and the broader public benefiting from enhanced grid resilience and clean energy integration. Stakeholders engaged include government bodies, private industry, and research institutions.

name: Neighborhood Autonomous Vehicle Innovation (NAVI)
description: NAVI is a fully autonomous public transit system operating in Jacksonville, Florida. It features a fleet of 14 autonomous, ADA-compliant Ford E-Transit vehicles equipped with Oxa’s automated driving system. The service aims to increase efficiency and safety, offering reliable first- and last-mile services and expanding access to more destinations.
novelty: NAVI is innovative due to its integration of autonomous, electric, multi-passenger vehicles in a public transit system, specifically designed to revitalize downtown areas and improve urban mobility.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Beep Inc., Jacksonville Transportation Authority, users of public transit in Jacksonville, and private communities along the Bay Street Innovation Corridor.

name: Wasteful and Inappropriate Service Reduction (WISeR) Model
description: The WISeR Model aims to ensure that people with Original Medicare receive safe, effective, and necessary care by testing an improved and expedited prior authorization process. It seeks to reduce unnecessary or inappropriate care and safeguard federal taxpayer dollars by leveraging enhanced technologies.
novelty: The project is innovative because it combines enhanced technologies, including AI, with experienced clinicians to streamline the prior authorization process, targeting services vulnerable to fraud, waste, and abuse.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Medicare beneficiaries, healthcare providers, suppliers, and companies specializing in enhanced technologies. Beneficiaries are the primary users, while providers and suppliers are involved in the authorization process.

name: New Federalism 2.0
description: New Federalism 2.0 is a decentralized federal government structure that uses modern management practices to improve government efficiency and responsiveness. It aligns budgets and program requirements with citizen-focused outcomes, using a regional organizational model for service delivery and incorporating advanced technologies like AI and predictive analytics.
novelty: The innovation lies in its decentralized approach, shifting from traditional government silos to a regional model that empowers local authorities and integrates modern management practices and technologies for improved service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal, state, and local governments, public/private partnerships, and citizens who benefit from more efficient and responsive government services.

name: AI Growth Zone
description: Wales will host a world-leading AI Growth Zone, focusing on fair work, cutting-edge technology, and ethical design. This initiative aims to transform public services using AI, upskill the workforce, and embrace AI opportunities with fairness and purpose.
novelty: Wales will become the first country in the world to offer open access to mental health support, achieving true parity with physical health. The AI Growth Zone will integrate AI into public services with ethical guardrails, ensuring fairness and protection for workers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Welsh Government, trade unions, public service workers, AI industry experts, and the Welsh population who will benefit from improved public services and mental health support.

name: Strategic AI Advisory Group
description: The Strategic AI Advisory Group is established to provide expert advice to Ministers on adopting AI across the public sector in Wales. It aims to improve citizen outcomes and drive efficiencies through AI while ensuring ethical deployment.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its cross-sectoral approach, involving experts from academia, industry, and the public sector to advise on AI's strategic opportunities and ethical deployment in public services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Welsh citizens and public sector entities. Stakeholders engaged are experts from academia, industry, and the public sector.

name: Two-tier Local Government Model
description: The two-tier local government model aims to simplify Vietnam's administrative structure by eliminating the intermediary district level, reducing the number of provinces and centrally run cities from 63 to 34, and the number of communes from over 10,000 to 3,321. This restructuring is designed to bring the government closer to the people, improve public service delivery, and support national economic restructuring.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its comprehensive restructuring of the administrative hierarchy, which involves legislative reforms, decentralisation, and the integration of digital services, including AI-powered virtual assistants for public service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Vietnamese citizens, local authorities, Party Committees, and telecom service providers like VNPT and Viettel. Beneficiaries are the general public and businesses who will experience improved public services and infrastructure.

name: AI Expert Advisory Panel
description: The AI Expert Advisory Panel is established to ensure the safe and responsible adoption of artificial intelligence within New Zealand's public service. It comprises experts from various sectors to provide advice on using AI to modernize public services and improve outcomes for citizens.
novelty: The panel provides independent expertise and guidance on AI adoption, ensuring responsible use in public services, which is a novel approach to integrating AI into government operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, public service users, and the panel members from private, public, and academic sectors.

name: GOV.UK App
description: The GOV.UK App is a new mobile application designed to provide easy access to government services, allowing users to manage tasks such as childcare payments and travel guidance directly from their smartphones. It aims to streamline interactions with government services, reducing the need for paper forms and long wait times.
novelty: The app offers a personalized experience by allowing users to customize their home page based on their needs and integrates a generative AI chatbot, GOV.UK Chat, to quickly provide answers to specific questions. It also plans to integrate various government benefits and services seamlessly.
stakeholders: Users of the app include the general public who need to access government services, such as parents, travelers, and business owners. The UK Government is a key stakeholder, aiming to improve service delivery and reduce fraud.

name: Drupal CMS
description: Drupal CMS is a content management system that enables government agencies to manage and update their websites efficiently.
