It provides a low-code/no-code experience and supports artificial intelligence-assisted tools, allowing non-technical staff to maintain content directly. This system supports collaboration, reduces bottlenecks, and ensures timely and accurate public communication.
novelty: The innovation lies in extending the power of authors with a low-code/no-code experience and integrating AI-assisted tools, which allows for more efficient content management by non-technical staff.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, policy advisers, program managers, communications officers, public engagement staff, and ultimately the citizens who benefit from timely and accurate government information.

name: AI Integration in U.S. Federal Agencies
organisation: OpenAI, Department of Defense, Palantir, Accenture Federal Services
description: The U.S. Department of Defense awarded OpenAI a $200 million contract to develop AI tools for administrative tasks, data analysis, and cyber defense. Palantir and Accenture Federal Services are also working to integrate AI-driven platforms into federal operations, focusing on real-time data delivery, supply chain automation, and financial intelligence.
novelty: The initiative marks OpenAI's first official partnership with the government, reflecting a shift in AI's role in national security. It also involves training 1,000 AI specialists to implement scalable solutions in federal systems.
stakeholders: U.S. federal agencies, Department of Defense, service members and their families, AI specialists, and technology firms like OpenAI, Palantir, and Accenture Federal Services.

name: AI Technology Implementation in Los Angeles County
organisation: Los Angeles County Department of Public Social Services (DPSS) and Internal Services Department (ISD)
description: The Los Angeles County DPSS and ISD are exploring the use of AI technologies to improve efficiency in public services, such as transcriptions and reverse engineering reports. The initiative aims to create cost efficiencies and improve service delivery amidst financial constraints.
novelty: The project focuses on integrating AI technologies to streamline operations and enhance efficiency in public services, while balancing concerns from labor unions about potential job reductions.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Los Angeles County DPSS and ISD, county employees, labor unions, vendors providing AI technologies, and the community and faith-based organizations that collaborate with the county.

name: AI Adoption in County Governments
description: Starbridge has developed a scoring model to assess how county governments in the U.S. are adopting AI. The report highlights counties like Miami-Dade for their structured approach to AI, including projects like AI assistants for public benefits and HR chatbots. It also emphasizes the importance of formal AI strategies and partnerships for upskilling staff.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a transparent scoring model to evaluate AI adoption across counties, highlighting the structured and strategic approaches taken by leading counties.
stakeholders: County governments, vendors offering AI tools, county staff, and the general public benefiting from improved public services.

name: Digital, Data and Technology-Enabled Justice System
description: A proposed initiative to use advanced technologies such as wearable devices, AI, and robotics to manage offenders and reduce prison overcrowding. The aim is to create a 'prison outside of prison' by using technology for monitoring, rehabilitation, and crime reduction.
novelty: The project proposes the use of subcutaneous tracking devices, AI advisers for rehabilitation, and robotics for prisoner management, which are innovative approaches to offender management.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include offenders, the Ministry of Justice, and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include tech companies like Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Palantir, as well as human rights organizations.

name: CivTech10
description: CivTech10 is a programme that provides funding and support to tech companies to develop innovative solutions for public sector challenges in Scotland. The programme focuses on delivering cost-savings and environmental protections, with projects addressing issues such as AI-powered documentation processing for teachers and real-time risk monitoring for firefighters.
novelty: CivTech is recognized as the world's first successful public sector-focused innovation accelerator, specifically targeting GovTech solutions. It uniquely combines public sector challenges with private sector innovation, fostering solutions with global relevance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include tech companies, the Scottish Government, public sector entities like the Scottish Fire and Rescue Service, and beneficiaries such as teachers, firefighters, entrepreneurs, and researchers.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan aims to prepare the UK for the AI revolution and ensure the country plays a key role in shaping it. It focuses on embedding AI into public services to transform workflows and processes, enhancing efficiency and creativity while keeping people at the center.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across public services, emphasizing systems thinking, human-centric design, and trust-building, rather than just adopting new technologies for their own sake.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include civil servants, public sector organizations, technology providers, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Generative AI and Process Automation in Federal IT
description: The innovation involves using generative AI and process automation to enhance incident management and optimize IT operations in federal agencies. This aims to improve efficiency, reduce downtime, and free up resources for higher-impact projects, despite budget constraints and workforce shortages.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its strategic use of generative AI to proactively diagnose and resolve IT issues, and the implementation of process automation to streamline operations, reducing human error and downtime.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, IT professionals, systems integrators, public sector beneficiaries, policymakers.

name: Navi (Neighborhood Autonomous Vehicle Innovation)
description: Navi is the nation's first fully autonomous public transportation system, launched in Jacksonville, Florida. It operates a fleet of 14 electric, autonomous Ford E-Transit vehicles along a 3.5-mile route in Downtown Jacksonville, enhancing efficiency and safety in public transit.
novelty: Navi is the first fully autonomous public transportation system in the United States, integrating autonomous vehicles into a municipal transit network to improve safety, access, and efficiency.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the residents and visitors of Jacksonville, Florida, particularly those in the Downtown area. Stakeholders engaged include Beep, Jacksonville Transportation Authority, Intel Capital, Blue Lagoon Capital, and the City of Jacksonville.

name: AI Integration in Public Services
organisation: Ministry of Innovation, Technology and Energy
description: The initiative focuses on integrating Artificial Intelligence into public services to create a 24/7 government and enhance the e-government platform, improving efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of government services.
novelty: The project emphasizes the ethical and effective integration of AI into public services, aiming to transform government operations into a 24/7 service model.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens of the Turks and Caicos Islands, with stakeholders including government officials and digital leaders involved in AI policy and governance.

name: AI Plan for Financial Sector
description: The AI plan focuses on enhancing the Ministry of Finance's digital readiness and operational efficiency through six key areas: Financial Foresight, Smart Operations, Data Governance, Capacity Development, Infrastructure, and Organisational Impact. It aims to incorporate AI best practices to create a more efficient and sustainable financial system.
novelty: The plan provides a clear roadmap for integrating AI into government financial workflows, promoting a culture of innovation, and collaborating with public and private sectors to lead in emerging technologies.
stakeholders: Senior ministry leaders, subject matter experts, strategic partners, public and private sector partners, and the Ministry of Finance itself.

name: Digital Morocco 2030
description: The 'Digital Morocco 2030' strategy aims to modernize Morocco's public services through technology, focusing on eGovernment services, positioning Morocco as a leading African tech hub, and fostering the growth of the start-up ecosystem. It includes initiatives like using AI to democratize access to public services and improve administrative efficiency.
novelty: The strategy emphasizes the use of AI to simplify access to public services, particularly for illiterate populations, and aims to digitalize cultural heritage and administrative documents, making them accessible and preserving them for future generations.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Moroccan citizens, particularly those in rural areas and with low literacy levels. Stakeholders engaged include the Moroccan government, UNESCO, and international tech companies like Oracle and Nvidia.

name: Big Plays for 2025
description: A collection of priorities focused on modernizing systems, eliminating inefficiencies, and leveraging emerging technologies like AI in Virginia's state government. It includes a network overhaul, cloud migration, application modernization, and initiatives like the Save Money Initiative and the Finance Cybersecurity Risk Modernization Program.
novelty: The project emphasizes a comprehensive modernization of state IT infrastructure, including a shift to cloud services, AI integration, and a focus on personalized cybersecurity strategies for different agencies.
stakeholders: State government agencies, Virginia citizens, IT professionals, and state employees involved in technology and service delivery.

name: Citizen Developer Programme
description: The Citizen Developer Programme empowers non-tech employees in the public sector to create applications that improve workflows and solve problems using tools like low-code platforms and generative AI. It aims to enhance productivity and streamline processes, such as the Interpreter Management System (IMS) and Residency Posting Announcement (RPA) process.
novelty: The programme democratizes technical skills by enabling non-technical staff to develop software solutions, fostering a culture of ground-up innovation and reducing reliance on traditional IT departments.
stakeholders: Non-tech employees in the public sector, government agencies, interpreters, enforcement officers, finance teams, and residents in Singapore.

name: AI in Financial Services Compliance and SupTech
description: The innovation involves the use of AI and machine learning technologies to enhance compliance, market surveillance, and regulatory enforcement in the financial sector. AI is used for anti-money laundering efforts, monitoring transactions for sanctions violations, and communications surveillance. Regulatory agencies like the CFTC use AI for supervisory functions, analyzing financial data to detect risks and regulatory violations.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its application of AI and machine learning in regulatory and compliance functions, enhancing efficiency, accuracy, and risk management in financial services. It also introduces the concept of 'suptech' for regulatory agencies.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include financial institutions, regulatory agencies such as the CFTC, SEC, and Federal Reserve, as well as market participants and investors who benefit from improved compliance and market integrity.

name: Digital Transformation of Public Estates
description: The initiative involves using data and digital tools to transform the management of public estates, making them safer, greener, and more adaptable. This includes implementing Integrated Workplace Management Systems, Building Management Systems, and cloud-based asset platforms to maintain live asset registers, monitor performance, and optimise space.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive use of data-driven approaches and smart technology, such as IoT sensors and AI, to enhance the efficiency and sustainability of public estates. This includes real-time monitoring of environmental factors and predictive maintenance using AI.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include public sector organisations such as schools, hospitals, local councils, and care facilities. Beneficiaries are the users of these public services, including students, patients, and residents, as well as the staff managing these facilities.

name: Digital Counties Survey 2025
organisation: Center for Digital Government, Government Technology magazine, National Association of Counties
description: The Digital Counties Survey recognizes counties in the United States that are leading in using technology to improve public services, enhance cybersecurity, modernize operations, and deliver more responsive, transparent government. The survey evaluates counties on their ability to align IT innovation with strategic priorities and real-world impact.
novelty: The survey sets a national benchmark for digital government innovation, highlighting counties that exemplify excellence in using technology for public service modernization and constituent engagement. It showcases the transformative potential of emerging technologies, including AI and generative AI.
stakeholders: Counties across the United States, county officials, county employees, and residents who benefit from improved public services and government transparency.

name: FedRAMP 20x
description: FedRAMP 20x is a new initiative aimed at helping businesses achieve FedRAMP authorization faster and at a lower cost. It introduces a pilot program to fast-track the FedRAMP low authorization process, focusing on continuous, automated validation of key security indicators.
novelty: The initiative streamlines the bureaucratic processes associated with FedRAMP, allowing smaller cloud service providers to gain authorization more easily and increasing competition in the government IT services ecosystem.
stakeholders: Government agencies, cloud service providers, technology providers, businesses seeking FedRAMP authorization.

name: AI-powered citizen services
description: Local authorities in Scotland are trialling AI-powered citizen services to achieve significant cost savings and improved user experience without job losses. These services aim to make the public sector smarter, more accessible, and efficient.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to enhance public services, leading to cost savings and improved user experiences, while maintaining employment levels.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local authorities, Scottish SMEs, citizens benefiting from improved services, and the Scottish Government.

name: NJ AI Hub
organisation: Princeton University, State of New Jersey, Microsoft, CoreWeave
description: The NJ AI Hub is a collaborative initiative launched to foster AI innovation in public services. It aims to make government operations more efficient, effective, and transparent by leveraging AI technologies. The initiative includes partnerships with Princeton University, Microsoft, and CoreWeave.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to integrating AI into public services, focusing on open-source technology and public accountability. It aims to transform government operations and enhance citizen engagement by using AI to automate tasks and improve transparency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public officials, government employees, citizens of New Jersey, and AI technology partners such as Microsoft and CoreWeave.

name: AI-enhanced and enabled healthcare
description: The innovation involves integrating AI technology into the healthcare system to enhance and enable healthcare services without allowing AI to make final decisions. This approach aims to improve efficiency, especially in preventive and continuous care, and address the challenges of an ageing population.
novelty: The project is innovative because it leverages AI to enhance healthcare delivery while maintaining human oversight and judgment, ensuring that technology supports rather than replaces human competencies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include healthcare institutions, healthcare professionals, patients, and the Ministry of Health in Singapore. Beneficiaries are primarily patients, especially the elderly, who will receive more efficient and community-based care.

name: AI Integration in Defense
organisation: Google Public Sector, Defense Department’s Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office
description: The innovation involves integrating advanced AI solutions and secure cloud infrastructure into the Defense Department's operations to support warfighters, enhance intelligence, business, and enterprise information systems, and maintain strategic advantages.
novelty: The project leverages commercially available AI solutions and integrates them into defense operations, utilizing cutting-edge technologies like Cloud Tensor Processing Units and Google’s Contiguous United States infrastructure for AI.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the Defense Department, Navy, Air Force, Defense Innovation Unit, Combatant Commands, the Office of the Secretary of Defense, and the Joint Staff. Stakeholders engaged include Google Public Sector and the General Services Administration.

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
description: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat is an AI assistant tool powered by OpenAI's GPT-4o, made available to nearly 30,000 City employees in San Francisco. It is designed to help city workers, such as nurses and social workers, by reducing administrative tasks, allowing them to focus more on delivering services to residents. The tool is integrated into city departments to accelerate tasks like drafting reports, analyzing data, and summarizing documents.
novelty: The innovation lies in the large-scale deployment of a generative AI tool within a local government, making San Francisco one of the largest local governments to leverage AI for improving public service delivery. It is implemented with enterprise-grade security and privacy standards, setting a precedent for responsible AI use in the public sector.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the city employees, including nurses, social workers, and administrative staff, who will use the tool to enhance their service delivery. The broader beneficiaries are the residents of San Francisco who will receive more efficient and responsive city services. Stakeholders also include Microsoft and the nonprofit InnovateUS, which are involved in training and implementation.

name: Advanced AI Capabilities for National Security
organisation: Defense Department’s Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office
description: The Defense Department awarded contracts to four tech companies to provide advanced AI capabilities aimed at addressing national security challenges. These contracts allow the deployment of the latest AI offerings, agentic AI workflows, and large language models to enhance defense operations.
novelty: The project integrates commercially available AI solutions into the Department's capabilities, accelerating the use of advanced AI in defense and intelligence systems, and scaling the adoption of agentic AI across enterprise systems.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the Defense Department, federal government departments and agencies, and tech companies like Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, and xAI. Beneficiaries are the U.S. military and national security operations.

name: AI in Government
description: The use of artificial intelligence and machine learning to automate processes, enhance security, analyze data, improve decision-making, and provide better services for citizens and stakeholders in state and local governments.
novelty: AI is transforming government services by enabling real-time threat detection, automating surveillance, streamlining emergency response, modernizing traffic systems, and improving citizen engagement through AI chatbots.
stakeholders: State and local government agencies, citizens, first responders, emergency management teams, and public transit users.

name: AI Lab
description: The AI Lab is an initiative aimed at accelerating the adoption and scaling of AI technologies within the NHS. It focuses on developing and commercializing early-stage AI innovations and deploying established AI technologies at scale. The Lab has contributed to national regulatory guidance and facilitated learning on AI technology development, implementation, and evaluation.
novelty: The AI Lab is innovative due to its experimental approach in a complex ecosystem, its focus on creating a national AI Deployment Platform, and its efforts to stimulate industry through targeted investments in new technologies.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include NHS providers, vendors, regulators, policy makers, and start-ups. Beneficiaries are the NHS and its patients, with involvement from the UK Government and the AI Lab Board.

name: AI Research Resource (AIRR)
organisation: UK Government, UK Research and Innovation (UKRI), Nvidia, HPE, Dell Technologies, Intel
description: The AI Research Resource (AIRR) is a system that expands the UK's compute capacity to support AI development, aiming to transform public services, drive economic growth, and enable scientific breakthroughs. It involves the use of powerful supercomputers like Isambard and Dawn to process data for AI research and applications.
novelty: The AIRR significantly increases the UK's compute capacity, enabling it to process in one second what would take the global population 80 years, thus reducing reliance on foreign computing power and positioning the UK as a leader in AI development.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK researchers, scientists, engineers, businesses, and public service providers who will benefit from enhanced AI capabilities and infrastructure. The beneficiaries are the British public, who will experience improved public services and economic growth.

name: Compute Roadmap
description: The Compute Roadmap is a strategy to enhance the UK's AI capabilities by expanding national computing infrastructure, including the AI Research Resource (AIRR) with powerful supercomputers. It aims to accelerate economic growth, revolutionize public services, and deliver scientific breakthroughs.
novelty: The roadmap introduces a significant upgrade in computing power, including the AIRR, which can process in one second what would take the global population 80 years. It supports high-impact AI projects and reduces reliance on foreign infrastructure.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include researchers, universities, businesses, and the public sector. Stakeholders engaged include the UK government, universities like University College London and University of Liverpool, and the University of Edinburgh.

name: AI Integration in National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) FY 2026
organisation: Department of Defense (DoD), Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office (CDAO)
description: The National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) for Fiscal Year 2026 includes strategies to secure, oversee, and coordinate AI development across the Department of Defense, focusing on cybersecurity, allied coordination, and the creation of oversight and innovation structures. It also involves awarding contracts to AI developers to accelerate AI adoption for national security.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive integration of AI into national defense strategies, including the establishment of cross-functional teams for AI oversight, cyber-innovation sandbox environments, and the acceleration of AI adoption through significant contracts with AI developers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Department of Defense, US allies and partners, AI developers, military personnel, and potentially civilians who benefit from improved military targeting accuracy and reduced collateral damage.

name: AI-powered video surveillance in public transit
description: The innovation involves integrating AI-powered video surveillance devices into public transit systems to optimize operations, improve performance, and enhance the passenger experience. This technology, initially used for security, now contributes to efficiency and service improvements by providing real-time updates, predicting maintenance needs, and optimizing scheduling and resource allocation.
novelty: The innovation is novel because it repurposes existing AI-powered video surveillance devices, traditionally used for security, to enhance operational performance and passenger experience in public transit systems. This integration allows for real-time data-driven decision-making without overhauling existing infrastructure.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public transit agencies, passengers, and transit operators. Passengers benefit from improved reliability and real-time information, while transit operators gain insights for better resource allocation and operational efficiency.

name: Automated Passenger Services (APS) Scheme
description: The APS scheme is designed to regulate and facilitate the rollout of self-driving vehicle services, including taxi, private-hire, and bus-like services, aiming to improve transport accessibility, especially in rural areas, and reduce road collisions by minimizing human error.
novelty: The scheme allows for the first time the piloting of small-scale self-driving vehicle services without a safety driver, which can be booked via an app, marking a significant step towards commercializing autonomous public transport.
stakeholders: Users include passengers in rural areas and those unable to drive, while stakeholders involve the UK government, industry representatives, trade unions, and the general public who are invited to contribute to the consultation process.

name: Agentic AI for Public Sector
description: Agentic AI is being implemented in the public sector to enable AI applications to reason and act independently without constant human oversight. Denodo's logical data architecture allows for seamless data access and integration, supporting AI applications to deliver more relevant and accurate outputs while reducing infrastructure costs and accelerating time-to-insight.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI, which can operate independently, and Denodo's logical data architecture that allows data integration without physical movement, enhancing AI's ability to deliver accurate outputs and reducing latency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector organisations, government agencies, and citizens who benefit from improved service delivery. Denodo collaborates with the health sector and other public agencies to deploy AI agents.

name: Public Benefit Innovation Fund (PBIF) Summer 2025 Open Call
description: The PBIF Summer 2025 Open Call offers $5 million in non-dilutive grants to support the development and deployment of AI-powered tools aimed at improving public benefit programs like Medicaid and SNAP. The initiative seeks to reduce administrative burdens, improve access, and enhance performance of these programs.
novelty: The project is innovative in its focus on AI-enabled solutions to streamline and enhance public benefit programs, addressing administrative burdens and improving access and performance in a scalable and equitable manner.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, technology teams, public-interest technologists, researchers, service organizations, and beneficiaries of public benefit programs.

name: AI Integration in Public Services
description: The UK government has partnered with OpenAI to enhance public services by integrating advanced AI models across sectors like justice, security, and education. The collaboration includes investments in AI infrastructure and aims to leverage AI to improve public service efficiency and support small businesses.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its strategic partnership between a national government and a leading AI company to integrate AI into public services, along with a focus on increasing public compute capacity and sharing technical insights for AI safety.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, OpenAI, civil servants, small businesses, and UK citizens who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Managed Lanes and Toll Road P3s
description: Managed lanes and toll road public-private partnerships (P3s) in the US are evolving from isolated projects to comprehensive, technology-driven mobility platforms. These projects focus on variable tolling, seamless digital payments, and real-time traffic management to enhance user experience and operational efficiency.
novelty: The innovation lies in the shift from static infrastructure projects to dynamic, customer-centric mobility services that integrate advanced technologies like AI for toll optimization and flexible payment platforms.
stakeholders: Users of managed lanes, state Departments of Transportation, developers, investors, and the Build America Bureau (BAB) are key stakeholders. Beneficiaries include commuters and the broader public who gain from improved mobility and congestion relief.

name: Deployment of Advanced AI Models in UK Government
description: OpenAI has signed a memorandum of understanding with the UK government to explore the deployment of advanced AI models in various sectors such as justice, security, and education. The collaboration aims to help civil servants work more efficiently and support citizens in navigating public services more effectively.
novelty: The innovation lies in the strategic partnership between a leading AI company and a national government to integrate advanced AI models into public services, addressing complex societal challenges and enhancing public sector efficiency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include OpenAI, the UK government, civil servants, and UK citizens who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Generative AI and Low-Code Development in Public Sector
description: The innovation involves the use of generative AI combined with low-code development to modernize public sector IT infrastructure. This approach aims to automate mundane tasks, extract insights from unstructured data, and augment decision-making, ultimately improving efficiency, transparency, and responsiveness in public services.
novelty: The innovative aspect lies in the combination of generative AI and low-code platforms, which allows for faster, more efficient modernization of legacy systems without the complexity of traditional software development. This approach enables public agencies to build adaptable systems that can evolve with policy shifts and administrative changes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector IT teams, federal, state, and local agencies, public servants, and ultimately, the citizens and communities who benefit from more efficient and responsive government services.

name: AI-powered Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for Invoicing
description: Euna Solutions has introduced an AI-powered Optical Character Recognition (OCR) capability to its Euna Procurement’s Invoicing module. This enhancement automates the process of receiving and rekeying invoices by converting scanned and emailed PDFs into structured digital data, thereby improving speed and accuracy in Accounts Payable operations.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI-powered OCR to automate invoice processing, reducing manual input and errors, and accelerating workflows in public sector procurement and Accounts Payable operations.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are public sector agencies and their procurement and Accounts Payable teams, who will experience improved efficiency and accuracy in invoice processing. Suppliers also benefit from faster and more accurate invoicing.

name: AI in County Governance
description: The innovation involves the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) by counties in Maryland to streamline operations, enhance service delivery, and analyze data. The initiative aims to improve governance and public service delivery while addressing concerns about transparency, ethics, and local autonomy.
novelty: The project is innovative as it explores the integration of AI in local governance, focusing on maintaining local decision-making power amidst increasing state and federal oversight.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include county leaders, tech experts, policy experts, industry leaders, and local government officials. Beneficiaries are the residents of the counties who will receive enhanced services.

name: Winning the Race: America’s AI Action Plan
description: A national action plan aimed at creating an environment for AI technologies to thrive in the U.S. by removing regulatory barriers, promoting open-source AI systems, and ensuring unbiased AI models.
novelty: The plan emphasizes the development of open-source and open-weight AI systems as global standards, and aims to ensure AI models are free from ideological biases.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, state governments, private sector companies, tech developers, and the general American public as beneficiaries.

name: NJ Summer EBT Program Enhancement
organisation: Office of Innovation, Department of Agriculture
description: The NJ Summer EBT Program Enhancement uses statistical pattern finding, a type of artificial intelligence, to identify and automatically enroll eligible children in the NJ Summer EBT Program, which provides a one-time $120 EBT card to low-income children during the summer.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of artificial intelligence to automatically identify and enroll eligible children in the program, reducing barriers for low-income families and optimizing resource allocation.
stakeholders: Beneficiaries are low-income children and families eligible for the NJ Summer EBT Program. Stakeholders include the Office of Innovation, Department of Agriculture, Department of Human Services, local school districts, and community partners like the Food Bank of South Jersey.

name: Competitively Bid AI Contracts for Local Government Use
organisation: North Central Texas Council of Governments (NCTCOG), Civic Marketplace
description: The initiative allows government agencies nationwide to deploy vetted, competitively awarded AI solutions and services for over 70 use cases without new RFPs. It supports small and local companies and aims to streamline procurement and enhance public services using AI.
novelty: The project is innovative because it provides a rigorously curated marketplace of AI solutions, allowing rapid deployment without new RFPs and supporting small and local companies, which is unprecedented in cooperative purchasing history.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are local government agencies across the United States, small and local businesses, and residents who will receive improved public services. Stakeholders include NCTCOG, Civic Marketplace, and the awarded AI suppliers.

name: Chief AI Officer Position
organisation: New York State Office of Information Technology Services (ITS)
description: The Chief AI Officer position was created to lead the AI strategy for New York State's ITS, focusing on the design, development, and implementation of AI technologies to keep the state ahead in AI innovation among public agencies.
novelty: The creation of a dedicated Chief AI Officer role within a state government agency to lead AI strategy and implementation is a novel approach to integrating AI into public services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the New York State government, ITS employees, and potentially the residents of New York State who would benefit from improved public services through AI innovation.

name: AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan is a policy document outlining the U.S. administration's priorities and deliverables in Artificial Intelligence. It focuses on accelerating AI innovation, building American AI infrastructure, and leading in international AI diplomacy and security. The plan aims to foster U.S. leadership in AI, support open-source AI, and enhance cybersecurity.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to AI policy, focusing on open-source AI models, secure-by-design technologies, and balancing AI export controls with international collaboration.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include U.S. technology companies, federal agencies, American workers, international allies, and partners. Beneficiaries are the U.S. government, private sector, and global AI community.

name: Winning the AI Race: America’s AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan aims to secure U.S. leadership in AI by emphasizing deregulation, innovation, and infrastructure development. It outlines over 90 federal policy actions across three pillars: accelerating AI innovation, building American AI infrastructure, and leading in international AI diplomacy and security.
novelty: The plan avoids sweeping mandates or new regulatory frameworks, focusing instead on messaging and directives for executive branch agencies. It emphasizes deregulation and innovation, prioritizing AI infrastructure development and international leadership.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, the private tech sector, AI developers, researchers, and the broader U.S. workforce. Beneficiaries are American AI developers, the tech industry, and potentially the global AI community through export initiatives.

name: VMware Cloud Foundation Implementation
description: Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust implemented VMware Cloud Foundation to modernize its IT infrastructure, allowing for centralized and automated management across multiple sites. This innovation aims to improve service availability, enhance data security, and free up resources for strategic initiatives, ultimately improving patient care and clinician productivity.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of VMware Cloud Foundation to create a private cloud that centralizes and automates IT management, reducing manual maintenance and enabling the Trust to focus on strategic digital initiatives and innovation.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the IT team led by Yusuf Mangera, clinicians who benefit from improved system availability, and patients who receive more efficient and effective care.

name: AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan is a comprehensive strategy released by the White House to promote AI innovation, infrastructure, and international diplomacy and security. It includes guidance on cybersecurity, updates to the NIST AI Risk Management Framework, and federal procurement guidelines. The plan aims to accelerate AI innovation, build American AI infrastructure, and lead in international AI diplomacy and security.
novelty: The plan introduces new approaches and structures, such as the AI Information Sharing and Analysis Center (AI-ISAC) within DHS, and emphasizes reducing regulatory burdens to promote AI development and deployment.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal government agencies, private sector AI developers, and international partners. Beneficiaries are American industries, government agencies, and the general public who will benefit from enhanced AI capabilities and security measures.

name: Winning the Race: America’s AI Action Plan
description: The Action Plan aims to promote US global dominance in AI and expand AI use in private and public sectors. It includes policy recommendations to accelerate AI innovation, build AI infrastructure, and lead in international AI diplomacy and security.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to removing regulatory barriers, promoting open-source AI models, and fostering international adoption of American AI systems.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal agencies, AI developers, private sector companies, and international partners. Stakeholders engaged include the White House, Department of Commerce, and National Institute of Standards and Technology.

name: America’s AI Action Plan
description: A comprehensive national strategy designed to strengthen the United States’ position in artificial intelligence through investment in innovation, infrastructure, and international diplomacy and security. It focuses on enabling responsible AI development and deployment at scale, modernizing infrastructure, and advancing international diplomacy and security.
novelty: The plan reflects a shift away from regulation and toward enabling frameworks, creating opportunities for private-sector leadership in shaping standards, tools, and data ecosystems. It emphasizes 'security by design' and addresses synthetic media threats like deepfakes.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, private sector, Department of Labor, NSF, Department of Education, Department of Justice, Department of Defense, AI developers, workforce involved in AI infrastructure, international partners.

name: Autonomous Robots for Urban Maintenance
description: Deployment of autonomous robots in Detroit for urban maintenance tasks such as mowing grass, clearing snow, and collecting garbage. These robots aim to enhance efficiency, safety, and reduce costs in municipal operations.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of autonomous, multifunctional robots for essential urban maintenance tasks, integrating AI and robotics to address labor shortages and budget constraints.
stakeholders: Users include city maintenance crews and municipal departments; beneficiaries are the residents of Detroit. Stakeholders include local startups, city officials, and venture capitalists.

name: Nepal’s National AI Policy
description: Nepal’s National AI Policy aims to leverage AI for inclusive development, ethical innovation, and global competitiveness. It includes setting up a National AI Center, AI Excellence Centers in universities, Provincial AI Innovation Hubs, and integrating AI in various sectors like governance, education, agriculture, disaster response, and healthcare.
novelty: The policy focuses on democratizing AI access, promoting ethical AI entrepreneurship, and ensuring inclusivity by supporting local languages and accessibility for marginalized groups.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Government of Nepal, academia, industry, youth, persons with disabilities, elderly populations, women, children, and global technology leaders.

name: Integrated Capital Works Platform (iCWP)
organisation: Development Bureau (DEVB) of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government
description: The Integrated Capital Works Platform (iCWP) consolidates data from three key systems to provide a unified view of data across the lifecycle of over 200 public works projects. It incorporates interactive dashboards, GIS, and BIM to enable transparent, data-driven decision-making.
novelty: The iCWP acts as a single window for data integration, allowing for seamless data flow and advanced AI-driven analytics. It supports the creation of digital twins and enhances decision-making through predictive intelligence.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include more than 700 government officials involved in public works projects, as well as the broader public benefiting from improved infrastructure outcomes.

name: Hawai‘i AI and Cloud Innovation Summit
organisation: Department of Business, Economic Development and Tourism, Google Public Sector, TRUE Initiative
description: The Fourth Annual Hawai‘i AI and Cloud Innovation Summit aims to explore the transformative power of AI and cloud technologies in government, workforce development, and public-private collaboration. It focuses on enhancing productivity, bolstering cybersecurity, and fostering innovation within public administration.
novelty: The summit delves into practical applications of AI and cloud technologies, offering insights into future tech trends and critical workforce development needs, and includes live demonstrations of AI-powered government services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government officials, industry experts, small businesses, and the local community. Stakeholders engaged include government agencies, the University of Hawai‘i, and the Small Business Development Center.

name: AI Deregulation Decision Tool
description: An AI tool designed to analyze and eliminate outdated or redundant regulations from the U.S. federal code, with the aim of reducing federal regulations by 50%.
novelty: The use of AI to process and propose deregulations for approximately 200,000 existing rules, showcasing a transformative approach to administrative reform.
stakeholders: Users include federal agencies like the Department of Housing and Urban Development and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. Beneficiaries are businesses facing reduced compliance costs, and stakeholders include government officials and the public concerned with regulatory impacts.

name: America’s AI Action Plan
description: A national framework to guide artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure initiatives and federal policymaking across priority areas, aiming to foster AI development and growth.
novelty: The plan includes directives for accelerating AI innovation, building AI infrastructure, and leading in international AI diplomacy and security, with specific measures like expediting data center construction and establishing an AI Information Sharing and Analysis Center.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, counties, local governments, workforce and education sectors, Department of Energy, Department of Labor, Department of Education, National Science Foundation, Department of Commerce, Department of Homeland Security, Federal Communications Commission, Federal Trade Commission.

name: IT Modernization with Customer Experience
organisation: Department of Defense, Air Force, Space Force
description: The Department of Defense is aligning IT modernization with customer experience to accelerate innovation and better serve service members by using cloud and AI technologies to streamline operations and empower personnel.
novelty: The project innovatively combines IT modernization with a focus on customer experience, using advanced technologies like cloud and AI to enhance operational efficiency and mission readiness.
stakeholders: Service members of the Air Force and Space Force, personnel involved in IT and operations, and technology partners like Maximus and AWS.

name: Amazon Connect for Non-Emergency 911 Call Handling
description: The Arlington County Emergency Communications Center implemented Amazon Connect to automate non-emergency 911 call handling, using AI to triage routine requests like storm damage and towing. This innovation ensures critical calls are still handled by live operators while pioneering AI-driven public safety communications.
novelty: It is one of the first centers in the country and the first in the Northeast to adopt AI-driven public safety communications for non-emergency 911 call handling.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Arlington County residents and emergency communication staff. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the Arlington County Emergency Communications Center and AWS.

name: National AI Policy 2025
description: The National AI Policy 2025 aims to democratize access to artificial intelligence, enhance public services, and create employment opportunities. It includes training one million AI professionals by 2030, establishing AI Innovation and Venture Funds, creating AI-driven projects and products, and ensuring inclusivity in AI education and financing.
novelty: The policy's focus on democratizing AI access, establishing dedicated AI funds, and setting ambitious targets for AI-driven projects and professional training makes it innovative.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the youth of Pakistan, women, persons with disabilities, and the private sector involved in AI. Stakeholders engaged include the government, educational institutions, and tech startups.

name: Winning the AI Race: America’s AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan outlines over 90 federal actions aimed at reshaping the U.S. AI landscape. It focuses on accelerating AI innovation, building domestic AI infrastructure, and leading in global AI diplomacy and security. The plan emphasizes deregulation, infrastructure expansion, workforce development, and international leadership to ensure U.S. dominance in AI technologies.
novelty: The plan represents a significant shift in federal approach to AI, focusing on deregulation and proactive government involvement to drive AI development and adoption. It introduces new procurement opportunities, infrastructure support, and export strategies for AI technologies.
stakeholders: AI developers, supporting industries such as data centers, federal agencies, private sector companies, American workers, and international allies. Beneficiaries include businesses involved in AI development and export, as well as workers gaining new skills through AI training initiatives.

name: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
organisation: Securities and Exchange Commission, Federal Reserve, Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, National Credit Union Administration, Federal Deposit Insurance Corp., Federal Housing Finance Agency
description: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act directs federal financial agencies to create in-house AI innovation labs to serve as sandboxes for testing AI projects without burdensome regulations. The goal is to balance innovation with consumer protection, strengthening the financial system and maintaining the U.S.'s leadership in financial technology.
novelty: The innovation lies in establishing AI innovation labs within financial regulatory agencies to allow for experimentation with AI projects in a controlled environment, promoting collaboration between the private sector and government while ensuring consumer protection.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include financial regulatory agencies, private sector companies, and consumers. Stakeholders engaged include lawmakers, financial agencies, and technology developers.

name: AI Action Plan
organisation: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
description: The AI Action Plan aims to export 'American AI' and expand AI use across federal agencies. It includes over 90 federal policy actions, such as developing full-stack AI export packages and accelerating AI adoption in government. The plan also involves creating a federal AI talent exchange program and establishing an AI procurement toolbox.
novelty: The plan organizes an 'AI stack' for easier export to foreign governments and emphasizes the integration of AI in public services, making it innovative in its comprehensive approach to AI deployment and international influence.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal agencies, foreign governments, and the general public who will experience improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, the Commerce Department, and the General Services Administration.

name: AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan sets out steps to reshape the U.S. approach to artificial intelligence, focusing on innovation, infrastructure, and AI diplomacy and security. It aims to accelerate AI innovation, improve public data infrastructure, and expand the AI workforce.
novelty: The plan emphasizes innovation and deployment, proposing to identify regulatory barriers and endorsing tools like regulatory sandboxes to facilitate AI adoption.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal agencies, the domestic AI workforce, and U.S. AI product and service exporters. Stakeholders engaged include the Office of Science and Technology Policy and other federal entities.

name: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
description: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act directs financial regulatory agencies to create Innovation Labs for regulated entities to test AI projects, allowing experimentation with cutting-edge technologies in a safe environment. The act aims to foster innovation, enhance efficiency, increase competitiveness, and ensure consumer protection in the financial services industry.
novelty: The act establishes regulatory innovation labs for AI test projects at major financial regulatory agencies, providing a controlled environment for testing new financial products and services that use AI, while ensuring consumer protection and systemic risk management.

name: America’s AI Action Plan
description: America’s AI Action Plan is a comprehensive strategy focusing on deregulation, infrastructure acceleration, and global AI exports to enhance speed and competitiveness in AI technology. It emphasizes the need for governance, risk management, and ethical AI deployment across various sectors such as government, healthcare, defense, energy, and financial services.
novelty: The plan introduces a structured collaboration between agencies and industries to reduce compliance costs while promoting safety and efficacy. It also calls for AI resilience as a national security priority and the establishment of formal AI sandboxes and cross-agency innovation networks.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, industry leaders, and sectors such as healthcare, defense, energy, and financial services. Beneficiaries are organizations and sectors adopting AI, as well as the general public benefiting from safer and more efficient AI systems.

name: AI in Licensing and Permitting
description: Cities are using AI to modernize licensing and permitting operations, resulting in faster processing times, improved compliance, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Examples include Hamilton's AI scanning for building permit compliance and Honolulu's integration of AI and GIS for residential permits.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to automate and streamline licensing and permitting processes, significantly reducing processing times and improving accuracy and decision-making.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include city governments, citizens applying for permits, and businesses. Stakeholders engaged include city planning departments and technology providers like AWS.

name: Winning the AI Race: America’s AI Action Plan
description: A comprehensive strategy aimed at advancing U.S. leadership in artificial intelligence through deregulation, private-sector leadership, and federal actions organized into three pillars: accelerating innovation, building domestic AI infrastructure, and leading globally on AI diplomacy and security.

name: Zero Government Bureaucracy Programme - Phase II
description: The initiative aims to simplify operations, boost efficiency, and enhance quality of life by reducing key services by 50%, focusing on licensing and accreditation services for healthcare professionals and facilities. It involves streamlining and digitising procedures to create a more agile and responsive government.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on eliminating unnecessary complexity, reducing documentation, consolidating digital platforms, and integrating AI to achieve 100% digital bureaucracy elimination.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include healthcare professionals, facilities, citizens, and professionals accessing government services. Stakeholders engaged include internal teams, the healthcare sector, and service users.

name: HKChat
organisation: Hong Kong Generative AI Research and Development Center (HKGAI)
description: HKChat is an AI-powered chatbot mobile app developed using Hong Kong's first large language foundation model. It aims to bring the benefits of AI directly to the public.
novelty: HKChat is innovative as it is powered by a homegrown large language foundation model, marking a significant advancement in AI technology developed locally in Hong Kong.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries of HKChat include the general public in Hong Kong, who will have access to AI-powered services. Stakeholders also include the Hong Kong Generative AI Research and Development Center and the broader AI research community.

name: AI Action Plan for Justice
description: The AI Action Plan for Justice aims to integrate AI technology to improve the operations of the Ministry of Justice and its agencies. Key initiatives include creating a single digital ID for offenders, using AI to predict risks in prisons, and developing tools to scan mobile phones for intelligence. The plan also includes providing AI assistants to staff and developing a digital assistant for family disputes.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive use of AI to link offender records, predict prison violence, and assist staff with AI tools, which were not previously possible due to data inconsistencies and lack of technology integration.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Ministry of Justice staff, offenders, prison officers, probation officers, and families involved in child arrangement disputes.

name: Economic Reform Roundtable
description: The Economic Reform Roundtable is an initiative aimed at building consensus on long-term economic reform in Australia, focusing on resilience, productivity, and budget sustainability. It involves sessions on various topics such as international trade, skills development, capital investment, regulation, competition, AI, government services, and taxation. The roundtable invites experts from various fields to contribute insights and has engaged nearly 900 organisations and individuals through a public submission process.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to economic reform by involving a wide range of stakeholders and experts across different sectors to build consensus and inform policy discussions.
stakeholders: Policy experts, economists, government agencies, business leaders, institutional investors, and the broader community are engaged as stakeholders. Beneficiaries include the Australian economy and society at large, as the reforms aim to enhance economic resilience and productivity.

name: Agentic AI in Public Sector
description: Agentic AI is being integrated into public sector operations to enhance capabilities in areas like case management and service delivery. It supports human decision-makers by augmenting human capabilities, distributing workloads, and accelerating productivity. The AI is implemented through SAP’s Business Technology Platform, allowing integration with legacy systems and gradual adoption of AI capabilities.
novelty: Agentic AI represents a collaborative, goal-oriented approach where multiple AI agents work together to solve complex challenges, mirroring the collaborative nature of public sector operations. It focuses on augmenting human capabilities rather than replacing them.
stakeholders: Public sector organisations, human decision-makers, citizens benefiting from enhanced services, SAP and DXC Technology as implementing partners.

name: EU AI Act
description: The EU AI Act is a comprehensive legal framework designed to regulate the use of artificial intelligence across the European Union. It aims to promote human-centric and trustworthy AI while ensuring protection of health, safety, and fundamental rights. The Act introduces a risk-based approach to AI regulation, categorizing AI applications into unacceptable, high-risk, and limited risk categories, with corresponding regulatory obligations.
novelty: The EU AI Act is the world's first comprehensive AI law, setting a uniform legal framework for AI across multiple countries, and applying to both local and foreign companies. It introduces a risk-based approach to regulation, balancing innovation with harm prevention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI developers, companies deploying AI systems, European citizens, and non-European companies like Anthropic, Google, Meta, and OpenAI. Beneficiaries are the general public who gain protection from harmful AI applications.

name: Digital Transformation of Public Services in Uzbekistan
organisation: Ministry of Digital Technologies of the Republic of Uzbekistan
description: Uzbekistan is implementing a comprehensive digital transformation strategy for its public services, including the establishment of a unified portal for e-government services, integration of government information systems, and the use of AI and biometrics to improve service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in the extensive use of digital technologies, including AI and biometrics, to transform public service delivery, making it more efficient and accessible, and in the integration of over 200 government information systems into a unified platform.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Uzbek citizens, businesses, government agencies, and technology companies operating in Uzbekistan.

name: Bloomberg Philanthropies City Data Alliance
description: The Bloomberg Philanthropies City Data Alliance is a program designed to help local governments expand their use of data, digital tools, and AI in public services. It provides technical assistance, coaching, and peer-learning opportunities to participating cities.
novelty: The program is innovative in its approach to customizing support for each city's unique needs and challenges, and in fostering peer-to-peer learning among city officials to share insights and solutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include city residents who will experience improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include city officials, Bloomberg Philanthropies, and experts from the Bloomberg Center for Government Excellence at Johns Hopkins University.

name: AI Permitting Process Streamlining
description: The City of Bellevue is partnering with Govstream.ai to pilot AI tools aimed at streamlining and accelerating the city's permitting processes. This initiative is designed to improve efficiency, create a consistent user experience, and support housing and economic development goals.
novelty: The project uses AI to transform city codes, GIS, records, and permit application history into step-by-step guidance, reducing turnaround time and complexity in permit applications.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Bellevue city staff, residents, and businesses applying for permits, as well as Govstream.ai as the technology provider.

name: OneGov Partnership for ChatGPT Enterprise
description: The U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) and OpenAI have partnered to provide ChatGPT Enterprise to federal agencies at a discounted rate, supporting the White House's AI Action Plan. This initiative aims to modernize government operations, improve productivity, and enhance decision-making.
novelty: The partnership offers universal access to ChatGPT Enterprise for all participating federal agencies at a nominal fee, making it the first-of-its-kind agreement to accelerate AI adoption across the federal government.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, federal employees, American taxpayers, GSA, OpenAI

name: Cyber AI Profile
organisation: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: The Cyber AI Profile is a guidance framework developed by NIST to help cybersecurity professionals manage the impact of AI on their work. It builds on NIST's existing Cybersecurity Framework and aims to address the security risks of AI by mapping AI aspects to cybersecurity outcomes.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI considerations into existing cybersecurity frameworks without overwhelming professionals with new guidance, thus bridging the gap between AI practitioners and cybersecurity experts.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are Chief Information Security Officers (CISOs), cybersecurity professionals, and organizations in both public and private sectors that rely on NIST's guidelines.

name: DevSecOps in Federal Agencies
organisation: Department of Veterans Affairs, Department of Labor, Department of State, Department of Energy
description: Federal agencies are adopting DevSecOps practices to modernize government platforms, improve user experience, and manage risk as AI becomes more integrated into operations. This approach emphasizes secure, responsive, and human-centered systems to build public trust and deliver impact.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating DevSecOps practices with a human-centered approach to modernize government operations, focusing on user experience and security from the onset, and applying safety practices from nuclear operations to AI deployment.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include veterans, citizens interacting with government services, and government employees. Stakeholders engaged include federal agencies, private sector vendors, and the White House.

name: AWS Backup Strategy Optimization
description: Service NSW implemented a tiered backup strategy using AWS Backup to manage database backups efficiently, achieving a 70% reduction in backup costs while maintaining compliance with data retention requirements.
novelty: The innovation lies in the sophisticated, tiered backup strategy that balances cost optimization with compliance and disaster recovery capabilities, along with automated testing and compliance reporting using AWS services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Service NSW, its cloud platform team, AWS account team, AWS storage specialists, and the 8.2 million residents of New South Wales who benefit from improved government service delivery.

name: California's GenAI Educational Initiatives
organisation: Adobe, Google, IBM, Microsoft, California Government
description: A series of partnerships between California and leading tech companies to integrate AI education into the state's educational systems. These initiatives aim to equip students and educators with AI skills and resources, ensuring workforce readiness and fostering innovation.
novelty: The initiative is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI education across various educational levels and its collaboration with major tech companies to provide resources and training at no cost.
stakeholders: Students, educators, educational institutions, tech companies, California state government

name: Winning the Race: America’s AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan focuses on maintaining global leadership in AI by emphasizing innovation, infrastructure, and international diplomacy and security. It promotes a deregulatory approach to AI governance, encouraging private sector leadership and discouraging restrictive state policies through funding disincentives.
novelty: The plan marks a significant shift towards deregulation in AI policy, contrasting with previous regulatory approaches, and aims to remove barriers to innovation by reducing bureaucratic red tape.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the federal government, state governments, private sector companies involved in AI development, and potentially the general public who will be affected by AI technologies.

name: Smart Phones and AI for Crime Fighting
organisation: South African Police Service, Santam, Business Against Crime
description: A public-private partnership where the South African Police Service collaborates with private companies to enhance crime-fighting capabilities using smart phones and artificial intelligence. The initiative includes pilot projects for Automatic Number Plate Recognition and AI-assisted CCTV monitoring, leading to significant recoveries of stolen vehicles.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of advanced digital technologies like AI and smart phones into traditional policing methods, enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of law enforcement.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the South African Police Service, private sector companies like Santam, and the general public who benefit from reduced crime rates. Stakeholders engaged include law enforcement agencies, private companies, and government bodies.

name: Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO)
organisation: General Services Administration (GSA), Office of Management and Budget’s (OMB) Office of Federal Procurement Policy (OFPP), Department of Defense (DoD), NASA
description: The Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) aims to modernize the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR), which guides Federal agencies in purchasing goods and services. The initiative seeks to lower barriers for smaller businesses to engage with the Federal government and streamline the acquisition process using advanced technologies like artificial intelligence.
novelty: The innovation leverages artificial intelligence and agentic tools to reduce laborious tasks in the acquisition process, making it faster and more efficient. It also focuses on lowering barriers for smaller, cutting-edge businesses to engage with the Federal government.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, smaller and cutting-edge businesses, subject matter experts, legal and policy professionals, and the American public as beneficiaries of improved government services.

name: America’s AI Action Plan
description: The America’s AI Action Plan is a policy initiative aimed at advancing U.S. AI innovation and infrastructure, including data centers and chip manufacturing, while using export controls and trade tools to strengthen AI leadership abroad. It involves deregulating AI development domestically and tightening export rules to protect U.S. technology from foreign adversaries.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its dual-track strategy that combines deregulation of domestic AI development with stringent export controls, aiming to enhance U.S. leadership in AI technology globally while protecting national interests.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include U.S. government agencies, AI technology companies, legal and compliance leaders, and international partners involved in AI standards and export controls.

name: Modernized Grant Management System
description: A modernized grant management system that uses AI and advanced analytics to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of federal STEM program grants. It eliminates unqualified applications, streamlines evaluation, and delivers funds more efficiently to high-potential candidates.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI and advanced analytics to transform grant evaluation and accelerate impact, making the process more strategic and outcome-focused.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, grant applicants, and beneficiaries of STEM programs are the primary stakeholders. The system also engages leaders who are responsible for proving grant value and ensuring transparency and accountability.

name: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
description: A bipartisan, bicameral bill reintroduced to establish regulatory guardrails at financial regulatory agencies for regulated entities to test AI projects. It aims to create regulatory sandboxes allowing financial institutions to test AI-enabled products and services without immediate risk of enforcement action, provided they meet transparency, consumer protection, and national security requirements.
novelty: The bill introduces AI Innovation Labs within financial regulatory agencies to allow experimentation with AI test projects without unnecessary regulation, promoting innovation while maintaining necessary oversight.
stakeholders: Financial institutions, financial regulatory agencies (SEC, CFPB, OCC, NCUA, FHFA, FDIC, Federal Reserve), private sector leaders in finance, consumers, and potentially smaller financial firms.

name: Taiwan Artificial Intelligence Government Talent Office
organisation: Directorate-General of Personnel Administration, Ministry of Digital Affairs (MODA)
description: The Taiwan Artificial Intelligence Government Talent Office is a national platform dedicated to cultivating AI talent and integrating AI into civil service operations. It provides a framework for government agencies to design training programs for AI-related roles, aiming to enhance AI literacy and application capabilities across Taiwan's civil service.
novelty: The innovation lies in establishing a standardized talent framework and an AI Bot Marketplace inspired by an app store, which compiles reusable AI bots to lower development costs and enhance service efficiency in government agencies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, civil servants, public and private training institutions, and citizens who will benefit from improved government services.

name: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
description: A bipartisan, bicameral bill reintroduced to establish regulatory guardrails at financial regulatory agencies for regulated entities to test AI projects. It aims to create regulatory sandboxes allowing financial institutions to test AI-enabled products and services without immediate risk of enforcement action, provided they meet transparency, consumer protection, and national security requirements.
novelty: The bill introduces AI Innovation Labs within financial regulatory agencies to enable experimentation with AI test projects without unnecessary regulation, promoting innovation while maintaining necessary oversight.
stakeholders: Financial institutions, financial regulatory agencies such as the SEC, CFPB, OCC, NCUA, FHFA, FDIC, Federal Reserve, and ultimately consumers who benefit from innovative financial services.

name: AI in Public Services
description: The UK Government is implementing AI in public services to connect multiple data sources, identify patterns, and improve social outcomes. This involves consolidating data into secure, well-governed environments to enable AI-driven insights while maintaining data protection and compliance.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI with secure data governance to transform public service delivery, allowing for advanced analytics and machine learning on sensitive datasets while maintaining compliance and public trust.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK government departments, public sector organisations, citizens benefiting from improved services, and industry partners providing secure data infrastructure.

name: Digital Transformation in the Public Sector
description: The digital transformation initiative in Vietnam's public sector aims to improve the quality of online public services, allowing citizens and businesses to complete administrative procedures anytime and anywhere. It focuses on personalizing services using big data and AI to analyze citizens’ needs and suggest business support policies.
novelty: The project is innovative as it aims to create a comprehensive digital economy by transforming governance models and enhancing the state's capacity to shape markets. It emphasizes the need for a unified technological architecture and the integration of digital tools in public services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include citizens, businesses, public officials, and the government. Beneficiaries are primarily citizens and businesses who will have improved access to public services. Public officials are also stakeholders as they need retraining to adapt to digital tools.

name: AWS-GSA Cloud Adoption Agreement
organisation: Amazon Web Services (AWS), US General Services Administration (GSA)
description: A $1 billion agreement between AWS and the GSA to provide federal agencies with discounts to accelerate cloud adoption, modernization, and AI innovation through 2028. The initiative aims to enhance government service delivery, leverage advanced technologies, and reduce costs.
novelty: The agreement offers 'incentive credits' for cloud services, application modernization, migration assistance, and training, marking a significant milestone in the digital transformation of government services.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, AWS, US General Services Administration, government service users, and potentially other tech companies involved in similar initiatives.

name: K-SMART
organisation: Information Kerala Mission, Government of Kerala
description: K-SMART is a comprehensive, integrated, and intelligent e-governance platform designed to unify over 23 functional modules of local government into a single application. It leverages emerging technologies such as AI/ML, IoT, blockchain, and analytics to replace slow, manual processes with streamlined, automated workflows, improving transparency, accountability, and efficiency in governance.
novelty: K-SMART is innovative due to its integration of multiple local government functions into a single application, utilizing advanced technologies like AI/ML, IoT, blockchain, and analytics to enhance governance processes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local government officials, citizens of Kerala, and technology providers. Beneficiaries are the residents of Kerala who will experience improved service delivery and governance efficiency.

name: Global AI Governance Action Plan
description: A 13-point framework establishing international cooperation principles for AI development and deployment, addressing infrastructure development, safety standards, data governance, environmental sustainability, open-source collaboration, and capacity building for developing nations.
novelty: The plan emphasizes global solidarity and multi-stakeholder participation to manage AI's potential and risks, promoting inclusive and fair AI governance on an international scale.
stakeholders: Governments, international organizations, enterprises, research institutions, social organizations, and individual citizens globally, with a focus on supporting developing nations.

name: AI Content Declaration Requirement
organisation: Institute for Risk and Strategic Studies (Salceda Research)
description: A proposal requiring institutions and content producers to disclose when generative AI is used in creating multimedia content, ensuring audiences are aware of AI-generated material.
novelty: The proposal introduces a standardized system for labeling AI-generated content, similar to a food label, to promote transparency and prevent misinformation.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public, content consumers, educational institutions, newsrooms, advertising agencies, and government offices.

name: Future of AI in Government Services Accelerator
organisation: Dubai Centre for Artificial Intelligence, Dubai Future Foundation
description: The 'Future of AI in Government Services Accelerator' programme invites tech innovators and AI companies to collaborate with Dubai's government entities to develop AI-driven solutions that enhance public services. The programme focuses on improving service personalisation, creating new services, boosting operational efficiency, and ensuring inclusive accessibility.
