It focuses on modernizing border security through digital innovation, automation, and customer-focused design, aiming to streamline operations and improve experiences for travelers, trade partners, and frontline officers.
novelty: The innovation lies in embedding customer experience into security operations, balancing strong national security with positive customer experience, and setting a new federal standard for 'people first' in digital government services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include travelers, trade partners, frontline officers, and the broader public who benefit from improved service delivery and security operations.

name: MyPay Payroll & Superannuation Shared Service – Superannuation Robotic Process Automation Project
description: The project involves the use of robotic process automation (RPA) to enhance the efficiency of payroll and superannuation services, aiming to streamline operations and improve service delivery.
novelty: The use of robotic process automation in public sector payroll and superannuation services is innovative as it automates repetitive tasks, reducing errors and increasing efficiency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Laois County Council employees, local government authorities, and the citizens of Laois and the Midlands who benefit from improved public services.

name: INFUSE Program and Milestone-Based Funding
description: The INFUSE program and milestone-based funding initiative by the DOE aims to accelerate fusion energy development through public-private partnerships. It provides financial support for projects addressing challenges like plasma stability and AI-driven diagnostics, and incentivizes private firms to take investment risks by offering funding upon achieving verifiable milestones.
novelty: The program is inspired by NASA's COTS model, reducing financial uncertainty and attracting venture capital by providing funding upon achieving milestones, thus encouraging private investment in fusion energy.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include private fusion energy companies, public utilities, venture capitalists, and the U.S. government. Beneficiaries are the companies receiving funding and the broader energy sector aiming for clean energy solutions.

name: Indonesia's National AI Roadmap
organisation: Ministry of Communication and Digital Affairs, Artificial Intelligence Industry Research and Innovation Collaboration (KORIKA)
description: Indonesia's National AI Roadmap is a strategic guide for developing human-centric AI, focusing on key principles like dignity, fairness, accountability, and transparency. It aims to develop strategic AI use cases in sectors such as food security, education, healthcare, and finance, while strengthening research and development.
novelty: The roadmap is innovative in its comprehensive approach to developing human-centric AI, emphasizing ethical principles and inclusivity, and involving a large working group from various sectors for its development.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government bodies, industry players, academia, civil society, and the general public who are engaged in providing feedback and benefiting from the AI developments.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a strategic initiative by the UK government to position the country as a global leader in artificial intelligence. It aims to improve public services and boost economic growth through accelerated AI research and adoption. The plan includes measures to build public trust in AI, address public attitudes, and ensure responsible governance and oversight of AI technologies.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into public services while addressing public concerns and trust issues. It emphasizes real-world evaluation, strategic communication, and inclusive training programs to ensure AI adoption is beneficial and sustainable.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK citizens, government agencies, public service providers, AI developers, and regulatory bodies. Beneficiaries are the general public who will experience improved public services and economic growth.

name: Office for AI Development and Regulation
description: A new office established by the Manx government to harness the benefits and mitigate the potential pitfalls of artificial intelligence (AI), aiming to digitise public services and deliver £50m of savings over five years.
novelty: The office aims to seize economic opportunities and address challenges presented by AI, enabling government and business collaboration to drive the island's response to the AI revolution.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the public sector, private businesses, and the general population of the Isle of Man. Stakeholders engaged include government officials and business leaders.

name: Government AI Workforce Upskilling
description: A strategic initiative to upskill federal employees in AI fundamentals to ensure successful implementation of AI technologies in government operations. The program aims to build AI fluency across various job roles, enabling informed decision-making and ethical use of AI.
novelty: The initiative emphasizes the importance of AI literacy across all levels of government roles, integrating interdisciplinary training that includes computer science, law, ethics, and public administration. It also proposes innovative approaches like tiered AI literacy programs and scenario-based training.
stakeholders: Federal employees across various roles such as program managers, procurement officers, policy advisors, and technical teams. Beneficiaries include government agencies and the general public who rely on efficient and ethical public services.

name: Manitoba's Economic Development Strategy
description: The strategy focuses on enhancing productivity, strengthening the private sector, creating higher-paying jobs, and retaining young talent in Manitoba through technology, intellectual property (IP), and artificial intelligence (AI). It includes initiatives for IP commercialization, AI deployment in public services, and expanding broadband and cellular networks.
novelty: The strategy emphasizes an IP-first approach and the use of AI for public sector modernization, marking a shift from traditional capital investments to digital leadership and innovation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Manitoba government, local businesses, Manitoba Hydro, post-secondary institutions, and the workforce in Manitoba who will benefit from enhanced skills and job opportunities.

name: FedRAMP 20x
description: FedRAMP 20x is an initiative to streamline and modernize the FedRAMP cloud security program by using automation and cutting processes to speed up authorizations. It aims to move from paper-based compliance processes to automated ones, allowing for faster and more efficient accreditation of technology services, including AI tools.
novelty: The project is innovative because it focuses on automating the FedRAMP authorization process, allowing for quicker deployment across different agencies. It emphasizes the use of machine-readable formats and aims to create a 'presumption of adequacy' process to reuse existing security work, reducing redundancy and saving time and money.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, cloud service providers, and technology vendors. Beneficiaries are the federal agencies that will have faster access to accredited technology services, and cloud service providers who will experience a streamlined authorization process.

name: Diella
description: Diella is an AI-powered chatbot designed to serve as Albania's Minister for Public Procurement, managing all bids for government contracts and purchases to eliminate corruption in public tenders.
novelty: Diella is the first AI agent to be designated as a government minister, specifically tasked with managing public procurement to ensure transparency and reduce corruption.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the Albanian government and its citizens, who will experience more transparent and corruption-free public procurement processes.

name: AI for the Commonwealth (AI4CW) initiative
description: The AI4CW initiative involves UMass-Amherst students working with state agency employees to use AI to improve public services and address government challenges. Projects include AI tools for permitting, call centers, HR inquiries, procurement, education, culinary training, and computing support.
novelty: The initiative uses a secure AI sandbox for developing generative AI tools, ensuring data privacy and ethical use. It integrates AI into state functions to enhance service efficiency and effectiveness.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Massachusetts residents, state government employees, and students. Stakeholders engaged are UMass-Amherst, state agencies, and the Healey-Driscoll Administration.

name: AI Efficacy Enhancement Team
description: A high-level team set up to implement AI in administrative procedures across government departments to improve efficiency and responsiveness. The initiative aims to incorporate AI into at least 200 administrative procedures by 2027, with 100 procedures targeted for 2026.
novelty: The project involves a citywide digital transformation led by a team of senior officials and private sector experts, focusing on integrating AI into government processes to enhance efficiency without additional costs.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government departments such as the Companies Registry, Transport Department, and Buildings Department, as well as the general public who will experience improved government services.

name: AI-powered voice support integration
organisation: Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine
description: The Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine is partnering with ElevenLabs to integrate AI-powered voice support into Ukraine’s government online services, including the Diia portal and app. This aims to make government services more interactive and accessible by allowing citizens to contact government platforms by voice.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of AI voice synthesis technology to create a digital twin and integrate voice support into digital services, making them more accessible and improving communication between government agencies and citizens.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Ukrainian citizens who will interact with government services more conveniently. Stakeholders engaged include the Ministry of Digital Transformation and ElevenLabs.

name: AI-powered voice support integration
organisation: Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine
description: The Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine is integrating AI-powered voice support into its government online services in partnership with ElevenLabs. This includes adding voice AI to the Diia portal and app, enhancing state education platforms, and improving internal tools with AI assistants.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of advanced AI voice synthesis technology, allowing citizens to interact with government services through voice, making the process faster and more convenient.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are Ukrainian citizens who will access government services more efficiently. Stakeholders include the Ministry of Digital Transformation and ElevenLabs, which provides the AI technology.

name: AI Framework Act
description: The AI Framework Act is a comprehensive statute that integrates strategy, promotion, and regulation of artificial intelligence (AI) into a single law. It aims to coordinate government direction, promote AI development and adoption, and manage risks associated with AI. The Act establishes a national AI strategy, supports industrial policy, and sets regulatory frameworks to ensure AI safety and trustworthiness.
novelty: The Act is innovative as it combines three AI policy levers—strategy, promotion, and regulation—into a single statute, making South Korea the first country to do so. This integrated approach aims to provide a coherent framework for AI development and governance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government bodies like the Ministry of Science and ICT (MSIT), AI business operators, SMEs, start-ups, larger firms, and the general public who are the beneficiaries of AI advancements and protections.

name: Office for AI
description: The Office for AI was launched to support the state's public service in rolling out AI pilot projects, providing assistance with trialling and evaluating AI tools. It aims to enhance the quality of future projects and prevent duplication by sharing learnings across the public sector.
novelty: The Office for AI represents a shift from a top-down approach to a more collaborative and shared learning model, allowing innovations found in one department to be shared with others, thus reducing costs and time to achieve productivity gains.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public servants in South Australia, various government agencies, and the broader public sector that will benefit from shared AI innovations.

name: AI Models for Data Analysis
description: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) has developed AI models to analyze millions of documents from major data leaks, enhancing the detection of offshore tax evasion. These AI tools use computer vision and natural language processing to extract and prioritize key information from documents, improving compliance targeting and audits.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to process and analyze large volumes of administrative data, such as the Panama Papers, which was previously unmanageable manually. This approach allows for more effective detection of tax evasion and enhances the evidentiary power of administrative data.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the ATO staff who utilize the AI outputs for audits and compliance checks. Indirectly, the Australian public benefits from improved tax compliance and revenue collection.

name: Cannie
organisation: National Association of Government Defined Contribution Administrators, major national public pension associations
description: Cannie is a conceptual AI-powered personal financial adviser-assistant designed to help state and local government employees manage their finances, invest wisely, and plan for retirement. It aims to provide personalized financial advice and services to public workers, enhancing their financial security and decision-making.
novelty: Cannie is innovative because it proposes a cooperative model for public retirement systems to collectively develop an AI-driven financial advisory platform, prioritizing workers' interests over profit-driven models. It aims to integrate personalized financial advice with public pension systems, offering a unique public-sector-focused solution.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state and local government employees, public retirement systems, the National Association of Government Defined Contribution Administrators, major national public pension associations, and potentially private-sector firms participating in the co-op network.

name: Proactive 311 Tool
organisation: South Bend Department of Innovation and Technology
description: The city of South Bend transformed its 311 service into a proactive tool for resident outreach, established a digital services office, and developed generative AI policies and training.
novelty: The innovation focuses on turning a traditional 311 service into a proactive outreach tool, emphasizing human-centered design and collaboration with residents.
stakeholders: Residents of South Bend, city departments, and partners involved in the innovation and technology projects.

name: SB 53
description: SB 53 establishes new requirements for frontier AI developers, focusing on transparency, innovation, safety, accountability, and responsiveness. It includes the creation of a consortium called CalCompute to advance AI development and deployment, and mechanisms for reporting safety incidents.
novelty: The innovation lies in the establishment of a comprehensive legal framework for AI development, including the creation of a public computing cluster consortium and mechanisms for safety and accountability.
stakeholders: Frontier AI developers, California Government, AI companies, public, California Department of Technology, Attorney General’s office.

name: GCtranslate
description: GCtranslate is an AI prototype for official languages translation, developed to improve efficiency, productivity, and service quality in the Canadian public service. It provides instant translations for texts that do not require linguistic expertise, reinforcing the use of both official languages within the public service.
novelty: GCtranslate stands out due to its secure, Canadian-based infrastructure and its adaptability to the federal public service, incorporating government terminology and Canada's cultural and linguistic realities.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include federal departments and agencies, public servants, and ultimately Canadian citizens who receive services from the government.

name: Project Rapid
description: Project Rapid is an initiative using AI to identify and take down phishing sites. It allows the Hong Kong Police Force to proactively identify and analyze suspicious websites, enabling faster verification of scam-related sites and reducing risk to the public.
novelty: The project uses AI to proactively identify and analyze phishing sites, allowing for rapid detection and takedown of scam-related websites, which enhances investigational efficiency.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public who are protected from phishing scams. Stakeholders engaged include the Hong Kong Police Force, INTERPOL, and internet service providers.

name: SkillsFuture Programme
description: The SkillsFuture Programme, launched in 2015, aims to provide Singaporeans aged 25 and above with access to education and training across various sectors. It emphasizes lifelong learning and collaboration between educational institutions and industries to prepare citizens for in-demand roles and emerging technologies.
novelty: The programme focuses on lifelong learning and upskilling, adapting to the digital era's demands and preparing the workforce for emerging technologies like AI.
stakeholders: Beneficiaries include Singaporeans aged 25 and above, educational institutions, industries, and businesses involved in collaborations for internships and training.

name: Talent Capital initiative
description: The Talent Capital initiative is a regional resource aimed at providing AI-driven job matching, training, reskilling, and career navigation services to residents of the Greater Washington region. It offers free services and personalized support to help individuals access high-quality jobs and grow their careers.
novelty: The initiative is the first-of-its-kind regional resource that combines cutting-edge AI technology with workforce services to enhance job matching and career development, focusing on keeping talent within the region.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include residents of DC, Maryland, and Virginia, particularly displaced public servants. Stakeholders engaged include local tech company BuildWithin, universities like UDC and George Washington University, and nonprofit partners.

name: Canadian AI Strategy National Sprint
description: A 30-day national consultation to shape a renewed AI strategy for Canada, focusing on accelerating AI adoption, scaling Canadian AI champions, strengthening sovereign infrastructure, and building public trust and skills.
novelty: The initiative is a comprehensive national consultation involving diverse stakeholders to define priorities for a renewed AI strategy, emphasizing Canada's leadership in responsible and secure AI.
stakeholders: Founders, researchers, workers, creators, students, public servants, community voices, and the general public in Canada.

name: AI in Government Services
description: Bahrain is using advanced technologies, such as artificial intelligence, to innovate government services, aiming for sustainable growth and prosperity.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its integration of artificial intelligence into government services, which is a forward-thinking approach to enhance efficiency and service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Bahraini government, citizens who will benefit from improved services, and the Information & eGovernment Authority responsible for implementation.

name: City of Bellevue's Artificial Intelligence (AI) Policy
description: The City of Bellevue's AI policy establishes guidelines for the ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI technologies to enhance public services. It aims to safeguard privacy, ensure compliance with laws, protect against bias, and maintain public trust while facilitating innovation.
novelty: The policy is innovative in its comprehensive approach to AI governance, emphasizing transparency, accountability, inclusion, and environmental stewardship. It encourages responsible experimentation and continuous learning to improve city services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include community members and city employees. Stakeholders engaged include city officials, employees, consultants, contractors, partners, vendors, and volunteers involved in AI system development and deployment.

name: National AI Strategy
description: Singapore's National AI Strategy aims to create a smarter, more connected future through AI innovation. It includes building large language models for public services, enhancing data protection, and aligning with the Smart Nation vision for better citizen services.
novelty: The strategy emphasizes responsible reinvention, focusing on ethical, transparent, and socially conscious AI advancements. It also involves localizing global innovations, such as an in-country AIOps platform for managing sensitive data.
stakeholders: Government agencies, private sector organizations, AI professionals, and citizens of Singapore are the primary stakeholders, benefiting from improved public services and enhanced data protection.

name: AI-Powered Roadway Monitoring Initiative
organisation: City of Alexandria, Virginia Tech Transportation Institute
description: The initiative aims to innovate how Alexandria monitors and manages its roadways using smart cameras and sensors to provide real-time insights about road conditions, such as identifying potholes or cracks, to inform proactive maintenance and traffic safety efforts.
novelty: The project leverages AI technology to provide real-time road condition insights, enabling proactive maintenance and enhancing traffic safety. It also involves partnerships with organizations to fill expertise gaps and improve technology through feedback.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include local government officials, residents of Alexandria, and technology vendors. Stakeholders engaged include the City of Alexandria and the Virginia Tech Transportation Institute.

name: SB 53
description: SB 53 is a California state law requiring large AI labs to be transparent about their safety and security protocols, specifically around preventing models from being used for cyberattacks or bio-weapons. The law mandates adherence to these protocols, enforced by the Office of Emergency Services.
novelty: SB 53 is the first state law in the United States to require transparency in AI safety and security protocols, aiming to protect innovation while ensuring product safety.
stakeholders: AI labs, policymakers, public safety officials, and potentially the general public as beneficiaries of safer AI technologies.

name: National Digital Development Strategy of Belarus for 2026-2030
description: The strategy aims to establish an environment in Belarus where artificial intelligence becomes a key driver of economic growth and improves the quality of life for citizens. It includes building AI readiness in innovative businesses, developing the data economy, and creating the country's first AI standards.
novelty: AI has been designated as a distinct objective in the draft National Sustainable Development Strategy through 2040, with AI and big data technologies identified as priority investment sectors.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the Belarusian public, businesses, and government institutions. Stakeholders engaged include over 10,000 professionals from the public and private sectors, academia, and civil society.

name: Digital Transformation in Espirito Santo
organisation: Espirito Santo Government, SAS Partner VERT Analytics
description: Espirito Santo is implementing a digital transformation initiative to enhance government responsiveness and transparency, aiming to improve interactions between government agencies and citizens.
novelty: The project leverages modern data analytics and AI tools to create a more agile, transparent, and citizen-focused government, representing a cultural shift in public service delivery.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are the citizens of Espirito Santo, while stakeholders include the Espirito Santo Government and SAS Partner VERT Analytics.

name: Agentic AI for Government Services
organisation: National Association of State Chief Information Officers (NASCIO)
description: Agentic AI is being developed to manage citizen interactions and automate multistep processes in government services, such as assisting with unemployment benefits applications by identifying stalled claims, notifying applicants, guiding document submission, and summarizing cases for human review.
novelty: Agentic AI is innovative because it proactively manages citizen interactions and automates complex processes while maintaining a human touch where necessary, enhancing efficiency in government service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state governments, citizens applying for government services, and state Chief Information Officers responsible for implementing AI technologies.

name: Apply AI Strategy
description: The Apply AI Strategy aims to accelerate the use of AI in key industries and the public sector within the EU, enhancing societal benefits such as healthcare and public service efficiency. It addresses challenges like time-to-market and workforce readiness, and includes initiatives like the Apply AI Alliance and AI Observatory.
novelty: The strategy introduces a comprehensive approach to speeding up AI adoption across sectors by linking infrastructure, data, and testing facilities, and by creating a collaborative forum through the Apply AI Alliance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include industry, the public sector, academia, social partners, civil society, and the EU workforce.

name: Android Enterprise for West Midlands Police
description: West Midlands Police implemented Android Enterprise to enhance officer productivity and secure sensitive data. The solution includes fully managed devices with end-to-end encryption, private certificates, and controlled app downloads, improving data security and operational efficiency.
novelty: The innovation leverages Android Enterprise's advanced security features and AI-driven capabilities to transform police operations, enabling officers to access critical information quickly and securely, thus enhancing productivity and public service.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the West Midlands Police officers and staff, who benefit from improved productivity and data security. The community also benefits from enhanced public service and engagement.

name: Chief Data Officer (CDO) Role in State Governments
organisation: Beeck Center for Social Impact and Innovation, National Association of State Chief Information Officers
description: The innovation involves the establishment and structuring of the Chief Data Officer (CDO) role within state governments to enhance data governance, sharing, and analytics, develop responsible AI policy, and support data literacy and cross-agency collaboration.
novelty: The innovation is novel because it formalizes the CDO role across states, providing a structured approach to data management and strategy in government, which is critical for leveraging AI and digital services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state governments, chief data officers, state leaders, and the public who benefit from improved data-driven decision-making and service delivery.

name: Real-time AI Translation Service for Meetings
description: The City of Hartford has implemented a real-time AI translation service for city council and school board meetings to facilitate participation for non-English speakers. This service is part of the city's transition to Google Workspace and is expected to save $2.1 million over five years.
novelty: The service provides two-way, real-time translation with the ability to infuse the speaker's emotion into the translation, distinguishing Hartford from other cities using AI for language barriers.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include non-English speaking residents of Hartford, particularly the Hispanic and Latino communities, city council members, and remote viewers of the meetings. Stakeholders include the City of Hartford and Google.

name: Iceland's Action Plan on Artificial Intelligence
description: Iceland's first Action Plan on Artificial Intelligence outlines 20 fully funded actions to promote innovation, strengthen digital infrastructure, and ensure ethical, inclusive use of AI. It covers five key areas including ethics, business competitiveness, education, public service, and healthcare.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across multiple sectors, with a focus on ethics, inclusivity, and alignment with international standards.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Icelandic government, educational institutions, businesses, healthcare providers, and the general public who will benefit from improved services and productivity.

name: Digital Government Services Initiatives
description: The Information & eGovernment Authority (iGA) of Bahrain is showcasing its latest initiatives and projects in digital government services at GITEX Global 2025. These initiatives leverage emerging technologies such as cloud computing and artificial intelligence to enhance government operations, strengthen public-private partnerships, and accelerate the growth of the national economy.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of emerging technologies like cloud computing and artificial intelligence to transform government services and promote knowledge sharing in the digital field.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include business leaders, tech experts, startups, innovators, and the general public who benefit from enhanced digital government services.

name: Government Innovation Showcase Aotearoa 2025
description: A platform as part of Government Innovation Week to bring together government executives for conferences, networking events, and masterclasses aimed at creating better, fairer, and more accessible public services through digital transformation.
novelty: The event focuses on integrating AI, data analytics, process automation, and IT modernisation to shape the future of public service excellence, emphasizing a culture of continuous improvement and cross-sector collaboration.
stakeholders: Government executives, public sector leaders, industry partners, and the not-for-profit sector, as well as the general public benefiting from improved public services.

name: Presidential Initiative for Women’s Health
description: A data-driven initiative aimed at improving women's healthcare through culturally sensitive screening methods, expanded access to primary healthcare and mobile clinics, enhanced health education, and the use of AI for mammogram analysis.
novelty: The initiative is the Middle East’s first to use an artificial intelligence platform for mammogram image analysis, allowing faster and more precise breast cancer detection.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include women, particularly in remote and rural areas, healthcare professionals, and international partners. Stakeholders engaged include the Egyptian government, global pharmaceutical partners, and the World Health Organization.

name: AI Factory Antenna
organisation: University of Galway, Irish Centre for High-End Computing (ICHEC)
description: The AI Factory Antenna is a project aimed at enhancing Ireland's AI capabilities by providing access to computing infrastructure, technical support, and training. It is designed to help Irish innovators develop, test, and scale AI models and applications.
novelty: The project links directly to leading AI hubs in France and Luxembourg and is part of the EuroHPC family, providing cutting-edge infrastructure and expert support to accelerate innovation across various sectors in Ireland.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include researchers, developers, public bodies, institutions, SMEs, and innovation stakeholders such as enterprise accelerators and digital skills networks.

name: OneGov
description: OneGov is a federal initiative that emphasizes cost savings and agility through direct OEM relationships, supporting cybersecurity responsiveness and guiding state and local procurement reforms.
novelty: The innovation lies in its focus on direct OEM relationships to enhance cost savings and agility, as well as its integration of AI tools to improve procurement processes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal, state, and local government procurement officers, vendors, and technology providers like HP.

name: Intuitive, Accessible and Responsive Digital Experiences
organisation: City of Virginia Beach Department of Information Technology
description: The City of Virginia Beach Department of Information Technology developed a system to deliver digital services that are accessible and responsive, ensuring compliance with web content accessibility guidelines and ADA standards. This includes making city websites and documents accessible, training staff, and implementing an intelligent chatbot.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to accessibility, ensuring 100% compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards across all city websites, correcting thousands of documents for ADA compliance, and training staff to maintain these standards. Additionally, the implementation of an intelligent chatbot enhances user interaction.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the residents of Virginia Beach, particularly those with disabilities who benefit from improved accessibility, city staff who are trained to maintain compliance, and the IT department responsible for implementation.

name: Global Government Summit 2025
description: The Global Government Summit 2025 brought together senior civil service leaders from 20 countries to discuss governance with AI, joining up government, building effective public services, and boosting trust in government. The summit focused on AI's potential in governance, ethical considerations, and international collaboration for AI adoption.
novelty: The summit emphasized the need for a whole-of-government AI strategy and international collaboration to share best practices and accelerate AI adoption in civil services globally. It highlighted the transformative potential of AI in governance and the importance of balancing technological capabilities with human-centric innovation.
stakeholders: National heads of civil service, departmental chiefs, public service leaders, and civil servants from 20 countries. Beneficiaries include government agencies and citizens who will experience improved governance and public services.

name: Texas by Texas (TxT) digital assistant platform
description: A comprehensive online portal for Texas residents to manage state-issued licenses, registrations, and receive proactive reminders, aimed at simplifying interactions with government and reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
novelty: The platform offers unprecedented efficiency and accessibility in citizen interaction with state agencies, leveraging AI to modernize citizen-facing services.
stakeholders: Texas residents, state agencies, Texas Department of Information Resources, and firms like B12Gov providing AI solutions.

name: AI-driven digital solutions
description: A series of AI-driven digital solutions developed to support government institutions in their digital transformation and dematerialization efforts. These include platforms like Sentinel, AI Moore, and AI Faso Opps, which aim to enhance the efficiency, accessibility, and automation of public services. A notable initiative is a chatbot for simplifying access to information such as job opportunities, scholarships, and public announcements, as well as a solution for interacting with public service information via WhatsApp in local languages.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to create accessible and automated platforms for public services, including a chatbot and WhatsApp interaction in local languages, enhancing inclusivity and accessibility.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Burkinabè citizens, particularly those seeking information on job opportunities, scholarships, and public announcements. Stakeholders engaged include the Ministry of Digital Transition and Go AI Corporation.

name: Civic Innovation Challenge
description: The City of Bellevue's Civic Innovation Challenge involves four tech innovators piloting AI projects to address municipal challenges. Participants receive design partnerships, coaching, testing support, and a stipend. The projects include AI-driven platforms for vendor compliance, municipal code analysis, smart lighting solutions, and a lost-and-found system.
novelty: The challenge provides a real-world testing ground for AI solutions in municipal services, fostering innovation through collaboration between tech innovators and city operations.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include city staff, Bellevue College students, staff, and visitors. Stakeholders engaged include tech innovators, city staff, and seasoned entrepreneurs.

name: Texas by Texas (TxT)
description: Texas by Texas (TxT) is the state's official digital assistant that allows residents to manage multiple state services through a single account. The latest features enable online renewal of boat registrations and upgrading of driver's licenses, eliminating the need for in-person visits.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to consolidate multiple state services into a single digital platform, offering convenience and efficiency by allowing users to perform tasks that previously required in-person visits.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are Texas residents who need to manage state services such as driver's licenses and boat registrations. The stakeholders include the Texas Departments of Public Safety and Parks and Wildlife.

name: Abu Dhabi Government Digital Strategy 2025-2027
organisation: Abu Dhabi’s Department of Government Enablement (DGE), NVIDIA, Oracle, Deloitte, Core42
description: The Abu Dhabi Government Digital Strategy 2025-2027 aims to transform government operations into an AI-native environment, leveraging NVIDIA’s AI computing platforms and Oracle’s cloud infrastructure to deliver next-generation citizen services. It includes the digitization and automation of all government processes, with AI-powered capabilities like multilingual AI assistants and intelligent compliance systems.
novelty: The strategy represents a landmark initiative in sovereign AI, demonstrating how AI and cloud capabilities can transform government operations at scale, ensuring data sovereignty while delivering high-performance AI solutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Abu Dhabi's government entities and citizens, with stakeholders such as NVIDIA, Oracle, Deloitte, and Core42 involved in the implementation.

name: AI Tool Revolutionizing Legislative Review
description: An AI tool implemented by the State of Minnesota to revolutionize the legislative review process, making it more efficient and effective.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of artificial intelligence to streamline and enhance the legislative review process, which is traditionally manual and time-consuming.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the legislative staff and government officials in Minnesota, as well as the citizens who benefit from more efficient legislative processes.

name: AI Regulatory Sandboxes
description: AI regulatory sandboxes are controlled environments where startups and technology firms can test innovative AI products and services under regulatory supervision. These sandboxes aim to facilitate regulatory experimentation and learning, informing evidence-based rulemaking and reform.
novelty: The innovation lies in adapting the concept of regulatory sandboxes, originally used in fintech, to AI governance. This approach allows for structured experimentation with AI applications, providing insights into how they interact with existing or proposed regulations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include startups, technology firms, regulators, and policymakers. Beneficiaries are the public, who gain from improved AI governance and consumer protection.

name: AI Implementation in Government Services
description: The innovation involves the implementation of artificial intelligence (AI) in government services to improve service delivery, enhance data-driven decision-making, and optimize resources. The initiative focuses on addressing specific pain points such as reducing service backlogs and improving citizen experience.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI in government services, emphasizing a problem-first approach, data governance, accountability, ethics, and risk management.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public administrators, government agencies, citizens, and community stakeholders. Beneficiaries are the citizens who experience improved government services.

name: Generative AI Guide and Permit Drafter Tool
description: New Jersey has released a guide to assist officials in developing generative AI tools, including a specific tool that drafts permit instructions in plain language to expedite business processes.
novelty: The guide provides a comprehensive framework for implementing generative AI in government processes, emphasizing the importance of appropriate use cases, efficient data processing, and proactive testing.
stakeholders: Government officials, businesses in New Jersey, residents seeking permits, and AI developers.

name: Local Innovation Partnerships Fund (LIPF)
description: The Local Innovation Partnerships Fund (LIPF) is a £500 million initiative by the UK Government to support local innovation priorities in various regions, including Greater Manchester, West Midlands, and Glasgow City Region. Each region receives £50 million to fund innovations in science and technology, such as life-saving medicines and clean fuels. The fund aims to boost local economies, create jobs, and enhance health resilience.
novelty: The LIPF is innovative in its approach to decentralize funding, allowing local leaders to focus on regional strengths and priorities in science and technology. It supports diverse innovations, from AI in healthcare to clean energy solutions, fostering regional economic growth and technological advancement.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local leaders, businesses, universities, research institutions, start-ups, SMEs, and the general public in the regions of Greater Manchester, West Midlands, and Glasgow City Region. Beneficiaries are local communities who will experience economic growth, job creation, and improved public services.

name: The Bank of England's approach to innovation in artificial intelligence, distributed ledger technology, and quantum computing
description: The Bank of England has published a strategy document outlining its approach to fostering innovation in artificial intelligence (AI), distributed ledger technology (DLT), and quantum computing. The strategy focuses on creating an environment for responsible innovation through infrastructure, regulations, and coordination. It highlights the potential of these technologies to transform the UK economy and financial services.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI, DLT, and quantum computing into the financial sector, focusing on infrastructure, regulations, and public-private collaboration to drive transformative outcomes.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include UK financial services firms, AI experts, the Financial Conduct Authority, the Bank for International Settlements, and other international and domestic authorities. Beneficiaries are the UK economy and financial sector participants.

name: Saudi Netzero platform
organisation: Saudi Arabia’s Ministry of Environment, Water and Agriculture
description: The Saudi Netzero platform is a strategic initiative aimed at advancing Saudi Arabia's 2060 net-zero targets by accelerating nature-based climate solutions across the Middle East, North Africa, and Asia. It focuses on connecting afforestation projects with businesses to offset carbon emissions, restore ecosystems, and promote sustainable development.
novelty: The platform integrates Saudi-developed environmental technologies with field conservation efforts, using cutting-edge digital infrastructure to track environmental impacts, monitor vegetation recovery, and optimize tree-planting campaigns. It offers unprecedented transparency in measuring outcomes and improving climate gains through artificial intelligence and geographic data analysis.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local communities, businesses aiming to meet sustainability goals, environmental entrepreneurs, and global conservation networks like the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN).

name: Chief Innovation Officer role
description: Creation of a Chief Innovation Officer role to spearhead New York City's tech and crypto innovation, supporting emerging industries such as AI, blockchain, and fostering public-private collaboration.
novelty: Establishment of a senior administration position specifically focused on coordinating multi-tech initiatives and advising on policy developments in emerging technologies.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the city's public sector, emerging tech industries such as AI, blockchain, and biotech, and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include leaders from these tech fields and public-private partners.

name: Uncrewed Aircraft Trials
description: NPAS is leasing a Schiebel aircraft for six months to conduct trials on the use of uncrewed aircraft as a complementary asset to their current fleet. The trials aim to explore the capabilities of uncrewed aircraft in various incident types and assess the potential integration of AI for enhanced surveillance and search operations.
novelty: The project introduces uncrewed aircraft with active radar and VHF communications, allowing ground-based pilots to control the aircraft remotely. It explores the integration of AI for real-time analysis and enhanced search capabilities, offering a new approach to police aviation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include NPAS leadership, police forces across England and Wales, the Civil Aviation Authority, regulatory bodies, government, and potentially other emergency services interested in the technology.

name: Customer-Centric Service Model with AI Empowerment
description: Illinois is shifting to a customer-centric service model for its state agencies, emphasizing the use of AI to empower the state workforce and improve efficiency. This approach aims to maintain high service levels amid budget cuts and workforce reductions.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI to empower the workforce and enhance efficiency, while maintaining a strong focus on customer service for state agencies.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are the 36 client state agencies in Illinois, state tech leaders, and the state workforce who are being trained to use AI tools.

name: AI Growth Lab
description: The AI Growth Lab is a government initiative to create regulatory sandboxes for testing AI products in real-world conditions. It aims to accelerate innovation by temporarily relaxing certain regulations under strict supervision, focusing on sectors like healthcare, professional services, and transport. The initiative is expected to improve public services, reduce bureaucracy, and drive economic growth.
novelty: The AI Growth Lab introduces a flexible regulatory approach by using sandboxes to allow testing of AI innovations with temporarily relaxed regulations, promoting faster adoption and development of AI technologies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK businesses, AI startups, regulators, healthcare providers, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services and economic growth.

name: Chief Innovation Officer for NYC
description: If elected, Andrew Cuomo plans to install a chief innovation officer to lead the adoption of AI and blockchain technologies across New York City agencies. The role aims to support emerging industries, foster public-private collaboration, and improve city services and efficiency.
novelty: The creation of a dedicated chief innovation officer role to integrate AI and blockchain technologies across city agencies is a novel approach to maintaining New York City's leadership in global innovation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include city agencies, business, labor, academia, community groups, and residents of New York City.

name: 9 Tips for Building GenAI Tools for Public Sector Professionals
description: A guide created by New Jersey’s Office of Innovation to help other governments build and work with generative AI tools. It is based on New Jersey's experiences with tools like the New Jersey AI Assistant and the state Department of Labor’s AI translation assistant.
novelty: The guide provides distilled insights and practical tips for developing internal generative AI tools, addressing challenges like timeouts and data-processing hurdles, and sharing learnings to facilitate rapid advancements in AI use in the public sector.
stakeholders: Public sector professionals, other state and local governments, engineering teams, and users of generative AI tools in government services.

name: Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO)
description: The Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO) is a dedicated team established to reduce regulatory burdens for innovative businesses, enabling them to bring new technologies to market faster. It supports various projects, including drones for medical deliveries and AI tools for fire risk assessment, by providing funding and regulatory support.
novelty: The RIO offers a holistic support system to regulators and businesses, facilitating the trial of new technologies and regulatory approaches. It aims to create a pro-innovation environment by cutting red tape and enabling faster market entry for groundbreaking innovations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include innovative businesses, regulators, public service providers like the NHS and London Fire Brigade, and beneficiaries such as patients and the general public who benefit from improved public services.

name: AI Growth Lab
description: The AI Growth Lab is a project consisting of AI sandboxes where companies and innovators can test new AI products in real-world conditions, targeting sectors like healthcare, professional services, transport, and advanced manufacturing. It aims to accelerate the responsible development and deployment of AI in the UK.
novelty: The AI Growth Lab is one of the UK's first ventures into regulatory sandboxes specifically designed for AI, allowing for faster and safer innovation by providing a controlled environment for testing AI products.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include companies and innovators in AI sectors, healthcare providers, professional services, transport, and advanced manufacturing industries. Stakeholders engaged include the UK government, technology and regulatory experts, and potentially the UK Information Commissioner’s Office.

name: Creative Modernization
description: An approach to government IT modernization that focuses on blending new technologies such as AI, automation, and low-code platforms with existing systems to improve service delivery and citizen experience without costly overhauls.
novelty: The project emphasizes incremental modernization over large-scale overhauls, focusing on extending the value of existing systems and delivering rapid wins while maintaining a long-term vision.
stakeholders: State CIOs, government agencies, citizens, and public-private partners.

name: Justice Transcribe
description: Justice Transcribe is an internal AI system implemented by the UK's Ministry of Justice to record and transcribe conversations with offenders. It aims to automate manual transcription, freeing up significant staff time and allowing probation officers to focus more on supervision and rehabilitation activities.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to automate the transcription process, potentially saving hundreds of thousands of working days and increasing efficiency in public services.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are probation officers and offenders within the UK's justice system. The stakeholders include the Ministry of Justice, OpenAI, and potentially other public service sectors like the NHS.

name: AI Sandboxes
description: AI Sandboxes allow companies to temporarily suspend or change regulations in key sectors such as healthcare, professional services, transport, and robotics to accelerate the development of AI products. This initiative is part of the AI Opportunities Action Plan aimed at transforming the UK's economy and public services through AI.
novelty: The project introduces a flexible regulatory approach by using 'sandboxes' to fast-track responsible innovations in AI, allowing for temporary regulatory adjustments to foster faster development and implementation of AI technologies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include companies developing AI products, sectors such as healthcare, professional services, transport, and robotics, and the UK Government. Beneficiaries are the public who rely on modernized public services and the economy at large.

name: National Programme for Digital Transformation (Tahawul)
organisation: Ministry of Transport, Communications and Information Technology (MTCIT)
description: A comprehensive initiative aimed at digitising government services, simplifying procedures, and improving user experience in Oman. It includes projects like the Unified Government Services Portal, National Open Data Portal, and National Digital Trust Services project, among others.
novelty: The programme integrates multiple digital projects under a single national strategy, enhancing transparency, digital governance, and access to government services through innovative solutions like AI-based monitoring and drone-assisted oversight.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include all segments of Omani society accessing government services, government entities involved in digital transactions, and communities empowered through digital awareness initiatives.

name: Artificial Intelligence Data Centres Strategy
description: The strategy aims to make Alberta the most attractive place in North America for building AI data centres by keeping taxes low, modernizing regulations, and providing funding opportunities. It seeks to attract $100 billion in investments, generating significant tax revenue for public services.
novelty: The strategy focuses on leveraging Alberta's geographical advantages, such as flat land and sunny winters, and its intellectual infrastructure, including universities, to attract AI data centres.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Alberta government, data centre companies, local communities, and educational institutions like the University of Alberta and the University of Calgary. Beneficiaries include the local economy and public services funded by increased tax revenue.

name: End-to-end automated online system for business processes
description: Quezon City implemented a fully automated online system for business processes, including the issuance of electronic official receipts, to simplify, accelerate, and make business processes more accessible.
novelty: First LGU in the Philippines to fully implement an end-to-end automated online system for business processes.
stakeholders: Local businesses, investors, Quezon City government officials, Economic Development Team, Business Permits and Licensing Department.

name: Gennai
description: Gennai is a generative AI-powered tool developed for government employees to streamline public services, enhance administrative efficiency, and maintain high standards of data protection and transparency. It operates within Japan’s secure digital infrastructure and is being expanded across ministries.
novelty: Gennai leverages OpenAI’s technology within Japan’s own secure infrastructure, ensuring data stays within the country's jurisdiction. It aligns with Japan's ambition to lead global AI governance and complies with national cybersecurity standards through ISMAP certification.
stakeholders: Government employees, Japan Digital Agency, OpenAI, cloud and security firms, international bodies like OECD and G7.

name: Emergency Alert System (EAS)
organisation: New York City Office of Technology and Innovation
description: The Emergency Alert System (EAS) is a pilot program that integrates an automated 911 emergency alert system across New York City's public schools to provide faster responses during active shooter situations. It allows schools to directly initiate 911 calls after weapon-based threats, providing immediate, secure, and automated alerts to emergency dispatchers.
novelty: The EAS is the first system in the United States to provide direct integration between public schools and 911 services, bypassing the regular 911 call flow for faster emergency response.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include students, faculty, and parents of New York City public schools. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the New York City Office of Technology and Innovation, Mayor's Office, New York City Public Schools, and NYPD's School Safety Division.

name: Fraud Prevention and Detection Initiative
description: An initiative by CMS to enhance fraud enforcement by leveraging advanced analytics and automation, and building cross-agency partnerships to improve oversight and collaboration.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of advanced analytics and automation for earlier and more accurate fraud detection, combined with a strong emphasis on cross-agency collaboration.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal programs and taxpayers, with stakeholders being CMS and other federal agencies involved in oversight and collaboration.

name: National LLM for Greece
description: Greece's initiative to develop an open-weight large language model (LLM) to address the underrepresentation of the Greek language in commercial models, while preserving both contemporary and historical linguistic heritage.
novelty: The project focuses on creating a national LLM that reflects Greece's linguistic nuances, cultural context, and regulatory frameworks, ensuring data sovereignty and preserving local languages.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Greek government agencies, citizens, and public sector organizations. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include AWS and the Greek Government.

name: Gen Wizard
organisation: Accenture Federal Services (AFS), Amazon Web Services (AWS)
description: Gen Wizard is a comprehensive modernization tool developed to address legacy system challenges in federal agencies. It leverages AWS's cloud and AI infrastructure to accelerate new platform implementations, reduce system upgrade time, and streamline application portfolios, thereby improving operational efficiency and service delivery to citizens.
novelty: Gen Wizard combines AWS's AI services like Amazon SageMaker, Amazon Comprehend, and Amazon Bedrock to modernize legacy systems. It provides AI-powered assistance for code migration and modernization tasks, significantly reducing development time and costs.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are federal agencies in the United States, their IT departments, and ultimately, the citizens who benefit from improved government service delivery.

name: Jakarta Smart City AI-powered analytics platform
description: An AI-powered analytics platform that forecasts flood risks up to six hours in advance, integrating data from rainfall sensors, river gauges, and weather services to enable preventive flood management actions.
novelty: The platform shifts flood management from reactive to preventive by using AI to forecast flood risks and enable timely interventions.
stakeholders: Residents of Jakarta, city authorities, and emergency response teams.

name: AI Skills Initiative
organisation: Department of Government Enablement – Abu Dhabi (DGE)
description: The initiative aims to equip over 20,000 government employees in Abu Dhabi with AI skills and tools, supporting the goal of becoming the world's first fully AI-native government by 2027. It includes AI learning programs, role-based learning pathways, and integration with platforms like AWS Skill Builder.
novelty: The project sets a global benchmark by aiming to transform Abu Dhabi into the first fully AI-native government, integrating advanced AI training and tools across all levels of government.
stakeholders: Government employees in Abu Dhabi, including executives, policy leaders, programme managers, data practitioners, engineers, and frontline teams. Beneficiaries include citizens, residents, and businesses in Abu Dhabi.

name: ChatGPT Enterprise Adoption by UK Ministry of Justice
description: The UK Ministry of Justice has adopted OpenAI's ChatGPT Enterprise technology to enhance privacy, accountability, and national resilience by storing data on British soil. The technology will aid routine tasks such as writing support, compliance, legal work, data and research processes, and document analysis, allowing frontline staff to focus more on human-centric tasks.
novelty: The innovation involves the use of generative AI in public services, specifically within the justice system, and includes a data residency agreement to store data on sovereign servers in the UK, enhancing privacy and accountability.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK Ministry of Justice, OpenAI, British businesses, government institutions, and public service workers who will use the AI tools to improve efficiency and focus on human-centric tasks.

name: £55 billion R&D Investment
organisation: UK Government, Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
description: A £55 billion investment by the UK Government to accelerate scientific breakthroughs, drive economic growth, and improve lives across the UK. The funding supports leading research bodies, universities, and businesses, with a focus on innovations from airport security tech to life-saving cancer treatments.
novelty: The scale of investment and its comprehensive support for a wide range of sectors, including climate science, AI safety, and health research, aiming to deliver significant economic and societal benefits.
stakeholders: UK research bodies, universities, businesses, researchers, innovators, and the general public who will benefit from advancements in health, security, and the economy.

name: Task Force on Artificial Intelligence
organisation: Governor's Office of Policy Innovation and the Future, Maine's Office of Information Technology
description: The Task Force on Artificial Intelligence was established to analyze the opportunities and implications of AI technology for Maine's people, economy, and institutions. It delivered 33 recommendations to bolster AI innovation while strengthening guardrails against potential harms, focusing on areas like education, healthcare, and public services.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to AI, involving a wide range of stakeholders and focusing on both maximizing benefits and mitigating risks. It emphasizes AI literacy, responsible use, and the transformation of public services through AI.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Maine residents, businesses, educational institutions, and government agencies. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include state and local officials, legislators, education representatives, business and non-profit leaders.

name: Scotland CardioMetabolic Impact Study (SCoMIS)
organisation: University of Glasgow, Novo Nordisk, IQVIA
description: The Scotland CardioMetabolic Impact Study (SCoMIS) is a multi-million-pound research project aimed at evaluating the real-world impact of incretin-based weight-loss medicines on obesity and related health inequalities in Scotland. The study will involve 3,000 to 5,000 participants from some of Scotland's most deprived communities, providing insights into the effectiveness and safety of these medicines.
novelty: The study employs a collaborative approach involving AI-driven digital technologies to support patient access, engagement, and data collection, focusing on real-world evidence to guide NHS commissioning and BMI thresholds for incretin-based treatments.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Scottish patients living with obesity, particularly from deprived communities, healthcare providers, the NHS, and academic and clinical leaders from the Universities of Glasgow, Dundee, and Edinburgh.

name: PDIC Online Assistant (POLA)
description: The PDIC Online Assistant (POLA) is an AI-powered chatbot designed to provide real-time, reliable, and user-friendly information to clients and stakeholders through the PDIC website. It aims to enhance the ability to respond quickly, accurately, and efficiently to public inquiries, especially for tech-savvy depositors and bank clients.
novelty: POLA represents a significant step in PDIC's digital transformation, showcasing the use of AI to improve public service and government accountability.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include tech-savvy depositors, closed bank clients, and other stakeholders engaging with PDIC online. The stakeholders involved in the launch include PDIC president and CEO Roberto Tan, Civil Service Commission assistant commissioner Judith Dongallo-Chicano, and CSC NCR field office director Cesar Garduque Jr.

name: Innovation and Prosperity Report
organisation: Government of Manitoba, Innovation and Productivity Task Force
description: The Innovation and Prosperity Report outlines a vision for Manitoba to transition from a resource-based economy to a knowledge-based economy by focusing on intellectual property (IP), data, and artificial intelligence (AI) as key assets. The report provides a framework for driving competitiveness, innovation, and inclusive prosperity by aligning economic goals with strategic priorities of a sovereign, value-added economy.
novelty: The report emphasizes the importance of owning and governing intangible assets like IP, data, and AI, treating them as sources of wealth and sovereignty. It proposes a shift from traditional economic models to one where prosperity is driven by controlling these intangible assets.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Government of Manitoba, Manitoba businesses, post-secondary institutions, Indigenous communities, and the broader workforce. Beneficiaries are expected to be the citizens of Manitoba who will gain from economic growth and technological advancements.

name: Sovereign HyperCloud
organisation: Beyon Solutions, Information and eGovernment Authority (iGA), Oracle
description: The Sovereign HyperCloud is Bahrain's first AI-ready, locally operated cloud platform, powered by Oracle Alloy. It is designed to enhance digital transformation and data sovereignty by providing a secure cloud infrastructure hosted entirely within Bahrain. It enables government entities to innovate while maintaining full control of their data within national borders.
novelty: The project is innovative as it combines global cloud innovation with local governance, being the only cloud hosted entirely within Bahrain. It supports AI, automation, and analytics, ensuring data protection and management within the country.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are Bahrain's government entities, with the iGA as the primary client. The stakeholders include Beyon Solutions, Oracle, and the Bahrain Economic Development Board.

name: AI Pilot Program
description: The AI Pilot Program is an initiative by the City of Oakland to invite AI companies and researchers to submit ideas for improving public services using AI. The program includes piloting AI solutions across various city operations, such as digital accessibility, civic engagement, public safety, and community impact, with the aim to enhance service delivery and accessibility for residents.
novelty: The program is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across city operations, focusing on equity, security, and privacy. It involves a citywide call for AI solutions and includes a robust data governance program to ensure responsible AI deployment.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI companies, researchers, City of Oakland staff, residents of Oakland, and various city departments. Beneficiaries are primarily the residents who will experience improved access to city services.

name: Kaizen's Unified Digital Platform for Government Services
description: Kaizen has developed a unified digital platform aimed at transforming how citizens interact with government services. The platform replaces outdated systems with a modern, people-first digital infrastructure, supporting services like recreation, transit, licensing, payments, and utility billing. It has already been implemented across 50 agencies in 17 states, reaching over 30 million residents.
novelty: The innovation lies in its AI-native, design-driven approach to modernizing civic infrastructure, offering seamless, intuitive, and accessible experiences similar to modern private-sector apps. It rapidly launches essential services and provides modular tools for administrators.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local, state, and federal government agencies, as well as residents who use public services such as parks, transit, and licensing. Beneficiaries are the citizens who experience improved interactions with government services.

name: Government Experience Awards 2025
description: The State of Hawaiʻi was recognized as an overall winner in the 2025 Government Experience Awards for its commitment to creating accessible, equitable, and innovative digital services. The awards highlight jurisdictions that set the standard for people-first services and use technology creatively to build trust with residents.
novelty: The innovation lies in the cautious and inquisitive use of artificial intelligence to enhance government accessibility and service delivery, ensuring technology is adopted for the right reasons to help people.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are the residents of Hawaiʻi, while the stakeholders include the State of Hawaiʻi government and the Center for Digital Government.

name: Fintech 2030
description: The 'Fintech 2030' strategy is a comprehensive plan by the HKMA to advance financial technologies through four pillars: data and payment infrastructure, artificial intelligence, resilience, and tokenisation of finance. It includes over 40 initiatives aimed at enhancing data sharing, AI adoption, financial sector resilience, and tokenisation of assets.
novelty: The strategy is innovative due to its holistic approach, integrating next-generation data infrastructure, AI, and tokenisation to transform the financial sector. It also includes pioneering efforts like regularising tokenised government bonds and developing a wholesale CBDC sandbox.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include financial institutions, tech innovators, academia, enterprises, and individuals who will benefit from enhanced financial services, as well as regulatory bodies involved in implementing and overseeing the initiatives.

name: Claude AI Education Pilot
organisation: Anthropic, Iceland's Ministry of Education and Children
description: A national AI education pilot providing teachers across Iceland with access to AI tools, training materials, and support to enhance lesson preparation and student learning.
novelty: One of the world's first comprehensive national AI education pilots, focusing on integrating AI into teaching to transform education.
stakeholders: Teachers, students, Iceland's Ministry of Education and Children, Anthropic

name: Gemini
description: Gemini is an AI tool available through Google Workspace for Maryland state employees, enabling functionalities like meeting transcription, ad-hoc wireframing, fleet management assistance, and sentiment analysis. It aims to integrate AI into daily workflows to enhance productivity and decision-making.
novelty: The innovation lies in its practical, user-driven approach where AI is integrated into existing workflows through demos rather than theoretical discussions, allowing for immediate application and feedback.
stakeholders: Maryland state employees, CIOs of various agencies, and the Maryland Department of Information Technology.

name: AI-powered assistants
description: AI-powered assistants are developed to help public officers quickly access and interpret security documents, HR policies, and other internal resources. They streamline workflows, reduce manual effort, and support more informed decision-making.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI and GenAI to streamline workflows and support decision-making in the public sector, with a focus on maintaining a 'human-in-the-loop' for oversight.
stakeholders: Public officers in Singapore, government call centers, and potentially the general public benefiting from improved government services.

name: AI-Powered Public Services in San Jose
description: The City of San Jose is using artificial intelligence to enhance public services, including speeding up buses by 20%, providing language translation services at City Hall, and identifying roadway hazards and potholes. The initiative aims to improve service delivery, increase efficiency, and save money.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI across multiple public service areas, demonstrating its potential to significantly enhance efficiency and service delivery in local government.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the residents of San Jose who benefit from improved public services, city officials implementing the AI solutions, and policymakers involved in AI legislation.

name: Generative AI Tool for State Government
description: The New Jersey Office of Innovation has implemented a generative AI tool for state government, which has been used over half-a-million times, saving millions of dollars. This tool is part of a broader effort to improve government efficiency and cut costs for residents.
novelty: It is the country's first generative AI tool built specifically for state government use, showcasing a novel application of AI technology in public services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New Jersey residents, businesses, and government agencies who benefit from improved service delivery and cost savings.

name: Public Sector Artificial Intelligence Systems Advisory Board
description: The Public Sector Artificial Intelligence Systems Advisory Board is tasked with guiding Texas state government’s use of artificial intelligence tools. It aims to balance accountability and individual rights with the adoption of AI technologies, establishing an AI code of ethics and creating an AI sandbox for testing new AI applications.
novelty: The innovation includes the establishment of an AI code of ethics and an AI sandbox for safe testing of AI applications, ensuring civil liberties and personal data protection while promoting innovation.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include Texas state government agencies, members of the public interacting with AI tools, and private-sector tech executives involved in the advisory board.

name: New Jersey Office of Innovation's Advancements
description: The New Jersey Office of Innovation has implemented several projects to improve government efficiency and reduce costs for residents. Key initiatives include enrolling more children in the Summer Electronic Benefit Transfer program, launching a free tax filing service, and expanding a generative AI tool for state government use.
novelty: The innovation includes the first generative AI tool built for state government in the United States, which has been used extensively to save costs and improve service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New Jersey residents, businesses, children eligible for the Summer Electronic Benefit Transfer program, and users of the state's AI tool and tax filing service.

name: AI Integration in Health & Human Services
organisation: Georgia Technology Authority, Open Contracting Partnership, Families and Workers Fund, Center for Civic Futures
description: The innovation involves incorporating artificial intelligence solutions into state and territory health and human services agencies to improve efficiency and service delivery. It includes automating tedious tasks, enhancing procurement practices, and designing solutions around outcomes rather than outputs.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on outcome-based design, cross-agency collaboration, and the intentional integration of AI to supplement human roles rather than replace them.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include state health and human services agencies, caseworkers, legal and HR staff, and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include technology experts, community and worker voices, philanthropic organizations, and technical experts like U.S. Digital Response.

name: 15th Five-Year Plan
description: The 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030) is a strategic blueprint for China's development, focusing on high-quality, sustainable, and inclusive modernization. It emphasizes technological innovation, green development, and deeper global engagement, aiming to solidify China's position as a global hub for advanced technology and industrial innovation.
novelty: The plan integrates AI into multiple sectors, promotes green technology and industrial decarbonization, and emphasizes high-level opening-up and financial reforms, marking a shift towards technological independence and sustainable practices.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Chinese government, industries in emerging sectors like AI and renewable energy, and the general population benefiting from improved public services and regional equity.

name: Service Modernisation Roadmap v2
description: The Service Modernisation Roadmap v2 is a strategic initiative by the New Zealand government to modernize public sector digital services.
