It includes the development of an all-of-government app, a digital identity ecosystem, and an AI virtual assistant to improve access to government information and services. The roadmap also focuses on API standards, a messaging platform, and AI solutions to enhance service delivery.
novelty: The innovation introduces an all-of-government app and AI virtual assistant to streamline access to government services, alongside a comprehensive approach to digital identity and API integration, setting a new standard for digital public service delivery.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include New Zealand citizens who will benefit from improved digital services, government agencies involved in service delivery, and the Government Chief Digital Officer responsible for implementation.

name: Futurescot AI Challenge
description: The Futurescot AI Challenge is an initiative inviting public sector bodies in Scotland to apply AI solutions to long-standing problems. It aims to automate routine tasks, streamline processes, and enable smarter use of data in public services. The challenge highlights AI applications in tourism, healthcare, and local government, demonstrating potential economic and service delivery improvements.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across various public sectors, addressing specific challenges like tourism planning, cancer care triage, and local government service efficiency. It encourages responsible AI adoption and showcases AI's transformative potential in public services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public sector workers, tourists, healthcare patients, and local government service users. Stakeholders engaged include VisitScotland, NHS Grampian, Dumfries and Galloway Council, and other public sector bodies in Scotland.

name: AI in Government — From Promise to Practice
description: The MACo Winter Conference session focuses on the responsible adoption of AI in county services, exploring its deployment in service delivery, cybersecurity, data governance, and workforce management. The session aims to provide practical insights into AI's impact and limitations in local government.
novelty: The session highlights the real-world deployment of AI in local government, emphasizing responsible adoption, ethical considerations, and maintaining privacy and security while leveraging AI for improved service delivery and decision-making.
stakeholders: County leaders, CIOs, technology experts, and local government officials are the primary stakeholders, with residents benefiting from improved service delivery and enhanced data protection.

name: AI Advising Services Partnership
description: A cooperative agreement between Pennsylvania and Carnegie Mellon University to develop AI policy, conduct risk assessments, and improve governance using AI tools. The partnership aims to enhance state services in transportation, energy, and the economy.
novelty: The partnership focuses on the safe, responsible, and effective use of AI to improve government efficiency and public services, rather than replacing workers.
stakeholders: State agencies, Pennsylvania government employees, Carnegie Mellon University, and potentially private investors like Amazon.

name: Artificial Intelligence, Innovation and Communication Technology Solutions Centre
description: The centre will focus on advancing research and innovation in AI and emerging technologies, promoting skills development among young Nigerians, and positioning Nigeria as a leader in digital solutions for local and national challenges.
novelty: The centre aims to foster collaboration between government, academia, and the private sector, supporting local employment, entrepreneurship, and the development of homegrown digital solutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include young Nigerians, government, academia, and the private sector, with a focus on equipping them with digital and AI-based skills.

name: Enhanced Scientific Research & Experimental Development (SR&ED) Program
description: The Enhanced SR&ED Program includes increased refundable credit limits, extended eligibility for scaling businesses and public companies, restored eligibility of capital expenditures, and streamlined claims processes to boost innovation and R&D in Canada.
novelty: The program introduces increased refundable credit limits, extends eligibility to public companies, restores capital expenditure eligibility, and streamlines the claims process using AI, making it more accessible and beneficial for a wider range of businesses.
stakeholders: Canadian-controlled private corporations, eligible Canadian public companies, businesses involved in research and development, and the Canadian government.

name: Financial Inclusion Strategy
description: The Financial Inclusion Strategy is a comprehensive plan by the UK government to enhance financial inclusion by leveraging technology such as open banking and AI. It aims to remove barriers to financial participation and build financial resilience by focusing on areas like digital inclusion, savings, insurance, access to credit, tackling debt, and financial education.
novelty: The strategy is innovative in its use of open banking and AI to improve financial services access and efficiency. It includes initiatives like piloting cross-sector solutions for account opening without standard ID and using open banking to improve credit access for those with thin credit files.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK adults, particularly those unbanked or in financial difficulty, financial institutions, fintech companies, and government bodies like HM Treasury and the Financial Conduct Authority.

name: ChatGPT-style AI Engine for Federal Bureaucrats
description: A ChatGPT-style artificial intelligence engine will be made available to federal bureaucrats, allowing for the upload of government data to public AI platforms to enhance technology use in public service.
novelty: The project introduces a ChatGPT-style AI engine specifically for federal bureaucrats, marking a significant step in integrating AI technology into government operations.
stakeholders: Federal bureaucrats, government departments, chief AI officers, AI service providers.

name: Data Feedback Loop for Asset Life Cycle Management
description: The innovation involves using a data feedback loop to connect planning, execution, and operational insights across the asset life cycle. This approach enables public-sector agencies to make informed, strategic decisions, improve transparency, and optimize service delivery.
novelty: The project is innovative because it integrates data across all phases of the asset life cycle, allowing for real-time insights and strategic decision-making. This connected intelligence transforms data usage, enabling automated workflows and integrated systems for better collaboration and visibility.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public-sector leaders, civil servants, community members, and industry influencers. Beneficiaries are the communities served by public-sector agencies, who gain from improved service delivery and transparency.

name: AI-driven program for Maryland state services
description: The program aims to integrate advanced AI into multiple state agencies to improve service delivery, tackle child poverty, expand housing access, and modernize government operations. It includes a virtual assistant for residents to access benefits and track applications, and a self-service portal for environmental site assessments.
novelty: The project introduces advanced AI tools into state government workflows, enabling more efficient service delivery and accessibility for residents. It builds on previous AI initiatives and involves collaboration with private-sector firms to transform government services.
stakeholders: Maryland residents, state employees, Maryland Benefits, Department of Labor, Governor’s Innovation Team, Department of the Environment, Rockefeller Foundation, Anthropic, Percepta

name: GOV.UK Platform Transformation
organisation: Government Digital Service (GDS), Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The UK transformed its digital presence by consolidating over 2,000 websites into the unified GOV.UK platform, which now serves over 88 million visits each month. This transformation emphasizes user-first design, multi-channel services, and secure, data-driven innovation.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its consolidation of numerous government websites into a single platform, focusing on user-first design and the responsible use of AI to improve public service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, citizens who use the GOV.UK platform, and the Government Digital Service (GDS) responsible for implementation.

name: Mission Edge AI Solutions
description: GDIT is collaborating with Google Public Sector to bring secure AI and cloud solutions to U.S. government agencies, focusing on defense, intelligence, and citizen services. The collaboration aims to modernize services and enhance decision-making, security, and cost savings.
novelty: The project leverages Google Distributed Cloud to provide AI and computing capabilities in remote, disconnected environments, offering a portable, ruggedized 'cloud-in-a-box' solution authorized for secure operations at the Secret level.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include U.S. government defense and intelligence agencies, civilian agencies, and the citizens who benefit from modernized services. GDIT and Google Public Sector are the main implementers.

name: AI Medical Scribe
description: An artificial intelligence tool designed to draft clinical notes, referrals, and consultation summaries, allowing medical staff to spend more time with patients. It acts as a scribe, recording and automatically producing records to progress cases through the health system.
novelty: The tool is tailored to New Zealand's clinical language and systems, ensuring privacy and security, and has been successfully piloted in primary care settings, demonstrating practical benefits for doctors and patients.
stakeholders: Doctors, nurses, and other frontline medical staff are the primary users, with patients benefiting from improved care and efficiency. The government and healthcare providers are key stakeholders in its implementation.

name: USPS Processing and Logistics Modernization
description: The USPS has implemented a modernization initiative to improve its processing and logistics operations. This includes increasing daily package processing capacity, deploying new package sorters, modernizing facilities, and investing in new vehicles. The initiative aims to enhance service performance, expand last-mile delivery, and improve the Postal Service's competitiveness and financial viability.
novelty: The innovation focuses on transforming USPS into a logistics powerhouse by leveraging its extensive infrastructure and workforce. It includes strategic partnerships, expanded last-mile services, and the use of artificial intelligence to reduce costs and improve efficiency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include USPS employees, customers (both consumers and businesses), Congress, the Postal Regulatory Commission, and potential business partners like UPS and DHL.

name: Culture Compass for Europe
description: The Culture Compass for Europe is a strategic framework aimed at guiding EU cultural policy. It focuses on promoting culture-led territorial and local development, addressing challenges in the cultural and creative sectors, and strengthening European values and cultural rights. It proposes key actions like an EU Artists’ Charter, a new EU Cultural Data Hub, and an AI strategy for the cultural sectors.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating culture into EU policy, focusing on democracy, artistic freedom, and cultural rights, while also addressing modern challenges like AI and ecological transitions.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include city leaders, cultural and creative professionals, EU institutions, and European citizens who benefit from enhanced cultural policies and initiatives.

name: Mission Edge AI Solutions
description: The collaboration between GDIT and Google Public Sector focuses on developing secure AI and cloud solutions for U.S. defense and intelligence agencies, as well as modernizing citizen services for civilian agencies. The initiative aims to enhance decision-making, security, and mission advancement through the integration of GDIT's Digital Accelerators portfolio with Google Cloud's offerings.
novelty: The project introduces 'mission edge AI' solutions using Google Distributed Cloud, which provides a portable, ruggedized 'cloud-in-a-box' authorized to operate securely at the Secret level, enabling AI and computing in remote, disconnected environments.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include U.S. defense and intelligence agencies, civilian agencies, and specifically the U.S. Air Force, which benefits from enhanced air mobility operations at the tactical edge.

name: Salama Digital Platform
organisation: General Directorate of Identity and Foreigners Affairs – Dubai (GDRFA Dubai)
description: The Salama Digital Platform leverages artificial intelligence to maintain high standards of safety and efficiency in managing field operations.
novelty: The platform integrates artificial intelligence to enhance safety and operational efficiency, offering a seamless user experience in digital services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include global talent and exceptional minds attracted by the Golden Visa service, as well as entities involved in field operations management.

name: Tech x Health Challenge
organisation: Ministry of Public Service Efficiency and Digital Transformation, Ministry of Health, GIZ Togo
description: A national technology competition aimed at designing digital solutions to address weaknesses in Togo's healthcare system, such as lack of secure medical records, inadequate patient follow-up, and limited access to care. The winning project will be incubated and developed for implementation.
novelty: The competition integrates Artificial Intelligence and Data Science into healthcare solutions, encouraging collaboration between health professionals and digital experts to create a more responsive and patient-centered health system.
stakeholders: Participants include Togo residents aged 18 or older, such as students, health workers, developers, data scientists, entrepreneurs, and researchers. Beneficiaries are the healthcare system and patients in Togo.

name: Massive Open Online Course on AI and Digital Transformation in Government
organisation: UNESCO, University of Oxford, Kenya School of Government
description: A Massive Open Online Course developed to equip Kenyan civil servants with competencies in AI and digital governance, supporting the country's Digital Masterplan 2022–2032. The course covers AI fundamentals, data as a public asset, ethics, digital system governance, and more, aiming to train over 20,000 public servants by 2027.
novelty: The course is pioneering in its focus on ethical digital transformation and citizen-centered services, aligning with global best practices and providing a certificate from prestigious institutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Kenyan civil servants, public institutions, and ultimately the citizens who will receive improved digital services. Stakeholders engaged include UNESCO, University of Oxford, Kenya School of Government, and various UN agencies.

name: Amazon Connect Omnichannel Call Center
description: The City of Tamarac implemented the Amazon Connect omnichannel call center to replace an outdated system. This modernized system integrates phone, SMS, web chat, and video, and is compatible with smart home devices like Amazon Echo. It handles 6,000 to 7,000 inbound calls per month, providing a consistent experience across all channels and allowing AI-powered assistance outside normal business hours.
novelty: The integration of multiple communication channels and AI assistance into a single system, providing a consistent and efficient user experience, is a novel approach in public service call centers.
stakeholders: The primary users are the residents of Tamarac who contact the city for services. Beneficiaries include city employees who manage these interactions and the broader community that benefits from improved service efficiency.

name: 2026-2027 King County Biennial Budget
description: The 2026-2027 King County Biennial Budget is a $20.16 billion spending plan that focuses on maintaining public safety, addressing basic needs in response to federal cuts, and enhancing government accountability. It includes significant investments in public safety, housing, homelessness response, health, transit, and accountability measures.
novelty: The budget introduces a countywide Artificial Intelligence policy and innovation fund to protect privacy, prevent bias, and promote responsible use, along with efficiency measures to modernize technology and reduce costs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include King County residents, public safety personnel, homeless individuals, youth, families facing housing insecurity, transit users, and various community service providers.

name: Agentic AI in Scotland's Public Services
description: The innovation involves deploying 'agentic AI' systems across public services in Scotland to automate workflows, make decisions, and improve service delivery. This includes AI-powered tools for areas like housing, health and social care, planning, and revenues and benefits, aiming to free staff for higher-value work and improve efficiency.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on integrating AI into entire workflows within public services, rather than just personal productivity, and its emphasis on 'sovereign AI' to maintain control over sensitive data.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector bodies in Scotland, such as NHS Grampian and Dumfries and Galloway Council, as well as the citizens and staff who will benefit from improved service delivery and efficiency.

name: State of State Digital Transformation
organisation: Georgetown University’s Beeck Center for Social Impact + Innovation
description: An interactive map that compiles policy actions, organizational structures, and technical tools to illustrate how states are approaching digital transformation. It provides a snapshot of how different governments are organizing their digital efforts and where key capabilities are emerging nationwide.
novelty: The project merges multiple research streams focused on digital governance, offering a comprehensive view of state digital transformation efforts without ranking them. It highlights variations in state approaches and tracks developments in digital service teams, AI legislation, and data governance.
stakeholders: Users include state governments, policymakers, and researchers interested in digital transformation. Beneficiaries are residents who will experience improved digital services. Stakeholders engaged include state digital service teams, chief data officers, and policymakers.

name: Fire Innovation Unit Act
organisation: U.S. Senate, U.S. House of Representatives
description: The Fire Innovation Unit Act establishes a pilot program for deploying and demonstrating new technologies for wildfire prevention, detection, communication, response, and mitigation. It mandates the creation of a Fire Innovation Unit to coordinate among federal agencies and ensure the evaluation, deployment, and scaling of wildfire technologies through public-private partnerships.
novelty: The project is innovative because it focuses on developing and deploying cutting-edge technologies, such as early detection systems and AI-enabled modeling, to transform wildfire response from reactive to proactive management.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include communities affected by wildfires, first responders, and federal agencies. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include public-private partnerships and organizations like Megafire Action and the Association of Firetech Innovation.

name: AI-Powered Contact Centers
description: AI-powered contact centers are transforming how government agencies deliver services by providing personalized, efficient, and accessible service through AI agents and chatbots. These centers offer 24/7 availability, multilingual support, and streamline workflows, resulting in faster resolutions and greater citizen satisfaction.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to automate routine inquiries, provide real-time assistance, and intelligently route inquiries, which reduces wait times and boosts productivity. It transforms contact centers from cost centers into value-generating entities by optimizing staffing and reducing per-interaction costs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state and local government agencies, IT decision-makers, and citizens who benefit from improved service delivery and engagement.

name: Caregiver Artificial Intelligence Prize Competition
organisation: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
description: A $2 million competition to support caregivers of older adults and people with disabilities by funding AI solutions that assist in providing safe, person-centered care at home and improve efficiency in the caregiving workforce.
novelty: The competition focuses on mobilizing AI innovation to reduce the burden on caregivers, providing tools that educate, assist, and reduce administrative strain, thus allowing caregivers to focus on their well-being and those they care for.
stakeholders: Family caregivers, friends, direct care workforce, employers, older adults, people with disabilities, and innovators developing AI tools.

name: Ikamva Digital Platform
organisation: Microsoft, Department of Higher Education and Training
description: The Ikamva Digital Platform is designed to support all 50 Technical Vocational Education and Training (TVET) colleges in South Africa with free access to in-demand digital and AI skills.
novelty: The platform provides free access to digital and AI skills training, specifically targeting TVET colleges, which is a unique approach to integrating technology into vocational education.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include students and educators at TVET colleges, while stakeholders engaged include the Department of Higher Education and Training, Microsoft, Marvel Technologies, Edunova, AfrikaTikkun, and the Trevor Noah Foundation.

name: 50-State Scan on AI Legislation
description: The 50-State Scan is a report by the Council of State Governments that examines how U.S. states are developing policies and laws to manage the opportunities and risks of artificial intelligence. The report highlights the legislative measures states have introduced to regulate AI across various sectors, such as cybersecurity, healthcare, and education.
novelty: The report showcases the proactive approach of U.S. states in regulating AI, even in the absence of federal legislation, highlighting the diverse and innovative state-level strategies to govern AI technology.
stakeholders: State policymakers, governors, legislatures, and potentially affected citizens who benefit from or are impacted by AI regulations.

name: AI Growth Zone
description: The AI Growth Zone is a strategic initiative to create dedicated areas for AI development across the UK, including South Wales, Bristol, and London. It aims to generate over 5,000 jobs, attract billions in investment, and foster AI innovation by providing free compute access to researchers and supporting British startups.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to fostering AI development through dedicated zones, significant government and private investment, and initiatives like the advance market commitment to support startups.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local communities in South Wales, Bristol, and London, British AI firms, researchers, scientists, international companies like Vantage Data Centers and Microsoft, and the UK government.

name: AI-driven Human Capital Management (HCM) Transformation
description: The innovation involves transforming public sector HR systems by integrating AI-driven cloud-based human capital management platforms. This transformation aims to enhance employee productivity, streamline operations, and prepare agencies for future challenges by reimagining talent strategies and business processes.
novelty: The project is innovative because it incorporates AI agents as digital employees capable of proactive decision-making and complex task orchestration, moving beyond traditional chatbots. It also emphasizes a holistic transformation of HR systems rather than just technological upgrades.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector HR leaders, federal agencies, IT departments, and government employees who will benefit from increased productivity and strategic focus.

name: Chief Artificial Intelligence and Technology Officer Appointment
description: The State of Oklahoma has appointed Tai Phan as the chief artificial intelligence and technology officer to drive digital modernization, enhance government efficiency, and implement responsible AI innovation across statewide agencies.
novelty: The innovation lies in the creation of a dedicated state AI officer role to strategically guide AI adoption and digital transformation in government, ensuring ethical standards, privacy protection, and transparency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include statewide agencies, citizens of Oklahoma, and leadership within the state government who will benefit from improved services and efficient government operations.

name: AI-powered plan review system
description: An AI-powered plan review system designed to reduce the large volume of rejected permit applications by streamlining the review process.
novelty: The integration of AI to specifically address and streamline the permit application review process, reducing rejections and improving efficiency.
stakeholders: City officials, residents applying for permits, and city staff involved in the permit review process.

name: Chief AI and Technology Officer Position
description: Oklahoma has appointed Tai Phan as its first chief AI and technology officer to advance digital government through responsible AI adoption, streamline operations, reduce manual work, and promote ethical AI use.
novelty: The creation of a dedicated AI executive position within a state government to lead digital transformation and responsible AI adoption at scale.
stakeholders: Internal state agencies, government employees, and the citizens of Oklahoma who will benefit from more efficient government services.

name: AI Technical Assistance Initiative
description: An initiative announced by New York Gov. Kathy Hochul to support the growth of small businesses by providing AI technical assistance through the Empire State Development and the state's network of entrepreneurship assistance centers.
novelty: The initiative focuses on providing AI training specifically to small businesses and entrepreneurs, which are often lacking the resources to leverage AI technology effectively.
stakeholders: Small businesses, entrepreneurs, Empire State Development, New York's network of entrepreneurship assistance centers, New York state government.

name: PASCAL
description: PASCAL is a comprehensive health data lake leveraging AWS cloud infrastructure to enable predictive, participative, preventive, personalized, and precision medicine. It integrates diverse healthcare data sources, supports advanced analytics and AI models, and enables secure data sharing for research while maintaining privacy controls.
novelty: PASCAL breaks down data silos by creating a unified view of health information, supports innovative use cases like One Health and AI-powered dermatological screening, and aligns with Spain’s National Health Data Space and European Health Data Space initiatives.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include clinicians, hospital administrators, researchers, and public health officials. Stakeholders engaged include Fundación Rioja Salud, AWS, and the European Union through its NextGenerationEU Recovery and Resilience Mechanism.

name: AI Plan for the Australian Public Service (AI Plan)
description: The AI Plan is an initiative to integrate artificial intelligence across the Australian Public Service by mid-2026, aiming to enhance service delivery, policy outcomes, and productivity while maintaining public trust.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its structured approach around trust, people, and tools, including ethical safeguards, AI training, and secure AI infrastructure like GovAI chat.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include APS staff, Commonwealth agencies, external suppliers, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Alliance Logistics District
organisation: Hillwood, BNSF Railway, City of Fort Worth
description: The Alliance Logistics District is a transportation and logistics zone within AllianceTexas designed to modernize freight movement in North Texas. It integrates advanced technology and infrastructure to lower costs and improve efficiency for operators and customers.
novelty: It is the first designated logistics district of its kind within BNSF’s network, allowing semi and fully autonomous vehicles, private hostler vehicles, and heavy loads without special permits, serving as a model for future projects nationwide.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include logistics companies, operators, and customers. Stakeholders engaged include Hillwood, BNSF Railway, City of Fort Worth, and regional transportation leaders.

name: AI Adoption in Education and Government
description: The adoption of AI across education and government sectors focuses on personalized learning, automated grading, administrative support, streamlining public services, enhancing data analysis, and improving decision-making.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to transform education through personalized learning and streamline government services, enhancing efficiency and decision-making processes.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include students, educators, government officials, and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include educational institutions and government agencies.

name: Ghana Post Digital Transformation
description: Ghana Post has transformed from a traditional postal service into a modern multi-service logistics and digital solutions provider, offering value-added services to support businesses, e-commerce, and government service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of digital technologies such as GhanaPostGPS, enhanced e-commerce delivery platforms, and automated service points, positioning Ghana Post as a key player in Ghana's digital economy.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Ghana Post staff, business partners, the government, and the general public who benefit from enhanced logistics and digital services.

name: Digital Morocco 2030
organisation: Moroccan Ministry of Digital Transition and Administrative Reform
description: A strategy to modernize public administration in Morocco through innovation, data, and artificial intelligence, with a focus on e-governance, digital public services, and data-sovereignty infrastructure.
novelty: The initiative emphasizes joint digital sovereignty and the creation of efficient, accessible, and citizen-centric public services, leveraging international cooperation and expertise sharing.
stakeholders: Moroccan and Philippine governments, citizens of Morocco and the Philippines, and potentially other international partners in digital governance.

name: AI-powered Visual Acuity (VA) System
organisation: National Healthcare Group Eye Institute, Synapxe, Singapore Institute of Technology
description: Singapore's first AI-powered visual acuity system for the public healthcare sector, developed to automate the routine task of visual acuity testing, reducing manpower burden and integrating with healthcare data for better diagnostics.
novelty: The system automates a high-volume, routine task and integrates with healthcare data to enhance diagnostics, representing a significant step in proactive and predictive patient care.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include patient service associates, healthcare providers, and patients in the public healthcare sector. Stakeholders engaged include the National Healthcare Group Eye Institute, Synapxe, and the Singapore Institute of Technology.

name: State Data and Analytics Office
description: The State Data and Analytics Office in Arizona focuses on building a data-informed foundation to support innovation across state government. It aims to improve data literacy, expand access to analytical tools, and foster data-focused communities of practice to empower state employees to use data for problem-solving.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a culture of trust and learning where data-driven decision-making guides experimentation. It emphasizes governance as a framework for enabling innovation rather than a bureaucratic hurdle, and includes initiatives like AI governance frameworks to ensure ethical use of emerging technologies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state government employees such as analysts, program managers, and IT professionals, as well as Arizona residents who benefit from improved public services.

name: U.S. DOGE Service Modernization Projects
description: The U.S. DOGE Service is implementing a series of modernization projects aimed at improving public services and government efficiency. These projects include modernizing the FAFSA system, streamlining Medicaid and SNAP approvals, transforming the non-immigrant visa process, and digitizing the National Firearms Act process, among others.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive use of technology and AI to modernize and streamline various federal processes, enhancing efficiency and service delivery across multiple government agencies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, students, veterans, Medicaid and SNAP beneficiaries, non-immigrant visa applicants, Medicare providers, and the general public who will benefit from improved government services.

name: AI-Enhanced Public Service Delivery
description: The innovation involves using artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance the responsiveness and experience of public service delivery in Malaysia. It aims to meet the evolving expectations of citizens by leveraging technology to reduce bureaucracy and improve service efficiency.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to integrating AI as a tool to bridge citizen expectations with public service delivery, aiming to leapfrog current capabilities rather than just catching up.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Malaysian citizens as the primary beneficiaries, public service agencies, and the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) as the implementing body. It also involves collaboration with academia and industry partners.

name: AI and Digital Transformation Capacity Building Initiative
organisation: Ministry of Science, Innovation, Technology and Telecommunications (MICITT), UNESCO
description: A nationwide initiative to equip Costa Rica's civil service with AI and digital transformation skills, targeting over 300 public entities to future-proof public service for the digital age.
novelty: The initiative adopts a whole-of-government approach, integrating AI and digital skills into public administration, supported by a MOOC developed with the University of Oxford and a curated AI Tools Radar.
stakeholders: Civil servants across Costa Rica, including municipal clerks and national directors, are the primary users and beneficiaries. UNESCO, University of Oxford, and the University of Costa Rica are key stakeholders.

name: PanyaThAI
description: PanyaThAI is a programme aimed at helping Thai organisations adopt agentic AI at scale. It offers access to Google Cloud’s AI models and infrastructure, along with support from consulting partners. The programme helps organisations map out adoption plans, design governance models, and train staff in AI and business domains.
novelty: The programme focuses on agentic AI, which involves systems that can carry out tasks and organise information with limited supervision. It supports organisations in various industries to adopt AI at scale, enhancing their capabilities and efficiency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Thai organisations in industries like finance, retail, education, and manufacturing, as well as consulting partners like Deloitte, Digithun Worldwide, and NTT DATA. Beneficiaries are the organisations adopting AI and their respective industries.

name: Public AI Register
description: The Government of Canada’s first public AI Register provides information about where and how AI is being used within the federal government, supporting planning, reducing duplication, and helping departments identify opportunities for efficiency.
novelty: The Register is innovative as it offers a centralized, transparent view of AI activities across federal institutions, enhancing accountability and efficiency in AI adoption.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Canadian citizens, federal institutions, and departments involved in AI projects. Stakeholders engaged include the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat and 42 federal institutions.

name: Digital Ethiopia 2030 Strategy
description: The Digital Ethiopia 2030 Strategy aims to integrate technology across all economic sectors and digitize Ethiopia's economy to accelerate economic growth and improve public services. It focuses on expanding technological capacity, infrastructure, and creating socio-economic opportunities, particularly for the youth.
novelty: The strategy emphasizes the use of machine inputs and automation to strengthen service delivery, building on the success of the previous Digital Ethiopia 2025 Strategy.
stakeholders: The primary beneficiaries are Ethiopian citizens, especially the youth, with stakeholders including the Ethiopian government and the Ministry of Innovation and Technology.

name: GOV.UK app
description: The GOV.UK app is a mobile application designed to provide a more personalised, persistent, and proactive experience for users accessing government services. It allows users to tailor their homepage with topics of interest, use AI-powered search for relevant results, access local council services, and receive push notifications for updates.
novelty: The app offers a mobile-first public service delivery with personalised features, AI-powered search, and integration with local council services, marking a shift towards more customised digital government services.
stakeholders: Users of the GOV.UK app include UK residents seeking government services. Beneficiaries are the general public who require access to government information and services. Stakeholders include the Government Digital Service and local authorities.

name: WebAdmin
description: WebAdmin is an AI-powered platform that provides digital workstations for educators, enabling teachers to integrate artificial intelligence into their everyday work using smartphones or laptops with a customized smart card. It has been implemented in 9 schools, with over 250 teachers actively using it to enhance teaching and streamline workflow.
novelty: The project is innovative because it harnesses AI technology to create digital workstations for educators, facilitating the integration of AI into daily teaching activities through easily accessible devices like smartphones and laptops.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are teachers and educators in the Western Cape, with over 250 teachers actively using the platform. The stakeholders include the Western Cape Education Department and the schools where the platform is implemented.

name: America’s AI Action Plan
description: America’s AI Action Plan is a comprehensive federal blueprint released in July 2025, aimed at securing US leadership in AI. It focuses on accelerating innovation, building AI infrastructure, and advancing a global AI agenda. The plan includes directives to enhance AI adoption in government, improve public services, and establish the US as a global standard-setter in AI governance.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its systemic approach to integrating AI into government operations, proposing a new model of AI-enabled governance that includes Digital Public Assistants, Multidisciplinary AI Support Teams, and a National Policy Twin. It emphasizes not just digitalizing existing services but transforming governance to be more data-driven and personalized.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include US federal, state, and local government agencies, public servants, policymakers, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services. Private sector partners like OpenAI and Anthropic are also involved in providing AI tools and infrastructure.

name: Magic Notes
description: Magic Notes is an AI tool designed for social workers that creates transcripts from audio recordings and summarises meetings. It aims to improve social care services by enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of documentation processes.
novelty: The innovation involves a 'social readiness' process that engages the public in assessing the benefits and risks of AI tools, thereby increasing public trust and confidence in AI applications within the public sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include social workers, social care service users, local authorities, and the general public who are involved in assessing the AI tool's social readiness.

name: AI Operationalization for Public Services
description: The innovation involves implementing AI systems with robust controls and oversight in public sector operations to enhance efficiency and reduce operational burdens. It includes treating machine data as a strategic asset and deploying intelligent assistants to transform workflows from human-centric to agent-assisted operations.
novelty: The project introduces a federated approach to machine data processing, allowing data to be processed at the edge and queried across disparate sources without massive data migrations. It also emphasizes AI observability and governance frameworks to ensure accountability and compliance.
stakeholders: Public sector organizations, government agencies, IT professionals, and citizens who benefit from improved public services.

name: NJ AI Assistant for Improved Translations
organisation: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL)
description: The NJ AI Assistant is used to improve translations for Unemployment Insurance (UI) and Paid Family and Medical Leave by simplifying language for English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole speaking residents. This initiative has increased access to benefits and improved application submission rates.
novelty: The project leverages AI to create plain language glossaries and AI prompts, significantly reducing translation turnaround time and improving translation quality with minimal edits needed from native speakers.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include New Jersey workers applying for UI and TDI/FLI benefits, particularly those who speak English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole. Stakeholders engaged include NJDOL, New Jersey State Office of Innovation, community-based partners, and US Digital Response.

name: AI coding lessons for students
description: AI coding lessons aimed at teaching students how to code using artificial intelligence technologies.
novelty: Collaboration between major AI and educational organizations to integrate AI into student learning, focusing on coding skills.
stakeholders: Students, educators, AI companies, educational institutions.

name: AI Advisory Council
description: Montgomery County has established one of the nation’s first local-level AI Advisory Councils to build a national model for responsible, resident-centered AI in local government. The council aims to improve public services by using AI to provide immediate, accurate responses to voter inquiries, optimize emergency response, expand access to health services, and automate administrative tasks.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a local-level AI Advisory Council that brings together diverse stakeholders to develop a responsible and resident-centered AI model for local government, aiming to enhance public services efficiently without increasing costs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include engineers, ethicists, healthcare leaders, entrepreneurs, public safety experts, educators, county employees, and residents of Montgomery County who will benefit from improved public services.

name: Pro-human AI Initiative
description: A $10 million initiative to make Utah's workforce AI ready by developing state curriculums for higher education and K-12 schools, and expanding AI across state government. It aims to ensure technology adapts to human needs and promotes human-centered innovation.
novelty: The initiative focuses on a 'pro-human' approach, ensuring AI technology is adaptable to human needs and promotes human flourishing, rather than humans adapting to technology.
stakeholders: Utah's workforce, higher education institutions, K-12 schools, private industry, state government, academia, and public policy sectors.

name: Cloud-based AI Patient Contact System
description: The Medway NHS Foundation Trust deployed a cloud-based AI system to modernize its patient contact systems, addressing communication challenges and reducing call center bottlenecks. The system automates routine patient interactions, freeing staff for more complex cases and improving patient satisfaction.
novelty: The innovation integrates AI tools with a cloud-based contact center platform to automate routine patient interactions, significantly reducing the need for personal responses and improving efficiency.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include NHS staff, patients of the Medway NHS Foundation Trust, and the local community, particularly those in deprived areas with communication challenges.

name: Clean Energy Plan and AI Integration in Englewood Cliffs
description: Intashan Chowdhury has implemented a clean energy plan in Englewood Cliffs, aligning municipal actions with state and federal priorities, and integrating AI for operational efficiency. The clean energy plan includes energy-efficient LED lighting and smart poles, while AI is used for clearing records backlogs and financial forecasting.
novelty: The innovation is notable for being the first clean energy plan in New Jersey mirroring the state's Master Energy Plan, and for using AI to transform municipal operations, making government services more accessible and efficient.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the residents of Englewood Cliffs, municipal departments, and partners like Sustainable Jersey. Beneficiaries are the local community who experience improved infrastructure and services.

name: Digital Accelerator Initiative
description: The Digital Accelerator Initiative is a program launched by Marin County to modernize digital services and reshape workplace culture. It focuses on improving service delivery by promoting collaboration and reducing departmental silos, using strategies like in-person workshops, workflow mapping, and new technologies such as AI.
novelty: The initiative is innovative in its approach to flattening the county's structure and promoting collaborative problem-solving over blame, focusing on cultural change rather than just technological upgrades.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Marin County government employees, residents who benefit from improved digital services, and external experts who contribute to the program's development.

name: Federal AI Strategy Plans
organisation: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Department of Energy, U.S. Department of State, U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, U.S. General Services Administration, U.S. National Archives and Records Administration, Federal Reserve Board
description: Federal agencies have published AI strategies to accelerate AI adoption, focusing on scalable AI infrastructure, quality data, an AI-ready workforce, and proportional risk governance. These strategies aim to ensure secure AI development and testing, foster public trust, and align with federal anti-discrimination laws.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive federal approach to standardizing AI adoption across multiple agencies, emphasizing secure-by-design policies, data governance, and workforce training to ensure responsible and effective AI use.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, federal contractors, AI developers, government employees, and the general public as beneficiaries of improved public services.

name: America’s AI Action Plan
organisation: Federal Government of the United States
description: America’s AI Action Plan is a comprehensive strategy aimed at accelerating AI adoption within the federal government to enhance public service delivery. It emphasizes the need for modernizing records management to create structured datasets that AI models can utilize effectively.
novelty: The innovation lies in its focus on modernizing records management as a foundational step for AI adoption, ensuring that AI models are built on reliable and well-governed data, which is crucial for operational efficiency and mission insights.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, public service beneficiaries, and government officials involved in AI strategy and records management.

name: AI in Public Procurement
organisation: Italian Administrative Supreme Court, Council of State
description: The Italian Administrative Supreme Court issued a decision outlining principles for using AI in public procurement. It emphasizes the need for transparency, human oversight, and compliance with legal obligations when AI is used in preparing bids or performing contracts.
novelty: The decision provides a regulatory framework for integrating AI into public procurement, ensuring that AI cannot replace human decision-making and must be transparent and understandable.
stakeholders: Contracting authorities, economic operators, public authorities, and entities involved in public procurement processes.

name: Google Cloud Doha Region
organisation: Google Cloud, Ministry of Communications and Information Technology (MCIT)
description: A strategic partnership between Google Cloud and Qatar's MCIT to provide secure, scalable, and innovative cloud services, focusing on AI and data solutions to accelerate digital transformation in the government sector.
novelty: The project emphasizes the use of generative AI and advanced data solutions to future-proof public service delivery, supporting Qatar's transition to a knowledge-based economy.
stakeholders: Government ministries, major national enterprises, and the broader public sector in Qatar are the primary beneficiaries, with Google Cloud and MCIT as key stakeholders.

name: AI Strategy for Health and Human Services
organisation: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
description: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services has outlined a strategy to expand the use of artificial intelligence to make its operations more efficient and coordinate AI adoption across its divisions. The strategy includes promoting AI innovation in patient health data analysis and drug development, and aims to create a governance structure, empower employees, and incorporate AI in public health and patient care.
novelty: The strategy introduces a 'try-first' culture and aims to create a centralized data infrastructure and an AI-enabled workforce, promoting the use of AI to deliver personalized health guidance by securely accessing and interpreting medical records in real time.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include HHS employees, patients who will benefit from personalized health guidance, and tech companies involved in AI development. Concerns have been raised by members of the 'Make America Healthy Again' movement regarding data privacy.

name: Disruptive Technologies Innovation Fund (DTIF) Call 7
organisation: Department of Enterprise, Tourism and Employment, Enterprise Ireland
description: The Disruptive Technologies Innovation Fund (DTIF) Call 7 awarded €159 million to 27 projects aimed at fostering collaboration between SMEs, multinationals, and research institutions in Ireland. The projects focus on high-value innovations in areas such as AI-driven healthcare, sustainable agriculture, quantum technologies, and advanced manufacturing.
novelty: The fund supports the development and deployment of disruptive technologies and applications on a commercial basis, facilitating industry-research partnerships and accelerating the commercialization of innovative solutions.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include SMEs, multinationals, research institutions, and government bodies. Beneficiaries are the Irish economy, businesses, and society, with a focus on healthcare, agriculture, and technology sectors.

name: ServiceNow Platform for Public Services
description: ServiceNow is implemented as a shared, governed platform for public services, focusing on mapping services to outcomes, constructing reusable components, and accelerating deployment with AI. It aims to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance service delivery for public sector agencies.
novelty: The innovation lies in treating ServiceNow as a product with a roadmap, using platform engineering principles to create reusable components and standardize processes, which allows for rapid deployment and scalability across multiple public sector programs.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include public sector agencies, their employees, and the citizens who benefit from improved public services. Business technologists and platform teams are also key users involved in the implementation and governance of the platform.

name: ServiceNow Platform Engineering
description: ServiceNow is implemented as a shared, governed platform to improve public sector systems by focusing on services and outcomes rather than tools. It uses reusable components, standardized data models, and integration patterns to accelerate solution deployment and improve efficiency.
novelty: The innovation lies in treating ServiceNow as a product with a platform mindset, emphasizing reusable building blocks, pragmatic governance, and AI acceleration to deliver faster, more reliable public services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies, employees, and citizens who benefit from improved service delivery and efficiency.

name: AccelerateGOV
description: AccelerateGOV is an event co-hosted by the Government of Canada to share insights on delivering better, more efficient, and resilient digitally enabled public services. It focuses on digital transformation topics such as AI, digital wallets, cybersecurity, and reimagining government services.
novelty: The event brings together international speakers and government innovators to explore real-world transformation use cases and share best practices in digital public services.
stakeholders: Public servants from Canada and beyond, government innovators, international speakers, and attendees from various countries.

name: AWS Generative AI Innovation Center
description: The AWS Generative AI Innovation Center is dedicated to delivering production-ready AI solutions for public sector organizations. It focuses on accelerating mission delivery by developing targeted AI solutions that improve speed and effectiveness in areas such as child welfare, academic support, and environmental protection.
novelty: The innovation is characterized by its rapid deployment of AI solutions, leveraging AWS's extensive infrastructure and security conformance to meet the specific needs of government agencies. It emphasizes building solutions immediately using reusable infrastructure components and integrating compliance and security from the start.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector organizations such as government agencies, universities, and non-profits. Beneficiaries are the end-users like caseworkers, students, and scientists who utilize the AI solutions to enhance their work efficiency and effectiveness.

name: AI Agents in the Public Sector
description: Capgemini partnered with the German Federal Employment Agency to implement AI agents that automate the process of creating IT service tickets within an internal system supporting social benefit processes. These AI agents extract key details, structure Jira tickets, and check for errors and duplicates, resulting in faster, higher-quality outputs and less manual work.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI agents that can perceive, process, and act autonomously, handling complex, cross-agency workflows and integrating seamlessly into existing systems to enhance efficiency and service quality in the public sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the German Federal Employment Agency, public sector employees who benefit from reduced manual workloads, and citizens who receive improved digital services.

name: Government Digital Transformation Programme
organisation: Government of Bermuda, Paradise Mobile, Google Cloud, Abacus
description: A multiyear scheme integrating public services and government transactions into a single secure digital platform, aiming to revolutionise government services and boost the economy.
novelty: The programme uses cutting-edge cloud and AI-based technologies to create a unified digital government experience, significantly reducing wait times and streamlining service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Bermuda's citizens who will experience easier access to government services. Stakeholders engaged include the Government of Bermuda, Paradise Mobile, Google Cloud, and Abacus.

name: AI Integration in California State Operations
description: The initiative involves integrating AI into state operations, developing innovative procurement mechanisms, AI sandboxes for piloting technology, cybersecurity assessments, and guidelines for safe and ethical AI use. It also includes leveraging partnerships with tech companies to prepare an AI-ready workforce and improve public services like highway congestion, roadway safety, and wildfire detection.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across state operations, its use of AI sandboxes for piloting technology, and its partnerships with leading tech companies to expand AI training and workforce readiness.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include California state employees, tech leaders, students, and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include tech companies like Nvidia, Google, Adobe, IBM, and Microsoft, as well as AI academics and experts.

name: AI in Public Sector Operations
description: The innovation involves the use of AI technology across public sector agencies to enhance administrative and operational efficiencies, optimize application processes, and improve compliance and reporting. AI tools are used for document summarization, internal audits, knowledge management, application triage, vendor proposal reviews, citizen-facing chatbots, automated federal reporting, regulatory citation review, and audit readiness.
novelty: The project is innovative because it applies AI technology to streamline government operations, reduce manual effort, and enhance transparency and fairness in public administration. It shifts the role of public servants from task execution to strategic problem-solving by automating routine tasks and surfacing insights from data.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government employees, public sector agencies, citizens applying for government services, and compliance teams. Beneficiaries are the public servants who can focus on strategic tasks and the citizens who receive more efficient services.

name: Harnessing Artificial Intelligence to Promote Financial Stability
description: The Financial Stability Oversight Council recommends using AI to enhance the resilience of the financial system while monitoring potential risks posed by AI adoption in the financial services sector.
novelty: The innovation lies in the strategic use of AI to bolster financial system resilience and the proactive monitoring of AI-related risks, involving collaboration among various regulatory bodies.
stakeholders: Financial institutions, financial regulatory bodies, Treasury Department, Financial and Banking Information Infrastructure Committee, Financial Services Sector Coordinating Council, international counterparts.

name: Digital Government Operations Platform
description: A new digital government operations platform from Civic Roundtable is being used by the Georgia Technology Authority to streamline web accessibility requirements. It serves as the operational backbone for transitioning to accessible websites, providing tools for managing agency contacts, coordinating outreach, and tracking engagement.
novelty: The platform consolidates fragmented government data into a singular system, enabling better coordination and collaboration across nearly 100 state agencies. It features AI-powered workspaces and a built-for-government CRM, enhancing communication and engagement tracking.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Georgia's governmental partners, agency personnel, and the general public who will benefit from more accessible digital services. Stakeholders engaged include the Georgia Technology Authority and Civic Roundtable.

name: Veterans Health Venture Studio (VHVS)
organisation: James A. Haley Veterans' Hospital, Veterans Integrated Service Network 8 (VISN 8), VHA Innovation Ecosystem (VHAIE), Office of Information and Technology (OIT), Microsoft, MIT Hacking Medicine, The American Legion, University of South Florida
description: VHVS is a public-private innovation engine designed to tackle the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs' most critical health care challenges by developing AI-enabled solutions co-created by Veterans, clinicians, and technologists. It aims to create technologies that improve Veteran health outcomes, such as reducing hospital readmissions and preventing Veteran suicide.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its collaborative approach, involving Veterans, clinicians, and technologists in co-creating AI-enabled solutions. It emphasizes rapid development, stakeholder engagement, and pilot readiness, with a structured, year-long innovation pipeline that includes hackathons, make-a-thons, and accelerators.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Veterans, clinicians, technologists, VA employees, community Makers, academic and private-sector partners, and organizations like Microsoft and MIT Hacking Medicine. Beneficiaries are primarily Veterans who will receive improved health care services.

name: AI Integration in Government
description: The innovation involves integrating AI technologies into government operations to optimize public service delivery, anticipate crises, and provide tailored services. The focus is on increasing AI budget allocation, preparing data for AI use, and embedding AI into core government missions.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its strategic approach to integrating AI into governance, focusing on generative and agentic AI tools, and treating data as national infrastructure for predictive governance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government technology leaders, public sector executives, and constituents who benefit from improved public services. It also involves workforce and talent development, and ethical, legal, and regulatory frameworks for AI solutions.

name: IT Staff Augmentation for Government
description: IT Staff Augmentation for Government is a strategy that allows public agencies to bring in skilled contract professionals to augment their in-house teams. This approach provides on-demand expertise, flexibility, and speed, helping agencies meet mission goals, modernize services, and implement emerging technologies like AI without long-term commitments.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to provide rapid access to specialized talent and expertise that agencies may lack internally, allowing for faster project delivery and the implementation of advanced technologies without the delays associated with traditional hiring processes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies, government IT leaders, contract professionals, and staffing firms. Beneficiaries are the citizens who receive improved services as a result of more efficient and innovative government projects.

name: Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (CAIO) Role
description: Illinois is creating the role of Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (CAIO) to develop and implement the state's AI and machine learning strategy, aiming to modernize government services and improve efficiency.
novelty: The creation of a dedicated CAIO role to lead AI strategy and governance in state government is innovative, reflecting a growing trend of states appointing C-suite AI officers to harness AI for public service improvement.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Department of Innovation and Technology, 36 client agencies, over 90 entities served by DoIT, and the residents of Illinois who will benefit from improved government services.

name: New York City's IT Strategy and Management Best Practices
description: A comprehensive IT strategy and management best practices for New York City to prioritize technology investments, improve IT management, and enhance service delivery. The strategy includes appointing a dedicated CIO, restoring an independent cybersecurity agency, streamlining IT hiring, piloting IT initiatives, modernizing IT systems, and using AI strategically.
novelty: The project introduces a centralized IT strategy with a dedicated CIO for New York City, emphasizing pilot-based acquisitions, Agile project management, and strategic use of AI to improve city services.
stakeholders: New York City residents, city government agencies, IT staff, the mayor, agency commissioners, academia, labor, and businesses.

name: U.S. Tech Force
description: The U.S. Tech Force is a federal hiring initiative aimed at recruiting 1,000 early-career tech professionals into government roles to address workforce gaps in technology expertise and early-career talent. The program focuses on hiring individuals with skills in AI, software engineering, and data science for two-year stints to work on modernization projects across various government agencies.
novelty: The initiative is innovative due to its pooled hiring approach, allowing multiple agencies to select from a shared list of vetted candidates, and its scale, aiming to hire 1,000 tech professionals. It also emphasizes direct hiring into agencies, unlike previous project-based recruitment efforts.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include early-career tech professionals, federal agencies such as the departments of State, Treasury, Defense, and others, universities, non-profits, professional associations, and private sector companies partnering with OPM.

name: United States Tech Force (Tech Force)
description: A cross-government program to recruit top technologists to modernize the federal government, focusing on AI and technology modernization.
novelty: The program is a government-wide effort to bring in top private-sector talent and provide world-class technical training to tackle complex challenges and modernize government technology systems.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal government agencies and the American public. Stakeholders engaged include OPM, OMB, GSA, OSTP, private-sector technology companies, and various federal departments.

name: Digital Technology Innovation Leader award
description: The Digital Technology Innovation Leader award is a new category in the 2026 ACT Expo Fleet Awards, recognizing fleet organizations that transform operations through the strategic use of digital technologies. This includes the deployment and integration of AI, telematics, automation, data analytics, and other advanced tools to improve safety, efficiency, reliability, and environmental performance.
novelty: This award category is innovative because it specifically highlights the strategic use of digital technologies in fleet operations, emphasizing the integration of advanced tools like AI and data analytics to achieve significant improvements in various operational aspects.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include fleet organizations that are using digital technologies, as well as the broader commercial transportation industry that benefits from improved safety, efficiency, and environmental performance.

name: Government Artificial Intelligence Tool
organisation: Washoe County, Madison AI, Inc., M3 Planning, Inc. (OnStrategy)
description: An AI report-writing tool developed to automate research and assist with document generation referencing County Code, Board decisions, and policies, saving employees hundreds of hours each month.
novelty: The tool uses AI to automate repetitive tasks, allowing professional staff to focus on more valuable work. It is co-created with Madison AI and is used by over 50 other government jurisdictions across the United States.
stakeholders: Washoe County employees, local government jurisdictions, and potentially other commercial entities through product sales.

name: U.S. Tech Force
description: The U.S. Tech Force is a cross-government program designed to recruit top technologists to advance artificial intelligence adoption and address critical technological challenges within the federal government. It aims to recruit early-career technologists for two-year employment terms, providing them with technical training and opportunities to work with industry leaders and senior managers from partnering tech companies.
novelty: The program is innovative in its approach to fast-track AI adoption across the federal government by creating a pathway for private-sector talent to contribute to government projects, thus enhancing American AI leadership and technological competitiveness.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include early-career technologists, federal government agencies, private sector partners such as Adobe, Amazon Web Services, and others, as well as the American taxpayers who benefit from improved government services.

name: Digital Innovation in Local Government
description: The innovation involves adopting next-generation technologies, modernized services, and process redesign to transform service delivery in a citizen-centric manner. It includes modern digital infrastructure, data-driven decision making, enhanced citizen engagement, strategic cloud use, skilled workforce cultivation, and prioritizing security and compliance.
novelty: The project is innovative because it goes beyond mere digitization to encompass cultural change, strategic vision, and sustainable public value. It integrates modern infrastructure, AI, and cloud strategies to enhance service efficiency and citizen engagement.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens who interact with public services, local government leaders, technology leaders, procurement executives, and vendor partners involved in implementing the innovation.

name: AI Assistant and Training Program
description: New Jersey is expanding its AI assistant, launched in 2024, to improve state service delivery. The AI platform helps staff with tasks like drafting emails, summarizing documents, and processing feedback. The state also offers an AI training program with over 13,000 participants, aiming to enhance efficiency and access to services.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of generative AI to streamline government operations and improve service delivery, including error detection in benefit applications and data collaboration across agencies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New Jersey state employees, residents benefiting from improved service delivery, and participants in the AI training program. The Center for Civic Futures is also a key stakeholder through its grant support.

name: Tech Force
description: Tech Force is a federal hiring initiative aimed at recruiting 1,000 early-career technologists to modernize government systems and expand AI use across federal agencies. The program offers a two-year service term with salaries up to $200,000, focusing on AI implementation, software development, and data modernization.
novelty: The initiative is innovative as it seeks to bridge the gap between government and industry by recruiting early-career technologists to bring modern skills in AI, cloud computing, and data science into federal agencies, addressing the demographic gap in the federal workforce.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include early-career technologists, federal agencies such as the Departments of War, State, Homeland Security, and Health and Human Services, and private-sector partners like Apple, Microsoft, and Google Public Sector.

name: AI Integration in State Government Services
organisation: National Association of State Chief Information Officers (NASCIO)
description: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into state government services aims to revolutionize public service delivery by addressing workforce shortages, personalizing citizen experiences, and enhancing fraud detection. However, it faces challenges such as data quality, legacy system integration, and workforce skills gaps.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on responsible AI integration, emphasizing transparency, ethics, security, and inclusivity from the outset, unlike previous technology adoptions where ethical considerations were secondary.
stakeholders: State Chief Information Officers, state governments, citizens, AI companies, tech giants, startups, and public sector employees are the primary stakeholders involved in this innovation.

name: 406 JOBS Initiative
description: The 406 JOBS Initiative is a comprehensive plan to modernize Montana’s workforce development system, focusing on aligning government resources to support workers and employers. It includes initiatives like increasing Registered Apprenticeship Pathways, preparing the workforce for AI and automation, and establishing local sector partnerships.
novelty: The initiative represents a fundamental shift in state government approach by placing employers and workers at the center and fostering collaboration across state agencies to reduce redundancy and complexity.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Montana's workers, employers, veterans, individuals with behavioral health and developmental disabilities, and those reentering society. Stakeholders engaged include state agencies, the Montana Chamber of Commerce, industry leaders, and the public.

name: Genesis Mission
organisation: U.S. Department of Energy, Accenture Federal Services
description: The Genesis Mission is a national initiative led by the U.S. Department of Energy to accelerate American leadership in AI and transform the nation's science and technology ecosystem. It aims to establish an integrated American Science and Security Platform connecting supercomputers, scientific datasets, and AI solutions to advance national security, scientific discovery, and energy challenges.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its unprecedented integration of leadership-class supercomputers, unique scientific datasets, and cutting-edge AI solutions to create a comprehensive platform for advancing national security and scientific discovery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the U.S. Department of Energy, Accenture Federal Services, AI researchers, scientific community, and national security agencies.

name: Emerging Technology Advisory Board (ETAB) Recommendations
organisation: New York State Emerging Technology Advisory Board (ETAB)
description: The ETAB released a report with 12 recommendations to bolster New York's biotechnology ecosystem, focusing on commercialization of advanced technologies, public-private partnerships, AI development, and workforce development to advance economic growth and public health.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating public-private partnerships, AI, and workforce development to accelerate biotech commercialization and scientific breakthroughs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New York's business, non-profit, and research institutions, as well as public-private partners, biotech industry leaders, and the New York State government.

name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI)
description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is a second-generation cloud platform with built-in AI capabilities, designed to help state, local, and education (SLED) agencies modernize their IT infrastructure, automate processes, and make data-driven decisions while reducing complexity and costs.
novelty: OCI is the world's first second-generation cloud platform, re-architected to address the limitations of earlier cloud models, with built-in security and AI capabilities, offering a cost-efficient approach to AI with predictable pricing.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are state, local, and education (SLED) agencies, their employees, and the citizens they serve. Beneficiaries include government agencies looking to modernize and improve service delivery, as well as specific communities like the Choctaw Nation for language preservation.

name: RAISE Act
description: The RAISE Act requires large AI developers to create and publish information about their safety protocols, report incidents to the State, and establishes an oversight office within the Department of Financial Services to assess developers and ensure transparency.
novelty: The RAISE Act sets a nation-leading standard for AI transparency and safety, requiring detailed safety protocols and incident reporting, and creating an oversight office to ensure compliance.
stakeholders: Large AI developers, New York State Government, Department of Financial Services, AI technology users, and the general public.

name: AI-enabled Workflow for Veterans' Disability Benefits
organisation: Maximus, Veterans Evaluation Services (VES), AWS, government partners
description: An AI-enabled workflow that streamlines the process of reviewing veterans' disability benefits claims by reading and structuring unstructured medical records, highlighting important information, and surfacing patterns linked to specific conditions and rating criteria. This system has processed over 400 million documents in its first year, improving decision speed and consistency for veterans.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI into the operational infrastructure of government services, specifically for processing large volumes of medical records efficiently, thus reducing backlog and improving decision quality.
stakeholders: Veterans, examiners, clinicians, government agencies, AWS, Maximus, Veterans Evaluation Services (VES)

name: State CIO Top 10 Priorities for 2026
organisation: National Association of State Chief Information Officers (NASCIO)
