description: The State CIO Top 10 report by NASCIO outlines the strategic priorities for state CIOs in 2026, with a focus on artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, budget management, modernization, and digital government services.
novelty: The report elevates artificial intelligence, including generative and agentic AI, to the top of the strategic agenda, highlighting its importance in governance, security, workforce skills, and ethical use.
stakeholders: State CIOs, state IT leaders, government agencies, and potentially the general public who will benefit from improved state services.

name: Sunny chatbot
description: The Sunny chatbot is an AI-driven tool that improved resident communications, reduced 311 hold times, and made government services accessible in 72 languages, in compliance with digital accessibility legislation.
novelty: The innovation lies in its use of generative AI and multilingual capabilities to enhance government service delivery and accessibility.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the residents of Denver, who experience improved communication and access to government services. Stakeholders include Denver Technology Services and other city departments.

name: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)
description: SBOM is a tool that provides a machine-readable inventory of all components, including open-source libraries, frameworks, and third-party services, used in software applications. It helps government agencies quickly identify and address vulnerabilities by mapping known issues to specific components.
novelty: SBOMs offer a machine-readable 'recipe' of software components, allowing for automated scanning and integration into security tools, which enhances speed and clarity in vulnerability management.
stakeholders: State and local government agencies, security teams, software vendors, and subcontractors involved in software development and procurement.

name: FedRAMP 20x
description: FedRAMP 20x is an initiative launched by the GSA to accelerate the FedRAMP authorization process, making it faster and more outcome-oriented. It aims to reduce authorization friction and move towards automation-based validation, emphasizing security over paperwork.
novelty: The initiative represents a bold reimagining of the FedRAMP process, shifting towards a cloud-native, automation-driven framework with continuous security validation, demonstrating rapid evolution in federal compliance programs.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, vendors, security experts, platform engineers, data scientists, and the GSA.

name: Genesis Mission
organisation: Federal Government of the United States
description: The Genesis Mission is an initiative by the U.S. federal government to create an integrated AI platform that accelerates innovation and discovery across various sectors such as national security, health, transportation, and critical infrastructure. It aims to operationalize AI across the federal government, emphasizing the need for robust data infrastructure to support AI workloads.
novelty: The Genesis Mission is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across the entire federal government, focusing on building a vast AI infrastructure with open, high-performance object storage to ensure data agility and scalability.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, national security personnel, healthcare providers, transportation authorities, and other public service sectors that will benefit from enhanced AI capabilities.

name: My.gov.uz
description: My.gov.uz is a unified electronic government services portal that serves as a single gateway for citizens and businesses to access a wide range of public services online. It offers over 760 services through the portal and 540 services via its mobile application, significantly reducing administrative burdens and saving costs.
novelty: The innovation integrates over 200 government information systems and platforms, uses a unified billing system, and incorporates AI solutions like the MyID biometric identification system and Mukhlisa, an AI-powered virtual assistant, to streamline service delivery.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include citizens and businesses in Uzbekistan, while stakeholders engaged in the innovation include government agencies, the Ministry of Digital Technologies, and IT specialists.

name: European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs) Phase 2
description: The European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs) Phase 2 in Ireland aims to support SMEs and public service bodies in adopting AI and other digital technologies. The programme will deliver over 3,000 engagements, 1,100 'Test Before Invest' projects, and more than 200 training courses nationwide, leveraging co-funding from the EU’s Digital Europe Programme.
novelty: The innovation lies in providing a comprehensive one-stop-shop service that includes technical expertise, experimentation, financial advice, and training to facilitate digital transformation for SMEs and public sector organisations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include SMEs, public service bodies, the European Commission, and the Irish government. Beneficiaries are primarily SMEs and public sector organisations seeking to adopt advanced digital technologies.

name: AI-driven Predictive Modeling for Scenario Planning
description: AI-driven predictive modeling is used by city and state leaders for scenario planning to anticipate the impact of various events such as prolonged heat waves, storm surges, and budget cuts. This approach allows planners to simulate 'what if' scenarios to better prepare for potential challenges.
novelty: The innovation lies in using AI to simulate scenarios based on curated data, allowing for efficient planning without the need for excessive data processing and energy consumption.
stakeholders: City and state leaders, urban planners, local government agencies, and indirectly, the residents of the cities who benefit from improved infrastructure planning and management.

name: HTX
description: HTX is an agency focused on developing cutting-edge science and technology solutions for the Home Team in Singapore. It aims to enhance public safety and security through innovations such as AI-assisted solutions and advancements in marine systems.
novelty: HTX is innovative in its approach to integrating advanced technology like AI and robotics into public safety and security measures, providing solutions that are tailored to the diverse needs of citizens.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Home Team, public service members, and the general public who benefit from enhanced safety and security measures. HTX employees, referred to as Xponents, are also key stakeholders in developing these innovations.

name: SAMA-BIS Innovation Summit
organisation: Saudi Central Bank (SAMA), Bank for International Settlements (BIS)
description: The SAMA-BIS Innovation Summit is a regional event focused on financial innovation, particularly in cross-border payments and emerging technologies like AI, DLT, and Quantum computing. It aims to foster collaboration and learning to build a transformative financial infrastructure.
novelty: The summit emphasizes collective learning and collaboration in financial innovation, focusing on regional advantages and the unique context of the Middle East and Africa. It highlights the use of emerging technologies to reshape financial services.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include central banks, financial institutions, regulators, technology providers, and regional economies in the Middle East and Africa. Beneficiaries are the financial sector and economies looking to enhance digital transformation and innovation.

name: AI Grants for Public Services
organisation: Maryland Department of Human Services, Maryland Department of Health, Maryland Benefits, Maryland Health Benefit Exchange, Maryland Department of Labor
description: The innovation involves the use of AI to improve access to public services such as nutrition, housing, and health care in Maryland. The projects aim to streamline work verification for SNAP and Medicaid, improve service delivery, and support agency staff in navigating complex regulations.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its multi-state collaboration to develop AI tools that can be deployed across different states, and its focus on responsible AI use with rigorous standards for data, security, and privacy.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Maryland residents accessing public services like SNAP, Medicaid, and housing. Stakeholders engaged include state agencies, tech companies, and community-based organizations.

name: Request for Information on AI in Clinical Care
organisation: Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
description: HHS published a Request for Information to gather public input on accelerating the adoption and use of AI in clinical care, focusing on regulation, reimbursement, and research & development. The goal is to enhance productivity, reduce burden, lower healthcare costs, and improve health outcomes.
novelty: The initiative seeks to align federal incentives and reduce uncertainties that impede AI innovation, fostering public trust and confidence in AI technologies in healthcare.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include patients, caregivers, and communities. Stakeholders engaged include healthcare organizations, private sector innovators, and regulatory bodies.

name: FedRAMP 20x and USAi
description: FedRAMP 20x introduces innovative approaches to simplify and reduce the cost of the authorization process for AI tools in federal services. USAi is a shared platform for federal agencies to experiment with AI models without upfront costs, allowing them to evaluate tools before purchase.
novelty: FedRAMP 20x simplifies the authorization process for AI tools, making it more accessible and cost-effective. USAi provides a unique shared environment for federal agencies to test AI models, enhancing decision-making and compliance without upfront costs.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, GSA, commercial cloud providers, federal workers, and the American public as beneficiaries of improved government services.

name: Ireland's Charter for Digital Inclusion
organisation: Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment, OpenAI
description: The Charter for Digital Inclusion is an initiative to advance digital literacy, foster equitable access to emerging technologies, and strengthen Ireland’s position in ethical and inclusive digital innovation. OpenAI has signed the Charter, committing to various actions to support digital inclusion.
novelty: The innovation lies in the collaborative approach between public and private sectors to ensure digital technologies are accessible to all, focusing on ethical and inclusive digital transformation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public bodies, businesses of all sizes, communities, SMEs, and individuals across Ireland who will benefit from improved digital literacy and access to technology.

name: AI Public Service Projects
organisation: Maryland Department of Human Services, Maryland Department of Health, Maryland Department of Labor, Maryland Health Benefit Exchange
description: The innovation involves using artificial intelligence to improve access to public services such as food assistance, Medicaid, housing, and unemployment benefits in Maryland. The projects aim to streamline eligibility and service delivery, making government services more accessible and seamless for Marylanders.
novelty: The project is innovative because it leverages AI to streamline work verification for SNAP and Medicaid, and develops systems to support agency staff in navigating complex regulations. It also involves a multi-state cohort to develop AI tools deployable across states.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Maryland state agencies, community organizations, Maryland residents who are beneficiaries of public services, and the American Public Human Services Association. The project also involves collaboration with tech companies and research labs.

name: Oklahoma Department of Corrections Innovation Initiatives
description: The Oklahoma Department of Corrections (ODOC) has implemented several innovative initiatives aimed at enhancing public safety, supporting workforce development, and modernizing correctional facilities. Key initiatives include revamping the Correctional Officer Academy, ending private prison use, expanding rehabilitation programs, and launching new technology and education programs.
novelty: The innovation is characterized by a comprehensive approach to correctional facility management, including the introduction of a paramilitary-style training model, the first four-year divinity degree offered inside a U.S. prison, and the deployment of advanced technologies such as Electronic Health Records and AI for inmate management.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include correctional officers, incarcerated individuals, their families, educational institutions like Oklahoma Baptist University and Oklahoma State University, and organizations such as The Last Mile and CareerTech.

name: AI Center for Government®
description: The AI Center for Government® was launched to help federal, state, and local leaders better understand, use, and deploy AI to deliver on their agency’s mission. It includes the AI Government Leadership Program® and other Public Service Leadership Institute® programs, along with a suite of eLearning courses for public servants.
novelty: The innovation lies in its focus on applying AI to improve leadership and agency performance across federal, state, and local governments, supported by major tech companies.
stakeholders: Federal, state, and local government leaders and civil servants are the primary users and beneficiaries, with support from Google.org and Microsoft.

name: AI Initiatives for Public Benefits Access
organisation: Maryland Department of Human Services, Maryland Department of Health, Maryland Benefits, Maryland Health Benefit Exchange, Maryland Department of Labor
description: The initiative involves developing open-source AI tools to streamline work verification for SNAP and Medicaid eligibility, and testing AI applications to enhance service delivery in Maryland. It aims to improve access to public benefits by using AI to navigate complex regulations and process documents efficiently.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of AI to streamline public service delivery, improve access to benefits, and its collaborative approach across multiple state agencies and partners.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Maryland residents eligible for SNAP, Medicaid, housing, and unemployment benefits. Stakeholders engaged include state agencies, the American Public Human Services Association, Nava PBC, Government Performance Lab, and Computational Policy Lab.

name: AI Action Plan
description: The AI Action Plan outlines over 90 federal policy actions to accelerate AI innovation, build American AI infrastructure, and lead in international AI diplomacy and security. It includes procurement guidance, regulatory adjustments, and strategic plans by various federal agencies to integrate AI responsibly into their operations.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its comprehensive approach to AI policy, addressing procurement, regulation, and international diplomacy while promoting unbiased AI principles and reducing regulatory barriers.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, AI vendors, policymakers, and ultimately the American public who will benefit from improved government services and AI-driven innovations.

name: Modern Secure Form Platforms
description: Modern secure form platforms offer FedRAMP authorization, end-to-end encryption, multi-factor authentication, comprehensive audit logging, and automated compliance monitoring to replace legacy systems while improving user experience, reducing operational costs, and meeting evolving security requirements.
novelty: The innovation lies in replacing outdated government web forms with modern platforms that provide enhanced security features such as end-to-end encryption, multi-factor authentication, and comprehensive audit trails, which are compliant with federal security standards.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal government agencies, citizens who use government services, IT and security teams responsible for implementing the new systems, and vendors providing FedRAMP-authorized platforms.

name: AI Cybersecurity and Risk Management
description: The innovation focuses on prioritizing AI cybersecurity and risk management for state chief information officers and technology leaders. It addresses challenges such as deepfakes, shadow AI, and quantum computing threats, aiming to enhance security and governance frameworks in public sector organizations.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into cybersecurity, addressing emerging threats like deepfakes and quantum computing, and emphasizing the need for new governance frameworks to handle AI-driven decision-making.
stakeholders: State chief information officers, technology leaders, government agencies, public sector organizations, and citizens who rely on secure digital services.

name: New Jersey Innovation Authority (NJIA)
description: The New Jersey Innovation Authority (NJIA) was established to ensure state government delivers more efficient and effective services. It aims to position New Jersey as a leader in AI and government technology, having already helped launch over 65,000 businesses and generated $168 million in economic activity.
novelty: The NJIA is the first state innovation office enshrined into law, emphasizing a data-driven, human-centered, and agile approach to improving government services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New Jersey residents, businesses, the state government, and experts in data science and public engagement.

name: New Jersey Innovation Authority (NJIA)
organisation: New Jersey State Office of Innovation (OOI)
description: The New Jersey Innovation Authority (NJIA) is established to modernize services for residents, businesses, and institutions in New Jersey. It aims to deliver more efficient and effective services, advance responsible AI use, streamline processes, and develop new solutions to real-world problems.
novelty: New Jersey is the first state in the U.S. to enshrine a state innovation office into law, emphasizing human-centered, agile technology approaches.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include New Jersey residents, businesses, and institutions. Stakeholders engaged include the New Jersey State Legislature, Governor Phil Murphy, and the NJBIA.

name: AI-assisted design tools pilot
organisation: Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command (NAVFAC) Southeast
description: A pilot project led by Lt. Cmdr. John Nurthen at NAVFAC Southeast's Expeditionary Warfare Center, utilizing AI-assisted design tools to potentially reduce design timelines by up to 80%.
novelty: The project demonstrates the potential of AI-assisted design tools to significantly accelerate design processes in military construction, reducing timelines by up to 80%.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the U.S. Navy and related military engineering teams, with stakeholders including NAVFAC Southeast and the Expeditionary Warfare Center.

name: New Jersey State Office of Innovation
description: The New Jersey State Office of Innovation was created to improve digital innovation in state government. It has been made permanent and codified into law, allowing it to fund projects more easily and potentially generate revenue by selling technology to other governments. The office has worked on projects like modernizing communication for the Department of Labor, using AI to identify students eligible for food assistance, and creating an AI chatbot for state employees.
novelty: New Jersey is the first state to codify its digital innovation office into law, making it a permanent fixture within the government. This move allows for easier funding and potential revenue generation through technology sales.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New Jersey state government agencies, residents, business owners, and potentially other state and local governments. Beneficiaries are residents who will experience more efficient government services and businesses that can launch more quickly through streamlined processes.

name: AI-powered platforms for public safety
description: The Tampa Police Department has implemented AI-powered platforms to enhance public safety. These include Prepared 911 for faster call processing and real-time translation, Axon Assistant & Policy Chat for improved body-worn camera functionality, Virtual Reality Immersive Training Pods for officer training, and the Drone as First Responder program for quicker emergency response.
novelty: The innovation integrates AI and real-time technologies to improve response times, remove language barriers, enhance officer training, and provide real-time intelligence, making it a comprehensive approach to modernizing public safety.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Tampa Police Officers, Communications personnel, and the diverse community of Tampa, who benefit from improved safety and communication.

name: CODE PA
description: CODE PA is an initiative by the Office of Administration to modernize legacy technologies, improve online services, and enhance cybersecurity for Pennsylvania's state government. It has achieved $37 million in cost savings through strategic investments, cloud hosting, vendor discounts, and insourcing projects. The initiative also focuses on developing enterprise platforms for grants and permits and a secure online login for residents.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive modernization of outdated systems, strategic financial management, and the integration of advanced cybersecurity tools to protect against evolving threats, including AI-driven attacks.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Pennsylvania residents, Commonwealth business operations, and state agencies under the Governor’s jurisdiction. Beneficiaries are primarily the residents who will experience improved digital services and enhanced cybersecurity.

name: GenAI.mil
organisation: Department of the Air Force Artificial Intelligence, Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office
description: GenAI.mil is an artificial intelligence platform launched to provide AI capabilities to three million personnel across the Department of Defense. It aims to ensure cybersecurity protection for AI tools and reduce workloads by offering frontier models from leading AI companies.
novelty: The platform is innovative as it integrates AI capabilities from leading companies like Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI, and is specifically designed to handle controlled unclassified information within a military context.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the personnel of the Department of Defense, specifically those with a common access card on the War Department's nonclassified network. The initiative involved collaboration across various Air Force organizations and departments.

name: Sovereign AI Initiative
organisation: Indian Government, NxtGen Cloud Technologies, Red Hat
description: The initiative aims to develop and democratize AI in India through open innovation, with a focus on becoming self-reliant in AI capabilities. It involves a US$1.2 billion fund to build AI solutions and infrastructure, leveraging open source models to enhance security, reliability, and local talent development.
novelty: The project emphasizes AI sovereignty by using open source models to avoid vendor lock-in, allowing for flexibility and independence in AI development. It also focuses on creating a platform-agnostic cloud infrastructure to support local AI innovation.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government agencies, local startups, innovators, and AI developers in India. Stakeholders engaged include the Indian Government, NxtGen Cloud Technologies, Red Hat, and local talent.

name: AI Engineering Lab
organisation: Government Digital Service, Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
description: The AI Engineering Lab is an initiative to help public sector software teams adopt AI coding assistants in their development workflows. It provides teams with time-limited licenses for AI coding tools, technical support, and structured training to integrate AI tools across the software development lifecycle.
novelty: The project introduces a coordinated approach to AI adoption across government software teams, focusing on capability, governance, and consistent use rather than isolated experimentation. It builds on previous trials and emphasizes workforce capability, evaluation, and community activities like hackathons.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government software teams and public sector organizations. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the Government Digital Service and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

name: Chief AI Officer Position
organisation: New York State Office of Information Technology Services (ITS)
description: The Chief AI Officer position in New York State is designed to manage, coordinate, and expand the use of AI across state government. The role involves guiding AI tool selection, introduction, and governance, with a focus on expanding AI education, access to generative AI tools, and updating AI technology policies.
novelty: The position spans the entire state government rather than focusing on a single agency, aiming to bring consistency to AI use across various applications, from internal automation to public-facing services.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include state employees and New York residents. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include ITS, partner agencies, and the Chief AI Officer.

name: Empire AI
organisation: New York State Office of Information Technology Services
description: Empire AI is a heavily funded supercomputing consortium intended to support university research projects across a wide range of disciplines, aimed at using 'AI for good'. It was advanced by the New York State Office of Information Technology Services to enhance AI initiatives within the state.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on using AI for good and its support for a wide range of university research projects through a supercomputing consortium.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include university researchers, state employees, and New York State residents who benefit from the AI initiatives and education programs.

name: Expansion of Tyndall National Institute
organisation: University College Cork, Tyndall National Institute
description: The expansion of the Tyndall National Institute aims to double its physical size and enhance Ireland's research infrastructure in semiconductors and related technologies. It supports regional growth, increases research impact, and underpins industrial development in computing, AI, and communications.
novelty: The project is innovative as it significantly strengthens Ireland's position in semiconductor research and innovation, supporting breakthroughs in AI, quantum technologies, and next-generation computing, and is a key component of the INSPIRE programme.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include University College Cork, Tyndall National Institute, the Irish Government, researchers, the semiconductor industry, and the wider knowledge economy in Ireland.

name: AI Division
description: The AI Division is a new initiative within the Texas Department of Information Resources, aimed at supporting responsible AI deployment across Texas government. It focuses on innovation, AI strategy, and modernization of statewide digital services.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on responsible AI deployment and the establishment of a dedicated division to oversee AI strategy and modernization efforts within state government.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Texas government agencies, public-sector technology users, and potentially the citizens of Texas who will benefit from improved digital services.

name: Artificial Intelligence Enabled Fraud Prevention Engine
description: The PRAC is developing an AI-enabled fraud prevention engine trained on 5 million pandemic applications and other data to identify anomalies, trends, patterns, and hidden connections in future applications before payments are made. This tool aims to prevent fraud before funds are dispersed, potentially saving billions in fraudulent claims.
novelty: The innovation leverages artificial intelligence to proactively identify fraud risks before funds are dispersed, rather than the traditional 'pay and chase' method. It is trained on a large dataset of pandemic applications to enhance its predictive capabilities.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include federal agencies, inspectors general, and ultimately American taxpayers who benefit from reduced fraud and more efficient use of federal funds.

name: Reimagined State
organisation: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change
description: The Reimagined State is a reform agenda that leverages data, digital technology, and artificial intelligence to transform public services in the UK. It aims to create a new model of public services that are personalised, preventative, and always accessible. The initiative focuses on using AI to improve service delivery, enhance personalisation, and enable proactive interventions.
novelty: The innovation lies in replacing the outdated labour-intensive, standardised, and reactive public service model with an AI-driven approach that allows for scalable, personalised, and proactive service delivery. It integrates digital ID, modular platforms, and real-time intelligence to create a continuously improving system.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK citizens who will benefit from improved public services, government agencies responsible for implementing the reforms, and public service professionals who will be empowered with AI tools to enhance service delivery.

name: Agentic Process Automation
description: Agentic process automation uses AI to autonomously manage complex processes such as permit approvals and benefit assessments, improving efficiency and speeding up service delivery in government agencies.
novelty: Unlike traditional RPA, agentic automation can handle uncertainty, interpret handwritten notes, check across databases, and make reasoned judgments, learning from outcomes to improve processes.
stakeholders: State and local government employees, citizens requiring government services, IT and leadership teams in government agencies.

name: Eliminating Bias in Algorithmic Systems (BIAS) Act
description: The Eliminating BIAS Act mandates that every federal agency using, funding, or overseeing AI establish or strengthen civil rights offices to combat AI-driven bias and discrimination. These offices will report to Congress on AI's impact on vulnerable communities and recommend legislative actions to address harms.
novelty: The innovation lies in the establishment of dedicated civil rights offices within federal agencies focused on algorithmic accountability and civil rights enforcement, ensuring transparency and accountability in AI systems.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include vulnerable communities such as Black, brown, low-income, and other marginalized groups. Stakeholders engaged include federal agencies, Congress, civil rights organizations, and advocacy groups.

name: Chief Artificial Intelligence and Innovation Officer
description: The role of Chief Artificial Intelligence and Innovation Officer was created to lead statewide efforts in adopting AI and other emerging technologies in Texas.
novelty: The position is innovative as it focuses on integrating AI and emerging technologies at a state level, which is a pioneering approach in public sector technology management.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state government agencies, local government agencies, and the public sector entities that will benefit from enhanced technology integration and cybersecurity measures.

name: State Government Modernization
description: States are modernizing their processes and increasing efficiency to address budget constraints and rising public expectations. This includes the use of data, technology, digitization, and automation to deliver services with fewer resources and reduce costs.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of generative artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies to achieve efficiency goals that were previously unattainable, as well as the establishment of government groups modeled after successful efficiency divisions.
stakeholders: State governments, residents of the states, and government officials are the primary stakeholders. Beneficiaries include the general public who receive improved services and the government entities that achieve cost savings.

name: Diia.AI
description: Diia.AI is the world's first government AI agent developed as part of Ukraine's Diia ecosystem of public services. It was built on a low-code platform to provide a wartime lifeline for public services and to enhance digital government services.
novelty: Diia.AI is innovative as it represents the first instance of a government AI agent, developed on a low-code platform, which allows for rapid deployment and updates of digital services.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are Ukrainian citizens who rely on public services, especially during wartime. Stakeholders include Kitsoft, the Ministry of Digital Transformation, and potentially other government entities involved in digital services.

name: OneGov initiative
description: The OneGov initiative aims to reduce the price of commonly used software across government by signing agreements and creating enterprisewide agreements for tools like artificial intelligence, enhancing acquisition speed and efficiency.
novelty: The initiative represents a radical shift in procurement strategy by centralizing and streamlining software acquisition across multiple government agencies, requiring changes on both government and industry sides.
stakeholders: Government agencies using the software, industry partners providing the software, and GSA as the implementing body.

name: CustomerFirst
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: CustomerFirst is a new unit within DSIT aimed at testing innovative ways to improve government services by reducing delays and frustrations, transitioning services to app or online platforms, and saving costs. It focuses on enhancing customer service across government departments, starting with the DVLA.
novelty: The innovation lies in its approach to transforming government services by leveraging technology, including AI, to enhance customer service while supporting civil service workers, rather than replacing them.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government departments, civil service workers, and citizens who rely on government services. The initiative is chaired by Tristan Thomas and co-chaired by Greg Jackson.

name: AI-Ready Data Guidelines
description: Guidelines for releasing government datasets for AI applications, providing steps for preparing datasets to support AI capabilities and promoting responsible data stewardship.
novelty: The guidelines focus on making datasets technically fit for AI by structuring, documenting, and governing them effectively, ensuring legality, security, and public confidence.
stakeholders: Data and AI practitioners, policy leaders, decision makers, public sector organisations, and the general public as beneficiaries of improved AI-driven public services.

name: CustomerFirst
organisation: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: CustomerFirst is a digital public service delivery unit aimed at making government services more accessible, efficient, and user-friendly by utilizing innovation, AI, and modern solutions. It seeks to eliminate long phone queues, repeated form-filling, and paperwork, potentially saving £4 billion by transitioning to online processes.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to digital transformation of public services, focusing on user experience and efficiency through AI and modern technology, and its potential to significantly reduce costs and improve service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include UK citizens accessing government services, with stakeholders including government bodies like the DVLA and private sector leaders involved in the initiative.

name: Apply AI Strategy
organisation: European Commission, European Economic and Social Committee (EESC)
description: The Apply AI Strategy aims to transition AI from research to practical use in business and public services across the EU. It promotes an 'AI first' policy approach, encouraging companies and the public sector to prioritize AI solutions. The strategy seeks to build sovereign European AI capabilities and develop advanced AI models aligned with EU values.
novelty: The strategy emphasizes building AI systems that are trustworthy, transparent, and human-centric, aligning with European values. It also introduces the 'AI gigafactories' initiative to involve private sector investment and the frontier AI initiative to develop advanced AI models.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include SMEs, scale-ups, public services, and various sectors like healthcare, finance, and e-commerce. Stakeholders engaged include civil society, social partners, workers, and the private sector.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence in Local Government
description: The integration of advanced artificial intelligence into local government operations to perform analytical, planning, and drafting functions traditionally requiring professional judgment. This innovation aims to transform tasks within jobs, enhance performance management, and improve public value through AI-driven insights.
novelty: Unlike previous technological innovations that digitized existing processes, this AI integration transforms tasks within jobs, augmenting or partially performing core professional functions such as analytical forecasting, document drafting, and pattern recognition.
stakeholders: City managers, county executives, elected officials, professional managers, labor organizations, and the public.

name: Total Experience Management (TXM)
description: Maximus's Total Experience Management (TXM) approach aims to address service delivery challenges within federal contact centers by providing consistent, unified services across communication channels. It leverages a strategic mix of customer experience technologies to transform service delivery from fragmented to proactive and personalized, improving service quality and reducing costs.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to integrating secure, cloud-based omnichannel platforms, self-service portals, intelligent virtual assistants, and AI-powered predictive analytics to enhance citizen engagement and service delivery in federal agencies.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include federal agencies, citizens who interact with these services, and service agents who benefit from improved tools and processes.

name: Fund Management System (FMS) Modernization
description: ICF was awarded a $64 million contract by the Pennsylvania Insurance Department to modernize and operate its environmental claims management system. The project involves building a scalable, modernized Fund Management System (FMS) to support the Pennsylvania Underground Storage Tank Indemnification Fund (PA USTIF) by migrating core applications to the cloud to improve service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in leveraging data and automation to streamline state workflows, improve performance, reliability, and security, and deliver real-time analytics, thus driving operational and cost efficiencies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Pennsylvania Insurance Department, state and local governments, and the communities in Pennsylvania benefiting from improved health and environmental outcomes.

name: Future of Medi-Cal Commission
description: The Future of Medi-Cal Commission is tasked with delivering a 10-year roadmap to California’s next governor, focusing on transforming Medi-Cal to advance health equity and provide financial security. The initiative includes a call for bold ideas to fundamentally reimagine Medi-Cal's operations, with selected concepts receiving grants for further development.
novelty: The project seeks transformative, not incremental, changes to Medi-Cal, encouraging innovative ideas such as new financing frameworks, AI integration, and unified care models. It emphasizes open, inclusive participation from diverse stakeholders to reimagine the future of Medi-Cal.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Medi-Cal enrollees, healthcare providers, health plans, researchers, community leaders, and the broader healthcare community in California.

name: WISeR (Wasteful and Inappropriate Service Reduction)
description: WISeR is a pilot program introduced to traditional Medicare to implement prior authorization using artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies. It aims to reduce costs and improve efficiency while maintaining or improving the quality of care by targeting services with a higher risk of waste, fraud, and abuse.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI and machine learning technologies for prior authorization in traditional Medicare, which is a new approach compared to its limited use in Medicare Advantage plans.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Medicare beneficiaries, healthcare providers, tech companies involved in the authorization process, and the CMS Innovation Center overseeing the pilot.

name: IndiaAI Transforming Governance Challenge
organisation: IndiaAI, Real Time Governance Society, Government of Andhra Pradesh
description: The IndiaAI Transforming Governance Challenge aims to develop AI solutions across six domains to enhance transparency, operational efficiency, and inclusive growth in government processes. The challenge focuses on AI applications in areas such as rural entrepreneurship, supply chain optimization, renewable energy land allocation, urban infrastructure planning, land-use monitoring, and predictive infrastructure management for schools.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across multiple domains of public service, aiming to drive significant socio-economic impact through increased transparency and efficiency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Indian companies, start-ups, autonomous bodies, public sector organizations, non-profit organizations, research institutions, universities, and government departments in Andhra Pradesh. Beneficiaries are rural entrepreneurs, government supply chain managers, urban planners, and school administrators.

name: AI Research Resource (AIRR) Expansion
description: The UK government is investing £36 million to increase the AI Research Resource supercomputing capacity at Cambridge sixfold by spring 2026. This expansion will provide UK researchers and start-ups with free access to advanced AI chips and computing power, enabling breakthroughs in healthcare, environmental modelling, and more.
novelty: The project significantly boosts the computing power available to UK researchers and start-ups, providing access to cutting-edge AI chips and infrastructure typically reserved for large tech companies, thus democratizing AI research capabilities.
stakeholders: UK researchers, tech start-ups, University of Cambridge, Dell Technologies, AMD, StackHPC, and the UK government.

name: AI-driven Defenses and Unified Cloud Governance
description: Federal agencies are set to integrate AI-driven defenses and unified cloud governance to create more resilient and secure IT systems by 2026. This initiative aims to transform generative AI pilots into full-scale production systems, enhancing operational efficiency and mission outcomes.
novelty: The innovation lies in the transition from AI pilots to full-scale production systems, marking the maturation of the AI ecosystem and the integration of AI across the entire IT stack, from infrastructure to software and services.
stakeholders: Federal CIOs, chief AI officers, public-private partnerships, government agencies, and technology experts are involved, with beneficiaries being the federal agencies and the public receiving improved government services.

name: AI in State and Local Government
description: State and local governments are embedding AI into service delivery and core workflows, moving from experimentation to operational maturity. AI is being used for productivity, automation, and confidence in outcomes, becoming integral to citizen-facing services and internal processes.
novelty: The innovation lies in the transition of AI from pilot programs to enterprisewide operations, embedding AI agents and copilots into workflows for enhanced productivity and automation.
stakeholders: State and local government agencies, citizens as beneficiaries of improved services, and CDW as the implementing organization.

name: AI Integration in Government Services
description: State and local governments are embedding AI into service delivery and core workflows to improve productivity, automation, and confidence. AI is being integrated into citizen-facing services and internal processes, with a focus on transparency, traceability, and measurable value.
novelty: AI has moved from pilot programs to enterprisewide operations, becoming integral to government services and decision support, with a focus on outcomes rather than experimentation.
stakeholders: State and local government agencies, citizens, government employees, CDW as a technology provider.

name: AI Workforce Readiness
description: The initiative focuses on preparing government workforces for AI integration by fostering a mindset shift from passive consumption to active engagement with AI tools. It emphasizes the importance of human-centered AI, both internally and externally, and addresses cultural and emotional factors related to AI adoption.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to AI as a collaborator rather than a replacement, promoting active engagement and judgment in AI interactions. It also highlights the importance of AI governance beyond data management, focusing on reasoning and transparency.
stakeholders: Government employees, public-sector agencies, and constituents who will benefit from improved public services through effective AI integration.

name: AI Assistants for Public Services
organisation: UK Government, Meta, Anthropic, Alan Turing Institute
description: The UK government, in collaboration with Meta, Anthropic, and the Alan Turing Institute, is developing AI assistants to improve public services such as transport and national security. The initiative includes creating AI tools for job seekers and enhancing infrastructure maintenance and national security systems.
novelty: The project involves the integration of advanced AI technologies into public services, utilizing open-source tools and AI models to enhance efficiency and decision-making in critical areas like transport and national security.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public, job seekers, and public service providers. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include AI experts, government officials, and private sector partners like Meta and Anthropic.

name: AI Opportunities Action Plan
description: The AI Opportunities Action Plan is a comprehensive strategy by the UK Government to harness Artificial Intelligence for economic growth, public service improvement, and societal benefit. It includes initiatives like AI Growth Zones, AI Diagnostic Fund, and AI upskilling programs, aiming to integrate AI into public services, boost the economy, and upskill the workforce.
novelty: The plan is innovative in its holistic approach to embedding AI across various sectors, including healthcare, education, and public services, while also focusing on infrastructure development like AI Growth Zones and compute capacity expansion.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK citizens, public service users, healthcare professionals, educators, students, AI companies, and the workforce undergoing upskilling.

name: AI Learning and Innovation Hub
description: The AI Learning and Innovation Hub provides a sandbox environment for governments to explore AI technology applications securely. It aims to help governments navigate the AI landscape, improve procurement processes, and empower them with knowledge before signing contracts with vendors. The hub allows governments to experiment with AI, build solutions, and focus on outcomes-based contracts.
novelty: The hub offers a secure sandbox environment for governments to experiment with AI before procurement, emphasizing outcomes-based contracts and leveraging an open-source model for shared solutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government officials and departments, such as the city of Alexandria's Department of Code Administration. Stakeholders engaged include Social Finance and participating local and state governments.

name: AI, Data and Design Thinking in Federal Services
description: Federal leaders are using AI, data, and design thinking to simplify services, enhance personalization, and build trust in public services.
novelty: The project integrates AI, data analytics, and design thinking to transform public service delivery, focusing on personalization and trust-building.
stakeholders: Federal agencies such as CBP, ITA, State, VA, and the general public as beneficiaries of improved services.

name: Sacramento State Downtown Campus Redevelopment
organisation: California State University, Sacramento (CSUS), Meta, Governor's Office of California
description: A major redevelopment project in downtown Sacramento to create affordable housing for students, a new School of Public Affairs, an AI Center, a mixed-use performing arts venue, and a boutique hotel. The project aims to stimulate economic growth and enrich the downtown community with educational and cultural opportunities.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its public-private partnership model, involving significant investment from Meta, and its comprehensive approach to urban revitalization by integrating education, housing, and economic development in a central urban area.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include CSUS students, faculty, staff, the local community, Meta, the California state government, and local businesses.

name: One Government Blueprint for Overhauling Digital Services
description: The initiative aims to overhaul federal digital services by leveraging artificial intelligence to redesign websites, improve customer experience, and create a unified digital presence across government agencies. It focuses on delivering faster, digital-first services and reducing complexity and fragmentation in service delivery.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of artificial intelligence to enable self-service options, streamline digital experiences across agencies, and address root causes of inefficiencies rather than just symptoms.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include federal agencies, technologists recruited through the Tech Force initiative, and the American public who will benefit from improved digital services.

name: National AI Plan
description: The National AI Plan aims to harness AI as a catalyst for economic growth and societal benefit in Australia, focusing on balanced regulation and AI capability uplift across the workforce.
novelty: Australia's approach integrates AI as both a regulatory challenge and an innovation opportunity, emphasizing balanced regulation and lifelong learning.
stakeholders: Australian workforce, government, businesses, and the general public as beneficiaries of economic growth and improved public outcomes.

name: Robot Supervisor
description: The Robot Supervisor is a service robot deployed in the Gumi City Council to assist in administrative tasks, working from 9am to 6pm daily. It was integrated into the overwork culture of South Korea, which is known for its high robot density and long working hours.
novelty: The Robot Supervisor represents an innovative integration of AI and robotics into public service roles, functioning as a civil servant within the city council, highlighting the potential for robots to take on administrative duties traditionally performed by humans.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Gumi City Council, the citizens of South Korea who benefit from enhanced administrative efficiency, and the broader public service sector observing the integration of AI in government roles.

name: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Improve Public Service Delivery
description: The project aims to enhance public service delivery by using AI to improve efficiency, transparency, responsiveness, and citizen satisfaction. It involves adopting AI-driven solutions across sectors like health, education, social protection, and urban services.
novelty: The project is innovative in its structured approach to integrating AI in public services, focusing on ethical, responsible, and inclusive AI adoption to transform service delivery.
stakeholders: Primary beneficiaries are government ministries, departments, local authorities, and citizens, especially women, youth, and marginalized groups. Secondary beneficiaries include technology providers, academic institutions, and civil society organizations.

name: Federal AI Implementation Strategies
description: Federal agencies are transitioning from AI experimentation to real-world implementation, focusing on strategy, governance, and measurable mission impact. The initiative includes building AI roadmaps, incorporating generative and agentic AI, and preparing workforce, data, and infrastructure for future AI developments.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into federal operations, including the development of AI roadmaps and the incorporation of the latest AI executive orders.
stakeholders: Federal government agencies, government and industry leaders, and the federal workforce are the primary stakeholders and beneficiaries of this innovation.

name: AI-Driven Regulation Drafting
description: The US Department of Transportation plans to use artificial intelligence to draft federal transport regulations, aiming to streamline the process and reduce the time it takes to move from an idea to a complete draft regulation for review.
novelty: The DoT is the first federal agency fully enabled to use AI for drafting regulations, potentially setting a precedent for other government departments.
stakeholders: DoT employees, US Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs, federal government departments, and potentially the general public benefiting from improved transportation regulations.

name: Presidential Management Agenda (PMA)
description: The PMA is a blueprint for using emerging technology, particularly AI, to improve mission delivery and citizen services by breaking down bureaucratic barriers and encouraging experimentation. It aims to create a unified digital experience for citizens interacting with government services.
novelty: The PMA shifts focus from compliance-based regimes to a culture of innovation and experimentation, empowering federal employees to use AI tools without needing prior permission, and creating a unified digital front door for government services.
stakeholders: Federal employees, taxpayers, citizens interacting with government services, agency-level service delivery leads.

name: Drone Box project
description: The Drone Box project is an advanced smart platform that enables the automated deployment of unmanned aerial vehicles from strategically distributed stations across Dubai, significantly reducing response times to traffic and criminal incidents by transmitting live video feeds directly to the Command and Control Centre.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its rapid response capability, autonomous drone operations, beyond visual line of sight live streaming, and advanced sensor technologies, which enhance security and traffic patrols without direct human intervention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Dubai Police as the implementers, the residents of Dubai as beneficiaries, and the General Department of Operations as developers.

name: City-wide Operations Improvement Plan
description: A comprehensive plan to reshape how Detroit residents and businesses interact with city government, focusing on improving customer service, efficiency, equity, and innovation in accessing basic services and navigating city processes.
novelty: The project leverages data, AI, human-centered design, and cutting-edge technology to solve complex problems and improve service delivery, making city operations more customer-centric and efficient.
stakeholders: Detroit residents and businesses are the primary beneficiaries. The plan involves 17 city departments and engages with the workforce, business community, and residents for feedback and collaboration.

name: Technology Quality Assurance Board (TQAB)
description: The Technology Quality Assurance Board (TQAB) is established to ensure the responsible, secure, and effective deployment of emerging technologies across Alabama executive-branch agencies. It aims to guide the adoption of technologies like artificial intelligence, ensuring they are used securely, ethically, and in alignment with public interest.
novelty: The TQAB is innovative as it creates a centralized governance body to evaluate and guide the use of generative AI and other novel technologies in state government, ensuring cybersecurity, privacy, and operational excellence.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Alabama executive-branch agencies such as the Alabama Office of Information Technology, Department of Finance, Law Enforcement Agency, Department of Education, Medicaid Agency, Department of Revenue, and Department of Transportation. Beneficiaries are the citizens of Alabama who will experience enhanced government services.

name: Responsible AI Safety and Education (RAISE) Act
description: The RAISE Act is a regulatory framework for large frontier AI developers in New York, imposing transparency, safety, and incident-reporting obligations, along with civil penalties for non-compliance. It aims to regulate development practices and ensure safety in AI deployment.
novelty: The RAISE Act introduces stringent safety protocols and incident reporting requirements, including a 72-hour incident reporting window, and establishes a new governance structure, the Office of Digital Innovation, Governance, Integrity, and Trust (DIGIT), to centralize AI oversight.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include large AI developers with over $500 million in annual revenue, the New York attorney general, and the Department of Financial Services. Beneficiaries are the general public and industries relying on safe AI deployment.

name: Responsible AI Training for DC Government Employees
description: A mandatory Responsible AI training for all DC Government employees and contractors, aimed at equipping them to use generative AI tools safely and effectively, reinforcing responsible innovation, oversight, and accountability.
novelty: First major US city to implement a comprehensive framework for responsible AI use in city government, including a mandatory training program for all government employees and contractors.
stakeholders: DC Government employees and contractors, DC residents, AI Taskforce, AI Values Alignment Advisory Group

name: AI Grand Challenges Act of 2026
description: The AI Grand Challenges Act of 2026 aims to create a program to incentivize new research in AI fields, focusing on areas like national security, cybersecurity, health, energy, transportation, manufacturing, quantum computing, and materials science. It includes a mandated health-AI contest for cancer detection.
novelty: The project is innovative as it establishes a structured program of award competitions to drive AI research and development across multiple critical policy areas, involving collaboration between various federal agencies and the private sector.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include AI researchers, developers, and potentially the general public benefiting from advancements in AI applications. Stakeholders engaged include the National Science Foundation, National Institutes of Health, National Artificial Intelligence Advisory Committee, and private sector judges.

name: Oracle Permitting and Licensing (OPAL)
description: Oracle Permitting and Licensing (OPAL) is being implemented by the City of Atlanta to modernize city planning, zoning, permitting, code enforcement, and inspections. This system aims to streamline citizen services, reduce processing times, and support economic growth by automating key workflows and making services more accessible for residents and businesses.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of Oracle's AI-powered cloud applications to create a unified IT environment that enhances transparency, automates workflows, and minimizes approval bottlenecks, thereby improving efficiency and accessibility of city services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the City of Atlanta staff, residents, businesses, and Deloitte as the technology partner. Beneficiaries are the citizens and businesses in Atlanta who will experience improved and more efficient city services.

name: OneGov deals and USAi
description: An initiative to increase the affordability and accessibility of top-tier AI software, as well as simplifying the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to reduce IT barriers and enhance cybersecurity standards.
novelty: The project introduces innovative procurement strategies to make advanced AI software more accessible and affordable for government agencies, while also streamlining regulatory processes and improving cybersecurity.
stakeholders: Government agencies, GSA employees, technology providers, and the public benefiting from improved government services.

name: Zero-Bureaucracy Programme
description: The zero-bureaucracy programme aims to eliminate unnecessary bureaucracy and streamline government procedures and processes in public services using artificial intelligence. It has significantly reduced the time required for services, with some processes now taking less than 10 minutes instead of a week.
novelty: The programme leverages AI technologies like facial recognition and optical character recognition to automate and expedite government processes, drastically reducing service times and eliminating queues, as seen in smart gates at airports.
stakeholders: The primary beneficiaries are UAE citizens and residents who interact with public services. Stakeholders include government officials, technology providers, and international collaborators engaged through platforms like the World Government Summit.

name: AI for Development (AI4D) programme
description: The AI for Development (AI4D) programme is a UK initiative aimed at ensuring developing countries benefit fully from the AI revolution. It includes projects like the Asian AI4D Observatory and the Masakhane African Languages Hub, which support responsible AI innovation and governance, and make AI accessible in 40 African languages.
novelty: The programme focuses on making AI inclusive and accessible, particularly in developing regions, by supporting responsible AI innovation and governance, and addressing language barriers with the Masakhane African Languages Hub.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK Government, international partners, tech firms, and beneficiaries in developing countries, particularly in Africa and Asia, who will gain access to AI technologies and innovations.

name: AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service
description: The AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service aims to modernize and streamline AI adoption across Canadian federal departments. It includes initiatives like GCtranslate, an AI-powered translation tool, and ParlBrief, an AI tool for transcribing and summarizing parliamentary meetings. The strategy focuses on responsible AI adoption, training, and creating a centralized AI registry to track AI initiatives.
novelty: The strategy introduces whole-of-government AI tools and a centralized AI registry to identify duplication and streamline AI initiatives across departments. It emphasizes responsible AI adoption and includes comprehensive training programs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canadian federal departments, public servants, the Canadian Digital Service, Shared Services Canada, and the Canada School of Public Service. Beneficiaries are public servants and Canadian citizens who will experience improved public services.

name: Sovereign Technology Alliance
organisation: Government of Canada, Government of Germany
description: The Sovereign Technology Alliance is a collaborative initiative between Canada and Germany aimed at strengthening cooperation on advanced technologies, particularly artificial intelligence (AI). It focuses on expanding secure compute infrastructure, accelerating AI research and commercialization, and strengthening talent development to address critical skills gaps.
novelty: The Alliance is innovative in its approach to creating a practical framework for bilateral cooperation in AI, emphasizing secure, resilient, and sovereign AI capabilities while reducing strategic technology dependencies.
stakeholders: Researchers, startups, industries in Canada and Germany, and leading research organizations such as Canada's LawZero.

name: Integration of ChatGPT into GenAI.mil
description: The U.S. War Department has partnered with OpenAI to integrate ChatGPT into its enterprise AI platform, GenAI.mil, to expand access to advanced large language models across the joint force. This integration aims to enhance mission execution and readiness by embedding generative AI into defense operations.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves integrating a cutting-edge AI model, ChatGPT, into a secure, mission-ready AI environment for defense operations, marking a significant step towards an AI-first enterprise in the defense sector.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the U.S. War Department, OpenAI, and the three million Department personnel who will use the platform. Beneficiaries are the military services and defense workforce who will integrate AI into their daily workflows.

name: India AI Impact Summit
organisation: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change
description: The India AI Impact Summit focuses on demonstrating how AI can be safely and effectively used to improve public services and deliver tangible benefits to citizens. It emphasizes the importance of building digital public infrastructure and embedding AI into essential services to enhance public value.
novelty: The summit highlights the potential for low- and middle-income countries to leapfrog by adopting AI at scale, leveraging fewer legacy systems and digitally confident populations to build state capacity for the digital era.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include governments, public institutions, industry partners, and citizens who benefit from improved public services and infrastructure.

name: Next Generation IT Services (NEXT)
description: The NEXT program aims to establish approximately six blanket purchase agreements (BPAs) to support enterprise-level information technology services across multiple support areas, providing flexible, scalable, and outcome-based IT solutions for evolving mission needs.
novelty: The innovation lies in the creation of flexible and scalable BPAs that allow for on and off-ramping, supporting a wide range of IT services including DevSecOps, digital services, cloud infrastructure, identity verification, fraud detection, and AI strategy, with an emphasis on quick, low-dollar value awards and reaching new technology companies.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies requiring IT services, vendors and contractors who will provide these services, and the GSA which manages the agreements. Beneficiaries are the government agencies and their missions supported by these IT solutions.

name: AI in Public Administration
description: The UK government is embedding AI across public administration to achieve better outcomes at lower costs. This includes AI tools for VAT anomaly detection, chatbots for public service triage, and risk assessment models for welfare benefits.
novelty: The innovation lies in the large-scale deployment of AI tools in public administration, supported by a government-led transparency register and a shift towards a bespoke legislative framework for AI governance.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public service recipients, government departments like the Department of Work and Pensions, and regulatory bodies. Stakeholders also include the Law Commission and the judiciary.

name: IndiaAI Mission
description: The IndiaAI Mission is a government initiative supported by NVIDIA, aiming to enhance India's AI ecosystem through significant investments in AI infrastructure, development of sovereign AI datasets, and fostering AI education and startup innovation. It includes building AI cloud offerings with NVIDIA GPUs and developing multilingual AI models for public services and enterprise applications.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its large-scale integration of NVIDIA's advanced AI technologies to build sovereign AI infrastructure and models tailored for India's multilingual context, supporting a wide range of applications from public services to enterprise solutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include Indian enterprises, public sector customers, startups, researchers, and the general population. Stakeholders engaged include government agencies, educational institutions, cloud providers, and AI-native companies.

name: Generative AI Decision Support Tool
organisation: University of Eastern Finland, Aalto University
description: A large language model (LLM)-based generative AI decision support tool designed to help claims specialists navigate complex, scattered, and ever-changing guidance documents in public administration.
novelty: The innovation is sustained through nine justificatory frames that emphasize efficiency, cost savings, employee well-being, fairness, and desirability, even when the tool underperforms. It also involves effective boundary work to build alliances and reconfigure organizational boundaries.
stakeholders: Public sector innovators, managers, consultants, claims specialists, and public administration users are engaged in the innovation.

name: Connecticut Tech Talent Accelerator (TTA 3.0)
organisation: Connecticut Office of Workforce Strategy, Business-Higher Education Forum, New England Board of Higher Education
description: The Connecticut Tech Talent Accelerator (TTA 3.0) is a statewide higher education innovation challenge aimed at expanding artificial intelligence (AI) education and workforce training. It involves partnerships between seven colleges and universities and 12 business partners to integrate AI skills into curricula, offer microcredentials, and deliver tech training.
novelty: TTA 3.0 is innovative in its approach by embedding AI competencies into diverse educational programs across multiple institutions, creating industry-inspired microcredentials, and fostering collaboration between higher education and business to align curricula with industry needs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Connecticut's colleges and universities, business partners, students, workers, and employers across the state who benefit from a skilled AI workforce.

name: Accountable Innovation in AI
description: Oklahoma has implemented AI to modernize government operations, aiming to reduce workforce size and increase efficiency. The state uses AI for tasks like auditing procurement data and improving employee productivity.
novelty: Oklahoma is one of the first states to appoint a chief AI officer and release task force recommendations for AI use, focusing on 'accountable innovation' to balance modernization with safety and transparency.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state government employees, agency heads, and the general public who benefit from more efficient government services.

name: Global AI Sprint
description: A global initiative by UNDP to support over 50 countries in integrating AI into public systems. It involves AI Landscape Assessments, trust and safety support, initiatives on low-resource language data, capacity-building for governments, and targeted support for small and vulnerable states.
novelty: The initiative focuses on embedding AI into existing public systems through practical pathways, rather than standalone AI adoption. It emphasizes equitable AI distribution and addresses the AI Equity Gap by considering linguistic and cultural diversity.
stakeholders: Governments, technical partners, local stakeholders, small and vulnerable states, and communities in over 50 countries.

name: CoCounsel Legal
description: CoCounsel Legal is a professional-grade AI solution designed for government legal professionals. It ensures that sensitive government data is never used to train AI models and maintains absolute data containment to prevent leaks. It meets rigorous federal security requirements while providing AI capabilities needed for government legal operations.
novelty: CoCounsel Legal is innovative because it is specifically built to meet federal compliance and security standards, ensuring zero customer data training and absolute data containment, which are critical for handling sensitive government information.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government legal professionals, federal courts, and agencies handling sensitive legal matters and national security issues.

name: Digital Procurement Modernization
description: The City of Somerville transitioned from a paper-based to a digital procurement process to improve efficiency, compliance, and accessibility. This modernization allows for electronic bid submissions, reducing physical storage needs and administrative tasks.
novelty: The innovation lies in transitioning a century-old paper-based procurement process to a digital system, enhancing compliance, reducing barriers for vendors, and laying the groundwork for AI integration in procurement.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local government procurement staff, vendors (especially small and local businesses), and the broader community benefiting from improved government efficiency and transparency.

name: AI Cybersecurity and Risk Management Initiative
description: A public-private initiative aimed at strengthening cybersecurity and risk management for AI in the U.S. financial services sector. It involves the development of resources to enable secure and resilient AI adoption across the financial system.
novelty: The initiative focuses on practical implementation tools for AI-specific cybersecurity risks, emphasizing collaboration between government and industry to support secure AI adoption and innovation in the financial sector.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include financial institutions, particularly small and mid-sized ones, as well as federal and state financial regulators. Key stakeholders include the Financial and Banking Information Infrastructure Committee and the Financial Services Sector Coordinating Council.

name: Modernization of Ohio's Government Technology Systems
organisation: State of Ohio, Ohio Department of Health, Ohio Job and Family Services
description: The innovation involves modernizing legacy systems in Ohio's government services to make them more reliable and intuitive, particularly for citizens in crisis. This includes streamlining the technology stack and implementing AI-driven solutions to enhance service delivery and infrastructure.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to modernizing government technology, including AI-driven service delivery and workforce transformation, aiming to make government services as intuitive and reliable as consumer technology.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are Ohio citizens, particularly those in crisis situations, as well as government employees who are being re-skilled to adapt to new technologies. Emerging tech companies in Ohio are also stakeholders due to the state's push to attract them.

name: Agentic AI in Government Workflows
organisation: Google, New York City Metropolitan Transportation Authority, City of Los Angeles
description: Agentic AI is applied to government workflows to enhance efficiency and responsiveness by automating tasks such as caseworker support, inspection scheduling, meeting preparation, and financial management. It helps in identifying missing information, drafting reports, and optimizing resources.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI to autonomously manage and streamline government processes, leveraging AI's ability to learn from data and take autonomous actions while ensuring ethical governance.
stakeholders: Government agencies, caseworkers, inspection teams, financial management teams, city employees, and constituents.

name: Citizen Relationship Management (CzRM) system
description: The Citizen Relationship Management (CzRM) system is a unified platform designed to consolidate data, streamline processes, and enable omnichannel communication for citizen interactions in the Autonomous Province of Bolzano—South Tyrol. It supports service delivery in Italian and German and integrates Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Service and Dynamics 365 Contact Center to provide a centralized view of citizen interactions and efficient case management.
novelty: The innovation lies in its integration of multiple siloed systems into a single platform, enabling omnichannel communication and real-time data sharing, which enhances service delivery and citizen engagement.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the citizens of Bolzano, public administration employees, and the Autonomous Province of Bolzano—South Tyrol, who benefit from streamlined services and improved data management.

name: Artificial Intelligence Lexicon and Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (FS AI RMF)
description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury released the Artificial Intelligence Lexicon and the Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework to guide AI use in the financial sector. These resources aim to establish common terminology and a tailored framework for managing AI risks, supporting responsible innovation and consumer protection.
novelty: The innovation provides a shared language and a risk management framework specifically tailored to the financial sector, facilitating clearer communication and consistent AI implementation across institutions.
stakeholders: Financial institutions, regulators, technology providers, federal and state regulators, industry leaders, and other stakeholders in the financial sector.

name: Procurii
description: Procurii is a chatbot designed to assist procurement staff in Mississippi by helping them quickly find and interpret relevant policies, procedures, and regulations.
