It provides answers from official Department of Information Technology Services materials and tracks conversation context, flags policy updates, and follows a structured retraining process.
novelty: Procurii is innovative due to its retrieval-based architecture tailored to state procurement documentation, its ability to track conversation context, and its potential to be adapted for other compliance-driven government use cases.
stakeholders: The primary users are procurement professionals within Mississippi's Department of Information Technology Services. Beneficiaries include other government agencies and potentially other states if the tool is adapted for broader use.

name: AI Lexicon and Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (RMF)
description: The Treasury Department has issued resources to help financial institutions, particularly small and mid-sized ones, harness AI to strengthen cyber defenses and deploy AI securely. The resources include an AI Lexicon and a Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (RMF) that complements the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s AI RMF.
novelty: The innovation lies in the development of AI-specific tools and frameworks that address the unique challenges posed by AI in the financial sector, enabling secure AI adoption and increasing the resilience of the financial system.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include financial institutions of all sizes, particularly small and mid-sized ones, as well as federal and state financial regulators. Stakeholders engaged include the Financial and Banking Information Infrastructure Committee, the Financial Services Sector Coordinating Council, and senior executives from financial institutions.

name: IRS AI Modernization
description: The IRS deployed AI to improve taxpayer service, compliance, and operational efficiency. This included using domain-specific AI to enhance the taxpayer service hotline, reducing response times from 28 minutes to three, and implementing custom AI for case management to prevent and recover billions in fraud and improper payments.
novelty: The innovation lies in the IRS's strategic use of AI across three lanes: general-purpose AI for productivity, domain-specific AI for precision, and custom AI for unique challenges, tailored to the specific needs of tax administration.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include IRS employees, taxpayers, and the broader public who benefit from improved service efficiency and reduced fraud.

name: Agile Governance for AI
description: Agile governance is a model introduced to allow AI projects to progress rapidly by reducing bureaucratic delays. It involves a small, empowered team that meets bi-weekly to oversee data governance, data readiness, and AI initiatives, enabling quicker decision-making and adaptation to new developments.
novelty: The innovation lies in its agile approach, allowing for rapid decision-making and adaptation in AI projects, contrasting with traditional, slower bureaucratic processes.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the governance team members, AI project teams, and ultimately the federal agencies benefiting from improved AI project implementation.

name: AI Service Hubs
description: AI service hubs connect conversational AI directly to back-office systems, allowing citizens to complete transactions in a single interaction, improving accessibility and providing consistent service regardless of location or communication method.
novelty: AI service hubs go beyond traditional chatbots by enabling end-to-end transaction completion, integrating conversational AI with back-office systems, and supporting multilingual and adaptive accessibility features.
stakeholders: Citizens, government agencies, rural residents, people with disabilities, and anyone who cannot easily visit a government office during business hours.

name: Kansas Task Force on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies
description: The Kansas Task Force on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies is proposed to study the impacts, risks, workforce implications, and governance considerations associated with AI and related technologies. It aims to provide findings and recommendations to inform future legislative or administrative action.
novelty: The task force is innovative in its comprehensive approach to studying AI impacts and its advisory role in shaping future legislative actions, with a structured membership and reporting requirements.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include legislators, the executive branch, the attorney general’s office, higher education institutions, and private sector representatives. The task force engages these stakeholders to build a knowledge base and provide informed recommendations.

name: Behavioral Health IT Initiative
description: The Behavioral Health IT Initiative aims to standardize behavioral health data and reduce provider reporting burden, enhancing access, quality, and measurable outcomes in behavioral health care.
novelty: The initiative introduces standardization of behavioral health data and integrates modern EHR and AI capabilities to improve care delivery and outcomes.
stakeholders: Providers, health IT implementers, policymakers, and innovators in the behavioral health sector.

name: Genesis Mission Consortium
description: The Genesis Mission Consortium is a public-private partnership launched by the U.S. Department of Energy to accelerate scientific discovery through artificial intelligence and strengthen American leadership in emerging technologies. It aims to apply AI tools across research and innovation efforts to speed up breakthroughs in energy, science, and national security.
novelty: The project is innovative because it creates a coordinated framework for collaboration between national laboratories, private industry, and academic institutions, focusing on AI model development, data integration, high-performance computing, and robotics.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include national laboratories, private industry, academic institutions, researchers, and industry partners. Beneficiaries are expected to be the scientific community and sectors involved in energy, science, and national security.

name: Equifax Social Services Outlook Index
description: The Equifax Social Services Outlook Index is a survey conducted to assess the readiness and advocacy of federal, state, and local government social service workers for a shift towards digital-first services. It highlights the potential for automation and technology to improve efficiency, accountability, and service delivery in social services, particularly in managing eligibility and benefits for programs like SNAP.
novelty: The innovation lies in leveraging automation and instant data integration to streamline social service processes, reduce manual bottlenecks, and improve program integrity, especially in the context of modern income complexities like gig economy work.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal, state, and local government social service workers, benefit recipients, taxpayers, and agencies managing programs like SNAP.

name: Artificial Intelligence Bill of Rights
description: The Artificial Intelligence Bill of Rights is a legislative proposal in Florida that aims to regulate the use of AI by making it illegal to use a person’s name, image, or likeness without consent, requiring parental permission for minors to engage with chatbots, and mandating that chatbots identify themselves as non-human.
novelty: The bill addresses privacy and consent issues related to AI, focusing on user safety and ethical AI usage, which is a growing concern as AI technology becomes more prevalent.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Florida residents, particularly minors and their parents, tech companies operating in Florida, and state legislators.

name: Future of AI Innovation Act
organisation: Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation
description: The Future of AI Innovation Act aims to develop voluntary guidelines and standards for AI in industry and the public sector. It includes provisions for creating a testbed program, a prize competition to incentivize innovation, and making curated datasets publicly available for AI applications.
novelty: The project is innovative as it brings together private sector and government experts to develop voluntary AI standards, create assessment tools, and conduct testing to ensure the U.S. leads in AI-driven innovation and competitiveness.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include industry professionals, government agencies, and the public sector. Stakeholders engaged include the National Institute of Standards and Technology, the Department of Energy, the National Science Foundation, and various industry and advocacy organizations.

name: Bailey B.
description: Bailey B. is an AI-powered chatbot designed to help renters in Florida understand their rights and navigate housing issues. It provides guidance on evictions, landlord-tenant disputes, and basic filings, offering 24/7 assistance, document drafting support, and connections to local legal resources.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to provide Florida-specific legal guidance using attorney-reviewed content, expanding access to legal aid for renters through a web-based tool.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are renters in Florida, particularly those facing housing issues. Stakeholders include Bay Area Legal Services and local legal resources that the chatbot connects users to.

name: Future of AI Innovation Act
organisation: National Institutes of Standards and Technology
description: The Future of AI Innovation Act aims to establish uniform standards for AI research and development, promote innovation in the private sector, and support U.S. leadership in AI through public-private partnerships, codification of the Center for Artificial Intelligence Standards and Innovation, increased interagency coordination, and international coalitions.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on creating voluntary standards for AI, developing new assessment tools, and conducting testing to ensure the U.S. leads in AI-driven innovation and competitiveness.
stakeholders: Private sector and government experts, companies, consumers, and the National Institutes of Standards and Technology.

name: Republic of Korea Artificial Intelligence Action Plan
description: The Republic of Korea Artificial Intelligence Action Plan is a comprehensive strategy designed to embed AI across government, industry, and society from 2026 to 2028. It includes 99 execution tasks and over 300 policy recommendations to drive national growth through AI by building an innovation ecosystem, transforming foundational infrastructure, and expanding global contributions.
novelty: The plan outlines a long-term framework for coordinated national AI policy, focusing on building an innovation ecosystem, transforming infrastructure, and expanding global contributions. It includes initiatives like AI computing and data resources expansion, AI talent development, regulatory reform, and industry-specific AI models.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, private-sector experts, representatives from multiple ministries, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services and infrastructure.

name: Technology Quality Assurance Board (TQAB)
description: The Technology Quality Assurance Board (TQAB) is established to oversee the secure and responsible deployment of emerging technologies across Alabama state government agencies. It aims to ensure technology use is innovative, secure, ethical, and aligned with public interest, focusing on cybersecurity, privacy protections, and operational effectiveness.
novelty: The TQAB is innovative as it provides a formal governance structure specifically for evaluating and monitoring the use of generative AI and other digital tools in state operations, fulfilling recommendations from the Governor’s Task Force on Generative Artificial Intelligence.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include representatives from the Alabama Office of Information Technology, Department of Finance, Law Enforcement Agency, Department of Education, Medicaid Agency, Department of Revenue, and Department of Transportation. Beneficiaries are the state agencies and citizens who will experience improved public services.

name: AI Center for Civic and Social Good
organisation: City of San José, San José State University
description: The AI Center for Civic and Social Good is a public space located at the Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Library in San José. It expands access to artificial intelligence education tools and research for residents, students, and workers, aiming to ensure emerging technology is accessible, inclusive, and available to all.
novelty: The center is the first-of-its-kind public space that combines civic leadership and academic expertise within a joint city-university library to provide AI education and research opportunities.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include nearly a million residents of San José, the SJSU campus community of nearly 40,000 students and faculty, and organizations like The Tech Interactive and LEGO Education. Beneficiaries are students, library cardholders, and the general public who gain access to AI literacy programs and resources.

name: Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery Services with FedRAMP support
description: Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery Services now supports FedRAMP authorization, allowing federal agencies to modernize their digital experiences while maintaining compliance with federal security standards. This innovation enables agencies to improve service delivery, strengthen security, and reduce operational overhead by deploying a modern, responsive, and scalable architecture.
novelty: The innovation lies in combining FedRAMP compliance with a modern CDN-based architecture that shifts delivery closer to the edge, reducing infrastructure complexity and improving performance. It also introduces AI-driven modernization, allowing rapid migration from legacy CMS solutions, and offers flexible authoring options to enhance operational agility.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are federal agencies, including those serving citizens, veterans, and military personnel. Beneficiaries include government employees responsible for digital content management and the public who access these services. Adobe and its partners are also stakeholders, providing ongoing support and integration services.

name: AI-Driven Resident Support Initiative
description: An initiative that uses artificial intelligence to interpret data about residents, such as identifying families falling behind on water bills, to offer them services and support proactively, preventing larger issues.
novelty: The project integrates AI technology to proactively deliver city services based on resident data, aiming to meet residents' needs before issues escalate.
stakeholders: Residents of South Bend, particularly those at risk of falling behind on utility payments, and the city government.

name: Project R.E.D. (Recovery Exploitation Drone)
description: Project R.E.D. utilizes a drone equipped with AI-enabled object-recognition software to identify downed enemy or friendly drones. It features a robotic, 3D-printed, carbon fiber arm with a claw to grab the downed drone and fly it back for intelligence exploitation or repair.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI-enabled object-recognition software with a robotic arm on a drone, allowing for the recovery and exploitation of downed drones on the battlefield.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the soldiers of the Pennsylvania National Guard and potentially other military units. Stakeholders include the Pennsylvania Department of Military and Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Army.

name: Digital Ireland – Connecting our People, Securing our Future
description: A comprehensive strategy to expand digital public services, strengthen AI adoption, and position Ireland as a global hub for applied AI innovation. It includes objectives like full digitalization of key public services by 2030, establishment of an AI Advisory Unit, and creation of a large-scale AI research center.
novelty: The strategy's innovative approach includes establishing a National AI Fellowship program for civil servants, launching a GovTech 2026 Challenge, and creating a regulatory sandbox for testing new AI systems.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include businesses, workers, and communities across Ireland. Stakeholders engaged include government departments, agencies, and the private sector.

name: FUZE Innovation Engine
description: FUZE is an innovation engine designed to accelerate the development and deployment of emerging military technologies by funding prototypes quickly and scaling successful technologies in months instead of years. It aims to bridge the gap between early prototyping and fully fielded capabilities, focusing on technologies like artificial intelligence and autonomous systems.
novelty: FUZE combines venture capital-style agility with rapid operational feedback, allowing for a 'fail fast' approach that departs from traditional, risk-averse military acquisition processes. It emphasizes rapid prototyping and real-world testing to ensure technologies meet operational needs.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the United States Army, including soldiers who benefit from faster deployment of new technologies, and technology companies that participate in the innovation process. Forward-deployed acquisition officers in Europe, Africa, and the Pacific also play a role in providing operational feedback.

name: AI Governance Frameworks in City Government
description: Minerva led initiatives in city government involving free Wi-Fi expansion, open data legislation, privacy laws, and early artificial intelligence (AI) governance frameworks. These initiatives aimed to improve public services and governance through technology.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into city governance, including the development of early AI governance frameworks and the seamless integration of new technologies across departments.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include city residents and government departments such as fire and police departments, as well as 311 and 911 systems. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include city government officials and technology leaders.

name: AI-native public infrastructure
organisation: Various cities including Singapore, Barcelona, Copenhagen, Amsterdam, Los Angeles, New York City, Dubai, Pittsburgh, Hangzhou, Seoul
description: AI-native public infrastructure transforms city management by integrating AI, sensing, and data infrastructure to manage complex systems like transport, energy, and water as continuously operating systems. This approach allows for real-time decision-making and autonomous operations, improving efficiency and reducing disruptions.
novelty: The innovation lies in shifting from traditional smart-city models to AI-native models where technology not only observes but also decides and acts autonomously within policy constraints. This enables cities to operate like distributed computing systems with real-time data processing and autonomous control.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include city residents and municipal service providers. Stakeholders engaged include city governments, technology providers, and infrastructure management entities.

name: Canada's Renewed AI Strategy
description: The Government of Canada conducted a national consultation to develop a renewed AI strategy, focusing on ethical AI research, transparent governance, sovereign infrastructure, national AI literacy, and security frameworks. The strategy aims to shape AI governance, support, and deployment across Canada's economy and public sector.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive national consultation process involving over 11,000 Canadians and a diverse AI Strategy Task Force, emphasizing a balanced approach to AI that integrates innovation with sovereignty, ethics, and public trust.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canadian citizens, industry professionals, academia, government agencies, and members of the AI Strategy Task Force. Beneficiaries are expected to be the Canadian public and industries adopting AI.

name: AI Tools for Workforce Optimization
description: The General Services Administration (GSA) is implementing AI tools to optimize its workforce after experiencing a significant reduction in staff. The aim is to automate repetitive processes, allowing employees to focus on strategic tasks and improve productivity.
novelty: The innovation lies in the strategic use of AI to enhance workforce efficiency and productivity, particularly in a government setting that has experienced significant staffing cuts.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the remaining GSA employees who will benefit from increased productivity and efficiency. The broader federal government and taxpayers are also stakeholders, as the initiative aims to save money and improve service delivery.

name: AI-powered Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)
description: AI-powered Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) is used to enhance the handling of state program data, improving speed and precision in agency workflows by integrating physical and digital information.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to process different types of forms, various data formats, and unstructured data without needing to train on every possible variation, unlike traditional OCR.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include state government programs and the people they serve, with stakeholders being the agencies implementing these workflows.

name: Oracle Permitting and Licensing
description: Oracle Permitting and Licensing (OPAL) is implemented by the City of Miami to improve public service delivery and simplify processes for local residents and businesses. It consolidates services from multiple departments into a single system, automating review processes and offering accessible services.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its integration of AI-powered cloud applications to automate and streamline permitting processes, reducing approval times and increasing efficiency in municipal services.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are local residents and businesses in Miami. Stakeholders include the City of Miami government, Oracle Corporation, and city employees involved in permitting processes.

name: Oracle Permitting and Licensing (OPAL)
description: Oracle Permitting and Licensing (OPAL) is implemented by the City of Miami to improve public service delivery and simplify processes for local residents and businesses. It consolidates services from multiple departments into a single system, automating review processes and offering accessible services to reduce delays and complexity in permitting.
novelty: The innovation lies in its integration of AI-powered cloud applications to automate and streamline permitting processes, reducing approval times and optimizing technology spend while enhancing transparency and progress tracking.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the City of Miami, its residents, local businesses, and city employees who benefit from improved permitting processes and service delivery.

name: AI Data Quality Protocol
description: The AI Data Quality Protocol is a series of practical actions that Texas agencies can take to prepare their data for AI applications. It includes employing purpose-built solutions to address technical issues like duplicative records and defining fairness metrics for AI-driven decisions, with the aim of improving data quality and reliability for analytics and AI models.
novelty: The innovation lies in its structured approach to improving data quality specifically for AI applications, including defining fairness metrics and publishing results quarterly to enhance public trust.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Texas government entities, public sector agencies, and the general public who benefit from improved government services through better data management and AI applications.

name: Sovereign Technology Alliance
organisation: Federal Economic Development Agency for Southern Ontario, Norwegian Ministry for Digitalisation and Public Governance
description: The Sovereign Technology Alliance aims to deepen coordination between Canada and Norway to strengthen sovereign AI capacity and reduce strategic technology dependencies in key sectors. It focuses on expanding AI compute capacity, promoting sustainable digital infrastructure, and fostering AI adoption across industries and governments.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to international collaboration on AI, focusing on sovereign capacity building and reducing strategic dependencies, while promoting sustainable and responsible digital infrastructure.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include AI researchers, technology companies, industries, and government agencies in Canada and Norway. Stakeholders engaged include the Federal Economic Development Agency for Southern Ontario and the Norwegian Ministry for Digitalisation and Public Governance.

name: AI Integration in Raleigh's City Services
description: Raleigh, N.C. has expanded its use of AI across city services, including internal chatbots for city employees, traffic analysis systems, and experimental AI in municipal services. The city is testing AI to automate IT service requests and analyzing traffic patterns using visual language models.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to not only respond to queries but to perform tasks autonomously, such as managing IT service requests and analyzing traffic patterns with generative AI capabilities.
stakeholders: City employees, residents of Raleigh, city planners, IT department, Solid Waste Services department, and regional cybersecurity collaborators.

name: Futureproofing Local Operations for Water Systems (FLOWS) Act
description: The FLOWS Act is a bipartisan bill aimed at helping small and rural water utilities adopt modern cybersecurity protections and digital monitoring tools. It proposes a federal grant program to assist rural communities in upgrading water, wastewater, and stormwater systems with updated industrial control systems and modern technologies like real-time sensing tools, AI software, and advanced modeling platforms.
novelty: The innovation lies in providing federal grants without requiring local funding matches, enabling small utilities with limited resources to adopt advanced digital tools and cybersecurity measures, thus improving system reliability and resilience.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are small and rural water utilities, their operators, and the communities they serve. The Environmental Protection Agency and federal government are also key stakeholders in implementing the grant program.

name: VA Digital Modernization and AI Initiatives
description: The VA technology team modernized VA.gov into a veteran-focused digital platform, launched a flagship mobile application, expanded access to generative AI tools, deployed clinical decision support tools, and used AI-powered document processing to eliminate a backlog.
novelty: The integration of AI into VA systems, including generative AI tools for employees, clinical decision support tools, and AI-powered document processing, represents a significant technological shift in public service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include veterans, VA employees, clinicians, and developers. Stakeholders engaged include the Department of Veterans Affairs and its technology team.

name: Stanwell Modelling Platform (SMP)
description: The Stanwell Modelling Platform (SMP) is an AI-powered platform designed to optimize energy generation and trading decisions. It integrates AI into Stanwell's operations to improve grid reliability, support affordable electricity, and enhance decision-making processes across the organization.
novelty: SMP is innovative because it uses AI to significantly improve decision-making speed and accuracy in energy trading and demand forecasting, transforming complex data into actionable insights in real-time. It enhances battery performance by over 200% and improves forecast accuracy by 30%.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Stanwell Corporation's employees, energy traders, engineers, analysts, and ultimately, the electricity consumers in Queensland who benefit from more stable and affordable energy prices.

name: Cary's AI-powered Digital Twin
description: The Town of Cary, in collaboration with Lenovo, is developing an AI-powered digital twin to create a Cognitive Community. This digital twin will help the town adapt in real-time to community needs, improve governance, and enhance citizen engagement.
novelty: The innovation lies in building a digital twin from the ground up, rather than purchasing a vendor solution, and integrating AI to create a responsive, citizen-centric governance model.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Town of Cary's local government, its residents, and Lenovo as the technology partner.

name: AI Research Lab
description: The UK government plans to launch an AI research lab aimed at supporting bold and high-risk innovation in artificial intelligence.
novelty: The lab focuses on fostering high-risk AI innovations, which is a unique approach to encourage groundbreaking advancements in the field.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI researchers, innovators, and potentially the broader public who may benefit from advancements in AI technologies.

name: Colorado AI Act
description: The Colorado AI Act is a comprehensive framework for governing artificial intelligence, focusing on preventing algorithmic discrimination in areas like hiring, housing, and government services. The updated framework requires developers to disclose key details about their AI systems and mandates businesses and government agencies to notify individuals when AI is used in decision-making processes.
novelty: The innovation lies in its comprehensive approach to AI governance, being one of the first frameworks to address algorithmic discrimination and clarify responsibilities between developers and deployers of AI systems.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include technology companies, consumer advocates, government agencies, and individuals affected by AI-driven decisions, such as job applicants and tenants.

name: Advanced Automation Manufacturing project
description: The project involves establishing a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility at Red River Army Depot in Texarkana, Texas, to modernize the production capability of components for weapon systems, including small unmanned aerial systems.
novelty: The project leverages Hadrian Automation's advanced manufacturing capabilities to strengthen the Army's supply chain, increase production speed, and ensure high-quality components for weapon systems, representing a significant transformation of the Army's Organic Industrial Base.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include the U.S. Army and its personnel, particularly those involved in logistics and technology. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include Hadrian Automation Inc., the Army Materiel Command, and other military services and academia.

name: USAi
organisation: General Services Administration (GSA), Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI)
description: USAi is a secure generative AI platform and centralized procurement toolbox designed to enable federal agencies to confidently adopt AI at scale. It expands the federal government’s AI infrastructure by providing shared services for agencies to experiment with and adopt AI systems.
novelty: The innovation lies in the creation of a centralized platform that not only facilitates the adoption of AI across federal agencies but also incorporates robust methodologies for evaluating AI performance, security, and functionality within real-world workflows.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are federal agencies that will use the USAi platform for AI adoption. Beneficiaries include the American public, as the platform aims to enhance government services through AI. CAISI and GSA are key partners in this initiative.

name: Colorado AI Act Framework
description: A new framework for the Colorado AI Act aimed at regulating AI to protect consumers while fostering innovation. It focuses on AI developers and deployers, requiring disclosures and human-led reviews for consequential decisions made by AI.
novelty: The framework introduces a balanced approach to AI regulation, addressing previous concerns by allocating fault among developers and deployers and providing a cure period for violations.
stakeholders: AI developers, AI deployers, consumers affected by AI decisions, Colorado Attorney General's Office

name: Anaplan's Cloud-Native Platform for SCAS
organisation: South Central Ambulance Service (SCAS) National Health Service (NHS) Foundation Trust
description: SCAS implemented Anaplan's cloud-native platform to automate demand planning, allowing them to process billions of data points to model and forecast demand in precise 15-minute intervals. This innovation has improved operational efficiency and staff morale by providing clear, intuitive dashboards for resource allocation.
novelty: The platform replaces manual, spreadsheet-based forecasting with automated, real-time scenario modelling, allowing for agile readiness and precise demand forecasting.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the SCAS staff and management, who benefit from improved scheduling and operational efficiency, and the patients who receive timely ambulance services.

name: North Carolina IT Modernization
organisation: North Carolina Department of Information Technology
description: An initiative led by Teena Piccione to modernize North Carolina's IT systems, focusing on accelerating digital transformation, improving cybersecurity, harnessing AI, and streamlining IT procurement processes.
novelty: The project introduced a private-sector approach to government IT, emphasizing faster delivery timelines and prioritizing projects with immediate impact for residents.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include state agencies such as the Department of Transportation, environmental agencies, prisons, Veterans Affairs, and the residents of North Carolina who benefit from improved digital services.

name: Consultative Body on AI and Workplace Transformation
organisation: Ministry of Employment and Labor (MOEL), Korean Confederation of Trade Unions (KCTU)
description: A consultative body launched to discuss artificial intelligence, automation, and industrial restructuring, bringing union leadership into regular policy discussions with the government on AI deployment and workplace transformation.
novelty: The initiative integrates union leadership into digital policy discussions, ensuring a human-centered approach to technological change in workplaces.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include workers across various industries, particularly those in unionized sectors. Stakeholders engaged include the Ministry of Employment and Labor, Korean Confederation of Trade Unions, and potentially affected firms.

name: Scotland's AI Strategy
description: Scotland's AI Strategy is a five-year plan aimed at ensuring that people, businesses, and public services across Scotland benefit from artificial intelligence. The strategy includes measures to support businesses in expanding AI use, developing new products and services, and generating jobs and investment. It also involves establishing advisory boards, appointing AI Champions, and creating a Future Jobs Panel.
novelty: The strategy is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI across various sectors of the economy, with specific initiatives like AI Champions and a Future Jobs Panel to assess AI's impact on the workforce.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Scottish businesses, public services, the workforce, and communities, as well as enterprise agencies and partners like The Data Lab.

name: Responsible AI Adoption in Canadian Public Sector
description: The initiative focuses on bridging the gap between informal AI usage by public servants and formal AI adoption by public sector organizations in Canada. It aims to move from experimentation to trusted, sovereign, and value-driven AI at scale, emphasizing governance, data sovereignty, workforce enablement, and public confidence.
novelty: The project addresses the discrepancy between bottom-up AI usage and top-down readiness in the public sector, providing a structured approach to responsible AI adoption.
stakeholders: Public servants, public sector organizations, Canadian government leaders, and citizens who are affected by AI-driven public services.

name: Adopt, Innovate, Transform
description: The report 'Adopt, Innovate, Transform' advocates for involving civil servants and investing in AI to enhance productivity and achieve government savings. It suggests that AI can streamline routine tasks, allowing civil servants to focus on higher-value work and redesign public services. The report emphasizes the need for proper technology access, training, incentives, and collaboration with staff and unions to successfully implement AI in government.
novelty: The innovation lies in the strategic integration of AI into government operations, focusing on empowering civil servants with the necessary tools, training, and incentives to drive productivity and transformation in public services.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are civil servants, particularly senior managers and professionals in the civil service, who are the users and beneficiaries of the AI integration. The government and public are also stakeholders, benefiting from improved public services.

name: Xoli
organisation: Government of Mexico City, Digital Agency for Public Innovation, Ministries of Tourism and Culture
description: Xoli is a chatbot launched by the Government of Mexico City to provide information on services, tourism, and cultural offerings via WhatsApp in both English and Spanish. It aims to support the influx of visitors during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and will remain active post-event to promote economic activities and facilitate access to public services.
novelty: Xoli is innovative as it integrates cultural, tourism, recreation, and entertainment information into a single, accessible platform via WhatsApp, offering real-time, multilingual support to users.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include tourists, residents of Mexico City, and businesses involved in tourism and cultural activities. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the Government of Mexico City, the Digital Agency for Public Innovation, and the local Ministries of Tourism and Culture.

name: Project CONNECT
organisation: Salt Lake City Information Management Services (IMS)
description: Project CONNECT aims to better support Salt Lake City's 50 most-arrested individuals by using city, county, and state resources. Since its inception, 60% of these individuals have been arrested less frequently.
novelty: The project uses AI and data analytics to precisely allocate resources where needed, improving homelessness response processes and reducing arrests among targeted individuals.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are Salt Lake City's unhoused population, city government officials, and the Information Management Services department.

name: Azure Skills Development for Federal Agencies
description: Federal agencies are implementing strategies to close the skills gap in managing Microsoft Azure, a cloud platform. These strategies include role-based training, internal Centers of Excellence, strategic partnerships, and automation to enhance cloud capabilities and improve public services.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive approach to skill development, combining structured certification paths, practical training, internal knowledge hubs, and strategic partnerships to build Azure expertise within federal agencies.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are federal IT staff, federal agencies, and Microsoft as the cloud service provider. Beneficiaries include the public who receive improved services through enhanced cloud capabilities.

name: Financial Sector Innovation by the Federal Reserve
organisation: Federal Reserve Board's Division of Supervision and Regulation
description: The Federal Reserve's Division of Supervision and Regulation is facilitating innovation in the financial sector to improve customer experience, expand product offerings, lower costs, increase credit availability, and enhance efficiencies for banks, businesses, and consumers. This includes the adoption of AI, digital assets, and bank-fintech partnerships.
novelty: The innovation involves the integration of emerging technologies like AI, digital assets, and bank-fintech partnerships into the financial sector, while ensuring safety and soundness through transparent supervision and public feedback.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include banks, businesses, consumers, and the broader financial system. Stakeholders engaged include the Federal Reserve, banks, fintech companies, and the public.

name: California Education Learning Lab
organisation: Governor’s Office of Planning and Research (OPR), later part of the Office of Land Use and Climate Innovation (LCI)
description: The California Education Learning Lab provides competitive grants to faculty teams from California's public higher education segments to support innovation and improvement in lower-division STEM courses. The program aims to improve learning outcomes and close equity gaps by funding projects that leverage technology, such as AI-assisted platforms and professional development tools.
novelty: The Learning Lab fosters intersegmental collaboration across California's public higher education system, providing tailored grant opportunities for faculty-led innovations in STEM education, including the use of adaptive learning technology.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are faculty and students at the University of California, California State University, and California Community Colleges. Beneficiaries include faculty teams working on innovative projects and students who experience improved learning outcomes and reduced equity gaps.

name: Open Government Partnership (OGP) Strategic Foresight Framework
organisation: City of Austin, Austin Community College’s Center for Government and Civic Service, U.S. Open Government Secretariat
description: The City of Austin, in collaboration with various partners, is implementing a strategic foresight framework under the Open Government Partnership to address anticipatory governance, economic resilience, AI accountability, and equity assessments.
novelty: The project adapts a strategic foresight framework from the Houston Foresight Program, incorporating multiple plausible futures into the execution of OGP commitments.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the citizens of Austin, community-based organizations, and civil society partners engaged in the co-creation of action plans.

name: 406 JOBS Initiative
description: The 406 JOBS initiative aims to modernize Montana's workforce development by aligning the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) and the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act. This integration focuses on industry-identified priorities to better serve Montana's workers and employers.
novelty: The initiative unites previously separate federal programs (WIOA and Perkins) into a single integrated talent system, emphasizing AI literacy and AI-driven service modernization as statewide expectations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Montana's employers, workers, students, educational institutions (schools, colleges, universities), and government agencies involved in workforce and education systems.

name: 2026 Ontario Provincial Budget
description: The 2026 Ontario Provincial Budget introduces several measures to support economic growth, including tax cuts for small businesses, investment in strategic sectors like AI and life sciences, and funding for infrastructure and housing. It aims to enhance Ontario's competitiveness and resilience amid economic uncertainties.
novelty: The budget introduces significant tax relief for small businesses and strategic investments in high-growth sectors such as AI and life sciences, alongside a focus on strengthening domestic supply chains and infrastructure development.
stakeholders: Beneficiaries include small businesses, strategic industries like AI and life sciences, the arts, culture, and tourism sectors, and the general public through improved infrastructure and housing. Stakeholders engaged include the Ontario Chamber of Commerce and various government departments.

name: Hybrid Connectivity Models for Rural Broadband
description: A coordinated approach to rural broadband connectivity using hybrid models that combine fiber, 5G fixed wireless access, and LEO satellite technologies to provide scalable and resilient internet access in rural areas.
novelty: The innovation lies in the strategic blending of multiple connectivity technologies to match rural contexts, combined with AI-enabled operations to manage complexity and improve efficiency.
stakeholders: Rural communities, public sector stakeholders, private sector partners, schools, hospitals, government facilities, and rural residents.

name: AI-directed tool for navigating state services
description: A new AI-directed tool to help Californians navigate available programs and benefits by life event, such as starting a business or finding a job. It aims to improve state services delivery, increase transparency, and strengthen accountability.
novelty: The tool is part of a first-in-the-nation digital democracy platform, Engaged California, which facilitates communication and engagement with Californians, allowing them to shape state policy and response to AI's impact on the workforce.
stakeholders: Californians, including those starting a business or finding a job, state employees, and the California Government Operations Agency.

name: Canada Post Reform
description: Canada Post is implementing a series of reforms to address declining mail volumes and financial losses. These reforms include cost-cutting measures, increased automation, work sharing with private partners, and network redesign to sustain universal service without permanent subsidies.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive approach to reforming a national postal service by integrating automation, work sharing, and network redesign to align with modern demand and reduce costs.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Canada Post employees, Canadian households and businesses who rely on postal services, private logistics partners, and the Canadian government.

name: Agent Kyle
description: Agent Kyle is an AI agent launched by the city of Kyle, Texas, to assist with 311 customer service calls, aiming to improve service delivery by handling routine inquiries and tasks.
novelty: Agent Kyle represents the integration of agentic AI in public service, showcasing how AI can transform service delivery by automating routine tasks and allowing human workers to focus on more meaningful work.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include residents of Kyle, Texas, who use the 311 service, and city employees who can focus on more complex tasks. Stakeholders include city government officials and technology providers.

name: National University Hospital (NUH) Innovation Model
description: NUH integrates clinical services with teaching and research, using advanced digital systems and AI tools to improve patient care and operational efficiency. It serves as a national referral center and leads in complex care and transplantation.
novelty: NUH's model combines clinical excellence with academic research and teaching, embedding innovation as a core discipline with a significant portion of its workforce trained in innovation methodologies.
stakeholders: Patients, medical students, healthcare professionals, and academic partners in Singapore and across Asia.

name: 406 JOBS
description: 406 JOBS is an initiative launched to unify Montana’s workforce system and connect residents with in-demand careers. It focuses on increasing access to apprenticeships, work-based learning, and preparing the workforce for future opportunities in AI and automation.
novelty: The initiative identifies 12 priorities across the workforce system to drive innovation, remove bureaucratic barriers, and foster collaboration between state agencies, focusing on high-demand sectors.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Montana residents seeking employment, K-12 public schools, state agencies, and businesses in high-demand sectors.

name: Executive Order on AI Procurement
organisation: California Department of General Services (DGS), California Department of Technology (CDT)
description: The executive order signed by California Governor Gavin Newsom aims to enhance state procurement processes for AI technology. It mandates the California Department of General Services and the California Department of Technology to recommend changes to procurement processes, ensuring AI companies disclose their policies and safeguards. The order focuses on preventing misuse of AI, protecting civil rights, and ensuring transparency and accountability in government services.
novelty: The innovation lies in establishing new procurement guidelines that require AI companies to disclose their policies on exploitation, bias, and civil rights protection, thus promoting responsible AI use in government.
stakeholders: State employees, AI companies, California government departments, and the general public who will benefit from improved government services and protections.

name: National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence
description: The National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence outlines recommendations for a nationally uniform approach to AI regulation, focusing on areas such as child protection, AI infrastructure, intellectual property, censorship, innovation, workforce preparation, and preemption of state AI laws. It aims to establish a sector-specific, federally led regulatory model with significant preemption of state AI laws.
novelty: The Framework introduces a comprehensive, federally led approach to AI regulation, emphasizing preemption of state laws and sector-specific oversight without creating a new federal AI regulator. It also proposes regulatory sandboxes and expanded access to federal datasets for AI training.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Congress, state lawmakers, AI companies, technology providers, small businesses, educational institutions, and the general public, particularly children and parents.

name: Celonis Process Intelligence Graph
description: The Celonis Process Intelligence Graph is a digital twin of business operations that helps organizations move beyond isolated AI pilots to build and orchestrate composable AI solutions, transforming business-critical operations and achieving significant outcomes at scale.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a system-independent intelligence layer that allows AI to understand the operational context of a business, enabling the industrialization of AI by grounding it in the reality of business operations.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include organizations across India, particularly in sectors like Finance, Healthcare, and national welfare services. Stakeholders engaged include Celonis, local leaders, and the Celonis Global Innovation Hub in Bengaluru.

name: AI Innovation Series
organisation: Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) and the Treasury Department’s Artificial Intelligence Transformation Office (AITO)
description: The AI Innovation Series is a public-private initiative focused on the use of artificial intelligence in financial services. It aims to explore applications such as fraud detection, credit underwriting, cybersecurity, and operational risk management through roundtables with financial institutions, technology firms, regulators, and experts.
novelty: The initiative brings together regulators and industry to optimize regulation and support AI adoption in financial services, moving from a constraint-focused approach to one recognizing the risks of not adopting productivity-enhancing technology.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include financial institutions, technology firms, regulators, and subject-matter experts involved in the financial services sector.

name: Rewriting Government Procurement Rules for AI
description: The General Services Administration (GSA) is proposing new procurement rules aimed at promoting ideologically neutral American AI innovation. These rules include provisions that could make AI tools less safe and less useful by requiring contractors to license their AI systems to the government for all lawful purposes and disabling safety guardrails that prevent responding to government requests.
novelty: The innovation lies in using procurement rules to steer government funds towards ideologically neutral AI innovation, while also imposing broad provisions that could impact AI safety and utility.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government contractors, service providers, AI companies, and the federal government, with potential impacts on public privacy and safety.

name: Low-cost Uncrewed Combat Attack System (LUCAS)
organisation: Indiana National Guard, Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering
description: LUCAS is an affordable, autonomous combat attack system that was rapidly advanced from public demonstration to operational employment in just seven months, showcasing a new model for defense innovation.
novelty: The innovation lies in the rapid transition from concept to operational deployment, achieved through the T-REX framework and the 'speed of relevance' methodology, which accelerates traditional defense acquisition timelines.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Indiana National Guard, the Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering, operators, engineers, acquisition professionals, and the Joint Force as beneficiaries.

name: U.S. Tech Force
description: The U.S. Tech Force is an initiative launched by the Office of Personnel Management to hire 1,000 engineers and specialists to improve AI infrastructure in the government. The program collaborates with leading technology companies and allows participants to apply for full-time jobs with these companies after two years.
novelty: The initiative creates a bridge between the private sector and government work, allowing private sector workers to experience public sector roles without long-term commitment and enabling federal workers to explore private sector opportunities.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include private sector workers, federal employees, and technology companies such as Amazon Web Services, Apple, Google, Nvidia, Palantir, OpenAI, and Oracle.

name: GSA AI Procurement Clause Changes
description: The General Services Administration proposed changes to the federal AI acquisition stipulations, including government ownership of input data and custom AI developments, and permission to use AI systems for any lawful government purpose.
novelty: The proposal includes significant changes to AI procurement rules, such as total government ownership of input data and custom AI developments, and an 'eyes off' rule for data handling, which could impact vendor terms and civil liberties.
stakeholders: Federal government agencies, AI vendors, industry advocate organizations, and potentially the general public who may be affected by AI applications in government services.

name: AI-driven Cybersecurity Platform
description: A unified AI-driven platform that provides consistent protection and visibility to embed security into the digital ecosystem of Singapore's public sector. It aims to ensure secure, reliable, and resilient government services against rising cyber threats.
novelty: The platform uses AI to reduce noise by triaging alerts, enables faster response through orchestration and automation, and acts as a force multiplier for talent by handling repetitive tasks and accelerating investigations.
stakeholders: Public sector agencies in Singapore, cybersecurity teams, and citizens benefiting from secure digital services.

name: AI-powered course search tool, Clementine
description: Clementine is an AI-powered course search tool launched by Syracuse University to enhance transparency and accessibility in course selection. It allows students to search for courses with visible operating parameters, improving the course selection process.
novelty: The innovation lies in its transparency, as it makes the operating parameters of the AI system visible to users, promoting responsible AI deployment in educational settings.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are Syracuse University students, faculty, and staff. The stakeholders engaged include university administration and the digital transformation team.

name: AI Pro
organisation: New York Office of Information Technology Services
description: AI Pro is a custom artificial intelligence tool developed to provide New York state employees with a secure environment to enhance their AI skills and explore how AI can solve state agency problems. It includes a two-part training program on responsible AI use.
novelty: AI Pro is innovative as it represents the largest state-level deployment of a secure AI tool for an entire workforce, aiming to upskill employees and integrate AI into public sector problem-solving.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include New York state government employees, spread across 50 agencies, who are the primary users and beneficiaries of the AI Pro tool and training program.

name: AI Executive Order N‑5‑26
organisation: California Department of Technology (CDT), Department of General Services (DGS), California Government Operations Agency
description: The Executive Order N‑5‑26 issued by California Governor Gavin Newsom aims to leverage generative AI for the benefit of Californians while ensuring transparency, privacy, and civil liberties protection. It mandates new trust and safety obligations for AI companies contracting with California agencies and updates procurement processes to include transparency and privacy requirements.
novelty: The project is innovative as it establishes distinct state procurement standards for AI that differ from federal requirements, focusing on transparency, privacy, and civil liberties protection in AI deployment.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include California government agencies and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include the California Department of Technology, Department of General Services, California Government Operations Agency, and other state departments.

name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server with Agentic AI
organisation: City of Boston, Bloomberg Philanthropies, Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) at MIT
description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server is a software layer that allows AI models to securely interact with Boston's open data portal. It includes a set of open-source 'skills' or workflow templates that enable complex analytical workflows to produce formatted tables and plain language answers to various questions, aiming to expand transparency, improve policymaking, and broaden participation in data-driven decision-making.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI, which involves AI 'agents' that follow structured workflows to analyze data and provide insights, mimicking the processes of skilled data and policy analysts. This allows for more secure, accurate, and accessible data analysis.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Boston city employees, residents, and potentially other cities that adopt the technology. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the City of Boston, Bloomberg Philanthropies, and J-PAL at MIT.

name: USDA National Proving Grounds Network for AgTech (NPG-Ag)
description: A nationwide initiative designed to rigorously evaluate agricultural technologies under real-world U.S. farming and ranching conditions, providing farmers and ranchers with trusted, practical insights for technology-investment decisions.
novelty: Establishes a coordinated national research network to objectively validate new and emerging agricultural technologies, especially digital and AI-driven technologies, under real-world conditions.
stakeholders: American farmers and ranchers, USDA's Agricultural Research Service, land-grant universities, agricultural technology companies, and Grand Farm as the National Program Manager.

name: GovAI Coalition
description: The GovAI Coalition was launched to give government leaders, policymakers, and private companies a voice in shaping the future of AI in government. It has created policy templates, procurement guidance, and best practices for AI oversight. The coalition is transitioning into an independent nonprofit to scale and secure funding, ensuring long-term sustainability and support for government agencies nationwide.
novelty: The transition of the GovAI Coalition into a nonprofit organization allows it to move beyond a volunteer-driven model, establishing dedicated staff to focus on developing AI guidance around transparency, bias, and accountability for governments exploring AI adoption.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government leaders, policymakers, private companies, public agencies, and city employees. Beneficiaries are government agencies nationwide, and the coalition's members include over 3,000 individuals across 900 public agencies.

name: GovAI Coalition
description: The GovAI Coalition is an initiative founded by the City of San Jose to promote responsible AI use in government. It aims to share best practices and set a national benchmark for AI use in public services, enhancing service delivery through AI tools.
novelty: The innovation lies in transforming a city-led AI initiative into an independent nonprofit organization, allowing for greater scalability, sustainability, and access to philanthropic funding, while promoting cross-government collaboration on AI.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, academia, private sector representatives, and over 900 government agencies worldwide. The beneficiaries are public service users who will experience improved service delivery through AI.

name: AI in State Government Services
description: State governments are adopting artificial intelligence to improve the delivery of public services, enhance public trust, and achieve measurable outcomes. AI is used to ensure accurate and timely delivery of benefits in public safety net programs by analyzing data quickly and identifying patterns and trends.
novelty: The innovation lies in the pragmatic application of AI to enhance efficiency, modernization, and the government customer experience while maintaining rigorous standards for security, compliance, and accountability.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state government agencies, technology vendors, and residents who are the beneficiaries of improved public services.

name: Modernization of National Background Investigation Services (NBIS) systems
description: ICF has been awarded a position on a multiple-award blanket purchase agreement to modernize the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency's National Background Investigation Services systems. The project aims to enhance the performance, scalability, security, and user experience of the NBIS systems using cloud-native design, zero trust architecture, open-source technologies, agile DevSecOps practices, AI, and enhanced automation.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of cloud-native design, zero trust architecture, and agile DevSecOps practices to modernize critical national security systems.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency, U.S. federal government agencies, and the workforce being vetted and assessed by the NBIS systems.

name: Executive Order N-5-26
description: Executive Order N-5-26 directs state agencies in California to develop new standards for AI companies seeking state contracts and to expand the responsible use of generative AI in government operations. It includes new certification requirements for state contractors, independent review of federal supply chain designations, reforms to contractor responsibility provisions, and guidance on watermarking AI-generated content.
novelty: The Order adds a procurement-focused layer to AI governance, emphasizing public procurement as a tool to shape market behavior and encourage responsible innovation. It also introduces new certification requirements and independent review mechanisms for AI-related state contracts.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state agencies in California, AI companies seeking to contract with the state, and the general public who will benefit from improved government transparency and accountability through the use of GenAI.

name: Executive Order on AI Use in State Government
description: The executive order places new limits on AI use in state government to prevent harm, while encouraging the adoption of vetted AI tools to improve efficiency and public services. It includes creating safeguards against civil rights violations, unlawful surveillance, and harmful content, and updating the state's digital strategy to enhance transparency and accountability.
novelty: The innovation lies in balancing AI adoption with oversight, ensuring AI tools are used responsibly in public services while preventing misuse and protecting civil rights.
stakeholders: State agencies, California residents, AI companies, and government employees are the primary stakeholders involved in this innovation.

name: AI-powered Advocates for Medi-Cal
organisation: California Health Care Foundation, California Department of Health Care Services
description: AI-powered Advocates are proposed to help Medi-Cal members proactively navigate their health, benefits, and social services. These tools would securely access data to identify rising risks, alert members to services they qualify for, and guide them through enrollment and care navigation, aiming to reduce avoidable crises and improve coverage continuity.
novelty: The innovation shifts Medi-Cal from a reactive to a preventive system by using AI to proactively respond to rising risk levels and initiate outreach, navigation, and enrollment, leveraging existing state infrastructure without consolidating data into a single system.
stakeholders: Medi-Cal members, state agencies, health plans, certified technology vendors, counties, managed care plans, community-based organizations, and the California Department of Health Care Services.

name: Aikam–Deakin Hub for Research in Intelligent Technology & Innovation (Aikam–DHRITI)
organisation: Deakin University, Government of Telangana through Aikam™
description: A joint initiative to advance collaboration in applied artificial intelligence, skilling, research, and innovation. It aims to harness AI for real-world impact, focusing on public good, economic growth, and workforce transformation through AI skilling, research, and industry engagement.
novelty: The project establishes a Joint Centre of Excellence in Applied Artificial Intelligence, creating an open innovation ecosystem that combines global academic strengths with progressive policy and innovation ecosystems to deliver scalable, real-world solutions.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include AI researchers, industry partners, startups, and the workforce in Telangana. Stakeholders engaged include Deakin University, Government of Telangana, and Aikam™.

name: Sovereign AI for Public Administration
description: A project involving European AI providers and enterprise vendors to develop AI solutions for public administration that adhere to European digital sovereignty standards.
novelty: The project focuses on creating AI solutions that comply with European sovereignty indicators for cloud, AI, and cybersecurity, influencing procurement and state aid across EU member states.
stakeholders: European AI providers, enterprise vendors, public administration sectors in EU member states, and the Joint French-German Digital Sovereignty Taskforce.

name: Safe AI Innovation in Financial Services
organisation: Bank of England, Prudential Regulation Authority
description: The Bank of England and Prudential Regulation Authority have outlined plans to enable safe AI innovation in financial services, aiming to foster innovation, competition, and growth while maintaining the integrity of the financial system. This involves a technology-agnostic regulatory approach and annual reporting on AI-driven innovation.
novelty: The initiative is innovative due to its focus on integrating AI into financial services under existing regulatory frameworks, ensuring compliance while promoting technological advancement.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include financial institutions, regulators, and the broader financial sector. Stakeholders engaged include the Bank of England, Prudential Regulation Authority, and the UK Government.

name: Gemini for Government
description: Gemini for Government is an AI platform that transforms public sector workflows by enabling the use of AI agents to streamline processes, enhance productivity, and deliver personalized services. It is used by agencies like the CDAO, DOT, FDA, and DOE to advance scientific discovery and improve constituent services.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to move beyond AI exploration to real-world applications at scale, empowering public sector employees to create and run AI agents in a secure environment.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector organizations such as the CDAO, DOT, FDA, and DOE, as well as their employees and the constituents they serve.

name: Gemini for Government
description: Gemini for Government is an advanced AI platform designed to empower public sector organizations by moving beyond AI exploration to real-world applications at scale. It aims to streamline business processes and administrative tasks, enhance productivity, and drive mission impact across various government agencies.
novelty: The innovation lies in its integrated tech stack that offers velocity, precision, and cost efficiency, along with a secure environment for deploying AI agents at scale, making it a powerful tool for public sector transformation.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include public sector agencies such as the U.S. Department of Transportation, the FDA, and the Department of Energy, as well as state and local governments like the State of Iowa and the City of Los Angeles. It involves 3 million civilian and military personnel.

name: Shanghai and Hong Kong Digital Co-operation
organisation: Hong Kong Special Administrative Region government, Shanghai Municipal Bureau of Data
description: The initiative aims to deepen cooperation between Hong Kong and Shanghai in innovation and technology, focusing on the digital economy and digital government. It includes promoting cross-boundary public services, digital identity identification, and collaboration in industry spaces and communication platforms.
novelty: The project is innovative in its approach to fostering cross-boundary public services and the synergistic development of the digital economy between two major cities, Hong Kong and Shanghai. It also emphasizes the cross-boundary flow of personal information and AI+ Civil Services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials from Hong Kong and Shanghai, enterprises setting up businesses under the initiative, and members of the Shanghai-Hong Kong Artificial Intelligence Industry Acceleration Alliance. Beneficiaries are the public and enterprises in both cities.

name: National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence 2031
description: A ten-year roadmap to embed AI across the economy and public administration, aiming to position the UAE as a global leader in AI by 2031. It targets sectors like water, transport, energy, logistics, tourism, healthcare, and cybersecurity to drive AI adoption and showcase nation-scale use cases.
novelty: The strategy includes the world's first Minister of State for Artificial Intelligence and initiatives like the National Program for AI and Digital Dubai ethics frameworks. It also involves significant investment in AI infrastructure, such as the 'Stargate' project, a 5GW AI data center complex.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include various sectors such as water, transport, energy, logistics, tourism, healthcare, and cybersecurity. Stakeholders engaged include government entities, local and international investors, and technology partners like G42, MGX, OpenAI, NVIDIA, Microsoft, Cisco, Oracle, and Softbank.

name: AI Regulations and Implementation in California
description: The executive order signed by Governor Gavin Newsom aims to develop AI regulations that balance public safety with innovation. It requires AI companies to demonstrate safeguards before state agencies can purchase their services and proposes watermarks on AI content to prevent misinformation. The order also seeks to identify opportunities for AI to enhance government transparency, accountability, and service accessibility.
novelty: The innovation lies in the proactive approach to regulate AI while simultaneously leveraging its potential to improve government services, ensuring rights protection and exploring AI's role in enhancing transparency and efficiency in state operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI companies, state agencies, California residents, and taxpayers. Beneficiaries are the general public who will experience improved government services, and AI companies who will partner with the state.

name: AI Deployment for Public Services in Baltimore
organisation: Baltimore Mayor’s Office of Performance and Innovation
description: Baltimore is deploying AI to improve public services, focusing on proactive service delivery with initial use cases in traffic management and public safety. The city uses AI to automate traffic calming processes and identify illegal dumping through camera data analysis.
novelty: The innovation lies in transitioning AI from a niche capability to a foundational part of city workflows, moving from reactive to proactive service delivery, and automating processes that were previously manual.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Baltimore city residents, while stakeholders engaged include the city council, the mayor, and the general public.

name: ChatGPT Enterprise Pilot and Expansion
description: The Shapiro Administration expanded the use of generative AI tools to over 3,000 state employees across 35 agencies in Pennsylvania. The initiative aims to deliver better, faster services to Pennsylvanians by using AI for tasks like drafting communications, summarizing materials, and conducting research. The pilot demonstrated significant time savings and efficiency improvements.
novelty: First-in-the-nation pilot of ChatGPT Enterprise with Commonwealth employees, focusing on safe, human-centered AI expansion with strong governance and thoughtful implementation.
stakeholders: State employees, Commonwealth agencies, Pennsylvanians, Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Local 668, InnovateUS, Carnegie Mellon University, University of Pennsylvania.

name: www2.gov.bm Portal
organisation: Government of Bermuda, Department of Information and Digital Technology
description: A new government portal designed to improve access to information and services, making it simpler, faster, and more intuitive for users. It includes features like smart search, mobile optimization, and enhanced security and privacy measures.
novelty: The portal uses AI-assisted coding and modern tools to improve user interaction with government services, ensuring accessibility and security without relying on third-party services.
stakeholders: Users include residents and businesses in Bermuda. Beneficiaries are the general public and government employees who will experience improved service delivery and efficiency.

name: Digital Development Agenda
organisation: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), Senegal’s Ministry of Communication Telecommunications and Digital Economy (MCTN)
description: The initiative aims to support Senegal's digital transformation by leveraging technology to enhance public services, expand access to digital opportunities, and foster innovation across various sectors.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to digital transformation, focusing on digital governance, inclusion, artificial intelligence, and innovation to position Senegal as a digital innovation hub.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Senegalese government, public service users, and the broader community benefiting from enhanced digital services and opportunities.

name: AI System Evaluation Tool Pilot Process
organisation: US National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC)
description: The AI System Evaluation Tool is designed to help state insurance regulators understand and assess how insurers use AI and machine learning models. The pilot process involves 12 state insurance regulators using the tool with selected insurance companies to promote transparency and identify areas needing oversight or improvement.
novelty: The tool provides a structured way for regulators to review AI systems, promoting transparency and helping to identify where additional oversight or training is needed. It is innovative in its structured approach to AI governance in the insurance industry.
stakeholders: State insurance regulators, insurance companies, and potentially consumers who benefit from improved AI governance and transparency.

name: Project Glasswing
description: Project Glasswing is a cross-industry initiative that uses frontier AI to strengthen cyber defense among organizations that build or maintain critical systems. It aims to enhance AI-driven detection and defense capabilities to protect critical infrastructure sectors such as energy, water, transportation, and healthcare.
novelty: The project leverages cutting-edge AI models capable of identifying and exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities, pushing the boundaries of AI capabilities in cybersecurity.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include critical infrastructure operators, federal agencies, and organizations maintaining critical systems. Stakeholders engaged include Anthropic, federal agencies, and frontier AI labs.

name: AI Connect
description: AI Connect is a forum initiated by Skillsoft to facilitate shared learning and collaboration among employees on AI technologies. It allows workers to bring questions and share good use cases, aiming to demystify AI and provide practical tips for its application.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating a structured platform for employees to engage with AI technologies, share knowledge, and learn from each other's experiences, thus fostering a culture of innovation and continuous learning.
