novelty: AI TRiSM is innovative because it offers a structured framework for continuous oversight of AI systems, addressing the rapid evolution of AI technologies and the need for ongoing governance rather than one-time reviews.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, IT departments, and organizations using AI systems, as well as employees who might use AI tools without oversight.

name: Studio 26
description: Studio 26 is a venture-focused initiative at Mercyhurst University designed to support innovation, entrepreneurship, and industry collaboration in areas such as cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies. It aims to develop new ideas through collaboration, mentorship, and experimentation, providing new opportunities for students and contributing to the economic vitality of Northwest Pennsylvania.
novelty: Studio 26 is innovative in its approach to combining applied learning with industry collaboration, allowing students, faculty, and external partners to explore innovative ideas and test emerging technologies in a dedicated space.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Mercyhurst University students, faculty, industry partners, community and government partners, and the broader Pennsylvania innovation ecosystem.

name: AI Bootcamps and Tech Training
description: The UK Government has launched a new initiative to support young people into entry-level jobs in sectors affected by AI. This includes AI bootcamps and tech training for 400,000 young people from disadvantaged schools, aiming to help them into further education, training, and employment. The initiative involves a partnership between government, industry, and trade unions to reshape entry-level jobs and provide pathways to work.
novelty: The initiative is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI and tech skills training into the education and employment pathways for young people, particularly those from disadvantaged backgrounds. It combines practical experience with AI skills training and guarantees apprenticeships for those completing the bootcamp.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include young people from disadvantaged schools, government bodies, industry partners like JD Sports, BAE Systems, PA Consulting, local councils, and tech companies such as Accenture, Microsoft, and Sage. Beneficiaries are young people entering the job market, particularly those at risk of unemployment.

name: AI Hackathon
organisation: Ministry of Energy and Infrastructure (MoEI), 42 Abu Dhabi
description: A specialised Artificial Intelligence Hackathon aimed at creating technology-driven solutions to improve government services across key sectors such as energy, infrastructure, transport, and housing. The hackathon encourages the development of AI-powered tools to streamline processes and boost efficiency.
novelty: The hackathon provides a practical platform for translating the UAE’s vision of a government ecosystem driven by innovation and advanced technology, focusing on AI-powered solutions for real-world challenges.
stakeholders: Programmers, developers, entrepreneurs, young innovators, industry specialists, and experts from MoEI and 42 Abu Dhabi. Beneficiaries include government sectors and the general public in the UAE.

name: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
description: The executive order aims to enhance cybersecurity across federal and critical infrastructure by directing federal agencies to protect against cyber threats, creating an AI cybersecurity clearinghouse, and establishing a voluntary information-sharing system for AI developers.
novelty: The innovation lies in the creation of an AI cybersecurity clearinghouse and a voluntary framework for AI developers to share 'covered frontier models' with the federal government before broader release, promoting secure innovation and strengthening cybersecurity.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, AI developers, critical infrastructure operators, Department of Homeland Security, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, National Security Agency, Office of Personnel Management, and the Office of Management and Budget.

name: Mythos
description: Mythos is a frontier AI model developed by Anthropic, capable of identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities in software systems. It has been used to find thousands of vulnerabilities in financial institutions, allowing them to shore up defenses before potential exploitation by adversaries.
novelty: Mythos represents a significant advancement in AI capabilities, able to autonomously identify and exploit vulnerabilities at a scale and speed previously unattainable by human researchers.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include financial institutions, critical infrastructure operators, and government agencies that benefit from the model's ability to identify vulnerabilities. Additionally, AI developers and cybersecurity professionals are engaged in ensuring the model's safe deployment.

name: Digital Governance and Regulatory Reform in India
description: A comprehensive set of digital governance and regulatory reforms aimed at improving the ease of doing business in India. These reforms include digital platforms for business registration, integrated approval mechanisms, and simplified procedures, which have reduced paperwork and processing times. The initiatives have improved transparency, reduced administrative burdens, and strengthened investor confidence.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of digital platforms and systems to streamline business processes, reduce compliance burdens, and enhance transparency. This includes the use of AI-enabled compliance tools, digital land records, and a national single window system for approvals.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include entrepreneurs, startups, micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), government departments, and investors. Beneficiaries are businesses operating in India, which experience reduced regulatory burdens and improved service delivery.

name: Tech Force hiring program
description: The Tech Force hiring program aims to address federal technology needs by hiring early career workers and borrowing management-level workers from the private sector to serve in temporary federal service. The program seeks to modernize federal technology and create a pipeline between industry and government.
novelty: The program brings world-class engineering expertise into public service and creates pathways for Tech Force alumni into the private sector, enhancing AI adoption in government and addressing technology workforce shortages.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal agencies and the general public who rely on modernized public infrastructure. Stakeholders engaged include industry partners like Cisco, Scale AI, and others, as well as the Office of Personnel Management.

name: AI-driven support capabilities
description: A major civilian agency piloted AI-driven support capabilities to manage workload amid reduced back-office personnel, integrating AI into existing data environments and workflows to enhance efficiency and maintain service levels.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI models directly into existing data environments and workflows, moving beyond fragmented experimentation to create a cohesive system that enhances operational efficiency and maintains performance under constraints.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include federal agencies, their leaders, and the workforce, particularly in IT and HR departments, who benefit from improved efficiency and workload management.

name: Next-generation Security Operations Center (SOC)
description: GDIT has expanded its partnership with Splunk to deliver advanced cybersecurity solutions for the U.S. government. The collaboration focuses on building a next-generation Security Operations Center (SOC) using AI to enhance threat detection and response, integrate cybersecurity data, and develop zero trust capabilities.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of agentic AI to detect, isolate, and remediate threats rapidly, and its integration of AI to correlate data from multiple sources for improved situational awareness.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are U.S. federal agencies, including the U.S. Air Force and the Virginia Information Technologies Agency (VITA), which benefit from enhanced cybersecurity solutions. The users include government employees and residents served by these agencies.

name: Voice AI for Public Services
description: The UK government and ElevenLabs signed a memorandum of understanding to explore the use of voice AI to improve public service accessibility, conduct AI safety research, and develop AI talent in the UK. The initiative focuses on enhancing accessibility for citizens, particularly those with visual impairments, low literacy, and diverse linguistic backgrounds.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on using voice AI to improve accessibility in public services, its integration with AI safety research, and its emphasis on developing AI talent in the UK.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, ElevenLabs, citizens with accessibility needs, the UK AI Security Institute, and potential international AI talent.

name: Public Sector AI Centre of Excellence and AI Investment Fund
description: The innovation involves establishing a Public Sector AI Centre of Excellence and a $10 million AI Investment Fund in Western Australia. The initiative aims to enhance the public sector's capacity to use AI for improving community safety, reducing approval timeframes, and enhancing health and education outcomes. It also includes creating an AI uplift team to assist agencies in delivering scalable AI solutions.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its centralised approach to deploying AI solutions across the public sector, reducing duplication, and ensuring value from AI investments. It also includes a unique buying mechanism for government access to leading AI businesses and partnerships with industry and universities.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include the Western Australian public sector agencies and the community they serve. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include government agencies, AI businesses, industry partners, and universities.

name: Digital Employee Experience (DEX)
description: DEX is a digital infrastructure that unifies IT, HR, and business applications into a single self-service portal, enabling a proactive approach to IT support by providing real-time visibility into system, application, and device performance. It aims to improve how employees interact with technology, reducing downtime and improving overall performance in government services.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to shift IT support from a reactive to a proactive stance by using real-time telemetry and automation to anticipate and resolve issues before they impact service delivery.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are government IT teams and employees, while the stakeholders engaged include ServiceNow, IBM, and the National Association of Counties.

name: Explainable AI Approach
description: NEC promotes an explainable AI approach, known as white-box AI, where the reasoning behind AI outputs can be communicated to leaders, ensuring accountability even with fragmented data. This approach includes functions like Quality Checker, LLM Explainer, and Fact Checker to support explainability, traceability, and transparency in the public sector.
novelty: The innovation lies in the development of explainable AI functions that enhance transparency and accountability in AI systems, addressing issues like LLM hallucination and ensuring that AI outputs can be justified and understood by human decision-makers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies in Southeast Asia, NEC as the technology provider, and citizens who benefit from improved public services and accountability.

name: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
description: An executive order issued by President Trump to accelerate AI-enabled cybersecurity initiatives, design a voluntary framework for engagement with developers of frontier AI models, and prioritize criminal enforcement against AI-enabled cyberattacks.
novelty: The executive order introduces a voluntary framework for public-private coordination on AI model development, focusing on cybersecurity, without imposing mandatory licensing or preclearance requirements.
stakeholders: AI developers, critical infrastructure companies, U.S. government agencies, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), National Security Agency (NSA), and other federal departments.

name: AI Deployment in Government Services
description: The General Services Administration is implementing artificial intelligence to modernize government services, aiming to improve efficiency and provide faster access to services for citizens, such as simplifying access through Login.gov and automating repetitive tasks in the Department of Veterans Affairs.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to streamline government processes, enhance service delivery, and provide measurable outcomes, which is a significant shift from traditional government operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the American public, government employees, industry partners, and federal agencies like the Department of Veterans Affairs. Beneficiaries are primarily American citizens who will experience improved access to government services.

name: AI Innovation Lab
description: The AI Innovation Lab in Maryland provides state agencies with tools, infrastructure, and expert consultation to enhance AI adoption and experimentation. It offers a controlled sandbox environment for secure testing and rapid prototype creation of AI tools. The lab supports state efforts to improve service delivery and modernize government through AI-backed projects.
novelty: The lab offers a controlled sandbox environment for secure AI testing and rapid prototype creation, supporting state efforts to modernize government services and improve service delivery.
stakeholders: State agencies, state employees with AI expertise, Maryland government, and Maryland residents as beneficiaries of improved services.

name: Agentic AI (AAI) Systems in Government Services
description: Agentic AI (AAI) systems are being explored to transform public services by enabling AI to plan, coordinate, and execute complex tasks autonomously. The UAE aims to transition 50% of its government sectors to AAI, promising reduced administrative burdens, improved accessibility, and seamless service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in moving beyond simple automation to autonomous AI systems that can coordinate actions across multiple government departments, offering a new phase of public-sector transformation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, citizens, businesses, policymakers, and public service employees who will interact with or benefit from the AAI systems.

name: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
description: An Executive Order signed by President Trump to promote AI innovation and security by collaborating with the private sector to modernize and secure information systems against external threats. It includes a voluntary framework for AI developers to share models for cybersecurity review, upgrades to cybersecurity infrastructure, and prioritization of enforcement against AI-aided crimes.
novelty: The creation of a voluntary framework for AI developers to confidentially share models for cybersecurity evaluation before public release, representing a shift from a 'light-touch' regulatory approach to a more active federal role in AI and cybersecurity.
stakeholders: AI developers, federal agencies, private-sector 'trusted partners', cybersecurity industry, state and local governments, operators of critical infrastructure like hospitals, banks, and utilities.

name: The Future of the Finance Ministry
organisation: Global Government Finance, Global Government Forum, Microsoft
description: A global research study exploring the evolving role of finance ministries from traditional fiscal gatekeepers to strategic leaders in government. It emphasizes the integration of digital transformation, AI, and data analytics to improve efficiency, resilience, and decision-making in public finance.
novelty: The innovation lies in reimagining finance ministries as strategic nerve centers that leverage digital transformation, AI, and data analytics to enhance national resilience and decision-making capabilities, moving beyond traditional fiscal roles.
stakeholders: Public finance leaders, policymakers, business decision makers, and global finance leaders are the primary stakeholders, with beneficiaries including national governments and the public.

name: Internal Knowledge Center
description: The Internal Knowledge Center is a workforce enablement platform consisting of searchable webpages that serve as a repository for agency procedures, filing requirements, templates, policies, and training materials. It aims to enhance consistency, reduce information search times, and preserve institutional knowledge, allowing employees to navigate complex procedures and serve customers more efficiently.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating knowledge management and artificial intelligence into a single employee experience, leveraging existing enterprise technology to solve operational challenges and improve service delivery.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the employees of the WV Secretary of State's Office, who can access information more efficiently. The public also benefits from improved service delivery.

name: Secure and Accurate Data Capture
description: The innovation involves rethinking data gathering at the point of activity to ensure secure and accurate data capture, which serves as the foundation for future innovations like intelligent automation and real-time analytics. This approach is applied in public safety and public works to create digital records and improve asset visibility.
novelty: The innovation focuses on incremental, high-impact changes by using mobile devices and barcodes for data capture, transforming traditional processes into digital records that enhance data integrity and enable proactive management.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public safety officers, public works crews, building inspectors, and government agencies. Stakeholders engaged include government CIOs, IT Directors, and employees who benefit from streamlined processes.

name: Digital Transformation in Central Asia
description: Central Asian countries are investing in digital infrastructure, AI, startup ecosystems, e-government services, and technology education to diversify their economies and compete in the digital age. Initiatives include IT parks, innovation centers, and digital public services aimed at economic growth and modernization.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to digital transformation, focusing on building technology ecosystems, fostering startups, and integrating AI into public services, positioning Central Asia as a potential regional technology hub.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens, businesses, startups, investors, and international technology companies. Stakeholders engaged are Central Asian governments, educational institutions, and technology sector participants.

name: Maryland's AI Innovation Lab
description: Maryland's AI Innovation Lab provides state agencies with a secure environment to test and develop AI solutions aimed at improving government efficiency and service delivery. It is part of a broader strategy to use AI to support economic development, strengthen the workforce, and improve public services.
novelty: The lab offers a secure environment specifically for state agencies to experiment with and develop AI solutions, which is a novel approach to ensuring responsible AI adoption in government operations.
stakeholders: State agencies, government officials, private sector collaborators, and the general public who benefit from improved public services.

name: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
description: The executive order aims to enhance AI innovation and security by providing federal agencies with first access to AI models for review and response to associated threats. It includes a defensive track for deploying AI tools in federal networks and a structural track for a voluntary framework where AI developers can share frontier models with the government.
novelty: The innovation lies in its two-track approach: a defensive deployment of AI tools in critical infrastructure and a voluntary framework for AI developers to share models with the government, potentially laying the groundwork for formal AI oversight.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include federal agencies, state and local governments, critical infrastructure operators such as hospitals, community banks, and local utilities. Stakeholders also include AI developers and federal contractors.

name: Morocco Digital Transformation Acceleration Program
description: The program provides $250 million in financing to support Morocco's Digital Morocco 2030 strategy, focusing on deploying user-centric public digital services, transitioning to cloud systems, supporting startups, advancing AI innovation, transforming MSMEs, creating jobs in offshoring, and expanding digital talent.
novelty: The program emphasizes end-to-end digital access to public services through a unified national portal, reducing in-person visits, and introduces a National Sovereign Wallet for secure digital document storage and sharing.
stakeholders: The program targets citizens, businesses, youth, women, public administrations, startups, MSMEs, and the offshoring sector.

name: Government Website Consolidation Initiative
organisation: Commerce Department, Office of Management and Budget, General Services Administration
description: An initiative to reduce the number of federal government websites by eliminating confusing, duplicative, or outdated sites. The goal is to streamline online services, making it easier for users to find information and services provided by government agencies.
novelty: The project involves a governmentwide audit and consolidation of websites, using artificial intelligence to identify and eliminate duplicate content, and aims to create a unified digital experience across federal agencies.
stakeholders: Users of federal government websites, including citizens and businesses, as well as federal agencies and departments managing these websites.

name: Ericsson and Dubai Digital Authority Partnership
description: A partnership between Ericsson and Dubai Digital Authority to collaborate on digital initiatives aimed at enhancing public services, driving innovation, and supporting Dubai's digital ambitions through advanced connectivity, AI-enabled city operations, and digital inclusion.
novelty: The partnership focuses on next-generation initiatives that leverage advanced connectivity and AI to enhance public services and support Dubai's digital transformation goals.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government entities, healthcare, public safety, digital transformation, education, and community development sectors in Dubai. Stakeholders engaged include Ericsson, Dubai Digital Authority, and the UAE government.

name: Poppy
description: Poppy is a generative AI assistant launched by the California Department of Technology to help public servants safely experiment with AI and build tools tailored to their operational needs. It operates on a secure, state-government network, pulling information only from official CA.gov websites to ensure data security.
novelty: Poppy is innovative as it provides a secure platform for public servants to experiment with AI, ensuring data security by only using information from official state websites. It moves from pilot to production status, expanding its availability across the state.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public servants in California who will use Poppy to enhance their operational needs, and the residents of California who will benefit from improved government services.

name: Placemetry
description: Placemetry uses 3D cameras mounted on vehicles to collect street-level imagery, which is then analyzed by AI algorithms to generate actionable insights for public and private sector clients. This technology helps cities manage public space more effectively by providing comprehensive, up-to-date data, allowing for more efficient deployment of resources.
novelty: The innovation lies in its use of 3D street imaging technology to automate data collection, providing timely and comprehensive information that can be used for various municipal functions, including monitoring streets, public property, and road conditions.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies such as the NYPD, Department of Sanitation, Department of Homeless Services, and Department of Health and Mental Hygiene, as well as private sector clients. Beneficiaries are city residents who experience improved public services.

name: Private AI in Airgapped Environments
description: The innovation involves deploying AI models within airgapped environments to enhance security and compliance while accelerating AI adoption in government agencies. This approach allows agencies to run AI models on classified datasets without moving sensitive data to external platforms, thus maintaining data sovereignty and security.
novelty: The innovation is novel because it leverages airgapped environments, traditionally seen as barriers, as enablers of safe innovation. By keeping AI models within controlled environments, agencies can accelerate AI adoption without compromising security, thus aligning speed with sovereignty.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include government agencies, particularly those dealing with classified intelligence and sensitive citizen data. Beneficiaries are public sector leaders and intelligence teams who require secure and compliant AI solutions.

name: Cognitive Sovereignty in Digital Government
description: The innovation focuses on prioritizing human capabilities and cognitive sovereignty in digital government transformation. It emphasizes investing in people rather than solely on technology, ensuring that governments retain their decision-making capabilities and do not overly rely on external partners or AI.
novelty: The project is innovative in its emphasis on 'cognitive sovereignty,' advocating for governments to maintain their own thinking and decision-making capabilities rather than outsourcing them to AI or external partners.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, public sector employees, and citizens who benefit from a more human-centric approach to digital government.

name: European Technological Sovereignty Package
description: The European Technological Sovereignty Package is a set of laws and policies aimed at strengthening the EU's digital autonomy by promoting sovereign cloud, AI, microprocessors, and open source technologies. It includes measures like Union Assurance Levels (UALs) for digital sovereignty and a revamped Chips Act to boost semiconductor production within the EU.
novelty: The package introduces a comprehensive framework for digital sovereignty, including the innovative concept of Union Assurance Levels (UALs) and a significant shift towards open source as a mechanism for transparency and independence.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are public sector CIOs across Europe, European tech providers, and the broader EU tech supply chain. Beneficiaries include EU citizens and public sector organizations seeking greater digital autonomy.

name: Bangsamoro ICT Research and Innovation Conference
organisation: Bangsamoro Information and Communications Technology Office (BICTO)
description: The Bangsamoro ICT Research and Innovation Conference is an event organized by BICTO to showcase research studies aimed at addressing regional challenges, informing data-driven policymaking, and advancing technological innovation in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). The conference includes presentations, poster exhibits, and prototype displays to promote innovation and the adoption of emerging technologies.
novelty: The conference provides a unique platform for collaboration among government, academia, industry, and development partners, focusing on evidence-based policies and creative solutions to improve public services and create new opportunities in the region.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, academic researchers, industry professionals, and development partners, with beneficiaries being the residents of the Bangsamoro region who will benefit from improved public services and technological advancements.

name: KLID AI Issue Report
organisation: Korea Local Information Research & Development Institute
description: The KLID AI Issue Report analyzes AI-related pledges by 16 newly elected provincial governors in South Korea, focusing on utilizing AI for resident-focused services, industrial clusters, manufacturing transformation, and specialized industry convergence. It highlights the importance of tailored regional AI strategies for administrative innovation and regional industrial competitiveness.
novelty: The report emphasizes the shift from mere technology adoption to the practical application of AI in enhancing resident services and regional industries, advocating for tailored regional strategies and the creation of AI ecosystems.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include local government officials, residents benefiting from improved services, regional industries, and sectors such as education, healthcare, manufacturing, and specialized industries like bio and energy.

name: Free AI Training for City Employees
description: The City of Alexandria, in partnership with InnovateUS, is providing free AI training to its 2,000 employees to enhance their ability to serve residents effectively in a digital world. The training is tailored for the public sector and aims to ensure ethical and secure use of AI technologies.
novelty: The initiative offers no-cost, tailored AI training specifically for public-sector employees, emphasizing ethical and secure use of AI technologies.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the City of Alexandria employees who receive the training, and the residents who benefit from improved public services. InnovateUS is also a key stakeholder as the training provider.

name: Enterprise Cloud Services Agreement with AWS
organisation: New York State Office of Information Technology Services
description: The New York State Office of Information Technology Services signed an agreement with Amazon Web Services to provide AI and cloud services to state agencies, aiming to alleviate procurement hurdles, speed up innovation, and provide technology and training tools to the workforce, resulting in $58 million in savings.
novelty: The project consolidates enterprise agreements to reduce costs and streamline service delivery, making AI and cloud services more accessible to state employees, enhancing their capabilities and efficiency.
stakeholders: State agencies, New York State workforce, taxpayers, Amazon Web Services as a partner.

name: UKG file and case management platform
organisation: State of Connecticut, Department of Administrative Services
description: The UKG file and case management platform was implemented to improve efficiency and automation in state agencies' processes, such as maintaining employee records, processing transfers, and applying for ADA and FMLA.
novelty: The platform introduced automation and improved efficiency in handling HR processes within state agencies, which was a significant change from previous manual methods.
stakeholders: State employees, HR departments, and various state agencies in Connecticut are the primary users and beneficiaries of the UKG platform.

name: Portsmouth Site Redevelopment
description: The Portsmouth Site is undergoing decontamination and demolition of legacy buildings to make way for future industries, including AI infrastructure and nuclear energy, aimed at creating generational jobs and fostering long-term economic growth in southern Ohio.
novelty: The project innovatively repurposes a former industrial site for cutting-edge industries like AI and nuclear energy, transforming a legacy site into a hub for future economic development.
stakeholders: Local community members, potential future industry players in AI and nuclear energy, and the U.S. Department of Energy.

name: Extract tool
organisation: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG), Incubator for AI (i.AI)
description: The Extract tool automates the processing of complex planning documents, saving councils significant manual work by digesting documents into digital form. It has been rolled out to all councils across England after successful trials.
novelty: The tool leverages AI to automate and streamline the processing of complex planning documents, significantly reducing manual workload for local councils.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include local councils across England, particularly planning officers who handle complex documents. Stakeholders engaged include MHCLG, i.AI, and Google Cloud for infrastructure support.

name: AI Adoption and Innovation in Pennsylvania
description: Pennsylvania has been recognized as a national leader in AI adoption and innovation under Governor Shapiro's leadership. The state has implemented AI across various stages including readiness, piloting, implementation, and impact, with initiatives such as an AI Literacy Toolkit, an AI Task Force, and over 50 AI pilot projects.
novelty: The innovation lies in Pennsylvania's comprehensive approach to AI adoption, including the establishment of governance structures, piloting innovative AI use cases, and integrating AI into core IT strategies to improve government efficiency and service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Pennsylvania residents, Commonwealth employees, AI developers, and the state government, particularly the Department of State and the Attorney General's office.

name: AI Workforce Upskilling Programs
organisation: City Governments of Washington D.C., San Jose, Seattle, Cleveland
description: City governments in the United States are implementing AI workforce upskilling programs to ensure public employees are prepared to use AI systems effectively. These programs include mandatory AI training, hands-on development projects, and structured governance frameworks to integrate AI literacy into operational processes.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI training directly into workforce development and operational processes, ensuring that AI adoption is not just a technological upgrade but a comprehensive strategy involving employee engagement and governance.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are city government employees, public service departments, and residents who benefit from improved service delivery. Educational institutions and industry partners are also involved in training and capacity building.

name: Readiness Assessment Methodology (RAM) on Artificial Intelligence
description: The RAM on Artificial Intelligence is a methodology used to assess the readiness of countries in adopting AI technologies ethically and inclusively. It documents AI's impact on public services in El Salvador, such as AI-assisted diagnostics, telemedicine, and educational tools, projecting significant economic benefits.
novelty: The RAM is part of the first global normative framework for AI ethics, emphasizing human-centered development and inclusive technological progress. It highlights El Salvador's innovative legal framework and AI governance as a regional leader.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the Salvadoran public, students, and legal professionals. Stakeholders engaged include national authorities, UN agencies, private sector, academia, and civil society.

name: Mais Cidadão
description: Mais Cidadão is a project powered by artificial intelligence aimed at enhancing proactive communication between the government and citizens through multiple digital channels. It delivers personalized information based on government priorities and individual user profiles, such as personalized vaccination reminders and information about the nearest healthcare facilities.
novelty: The project reflects a shift toward designing public services around citizens’ real needs, focusing on personalized and proactive communication using AI.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are Brazilian citizens, while stakeholders include Serpro, Brazil’s Ministry of Health, and potentially other government entities involved in public service delivery.

name: AI and Quantum Computing in Government Services
description: The initiative explores the integration of AI and quantum computing in government services to enhance digital transformation while ensuring digital sovereignty. It focuses on governance, compliance, and risk management to secure innovation.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on integrating cutting-edge technologies like AI and quantum computing in public services, while maintaining strong governance and security frameworks.
stakeholders: Public sector employees, government departments, and citizens who benefit from improved government services and secure digital infrastructure.

name: Responsible AI Implementation in Denmark
organisation: Danish Ministry of Digital Affairs, Danish Task Force for Artificial Intelligence
description: Denmark's approach to AI governance focuses on the responsible adoption of artificial intelligence across the public sector, emphasizing the use of AI to free human resources for tasks requiring human judgement and interaction, thereby improving public services and strengthening trust in public institutions.
novelty: The innovation lies in Denmark's emphasis on using AI not just for efficiency but to enhance human-centric tasks, ensuring that AI supports people and public services rather than replacing human roles.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government representatives, researchers, experts, and the public sector, with beneficiaries being the general public who receive improved public services.

name: ITU Academy Training Centres and Digital Transformation Centres
description: The innovation involves the establishment of ITU Academy Training Centres and Digital Transformation Centres in Trinidad and Tobago to expand connectivity, digital inclusion, and support AI and Digital Public Infrastructure initiatives. It aims to enhance digital skills and foster innovation and entrepreneurship.
novelty: The project is innovative as it focuses on creating dedicated centres for digital transformation and training, which will serve as hubs for developing digital skills and supporting AI initiatives, positioning Trinidad and Tobago as a regional leader in digital transformation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the government of Trinidad and Tobago, the International Telecommunication Union (ITU), public servants, citizens, and businesses in Trinidad and Tobago who will benefit from enhanced digital skills and services.

name: National AI Roadmap 2026-2030
description: A strategic framework for the development and implementation of AI solutions in healthcare, agriculture, education, and public services to boost innovation and improve service delivery.
novelty: The roadmap integrates AI into multiple sectors, focusing on local needs and challenges, and is part of a broader digital transformation agenda.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens in need of improved public services, farmers, students, and healthcare providers. Stakeholders involved in the development include public administration, private sector, universities, research centers, civil society organizations, and international development partners.

name: Housing Helper
description: A generative AI-powered housing advice chatbot designed to help prevent homelessness by providing timely and personalised advice to individuals at risk.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of a generative AI chatbot specifically aimed at preventing homelessness, coupled with a rigorous evaluation through a randomised trial to assess its effectiveness in the public sector.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are individuals at risk of homelessness. Stakeholders include Southwark Council, policymakers, researchers, and evaluators involved in the trial and implementation.

name: AI Integration in County Government
description: The initiative involves using AI to support county employees by improving efficiency, service delivery, and data analysis. AI applications include streamlining permitting, enhancing customer service response times, and improving data-driven decision-making. The project aims to support staff without replacing them, ensuring privacy, transparency, and accountability.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into various county functions while maintaining human oversight and judgment. It emphasizes a 'human-in-the-loop' model and focuses on practical applications like case management and public works.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include county employees, residents of Montgomery County, and policymakers. Beneficiaries are county staff who gain efficiency and residents who receive improved services.

name: AI and Innovation in Public Services
description: A session exploring how artificial intelligence and digital innovation can improve public services by making interactions with local authorities faster and simpler, and enabling smarter decision-making about living and working environments.
novelty: The project is innovative in its focus on integrating AI and digital technologies into public services to enhance efficiency and decision-making, while addressing challenges such as trust, procurement, skills, and data governance.
stakeholders: Local authority leaders, digital teams, public service innovators, civic technology providers, researchers, and the general public interested in AI-enhanced service delivery.

name: Real-Time Information Center (RTIC)
description: The RTIC platform is designed to improve HCPD’s response efficiency, strengthen investigative work, and help officers anticipate criminal activity by consolidating live data from multiple sources into a single operational view. This includes data from body-worn cameras, drones, public and private security cameras, the County's 911 dispatch system, and automated license plate readers.
novelty: The RTIC provides a unified operating picture that supports faster, safer, and more coordinated decision-making by integrating various data sources into one real-time view, eliminating the need for officers to toggle between disconnected systems.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the Howard County Police Department officers and investigators. The stakeholders engaged include the County’s Department of Technology and Communication Services, County government, technology partners, and the community, including residents and business owners who can voluntarily register their security cameras.

name: Digital Public Infrastructure for AI Deployment
organisation: Ministry of Electronics & IT, Networks for Humanity, Biocon Group, World Bank Group, Wadhwani AI, Google & Alphabet
description: The innovation involves using digital public infrastructure and open network architectures to scale AI deployment across sectors like healthcare, agriculture, and public services in India. It focuses on creating inclusive, multilingual, and easy-to-use AI models to ensure wider access and global replicability.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of open networks and digital public infrastructure to enable AI deployment at a population scale, allowing for low-cost, multilingual, and plug-and-play AI solutions that are inclusive and globally replicable.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Ministry of Electronics & IT, Networks for Humanity, Biocon Group, World Bank Group, Wadhwani AI, Google & Alphabet, as well as users and beneficiaries in sectors like healthcare, agriculture, and public services.

name: Vaccine Innovation Centre
organisation: Department of Health – Abu Dhabi, Sanofi
description: The Vaccine Innovation Centre aims to advance vaccine innovation through AI-enabled vaccine discovery, mRNA research, clinical development, and manufacturing. It supports regional and global preparedness for future public health challenges and strengthens Abu Dhabi's contribution to global health security.
novelty: The project integrates AI, mRNA science, and new models connecting discovery, development, and manufacturing within a single ecosystem, accelerating the journey from discovery to patient impact.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include global communities benefiting from improved health outcomes. Stakeholders engaged include the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi, Sanofi, scientific researchers, and healthcare professionals.

name: AI Supercomputer Project
description: The project involves the acquisition of one of the world's 10 largest AI supercomputers, budgeted at nearly R$2 billion, to be used by the public sector, research centers, and private companies for AI training. It aims to enhance digital sovereignty and improve public services through AI applications in various sectors like agriculture, health, and education.
novelty: The supercomputer will be among the 10 fastest in the world, providing high-performance capacity for AI training domestically, which is currently done abroad. It supports Brazil's move towards technological sovereignty and strengthens its digital infrastructure.
stakeholders: Users include public sector entities, research centers, private companies, and Brazilian citizens who will benefit from improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include the Brazilian Government, Science, Technology, and Innovation Ministry, and Management and Innovation in Public Services Ministry.

name: Aspire to Innovate Programme
organisation: Government of Bangladesh, Information and Communication Technology Division, United Nations Development Programme
description: A citizen-centric digital transformation initiative in Bangladesh aimed at accelerating public service delivery through a 'Service Delivery Maturity Model' and a 'Product Management Approach'. The programme focuses on integrating artificial intelligence into public services and fostering transformational leadership among government officials.
novelty: The programme emphasizes a holistic approach to digital transformation by combining technological advances with mindset shifts among government officials. It prioritizes artificial intelligence sovereignty and responsible use, aiming to bridge the digital divide and enhance public service efficiency.
stakeholders: Beneficiaries include Bangladeshi citizens who will experience improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include the Government of Bangladesh, United Nations Development Programme, and international partners like Azerbaijan and Cambodia for knowledge exchange and collaboration.

name: Aires Tide
organisation: Department of Energy’s National Nuclear Security Administration (DOE/NNSA)
description: Aires Tide is a proof-of-concept flight test vehicle developed using artificial intelligence, high-performance computing, and additive manufacturing. It was designed and tested on a compressed timeline and at a lower cost, demonstrating NNSA's ability to rapidly design and deliver national security solutions.
novelty: The innovation combines AI, high-performance computing, and additive manufacturing to develop national security solutions 15 times cheaper and seven times faster than traditional methods.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the NNSA and its National Laboratories, the U.S. Army, and potentially other national security entities. Stakeholders engaged include Los Alamos, Lawrence Livermore, and Sandia National Laboratories, and the Kansas City National Security Campus.

name: National Artificial Intelligence Strategy: AI for All
description: The National Artificial Intelligence Strategy: AI for All aims to bolster AI investment and innovation in Canada, focusing on the health and life sciences sector. It includes a $500 million Canadian Tech Growth Fund, a $200 million AI Missions Program for healthcare, and $100 million each for a Health Sector Data Space and the VITAL health data platform.
novelty: The strategy emphasizes leveraging Canada's universal healthcare system and existing data strengths to become a global leader in AI, with a focus on building public trust in AI through improvements in healthcare.
stakeholders: Canadian founders, healthcare practitioners and providers, investors, and small and medium enterprises (SMEs) are the primary stakeholders engaged in the strategy.

name: Generative AI for Local Storm Reports (LSRs)
organisation: National Weather Service (NWS), Amazon Web Services (AWS)
description: The National Weather Service, in collaboration with AWS, developed a proof of concept using generative AI to automate the extraction and verification of weather and geolocation information from text and images for Local Storm Reports (LSRs). This innovation aims to streamline the creation of LSRs, increasing their consistency, coverage, and number while maintaining scientific accuracy.
novelty: The project uses a serverless AWS architecture and a human-in-the-loop AI system to automate and enhance the creation of Local Storm Reports, integrating generative AI and domain-specific verification agents to simulate expert meteorological review processes.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are NWS forecasters and emergency management partners. The stakeholders include AWS, the Generative AI Innovation Center, and the broader meteorological community.

name: AI and Data-Driven Government Transformation in the GCC
description: The innovation involves embedding AI and data into national strategies to drive economic resilience and long-term competitiveness in the GCC. It focuses on executing large-scale programmes with trusted data, robust governance, and measurable outcomes to enhance public services and economic impact.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on creating unified data platforms, sovereign digital infrastructure, and integrating AI into national strategies for economic transformation. It emphasizes responsible AI governance, citizen-centric services, and investment in talent.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include governments of the GCC countries, citizens benefiting from improved public services, and organisations involved in implementing AI strategies. Beneficiaries are the public sector entities and citizens who experience enhanced services and decision-making.

name: AI Upskilling Program
description: San Jose is training its workforce to use AI tools to improve government efficiency and preserve jobs. The program aims to train 2,500 employees by June 2027, helping them build AI tools for government operations to maintain public service levels amid budget constraints.
novelty: The program uses a train-the-trainer model, allowing employees to bring AI skills back to their departments and train colleagues directly. It focuses on responsible, transparent, and ethical AI implementation.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries are San Jose city employees and residents. Stakeholders include city officials, the Mayor, City Manager, and public agencies nationwide through the GovAI initiative.

name: AI Data Center
organisation: Kyivstar, VEON, Ministry of Economy, Environment and Agriculture of Ukraine
description: The AI Data Center is a planned sovereign artificial intelligence data center in Ukraine aimed at developing digital infrastructure, strengthening technological sovereignty, and supporting the Ukrainian economy. It will provide local, scalable computing power for AI applications across various sectors, ensuring data processing and storage within Ukraine.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on creating a sovereign AI data center that ensures data processing and storage within Ukraine, maintaining control over sensitive information and complying with national regulatory requirements.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government institutions, businesses, and sectors such as public services, finance, medicine, and defense. Stakeholders engaged include Kyivstar, VEON, and the Ukrainian government.

name: NJ AI Library Learning Network
organisation: New Jersey AI Hub, LibraryLinkNJ, New Jersey State Library
description: The NJ AI Library Learning Network is a statewide initiative in New Jersey that uses public libraries to deliver AI literacy and workforce skills programs. It aims to provide practical AI learning opportunities through training, curriculum, resources, and support, focusing on AI for All, AI for Jobseekers, and AI for Small Business.
novelty: The initiative leverages the existing library network to deliver AI education, extending learning opportunities beyond traditional educational institutions and commercial providers, and integrating AI literacy into community settings.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include New Jersey residents, jobseekers, and small business owners. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include public libraries, the New Jersey AI Hub, LibraryLinkNJ, the New Jersey State Library, and Microsoft TechSpark.

name: Pennsylvania's Digital Transformation
organisation: Pennsylvania Department of Transportation, Allegheny County
description: Pennsylvania agencies are using cloud platforms, artificial intelligence, and incremental modernization strategies to improve service delivery and strengthen operations.
novelty: The project focuses on practical use cases that deliver measurable outcomes through incremental improvements rather than large-scale technology overhauls.
stakeholders: State and local government leaders, Pennsylvania agencies, technology leaders, and constituents benefiting from improved public services.

name: Claude AI Assistant
description: California has negotiated a contract with Anthropic to provide state and local agencies access to Claude, an AI assistant, at a 50% discounted rate. The aim is to streamline administrative work, analyze information, draft documents, and improve constituent services, while ensuring accuracy, transparency, and privacy.
novelty: The innovation lies in the enterprise agreement approach, allowing multiple agencies to share best practices and establish governance standards, rather than isolated AI pilots. It also emphasizes responsible and transparent use of AI to augment, not replace, human workers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include California state and local government agencies, public employees, and ultimately the residents of California who benefit from improved public services.

name: Sovereign AI
description: SERPRO's Sovereign AI initiative aims to build a national AI platform using large language models trained in Portuguese, operated on Brazilian infrastructure, to modernize public-sector services, automate processes, improve decision-making, and reduce reliance on foreign technology.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on creating a secure, locally governed AI platform tailored to Brazil's needs, emphasizing multilingual capabilities and public-grade performance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Brazilian federal government, public-sector organizations, and potentially other companies in Brazil. Beneficiaries are the Brazilian public sector and citizens who will experience improved public services.

name: Palantir's Intelligent Engine with NVIDIA Nemotron Open Models
description: Palantir's new intelligent engine utilizes NVIDIA Nemotron open models to serve U.S. government agencies, allowing them to build custom frontier-quality AI models. This innovation enables government agencies to run customized models on their own infrastructure, train on their own data, and retain full ownership of the resulting models, enhancing operational efficiency and security.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of NVIDIA Nemotron open models into air-gapped environments, allowing for secure, customizable, and transparent AI model deployment in sensitive government operations.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are U.S. government agencies and their operators, who benefit from enhanced AI capabilities. Secondary stakeholders include enterprises and developers who can leverage the open models for various applications.

name: Automated Identity & Access Management (AIAM) system
description: The AIAM system is designed to address the high administrative burden and lack of standardised account management in Singapore's healthcare sector, simplifying identity management for healthcare professionals.
novelty: The system automates identity and access management, reducing complexity and administrative workload in the healthcare sector.
stakeholders: Healthcare professionals in Singapore, Synapxe as the implementing agency, and indirectly, patients benefiting from more efficient healthcare services.

name: Innovative Communication Methods for Audit Reports
organisation: Office of the State Comptroller and Ombudsman of Israel
description: The Office of the State Comptroller and Ombudsman of Israel has adopted innovative communication methods to make audit reports more accessible and engaging. These methods include video infographics, social media engagement, and AI tools to transform traditional audit reports into human-centered narratives that are easily understandable by the general public.
novelty: The innovation lies in transforming dense, jargon-heavy audit reports into engaging, visual, and emotionally resonant narratives using modern communication tools like videos, social media, and AI, making them accessible to a broader audience.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include the general public, government agencies, and decision-makers. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include auditors, communication specialists, and technology developers.

name: GridSearch
organisation: Brookhaven National Laboratory, Amazon Web Services
description: GridSearch is an AI-driven solution designed to transform how new generation facilities connect to America’s electric grid, using a Grid Foundation Model to enable faster grid simulations with accuracy and robustness.
novelty: GridSearch leverages AI to significantly accelerate grid simulations, providing critical decision support to stakeholders and optimizing the data center interconnection process with minimal disruption.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include stakeholders involved in the electric grid and data center interconnection processes, such as energy companies and grid operators.

name: CIA's New Acquisition Framework
description: The CIA has implemented a new acquisition framework to speed up the adoption of artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies. This framework aims to reduce the time for acquiring new technologies from 24 months to six months by centralizing vendor vetting and streamlining authorization processes.
novelty: The innovation lies in drastically reducing the acquisition timelines for new technologies, enabling the CIA to stay ahead in technological advancements and maintain national security effectively.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the CIA and its officers, who will benefit from faster access to cutting-edge technologies. The private sector and technology vendors are also stakeholders, as the new framework provides a more structured approach for industry partnerships.

name: No10 Innovation Fellowship
description: The No10 Innovation Fellowship recruits top talent in data science, coding, and AI to develop digital solutions for government departments. Fellows work on projects like creating AI tools for prison officers and developing a website for parents to check childcare entitlements.
novelty: The fellowship brings experts from prestigious institutions like CERN, NASA, and Y-Combinator into government roles, allowing them to create tailored digital solutions that improve efficiency and service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include government departments, civil servants, prison officers, parents checking childcare entitlements, and ultimately the public who benefit from improved government services.

name: Xoli
organisation: Mexico City’s Agencia de Innovacion Publica, University of California San Diego Halıcıoğlu School of Data Science and Computing
description: Xoli is an AI-powered tourism assistant designed to help tourists navigate Mexico City, discover attractions, access local information, and receive real-time assistance during the FIFA World Cup. The system was tested and scaled to handle large numbers of simultaneous users, ensuring efficient performance during the event.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of advanced computing resources and expertise in deploying large language models to scale an AI assistant for real-time, large-scale public service applications during a major international event.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include tourists visiting Mexico City for the FIFA World Cup. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the developers from Mexico City’s Agencia de Innovacion Publica and research scientists from the University of California San Diego.

name: AI25
description: AI25 is an annual honor recognizing 25 leaders in Ohio who are making significant impacts in artificial intelligence across various sectors such as startups, education, healthcare, and government. The initiative aims to highlight the real-world applications and benefits of AI in Ohio.
novelty: AI25 focuses on recognizing individuals who are actively implementing AI solutions in practical, impactful ways across Ohio, rather than just theoretical or future potential applications. It emphasizes the convergence of investment, innovation, and education in building Ohio's AI economy.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include AI innovators, enterprise leaders, public-sector champions, educators, and investors in Ohio. Beneficiaries are the broader Ohio community, including students, professionals, and public service sectors benefiting from AI advancements.

name: CloudSPARCC
description: CloudSPARCC is a multicloud FinOps solution powered by Amazon QuickSight, designed to deliver a unified visualization into cloud billing data. It centralizes data and insights to help FinOps teams make timely, actionable decisions, and is currently deployed at multiple large government agencies.
novelty: The innovation integrates CloudSPARCC with Amazon Quick capabilities, providing a centralized platform for cloud financial management. It features AI-powered chat agents for conversational data access, Quick Flows for automation, and Quick Research for complex analysis, enhancing the speed and efficiency of FinOps workflows.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are FinOps teams within large government agencies. Other stakeholders include IT departments and financial managers who benefit from streamlined cloud financial management and insights.

name: AI Acceptable Use Policies for Local Government Employees
description: Local governments are developing acceptable use policies for AI tools to establish guidelines for employees. These policies address data privacy, records retention, transparency, and accountability, ensuring responsible AI use in government workplaces.
novelty: The innovation lies in creating structured policies for AI use in government, focusing on responsible use, data privacy, and human oversight, which are not commonly addressed in traditional government operations.
stakeholders: Local government employees, public sector leaders, residents, and organizations like MRSC and the Partnership for Public Service are involved in implementing and benefiting from these policies.

name: Long-Term AI Governance Architecture
description: The initiative focuses on creating a sustainable institutional architecture and capacity for long-term AI governance in Georgia. It aims to establish clear institutional mandates and structures that support responsible AI development, align with European AI governance practices, and strengthen institutional capacity and AI literacy across government.
novelty: The innovation lies in its tailored approach to AI governance, emphasizing the creation of institutions based on practical experience and national needs, rather than adopting a one-size-fits-all model. It also integrates European AI governance practices to ensure future compatibility.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government institutions responsible for AI policy, innovation support, risk monitoring, and implementation evaluation. Beneficiaries are the public institutions and the broader society that will be impacted by responsible AI governance.

name: AI Nuclear Licensing Review Acceleration
description: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has implemented artificial intelligence (AI) to accelerate the nuclear licensing review process, reducing the time required from four years to nine months.
novelty: The use of AI significantly reduces the time required for nuclear licensing reviews, demonstrating a substantial improvement in efficiency and processing speed.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the NRC, nuclear energy companies seeking licenses, and potentially the General Services Administration, which provides AI tools to federal agencies.

name: Workforce Reimagined 2026
description: Workforce Reimagined 2026 is an initiative that brings together government and industry leaders to discuss and implement new workforce policies and strategies in the federal sector. It focuses on modernizing workforce practices, including skills-based hiring, performance management reforms, and AI-enabled talent strategies, to build a more agile and mission-ready workforce.
novelty: The initiative is innovative in its comprehensive approach to transforming federal workforce practices through skills-based hiring, AI integration, and performance management reforms, aiming to address critical skills gaps and improve recruitment and retention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, government leaders, industry experts, and federal employees who are the beneficiaries of the workforce modernization efforts.

name: Global Government Data Summit
description: The Global Government Data Summit is an annual event that brings together data leaders from around the world to discuss key challenges and share best practices in data governance, AI, data sovereignty, and data sharing in government. The summit aims to foster open dialogue and improve data infrastructure in government.
novelty: The summit provides a platform for candid discussions under the Chatham House Rule, allowing participants to speak freely about data governance, AI, and data sovereignty. It emphasizes the importance of data as a national asset and explores innovative solutions for data sharing and governance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include data leaders, civil servants, government departments, and knowledge partners like NTT Data and Deloitte. Beneficiaries are government agencies and the public sector, which aim to improve services through better data governance and AI integration.

name: AI Literacy Training for Civil Servants
description: A training program aimed at educating public officials on AI ethics, governance, risk management, and responsible use, rather than just technical skills. It helps officials connect ethical and governance issues to their professional responsibilities.
novelty: The program shifts focus from technical AI skills to ethical and governance issues, emphasizing the importance of responsible AI governance and the role of public officials in overseeing AI systems.
stakeholders: Public officials, civil servants, and the broader public who benefit from improved AI governance.

name: AI Triage Tool and AI Notetaking Tools
description: The NHS is rolling out new AI tools, including an AI triage tool in the NHS App to direct patients to appropriate services and AI notetaking tools to reduce administrative tasks for staff. These innovations aim to cut waiting times and improve care for millions of patients.
novelty: The AI triage tool adapts questions based on patient responses to provide a detailed view of conditions, directing patients to the right care. AI notetaking tools, or ambient voice technology, free up clinicians to spend more time with patients by reducing time spent on documentation.
stakeholders: Users include NHS patients and staff. Beneficiaries are patients who receive timely care and clinicians who can focus more on patient interaction. Stakeholders engaged include NHS trusts, clinicians, and technology providers.

name: Ultimate Urban Circulator
description: The Ultimate Urban Circulator is the nation's first deployment of an autonomous vehicle service in public transit, aimed at enhancing mobility solutions in Jacksonville.
novelty: It is innovative as it represents the first deployment of autonomous vehicles in public transit in the United States.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Jacksonville Transportation Authority, residents of Jacksonville and surrounding counties, and users of public transit in the region.

name: Improving Geneva Public Services via AI Agents
organisation: State of Geneva, Department of Institutions and Digital Technology
description: The project involves the use of AI agents to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of public services in Geneva. The initiative aims to streamline processes and improve service delivery to the public.
novelty: The innovation lies in the application of AI technology to public services, which is expected to transform traditional service delivery methods and improve interaction between the government and citizens.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the residents of Geneva who will benefit from improved public services, as well as the government officials and departments involved in implementing the AI solutions.

name: Armenia's AI Ecosystem Development
description: Armenia is developing a competitive, responsible, and inclusive AI ecosystem aimed at driving economic growth, modernizing the public sector, and improving public services. The initiative focuses on building digital infrastructure, enhancing AI literacy, and training specialists.
novelty: The project is innovative in its comprehensive approach to building an AI ecosystem that emphasizes AI literacy as a national priority and integrates ethical principles and human rights into AI governance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public servants, private sector professionals, educators, citizens, and policymakers, all of whom are engaged in using AI tools effectively and responsibly.

name: AI Integration in Public Services
organisation: Ministry of Finance and Economy, South Korea
description: The South Korean government is implementing AI technologies in public services, including tax payment and administrative services, to enhance efficiency and innovation in the service sector.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive integration of AI technologies across various public service domains, aiming to transform and modernize the service industry.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include South Korean citizens who will benefit from improved public services, businesses involved in AI technology, and government agencies responsible for implementation.

name: IIT Madras Zanzibar (IITMZ)
description: IIT Madras Zanzibar is the first international campus of IIT Madras, aimed at boosting innovation across multiple sectors in Zanzibar. It offers Master's programs in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, and Technology in Ocean Structure, providing a platform for young people to innovate and thrive.
novelty: This is the only IIT Madras campus operating outside Chennai, India, and one of only two in the world, representing a significant investment in the region's human capital and innovation ecosystem.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar, IIT Madras, students from East Africa and other parts of the world, and private-sector partners. Beneficiaries are young people in Zanzibar and East Africa who gain educational and innovation opportunities.

name: Artificial Intelligence Strategy for 2025-2028
organisation: Ministry of Digital Development and Transport
description: Azerbaijan is integrating AI into public-sector governance, standards, procurement, and e-government infrastructure as part of its 2026-2028 digital-development action plan. This includes legislative changes affecting 34 laws and aims to enhance the country's digital regulation framework.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive integration of AI into national policy, affecting governance, standards, and legal frameworks, rather than focusing on a single product launch.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government bodies, AI and data teams, public-sector entities, and potentially the general public who will benefit from improved e-government services.

name: Centre for the Fourth Industrial Revolution in Baku
description: The Centre for the Fourth Industrial Revolution in Baku is a regional center established to develop a modern digital economy ecosystem in Azerbaijan. It aims to position Azerbaijan among the leading forces in global transformation by fostering digitalization, attracting investment, generating employment, and enhancing workforce competitiveness.
novelty: The Centre is one of the relatively few Centres for the Fourth Industrial Revolution worldwide, showcasing Azerbaijan's commitment to integrating advanced technologies like artificial intelligence and cloud technologies into its economy and governance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Azerbaijani government, technology companies, innovative businesses, investors, and the general public who will benefit from improved public services and economic opportunities.

name: MY Bharat (Mera Yuva Bharat)
organisation: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
description: MY Bharat is a digital platform launched to engage the youth of India in nation-building through opportunities for volunteering, skill development, leadership, and civic participation. It connects the government, educational institutions, non-profit organisations, and industry to provide meaningful engagement for young Indians.
novelty: The platform integrates digital technology with physical engagement, offering a unified digital space for youth participation. It leverages India's Digital Public Infrastructure to provide seamless access to opportunities, learning resources, and government initiatives, using AI and multilingual technologies.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are the youth of India, including students and young professionals. Stakeholders include the government, educational institutions, non-profit organisations, and industries that collaborate to provide opportunities for youth engagement.

name: AI for Government Training Programme
organisation: Ethiopian Artificial Intelligence Institute, Addis Ababa University, United Nations Development Programme
description: A training programme designed to enhance the artificial intelligence capabilities of Ethiopian federal government employees, focusing on improving service delivery and institutional efficiency through AI.
novelty: The programme introduces government employees to generative AI systems and prompt engineering techniques, emphasizing practical application in public administration.
stakeholders: Federal government employees in Ethiopia, Ethiopian Artificial Intelligence Institute, Addis Ababa University, United Nations Development Programme.

name: AI Agents for Public Service Technology Redevelopment
organisation: Ministry of Technology and Innovation, Government of Alberta
description: The Alberta government is using advanced AI tools developed by Anthropic and Google to overhaul its decades-old public service technology. This initiative aims to modernize critical systems, enhance cybersecurity, and reduce costs significantly. The project includes the development of AI agents that can independently review and update computer code, replacing 165 systems with 16 new applications owned by the government.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of AI agents, specifically developed using Anthropic’s Claude AI models, to perform tasks that would traditionally take years in just hours. It demonstrates a practical approach to addressing technical debt and security issues in legacy systems, setting a precedent for other governments.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the Alberta government, public servants, and the general public of Alberta who will benefit from improved public service technology. Other governments can also benefit from the open-source Velocity White Papers shared by Alberta.

name: Claude Code Implementation
description: The Government of Alberta implemented Claude Code with Opus and Sonnet models to review and secure its systems by scanning 466 million lines of code in 20 hours, identifying and fixing vulnerabilities, and modernizing outdated systems.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to perform large-scale, rapid security reviews and code modernization, achieving in hours what would traditionally take years.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Alberta Ministry of Technology and Innovation, government workers, the public, and potentially other governments looking to adopt similar practices.
